text.NRSV: Over the table of the bread of the Presence they shall spread a blue cloth, and put on it the plates, the dishes for incense, the bowls, and the flagons for the drink offering; the regular bread also shall be on it;
text.Rotherham: And over the Presence-table, shall they spread a wrapper of blue and put thereon the dishes and the pans and the bowls and the libation cups, the perpetual bread also, thereupon, shall remain;
text.KJV: 4:7 And vpon the table of Shewbread they shall spread a cloth of blew, and put thereon the dishes, and the spoones, and the bowles, and couers to couer withall: and the continual bread shalbe thereon.
text.NABRE: On the table of the Presence they shall spread a violet cloth and put on it the plates and cups, as well as the bowls and pitchers for libations; the established bread offering shall remain on the table.

text.NRSV: then they shall spread over them a crimson cloth, and cover it with a covering of fine leather, and shall put its poles in place.
text.Rotherham: and they shall spread over them a wrapper of crimson, and cover the same with a covering of badgers’ skin,—and shall put in the staves thereof.
text.KJV: 4:8 And they shall spread vpon them a clothe of scarlet, and couer the same with a couering of badgers skinnes, and shall put in the staues thereof.
text.NABRE: Over these they shall spread a scarlet cloth and cover it with a covering of yellow-orange skin, and put the poles in place.

text.NRSV: They shall take a blue cloth, and cover the lampstand for the light, with its lamps, its snuffers, its trays, and all the vessels for oil with which it is supplied;
text.Rotherham: Then shall they take a wrapper of blue, and cover the light-giving lampstand, and the lamps thereof, and the snuffers thereof and the snuff-trays thereof,—and all the oil-vessels, thereof, wherewith they minister thereunto;
text.KJV: 4:9 And they shall take a cloth of blew, and couer the candlesticke of the light, and his lampes, and his tongs, and his snuffe dishes, and all the oyle vessels thereof, wherewith they minister vnto it.
text.NABRE: They shall use a violet cloth to cover the menorah of the light with its lamps, tongs, and trays, as well as the various containers of oil from which it is supplied.

text.NRSV: and they shall put it with all its utensils in a covering of fine leather, and put it on the carrying frame.
text.Rotherham: and shall put the same and all the vessels thereof, within a covering of, badgers’ skin,—and shall put it upon a frame.
text.KJV: 4:10 And they shall put it, and all the vessels thereof, within a couering of badgers skinnes, and shall put it vpon a barre.
text.NABRE: The menorah with all its utensils they shall then put in a covering of yellow-orange skin, and place on a litter.

text.NRSV: Over the golden altar they shall spread a blue cloth, and cover it with a covering of fine leather, and shall put its poles in place;
text.Rotherham: And upon the altar of gold, shall they spread a wrapper of blue, and shall cover the same in a covering of badgers’ skin,—and shall put in the staves thereof,
text.KJV: 4:11 And vpon the golden Altar they shall spread a cloth of blew, and couer it with a couering of badgers skinnes, and shall put to the staues thereof.
text.NABRE: Over the golden altar they shall spread a violet cloth, and cover this also with a covering of yellow-orange skin, and put the poles in place.

text.NRSV: and they shall take all the utensils of the service that are used in the sanctuary, and put them in a blue cloth, and cover them with a covering of fine leather, and put them on the carrying frame.
text.Rotherham: And they shall take all the utensils of attendance wherewith they attend in the sanctuary, and put them within a wrapper of blue, and cover the same in a covering of badgers’ skin,—and put them on a frame.
text.KJV: 4:12 And they shall take all the instruments of ministery, wherewith they minister in the Sanctuary, and put them in a cloth of blew, and couer them with a couering of badgers skinnes, and shall put them on a barre.
text.NABRE: Taking the utensils of the sanctuary service, they shall put them all in violet cloth and cover them with a covering of yellow-orange skin. They shall then place them on a litter.

text.NRSV: They shall take away the ashes from the altar, and spread a purple cloth over it;
text.Rotherham: Then shall they remove the fat-ashes from the altar,—and spread over it a wrapper of purple;
text.KJV: 4:13 And they shall take away the ashes from the Altar, and spread a purple cloth thereon:
text.NABRE: After cleansing the altar of its ashes, they shall spread a purple cloth over it.

text.NRSV: and they shall put on it all the utensils of the altar, which are used for the service there, the firepans, the forks, the shovels, and the basins, all the utensils of the altar; and they shall spread on it a covering of fine leather, and shall put its poles in place.
text.Rotherham: and put thereupon all the utensils thereof wherewith they minister thereupon—the fire-pans, the flesh-hooks—and the shovels and the tossing-bowls, all the utensils of the altar,—and shall spread over it a covering of badgers’ skin and put in the staves thereof.
text.KJV: 4:14 And they shall put vpon it all the vessels thereof, wherewith they minister about it, euen the censers, the fleshhookes, and the shouels, and the basons, all the vessels of the Altar: and they shall spread vpon it a couering of badgers skinnes, and put to the staues of it.
text.NABRE: On this they shall put all the utensils with which it is served: the fire pans, forks, shovels, basins, and all the utensils of the altar. They shall then spread a covering of yellow-orange skin over this, and put the poles in place.

text.NRSV: When Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all the furnishings of the sanctuary, as the camp sets out, after that the Kohathites shall come to carry these, but they must not touch the holy things, or they will die. These are the things of the tent of meeting that the Kohathites are to carry.
text.Rotherham: So shall Aaron and his sons make an end of covering the sanctuary, and all the utensils of the sanctuary, when the camp is to set forward, then after that, shall the sons of Kohath enter to bear it, but they must not put forth a touch unto that which, is holy, else should they die, these, shall be the burden of the sons of Kohath in the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: 4:15 And when Aaron and his sonnes haue made an end of couering the Sanctuary, and all the vessels of the Sanctuary, as the campe is to set forward; after that, the sonnes of Kohath shall come to beare it: but they shal not touch any holy thing, lest they die. These things are the burden of the sonnes of Kohath in the Tabernacle of the Congregation.
text.NABRE: Only after Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sacred objects and all their utensils on breaking camp, can the Kohathites enter to carry them. But they shall not touch the sacred objects; if they do they will die. These, then, are the objects in the tent of meeting that the Kohathites shall carry.

text.NRSV: Eleazar son of Aaron the priest shall have charge of the oil for the light, the fragrant incense, the regular grain offering, and the anointing oil, the supervision of all the tabernacle and all that is in it, in the sanctuary and in its utensils.
text.Rotherham: But the care of Eleazar son of Aaron the priest, [shall be] —the light-giving oil, and the fragrant incense, and the perpetual meal-offering, and the anointing oil, the care of all the habitation, and all that is therein, both as to the sanctuary, and as to the furniture thereof.
text.KJV: 4:16 ¶ And to the office of Eleazar the sonne of Aaron the Priest, perteineth the oile for the light, and the sweet incense, and the dayly meat offering, and the anoynting oyle, and the ouersight of all the Tabernacle, and of all that therein is, in the Sanctuary, and in the vessels thereof.
text.NABRE: Eleazar, son of Aaron the priest, shall be in charge of the oil for the light, the fragrant incense, the established grain offering, and the anointing oil. He shall be in charge of the whole tabernacle with all the sacred objects and utensils that are in it.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying:
text.KJV: 4:17 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, and vnto Aaron, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses and Aaron:

text.NRSV: You must not let the tribe of the clans of the Kohathites be destroyed from among the Levites.
text.Rotherham: Do not let the tribe of the families of the Kohathites be cut off out of the midst of the Levites.
text.KJV: 4:18 Cut ye not off the tribe of the families of the Kohathites, from among the Leuites.
text.NABRE: Do not let the group of Kohathite clans perish from among the Levites.

text.NRSV: This is how you must deal with them in order that they may live and not die when they come near to the most holy things: Aaron and his sons shall go in and assign each to a particular task or burden.
text.Rotherham: This, therefore, do ye for them, so shall they live and not die when they approach the most holy place,—Aaron and his sons shall enter and set them man by man over his labour, and unto his burden;
text.KJV: 4:19 But thus doe vnto them, that they may liue, and not die: when they approche vnto the most Holy things, Aaron and his sonnes shall goe in, and appoint them euery one to his seruice, and to his burden.
text.NABRE: That they may live and not die when they approach the most sacred objects, this is what you shall do for them: Aaron and his sons shall go in and assign to each of them his task and what he must carry;

text.NRSV: But the Kohathites must not go in to look on the holy things even for a moment; otherwise they will die.
text.Rotherham: but they shall not enter to see, for a moment, that which is holy, else should they die.
text.KJV: 4:20 But they shall not goe in to see when the holy things are couered, lest they die.
text.NABRE: but the Kohathites shall not go in to look upon the sacred objects even for an instant, or they will die.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 4:21 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Take a census of the Gershonites also, by their ancestral houses and by their clans;
text.Rotherham: reckon up the sum of the sons of Gershon even of them also,—by their ancestral house by their families;
text.KJV: 4:22 Take also the summe of the sonnes of Gershon, throughout the houses of their fathers, by their families:
text.NABRE: Take a census of the Gershonites also, by ancestral houses and clans,

text.NRSV: from thirty years old up to fifty years old you shall enroll them, all who qualify to do work in the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: from thirty years old and upwards even to fifty years, shalt thou number them,—All that may enter to take rank in the host, to do laborious work in the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: 4:23 From thirtie yeeres old and vpward, vntill fiftie yeeres old shalt thou number them: all that enter in to performe the seruice, to doe the worke in the Tabernacle of the Congregation.
text.NABRE: enrolling all between thirty and fifty years of age who will join the personnel to do the work in the tent of meeting.

text.NRSV: This is the service of the clans of the Gershonites, in serving and bearing burdens:
text.Rotherham: This, shall be the labour of the families of the Gershonites,—in labouring and in bearing:
text.KJV: 4:24 This is the seruice of the families of the Gershonites, to serue, and for burdens.
text.NABRE: This is the task of the clans of the Gershonites, what they must do and what they must carry:

text.NRSV: they shall carry the curtains of the tabernacle, and the tent of meeting with its covering, and the outer covering of fine leather that is on top of it, and the screen for the entrance of the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: they shall bear the curtains of the habitation and the tent of meeting, the covering thereof and the covering of badgers’ skin which is over above it,—and the screen, at the entrance of the tent of meeting;
text.KJV: 4:25 And they shall beare the curtaines of the Tabernacle, and the Tabernacle of the Congregation; his couering, and the couering of the badgers skinnes that is aboue vpon it, and the hanging for the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation:
text.NABRE: they shall carry the curtains of the tabernacle, the tent of meeting with its covering and the outer wrapping of yellow-orange skin, the curtain at the entrance of the tent of meeting,

text.NRSV: and the hangings of the court, and the screen for the entrance of the gate of the court that is around the tabernacle and the altar, and their cords, and all the equipment for their service; and they shall do all that needs to be done with regard to them.
text.Rotherham: and the hangings of the court, and the screen for the entrance of the gate of the court which is near the habitation and near the altar round about, and their cords, and all their articles of service, and all that is to be done to them when they shall do their labour,
text.KJV: 4:26 And the hangings of the Court, and the hanging for the doore of the gate of the Court which is by the Tabernacle, and by the Altar round about, and their cords, and all the instruments of their seruice, and all that is made for them: so shall they serue.
text.NABRE: the hangings of the court, the curtain at the entrance to the gate of the court that encloses both the tabernacle and the altar, together with their ropes and all other objects necessary for their use. Whatever is to be done to maintain these things, they shall do.

text.NRSV: All the service of the Gershonites shall be at the command of Aaron and his sons, in all that they are to carry, and in all that they have to do; and you shall assign to their charge all that they are to carry.
text.Rotherham: At the bidding of Aaron and his sons, shall be all the labour of the sons of the Gershonites, to the extent of their every burden, and to the extent of all their labour,—so shall ye appoint unto them in charge their every burden.
text.KJV: 4:27 At the appointment of Aaron and his sonnes, shall be all the seruice of the sonnes of the Gershonites, in all their burdens, and in all their seruice: and yee shall appoint vnto them in charge all their burdens.
text.NABRE: The service of the Gershonites shall be entirely under the direction of Aaron and his sons, with regard to what they must carry and what they must do; you shall list for them by name what they are to carry.

text.NRSV: This is the service of the clans of the Gershonites in the tent of meeting, and their duties are to be under the oversight of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
text.Rotherham: This, shall be the labour of the families of the sons of the Gershonites in the tent of meeting,—and, the charge of them, shall be in the hand of Ithamar, son of Aaron the priest.
text.KJV: 4:28 This is the seruice of the families of the sonnes of Gershon, in the Tabernacle of the Congregation: and their charge shalbe vnder the hande of Ithamar the sonne of Aaron the Priest.
text.NABRE: This, then, is the task of the clans of the Gershonites in the tent of meeting; and they shall be under the supervision of Ithamar, son of Aaron the priest.

text.NRSV: As for the Merarites, you shall enroll them by their clans and their ancestral houses;
text.Rotherham: As for the sons of Merari,—by their families by their ancestral house, shall ye number them;
text.KJV: 4:29 ¶ As for the sonnes of Merari, thou shalt number them after their families, by the house of their fathers:
text.NABRE: The Merarites, too, you shall enroll by clans and ancestral houses,

text.NRSV: from thirty years old up to fifty years old you shall enroll them, everyone who qualifies to do the work of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: from thirty years old and upwards even to fifty years, shall ye number them,—all that enter into the host, to do laborious work in the tent of meeting,
text.KJV: 4:30 From thirty yeeres old and vpward, euen vnto fiftie yeeres old shalt thou number them, euery one that entreth in to the seruice, to doe the worke of the Tabernacle of the Congregation.
text.NABRE: enrolling all between thirty and fifty years of age who will join the personnel to maintain the tent of meeting.

text.NRSV: This is what they are charged to carry, as the whole of their service in the tent of meeting: the frames of the tabernacle, with its bars, pillars, and bases,
text.Rotherham: And, this, shall be their charge of the burdens, to the extent of all their labour in the tent of meeting, the boards of the habitation, and the bars thereof and he pillars thereof and the sockets thereof:
text.KJV: 4:31 And this is the charge of their burden, according to all their seruice, in the Tabernacle of the Congregation, the boards of the Tabernacle, and the barres thereof, and the pillars thereof, and sockets thereof:
text.NABRE: This is what they shall be responsible for carrying, with respect to all their service in the tent of meeting: the boards of the tabernacle with its bars, columns and pedestals,

text.NRSV: and the pillars of the court all around with their bases, pegs, and cords, with all their equipment and all their related service; and you shall assign by name the objects that they are required to carry.
text.Rotherham: and the pillars of the court round about and their sockets and their pins and their cords, to the extent of all their articles, to the extent of all their labour, and by names, shall ye put under their care the articles of their charge of burdens.
text.KJV: 4:32 And the pillars of the Court round about, and their sockets, and their pinnes, and their coards, with all their instruments, and with all their seruice: and by name yee shall reckon the instruments of the charge of their burden.
text.NABRE: and the columns of the surrounding court with their pedestals, pegs and ropes, including all their accessories and everything for their maintenance. You shall list by name the objects they shall be responsible for carrying.

text.NRSV: This is the service of the clans of the Merarites, the whole of their service in the tent of meeting, under the direction of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
text.Rotherham: This, shall be the labour of the families of the sons of Merari, to the extent of all their labour in the tent of meeting,—in the hand of Ithamar, son of Aaron, the priest.
text.KJV: 4:33 This is the seruice of the families of the sonnes of Merari, according to all their seruice in the Tabernacle of the Congregation, vnder the hand of Ithamar the sonne of Aaron the Priest.
text.NABRE: This, then, is the task of the clans of the Merarites with respect to all their service in the tent of meeting under the supervision of Ithamar, son of Aaron the priest.

text.NRSV: Moses and Aaron and the leaders of the congregation enrolled the Kohathites, by their clans and their ancestral houses,
text.Rotherham: Then did Moses and Aaron, and the princes of the assembly, number the sons of the Kohathites, by their families, and by their ancestral house;
text.KJV: 4:34 ¶ And Moses and Aaron, and the chiefe of the Congregation, numbred the sonnes of the Kohathites, after their families, and after the house of their fathers;
text.NABRE: Number of Adult Levites. So Moses and Aaron and the leaders of the community enrolled the Kohathites, by clans and ancestral houses,

text.NRSV: from thirty years old up to fifty years old, everyone who qualified for work relating to the tent of meeting;
text.Rotherham: from thirty years old and upwards, even unto fifty years old, all that might enter the host, to labour in the tent of meeting;
text.KJV: 4:35 From thirtie yeeres old and vpward, euen vnto fiftie yeeres old, euery one that entreth in to the seruice, for the worke in the Tabernacle of the Congregation.
text.NABRE: all between thirty and fifty years of age who will join the personnel to work in the tent of meeting;

text.NRSV: and their enrollment by clans was two thousand seven hundred fifty.
text.Rotherham: and they who were numbered of them, by their families, were found to be two thousand, seven hundred and fifty.
text.KJV: 4:36 And those that were numbred of them by their families, were two thousand, seuen hundred and fiftie.
text.NABRE: their enrollment by clans was two thousand seven hundred and fifty.

text.NRSV: This was the enrollment of the clans of the Kohathites, all who served at the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron enrolled according to the commandment of the Lord by Moses.
text.Rotherham: These, were they who were num-bered of the families of the Kohathites, all that might labour in the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron did number, at the bidding of Yahweh by the mediation of Moses.
text.KJV: 4:37 These were they that were numbred of the families of the Kohathites; all that might doe seruice in the Tabernacle of the Congregation, which Moses and Aaron did number, according to the commandement of the Lord, by the hand of Moses.
text.NABRE: Such was the enrollment of the clans of the Kohathites, everyone who was to serve in the tent of meeting, whom Moses enrolled, together with Aaron, as the Lord directed through Moses.

text.NRSV: The enrollment of the Gershonites, by their clans and their ancestral houses,
text.Rotherham: And they who were numbered of the sons of Gershon,—by their families, and by their ancestral house;
text.KJV: 4:38 And those that were numbred of the sonnes of Gershon, throughout their families, and by the house of their fathers;
text.NABRE: As for the enrollment of the Gershonites, by clans and ancestral houses,

text.NRSV: from thirty years old up to fifty years old, everyone who qualified for work relating to the tent of meeting—
text.Rotherham: from thirty years old and upwards, even to fifty years old, all that might enter the host, to labour in the tent of meeting,:
text.KJV: 4:39 From thirtie yeeres old and vpward, euen vnto fiftie yeeres old, euery one that entreth in to the seruice, for the worke in the Tabernacle of the Congregation:
text.NABRE: all between thirty and fifty years of age who will join the personnel to work in the tent of meeting—

text.NRSV: their enrollment by their clans and their ancestral houses was two thousand six hundred thirty.
text.Rotherham: yea they who were numbered of them, by their families, by their ancestral house,—were found to be—two thousand, and six hundred, and thirty.
text.KJV: 4:40 Euen those that were numbred of them, throughout their families, by the houses of their fathers, were two thousand, and sixe hundred and thirtie.
text.NABRE: their enrollment by clans and ancestral houses was two thousand six hundred and thirty.

text.NRSV: This was the enrollment of the clans of the Gershonites, all who served at the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron enrolled according to the commandment of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: These, are they who were numbered of the families of the sons of Gershon, all who might labour in the tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron did number, at the bidding of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 4:41 These are they that were numbred of the families of the sonnes of Gershon, of all that might doe seruice in the Tabernacle of the Congregation, whom Moses and Aaron did number, according to the commandement of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Such was the enrollment of the clans of the Gershonites, everyone who was to serve in the tent of meeting, whom Moses enrolled, together with Aaron, as the Lord directed.

text.NRSV: The enrollment of the clans of the Merarites, by their clans and their ancestral houses,
text.Rotherham: And they who were numbered of the families of the sons of Merari, by their families by their ancestral house;
text.KJV: 4:42 ¶ And those that were numbred of the families of the sonnes of Merari, throughout their families, by the house of their fathers:
text.NABRE: As for the enrollment of the clans of the Merarites, by clans and ancestral houses,

text.NRSV: from thirty years old up to fifty years old, everyone who qualified for work relating to the tent of meeting—
text.Rotherham: from thirty years old and upwards, even unto fifty years old,—all that might enter the host to labour in the tent of meeting;
text.KJV: 4:43 From thirtie yeeres old and vpward, euen vnto fiftie yeeres old, euery one that entreth in to the seruice, for the worke in the Tabernacle of the Congregation:
text.NABRE: all from thirty up to fifty years of age who will join the personnel to work in the tent of meeting—

text.NRSV: their enrollment by their clans was three thousand two hundred.
text.Rotherham: yea they who were numbered of them, by their families, were found to be—three thousand and two hundred.
text.KJV: 4:44 Euen those that were numbred of them after their families, were three thousand and two hundred.
text.NABRE: their enrollment by clans was three thousand two hundred.

text.NRSV: This is the enrollment of the clans of the Merarites, whom Moses and Aaron enrolled according to the commandment of the Lord by Moses.
text.Rotherham: These, are they who were numbered of the families of the sons of Merari,—whom Moses and Aaron did number, at the bidding of Yahweh, by the mediation of Moses.
text.KJV: 4:45 These be those that were numbred of the families of the sonnes of Merari, whom Moses & Aaron numbred according to the word of the Lord by the hand of Moses.
text.NABRE: Such was the enrollment of the clans of the Merarites, whom Moses enrolled, together with Aaron, as the Lord directed through Moses.

text.NRSV: All those who were enrolled of the Levites, whom Moses and Aaron and the leaders of Israel enrolled, by their clans and their ancestral houses,
text.Rotherham: All they who were numbered, whom Moses and Aaron and the princes of Israel did number, even the Levites, by their families and by their ancestral house;
text.KJV: 4:46 All those that were numbred of the Leuites, whom Moses and Aaron, and the chiefe of Israel numbred, after their families, and after the house of their fathers:
text.NABRE: As for the total enrollment of the Levites, which Moses and Aaron and the Israelite leaders had made, by clans and ancestral houses,

text.NRSV: from thirty to fifty years old, everyone who qualified to do the work of service and the work of bearing burdens relating to the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: from thirty years old and upwards, even unto fifty years old, all that might enter, to toil in the work of labouring and in the work of bearing burdens, in the tent of meeting,
text.KJV: 4:47 From thirty yeeres old and vpward, euen vnto fifty yeeres old, euery one that came to doe the seruice of the ministery, and the seruice of the burden in the Tabernacle of the Congregation:
text.NABRE: all between thirty and fifty years of age who were to undertake tasks of service or transport for the tent of meeting—

text.NRSV: their enrollment was eight thousand five hundred eighty.
text.Rotherham: yea, they who were numbered of them were found to be—eight thousand, and five hundred, and eighty.
text.KJV: 4:48 Euen those that were numbred of them, were eight thousand, and fiue hundred, and fourescore.
text.NABRE: their total enrollment was eight thousand five hundred and eighty.

text.NRSV: According to the commandment of the Lord through Moses they were appointed to their several tasks of serving or carrying; thus they were enrolled by him, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: At the bidding of Yahweh, were they put in charge by the mediation of Moses, man by man, over his labour and over his burden,—yea, they who were put in charge by him, were they, whom Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: 4:49 According to the commandement of the Lord, they were numbred by the hand of Moses, euery one according to his seruice, and according to his burden: Thus were they numbred of him, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: As the Lord directed through Moses, they gave each of them their assignments for service and for transport; just as the Lord had commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:—
text.KJV: 5:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Chapter 5 The Unclean Expelled. The Lord said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Command the Israelites to put out of the camp everyone who is leprous, or has a discharge, and everyone who is unclean through contact with a corpse;
text.Rotherham: Command the sons of Israel, that they send forth out of the camp, every leper, and every one that hath a flux,—and every one that is unclean by the dead:
text.KJV: 5:2 Commaund the children of Israel, that they put out of the campe euery leper, and euery one that hath an issue, and whosoeuer is defiled by the dead:
text.NABRE: Order the Israelites to expel from camp everyone with a scaly infection, and everyone suffering from a discharge, and everyone who has become unclean by contact with a corpse.

text.NRSV: you shall put out both male and female, putting them outside the camp; they must not defile their camp, where I dwell among them.
text.Rotherham: whether male or female, ye shall send them forth, unto the outside of the camp, shall ye send them,—that they make not their camps unclean, in the midst whereof, I, have my habitation.
text.KJV: 5:3 Both male and female shal ye put out, without the campe shall yee put them, that they defile not their campes in the middest whereof I dwell.
text.NABRE: Male and female alike, you shall expel them. You shall expel them from the camp so that they do not defile their camp, where I dwell in their midst.

text.NRSV: The Israelites did so, putting them outside the camp; as the Lord had spoken to Moses, so the Israelites did.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel did so, and sent them forth unto the outside of the camp,—as Yahweh spake unto Moses, so, did the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 5:4 And the children of Israel did so, and put them out, without the campe: as the Lord spake vnto Moses, so did the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: This the Israelites did, expelling them from the camp; just as the Lord had commanded Moses, so the Israelites did. Unjust Possession.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 5:5 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the Israelites: When a man or a woman wrongs another, breaking faith with the Lord, that person incurs guilt
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel: When any man or woman, shall do aught of any human sin, in acting unfaithfully against Yahweh,—and that person shall become aware of his guilt,
text.KJV: 5:6 Speake vnto the children of Israel, When a man or woman shall commit any sinne that men commit, to doe a trespasse against the Lord, and that person be guiltie;
text.NABRE: Tell the Israelites: If a man or a woman commits any offense against another person, thus breaking faith with the Lord, and thereby becomes guilty,

text.NRSV: and shall confess the sin that has been committed. The person shall make full restitution for the wrong, adding one fifth to it, and giving it to the one who was wronged.
text.Rotherham: then shall they confess their sin which they have done, and he shall make good that wherein he is guilty, in the principal thereof, and the fifth part thereof, shall he add thereunto,—and give to him against whom he is guilty,
text.KJV: 5:7 Then they shall confesse their sinne, which they haue done: and hee shall recompense his trespasse, with the principall thereof, and adde vnto it the fifth part thereof, and giue it vnto him against whom he hath trespassed.
text.NABRE: that person shall confess the wrong that has been done, make restitution in full, and in addition give one fifth of its value to the one that has been wronged.

text.NRSV: If the injured party has no next of kin to whom restitution may be made for the wrong, the restitution for wrong shall go to the Lord for the priest, in addition to the ram of atonement with which atonement is made for the guilty party.
text.Rotherham: But if one have no kinsman unto whom he may make good that wherein he is guilty, then that wherein he is guilty, which is to be restored to Yahweh, shall be the priest’s,—besides the ram of propitiation, wherewith a propitiatory-covering is to be put over him.
text.KJV: 5:8 But if the man haue no kinsman to recompense the trespasse vnto, let the trespasse be recompensed vnto the Lord, euen to the Priest: beside the ramme of the atonement, whereby an atonement shall be made for him.
text.NABRE: However, if there is no next of kin, one to whom restitution can be made, the restitution shall be made to the Lord and shall fall to the priest; this is apart from the ram of atonement with which the priest makes atonement for the guilty individual.

text.NRSV: Among all the sacred donations of the Israelites, every gift that they bring to the priest shall be his.
text.Rotherham: And every heave-offering, of all the hallowed things of the sons of Israel which they bring near to the priest, unto him, shall it belong.
text.KJV: 5:9 And euery offering of all the holy things of the children of Israel, which they bring vnto the Priest, shall be his.
text.NABRE: Likewise, every contribution among the sacred offerings that the Israelites present to the priest will belong to him.

text.NRSV: The sacred donations of all are their own; whatever anyone gives to the priest shall be his.
text.Rotherham: And, every man’s hallowed things, shall be, his own,—what any man giveth to the priest, shall be his.
text.KJV: 5:10 And euery mans halowed things shall be his: whatsoeuer any man giueth the Priest, it shall be his.
text.NABRE: Each shall possess his own sacred offerings; what is given to a priest shall be his.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses saying:
text.KJV: 5:11 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Ordeal for Suspected Adultery. The Lord said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the Israelites and say to them: If any man's wife goes astray and is unfaithful to him,
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them,—When any man’s wife, shall turn aside, and commit against him an act of unfaithfulness;
text.KJV: 5:12 Speake vnto the children of Israel, and say vnto them, If any mans wife goe aside, and commit a trespasse against him;
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and tell them: If a man’s wife goes astray and becomes unfaithful to him

text.NRSV: if a man has had intercourse with her but it is hidden from her husband, so that she is undetected though she has defiled herself, and there is no witness against her since she was not caught in the act;
text.Rotherham: and a man shall lie with her carnally, and it shall be concealed from the eyes of her husband and be kept close but, she, hath committed uncleanness,—though witness, there is none against her, and she, hath not been caught;
text.KJV: 5:13 And a man lye with her carnally, and it be hid from the eyes of her husband, and be kept close, and she be defiled, and there be no witnesse against her, neither she be taken with the maner;
text.NABRE: by virtue of a man having intercourse with her in secret from her husband and she is able to conceal the fact that she has defiled herself for lack of a witness who might have caught her in the act;

text.NRSV: if a spirit of jealousy comes on him, and he is jealous of his wife who has defiled herself; or if a spirit of jealousy comes on him, and he is jealous of his wife, though she has not defiled herself;
text.Rotherham: but there shall pass over him a spirit of jealousy, and he shall become jealous of his wife, she having committed uncleanness,—or there shall pass over him a spirit of jealousy, and he shall become jealous of his wife, she not, having committed uncleanness,
text.KJV: 5:14 And the spirit of ielousie come vpon him, and he be ielous of his wife, and shee be defiled: or if the spirit of ielousie come vpon him, and hee be ielous of his wife, and she be not defiled:
text.NABRE: or if a man is overcome by a feeling of jealousy that makes him suspect his wife, and she has defiled herself; or if a man is overcome by a feeling of jealousy that makes him suspect his wife and she has not defiled herself—

text.NRSV: then the man shall bring his wife to the priest, and bring the offering required for her, one tenth of an ephah of barley flour. He shall pour no oil on it and put no frankincense on it, for it is a grain offering of jealousy, a grain offering of remembrance, bringing iniquity to remembrance.
text.Rotherham: then shall the man bring in his wife unto the priest, and shall bring in her offering for her, the tenth of an ephah of the meal of barley,—he shall not pour thereon oil, nor put thereon frankincense, for a jealousy gift, it is, a reminding gift bringing to mind iniquity.
text.KJV: 5:15 Then shall the man bring his wife vnto the Priest, and he shall bring her offering for her, the tenth part of an Ephah of barley meale: hee shall powre no oyle vpon it, nor put frankincense thereon; for it is an offering of ielousie, an offering of memoriall, bringing iniquitie to remembrance:
text.NABRE: then the man shall bring his wife to the priest as well as an offering on her behalf, a tenth of an ephah of barley meal. However, he shall not pour oil on it nor put frankincense over it, since it is a grain offering of jealousy, a grain offering of remembrance which recalls wrongdoing.

text.NRSV: Then the priest shall bring her near, and set her before the Lord;
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest bring her near,—and cause her to stand before Yahweh;
text.KJV: 5:16 And the Priest shall bring her neere, and set her before the Lord.
text.NABRE: The priest shall first have the woman come forward and stand before the Lord.

text.NRSV: the priest shall take holy water in an earthen vessel, and take some of the dust that is on the floor of the tabernacle and put it into the water.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall take hallowed water in an earthen vessel,—and of the dust which shall be upon the floor of the habitation, shall the priest take, and put into the water;
text.KJV: 5:17 And the Priest shall take holy water in an earthen vessell, and of the dust that is in the floore of the Tabernacle the Priest shall take, and put it into the water:
text.NABRE: In an earthen vessel he shall take holy water, as well as some dust from the floor of the tabernacle and put it in the water.

text.NRSV: The priest shall set the woman before the Lord, dishevel the woman's head, put the grain offering of remembrance in her hands, which is the grain offering of jealousy. In his own hand the priest shall have the water of bitterness that brings the curse.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall cause the woman to stand before Yahweh, and shall bare the head of the woman, and shall place upon her hands the reminding gift, it being a jealousy gift,—and in the hand of the priest, shall be the deadly water that bringeth a curse;
text.KJV: 5:18 And the Priest shall set the woman before the Lord, and vncouer the womans head, and put the offering of memoriall in her hands, which is the Ielousie offering: and the Priest shall haue in his hand the bitter water that causeth the curse.
text.NABRE: Making the woman stand before the Lord, the priest shall uncover her head and place in her hands the grain offering of remembrance, that is, the grain offering of jealousy, while he himself shall hold the water of bitterness that brings a curse.

text.NRSV: Then the priest shall make her take an oath, saying, "If no man has lain with you, if you have not turned aside to uncleanness while under your husband's authority, be immune to this water of bitterness that brings the curse.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall put her on oath and shall say unto the woman:—If no man hath lain with thee, and if thou hast not turned aside in uncleanness, [unto another] instead of thy husband, be thou clear from this deadly water that causeth a curse.
text.KJV: 5:19 And the Priest shall charge her by an othe, and say vnto the woman, If no man haue lyen with thee, and if thou hast not gone aside to vncleannesse with another in stead of thy husband, be thou free from this bitter water that causeth the curse.
text.NABRE: Then the priest shall adjure the woman, saying to her, “If no other man has had intercourse with you, and you have not gone astray by defiling yourself while under the authority of your husband, be immune to this water of bitterness that brings a curse.

text.NRSV: But if you have gone astray while under your husband's authority, if you have defiled yourself and some man other than your husband has had intercourse with you"
text.Rotherham: But, if, thou, hast turned aside, [to another] instead of thy husband and if thou hast made thyself unclean,—in that a man hath known thee carnally, other than thy husband,
text.KJV: 5:20 But if thou hast gone aside to another in stead of thy husband, and if thou be defiled, and some man hath lien with thee beside thine husband:
text.NABRE: But if you have gone astray while under the authority of your husband, and if you have defiled yourself and a man other than your husband has had intercourse with you”—

text.NRSV: —let the priest make the woman take the oath of the curse and say to the woman—"the Lord make you an execration and an oath among your people, when the Lord makes your uterus drop, your womb discharge;
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest put the woman on oath with an oath of cursing, and the priest shall say unto the woman, Yahweh give thee up for a curse and for an oath in the midst of thy people,—in that Yahweh shall give up thy thigh to fall away, and thy womb to swell:
text.KJV: 5:21 Then the Priest shall charge the woman with an othe of cursing, and the Priest shall say vnto the woman, The Lord make thee a curse, and an othe among thy people, when the Lord doth make thy thigh to rot, and thy belly to swell.
text.NABRE: so shall the priest adjure the woman with this imprecation—“may the Lord make you a curse and malediction among your people by causing your uterus to fall and your belly to swell!

text.NRSV: now may this water that brings the curse enter your bowels and make your womb discharge, your uterus drop!" And the woman shall say, "Amen. Amen."
text.Rotherham: so shall this water that causeth a curse enter into thy body, causing womb to swell and thigh to fall away. And the woman shall say, Amen, Amen.
text.KJV: 5:22 And this water that causeth the curse, shall go into thy bowels, to make thy belly to swell, and thy thigh to rot: and the woman shall say, Amen, Amen.
text.NABRE: May this water, then, that brings a curse, enter your bowels to make your belly swell and your uterus fall!” And the woman shall say, “Amen, amen!”

text.NRSV: Then the priest shall put these curses in writing, and wash them off into the water of bitterness.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest write these curses in a scroll,—and wipe them out into the deadly water;
text.KJV: 5:23 And the Priest shall write these curses in a booke, and hee shall blot them out with the bitter water:
text.NABRE: The priest shall put these curses in writing and shall then wash them off into the water of bitterness,

text.NRSV: He shall make the woman drink the water of bitterness that brings the curse, and the water that brings the curse shall enter her and cause bitter pain.
text.Rotherham: and shall cause the woman to drink the deadly water that causeth a curse,—and the deadly water that causeth a curse shall enter into her.
text.KJV: 5:24 And he shall cause the woman to drinke the bitter water, that causeth the curse: and the water that causeth the curse shall enter into her, and become bitter.
text.NABRE: and he will have the woman drink the water of bitterness that brings a curse, so that the water that brings a curse may enter into her to her bitter hurt.

text.NRSV: The priest shall take the grain offering of jealousy out of the woman's hand, and shall elevate the grain offering before the Lord and bring it to the altar;
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest take from the hand of the woman the jealousy meal-offering,—and shall wave the meal-offering before Yahweh, and bring it near unto the altar;
text.KJV: 5:25 Then the Priest shall take the ielousie offering out of the womans hand, and shall waue the offering before the Lord, and offer it vpon the Altar.
text.NABRE: But first the priest shall take the grain offering of jealousy from the woman’s hand, and having elevated the grain offering before the Lord, shall bring it to the altar,

text.NRSV: and the priest shall take a handful of the grain offering, as its memorial portion, and turn it into smoke on the altar, and afterward shall make the woman drink the water.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall take a handful from the meal-offering, a remembrancer thereof, and make a perfume at the altar,—and afterwards, shall cause the woman to drink the water.
text.KJV: 5:26 And the Priest shal take an handfull of the offering, euen the memoriall thereof, and burne it vpon the Altar, and afterward shall cause the woman to drinke the water.
text.NABRE: where he shall take a handful of the grain offering as a token offering and burn it on the altar. Only then shall he have the woman drink the water.

text.NRSV: When he has made her drink the water, then, if she has defiled herself and has been unfaithful to her husband, the water that brings the curse shall enter into her and cause bitter pain, and her womb shall discharge, her uterus drop, and the woman shall become an execration among her people.
text.Rotherham: And as soon as he causeth her to drink, the water, then shall it be, that, if she have fallen into uncleanness and committed unfaithfulness against her husband, as soon as the deadly water that causeth a curse hath entered into her, so soon shall her womb swell and her thigh fall away,—thus shall the woman become a curse in the midst of her people.
text.KJV: 5:27 And when he hath made her to drinke the water, then it shall come to passe, that if shee be defiled, and haue done trespasse against her husband, that the water that causeth the curse, shall enter into her, and become bitter, and her belly shall swell, and her thigh shal rot: and the woman shalbe a curse among her people.
text.NABRE: Once he has had her drink the water, if she has defiled herself and been unfaithful to her husband, the water that brings a curse will enter into her to her bitter hurt, and her belly will swell and her uterus will fall, so that she will become a curse among her people.

text.NRSV: But if the woman has not defiled herself and is clean, then she shall be immune and be able to conceive children.
text.Rotherham: But, if the woman have not fallen into uncleanness, but is pure, then shall she be clear and shall bear seed.
text.KJV: 5:28 And if the woman be not defiled, but be cleane, then she shall be free, and shall conceiue seed.
text.NABRE: If, however, the woman has not defiled herself, but is still pure, she will be immune and will still be fertile.

text.NRSV: This is the law in cases of jealousy, when a wife, while under her husband's authority, goes astray and defiles herself,
text.Rotherham: This, is the law of jealousies,—when a wife shall turn aside [to another] instead of her husband, and fall into uncleanness;
text.KJV: 5:29 This is the law of ielousies, when a wife goeth aside to another in stead of her husband, and is defiled:
text.NABRE: This, then, is the ritual for jealousy when a woman goes astray while under the authority of her husband and defiles herself,

text.NRSV: or when a spirit of jealousy comes on a man and he is jealous of his wife; then he shall set the woman before the Lord, and the priest shall apply this entire law to her.
text.Rotherham: or when there passeth over, a husband, a spirit of jealousy, and he becometh jealous of his wife, then shall he cause the woman to stand before Yahweh, and the priest shall execute upon her all this law:
text.KJV: 5:30 Or when the spirit of ielousie commeth vpon him, and hee be ielous ouer his wife, and shall set the woman before the Lord, and the Priest shal execute vpon her all this law.
text.NABRE: or when such a feeling of jealousy comes over a man that he becomes suspicious of his wife; he shall have her stand before the Lord, and the priest shall perform this entire ritual for her.

text.NRSV: The man shall be free from iniquity, but the woman shall bear her iniquity.
text.Rotherham: thus shall the man be clear of iniquity; but that woman, shall bear her iniquity.
text.KJV: 5:31 Then shall the man bee guiltlesse from iniquitie, and this woman shall beare her iniquitie.
text.NABRE: The man shall be free from punishment, but the woman shall bear her punishment.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 6:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Chapter 6 Laws Concerning Nazirites. The Lord said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When either men or women make a special vow, the vow of a nazirite, to separate themselves to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them,—When any man or woman, would make the special vow of One Separate, by separating himself unto Yahweh,
text.KJV: 6:2 Speake vnto the children of Israel, and say vnto them, When either man or woman shall separate themselues to vow a vow of a Nazarite, to separate themselues vnto the Lord:
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When men or women solemnly take the nazirite vow to dedicate themselves to the Lord,

text.NRSV: they shall separate themselves from wine and strong drink; they shall drink no wine vinegar or other vinegar, and shall not drink any grape juice or eat grapes, fresh or dried.
text.Rotherham: from wine and strong drink, shall he separate himself, neither vinegar of wine nor vinegar of strong drink, shall he drink,—and no liquor of grapes, shall he drink, no grapes fresh or dried, shall he eat.
text.KJV: 6:3 Hee shall separate himselfe from wine, and strong drinke, and shal drinke no vineger of wine, or vineger of strong drinke, neither shal he drinke any liquor of grapes, nor eate moist grapes, or dried.
text.NABRE: they shall abstain from wine and strong drink; they may neither drink wine vinegar, other vinegar, or any kind of grape juice, nor eat either fresh or dried grapes.

text.NRSV: All their days as nazirites they shall eat nothing that is produced by the grapevine, not even the seeds or the skins.
text.Rotherham: All the days of his separation, of nothing that is made from the grapevine from the seeds even to the skin, shall he eat.
text.KJV: 6:4 All the dayes of his separation shall he eat nothing that is made of the vine tree, from the kernels euen to the huske.
text.NABRE: As long as they are nazirites they shall not eat anything of the produce of the grapevine; not even the seeds or the skins.

text.NRSV: All the days of their nazirite vow no razor shall come upon the head; until the time is completed for which they separate themselves to the Lord, they shall be holy; they shall let the locks of the head grow long.
text.Rotherham: All the days of his vow of separation, no razor, shall pass over his head,—until the days are fulfilled for which he shall separate himself to Yahweh hallowed, shall he be, letting the locks of the hair of his head grow long.
text.KJV: 6:5 All the dayes of the vow of his separation, there shall no rasour come vpon his head: vntill the dayes bee fulfilled in the which hee separateth himselfe vnto the Lord, he shall be holy, and shall let the lockes of the haire of his head grow.
text.NABRE: While they are under the nazirite vow, no razor shall touch their hair. Until the period of their dedication to the Lord is over, they shall be holy, letting the hair of their heads grow freely.

text.NRSV: All the days that they separate themselves to the Lord they shall not go near a corpse.
text.Rotherham: All the days for which he hath separated himself unto Yahweh to no dead person, shall he go in.
text.KJV: 6:6 All the dayes that he separateth himselfe vnto the Lord, hee shall come at no dead body.
text.NABRE: As long as they are dedicated to the Lord, they shall not come near a dead person.

text.NRSV: Even if their father or mother, brother or sister, should die, they may not defile themselves; because their consecration to God is upon the head.
text.Rotherham: Neither for his father nor for his mother nor for his brother nor for his sister, shall he make himself unclean-not even for them, should they die,—because, his separation unto God, is upon his head,
text.KJV: 6:7 Hee shall not make himselfe vncleane for his father, or for his mother, for his brother, or for his sister, when they die: because the consecration of his God is vpon his head.
text.NABRE: Not even for their father or mother, sister or brother, should they defile themselves, when these die, since their heads bear their dedication to God.

text.NRSV: All their days as nazirites they are holy to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: All his days of separation hallowed, is he unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 6:8 All the dayes of his separation he is holy vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: As long as they are nazirites they are holy to the Lord.

text.NRSV: If someone dies very suddenly nearby, defiling the consecrated head, then they shall shave the head on the day of their cleansing; on the seventh day they shall shave it.
text.Rotherham: But if one that is dying should die by him in a moment suddenly, then shall he count unclean his head of separation,—and shall shave his head, on the day he cleanseth himself, on the seventh day, shall he shave it.
text.KJV: 6:9 And if any man die very suddenly by him, and he hath defiled the head of his consecration, then he shall shaue his head in the day of his cleansing, on the seuenth day shall he shaue it.
text.NABRE: If someone dies very suddenly in their presence, defiling their dedicated heads, they shall shave their heads on the day of their purification, that is, on the seventh day.

text.NRSV: On the eighth day they shall bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest at the entrance of the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: And on the eighth day, shall he bring in. two turtle-doves, or two young pigeons,—unto the priest, unto the entrance of the tent of meeting;
text.KJV: 6:10 And on the eight day he shal bring two turtles or two yong pigeons to the Priest, to the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation.
text.NABRE: On the eighth day they shall bring two turtledoves or two pigeons to the priest at the entrance of the tent of meeting.

text.NRSV: and the priest shall offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering, and make atonement for them, because they incurred guilt by contact with a corpse. They shall sanctify the head that same day,
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall make, of one a sin—bearer and of one an ascending-sacrifice, and so put a propitiatory-covering over him, for that he sinned in respect of the dead person,—thus shall he hallow his head on that day.
text.KJV: 6:11 And the Priest shall offer the one for a sinne offering, and the other for a burnt offering, and make an atonement for him, for that hee sinned by the dead, and shall hallow his head that same day.
text.NABRE: The priest shall offer up the one as a purification offering and the other as a burnt offering, thus making atonement for them for the sin they committed with respect to the corpse. On the same day they shall reconsecrate their heads

text.NRSV: and separate themselves to the Lord for their days as nazirites, and bring a male lamb in its first year as a guilt offering. The former time shall be void, because the consecrated head was defiled.
text.Rotherham: Then shall he separate unto Yahweh his days of separation, and shall bring in a he-lamb a year old as a guilt-bearer,—and, the first days, shall be lost because his separation, was made unclean.
text.KJV: 6:12 And hee shall consecrate vnto the Lord the dayes of his separation, and shall bring a lambe of the first yeere for a trespasse offering: but the dayes that were before shall be lost, because his separation was defiled.
text.NABRE: and rededicate themselves to the Lord for the period of their dedication, bringing a yearling lamb as a reparation offering. The previous period is not valid, because they defiled their dedicated heads.

text.NRSV: This is the law for nazirites when the time of their consecration has been completed: they shall be brought to the entrance of the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the law of the Separate One, on the day when he fulfilleth his days of separation, he shall be brought in unto the entrance of the tent of meeting:
text.KJV: 6:13 ¶ And this is the Lawe of the Nazarite: when the dayes of his separation are fulfilled, he shall be brought vnto the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation.
text.NABRE: This is the ritual for the nazirites: When the period of their dedication is complete they shall go to the entrance of the tent of meeting,

text.NRSV: and they shall offer their gift to the Lord, one male lamb in its first year without blemish as a burnt offering, one ewe lamb in its first year without blemish as a sin offering, one ram without blemish as an offering of well-being,
text.Rotherham: then shall he bring near as his offering unto Yahweh.—one he-lamb a year old, without defect for an ascending-sacrifice, and one ewe-lamb a year old without defect for a sin-bearer,—and one ram, without defect for a peace-offering;
text.KJV: 6:14 And he shall offer his offring vnto the Lord, one hee lambe of the first yeere without blemish, for a burnt offering, and one ewe lambe of the first yeere without blemish, for a sinne offering, and one lambe without blemish for peace offerings,
text.NABRE: bringing their offerings to the Lord, one unblemished yearling lamb for a burnt offering, one unblemished yearling ewe lamb for a purification offering, one unblemished ram as a communion offering,

text.NRSV: and a basket of unleavened bread, cakes of choice flour mixed with oil and unleavened wafers spread with oil, with their grain offering and their drink offerings.
text.Rotherham: and a basket of unleavened cakes—fine meal in round cakes overflowed with oil, and thin cakes of unleavened bread, anointed with oil,—with their meal-offering and their drink-offerings.
text.KJV: 6:15 And a basket of vnleauened bread, cakes of fine flowre mingled with oyle, and wafers of vnleauened bread anointed with oyle, and their meate offering, and their drinke offerings.
text.NABRE: and a basket of unleavened cakes of bran flour mixed with oil and of unleavened wafers spread with oil, along with their grain offerings and libations.

text.NRSV: The priest shall present them before the Lord and offer their sin offering and burnt offering,
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest bring [them] near before Yahweh,—and offer his sin-bearer and his ascending-sacrifice;
text.KJV: 6:16 And the Priest shall bring them before the Lord, and shall offer his sinne offering, and his burnt offering.
text.NABRE: The priest shall present them before the Lord, and shall offer up the purification offering and the burnt offering for them.

text.NRSV: and shall offer the ram as a sacrifice of well-being to the Lord, with the basket of unleavened bread; the priest also shall make the accompanying grain offering and drink offering.
text.Rotherham: and the ram, shall he offer as a peace-offering unto Yahweh, besides the basket of unleavened cakes,—and the priest shall offer the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offering thereof,
text.KJV: 6:17 And he shall offer the ramme for a sacrifice of peace offerings vnto the Lord, with the basket of vnleauened bread: the Priest shall offer also his meate offering, and his drinke offering.
text.NABRE: He shall then offer up the ram as a communion sacrifice to the Lord, along with the basket of unleavened cakes, and the priest will offer the grain offering and libation.

text.NRSV: Then the nazirites shall shave the consecrated head at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and shall take the hair from the consecrated head and put it on the fire under the sacrifice of well-being.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the Separate One shave, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, his head of separation,—and take the hair of his head of separation, and put upon the fire, which is under the peace-offering.
text.KJV: 6:18 And the Nazarite shal shaue the head of his separation, at the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation, and shall take the haire of the head of his separation, and put it in the fire which is vnder the sacrifice of the peace offerings.
text.NABRE: Then at the entrance of the tent of meeting the nazirite shall shave his or her dedicated head, take the hair of the dedicated head, and put it in the fire under the communion sacrifice.

text.NRSV: The priest shall take the shoulder of the ram, when it is boiled, and one unleavened cake out of the basket, and one unleavened wafer, and shall put them in the palms of the nazirites, after they have shaved the consecrated head.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest take the shoulder for boiling from the ram, and one unleavened round cake from the basket, and one unleavened thin cake,—and place them on the hands, of the Separate One after he hath shaven off his [hair of] separation;
text.KJV: 6:19 And the Priest shall take the sodden shoulder of the ramme, and one vnleauened cake out of the basket, and one vnleauened wafer, and shall put them vpon the hands of the Nazarite, after the haire of his separation is shauen.
text.NABRE: After the nazirite has shaved off the dedicated hair, the priest shall take a boiled shoulder of the ram, as well as one unleavened cake from the basket and one unleavened wafer, and shall put them in the hands of the nazirite.

text.NRSV: Then the priest shall elevate them as an elevation offering before the Lord; they are a holy portion for the priest, together with the breast that is elevated and the thigh that is offered. After that the nazirites may drink wine.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall wave them as a wave-offering before Yahweh, hallowed, it is for the priest, besides the wave breast, and besides the heave leg,—and, afterwards, may the Separate One drink wine.
text.KJV: 6:20 And the Priest shall waue them for a waue offring before the Lord: this is holy for the Priest, with the waue breast, and heaue shoulder: and after that, the Nazarite may drinke wine.
text.NABRE: The priest shall then elevate them as an elevated offering before the Lord. They are an offering belonging to the priest, along with the brisket of the elevated offering and the leg of the contribution. Only after this may the nazirite drink wine.

text.NRSV: This is the law for the nazirites who take a vow. Their offering to the Lord must be in accordance with the nazirite vow, apart from what else they can afford. In accordance with whatever vow they take, so they shall do, following the law for their consecration.
text.Rotherham: This is the law of One Separate what he shall vow, his offering unto Yahweh with respect to his separation, besides what his hand may obtain: as required by the vow that he shall vow, so, must he do, with respect to the law of his separation.
text.KJV: 6:21 This is the Law of the Nazarite, who hath vowed, and of his offering vnto the Lord for his separation, besides that, that his hand shall get: according to the vow which he vowed, so he must do after the law of his separation.
text.NABRE: This, then, is the law for the nazirites, that is, what they vow as their offering to the Lord in accord with their dedication, apart from anything else which their means may allow. In keeping with the vow they take so shall they do, according to the law of their dedication.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 6:22 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Priestly Blessing. The Lord said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to Aaron and his sons, saying, Thus you shall bless the Israelites: You shall say to them,
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron, and unto his sons saying Thus, shall ye bless the sons of Israel,—saying unto them:
text.KJV: 6:23 Speake vnto Aaron, and vnto his sonnes, saying, On this wise ye shall blesse the children of Israel, saying vnto them:
text.NABRE: Speak to Aaron and his sons and tell them: This is how you shall bless the Israelites. Say to them:

text.NRSV: The Lord bless you and keep you;
text.Rotherham: Yahweh bless thee, and keep thee:
text.KJV: 6:24 The Lord blesse thee, and keepe thee:
text.NABRE: The Lord bless you and keep you!

text.NRSV: the Lord make his face to shine upon you, and be gracious to you;
text.Rotherham: Yahweh cause his face to shine upon thee and be gracious unto thee:
text.KJV: 6:25 The Lord make his face shine vpon thee, and be gracious vnto thee:
text.NABRE: The Lord let his face shine upon you, and be gracious to you!

text.NRSV: the Lord lift up his countenance upon you, and give you peace.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh lift up his face unto thee, and appoint unto thee peace.
text.KJV: 6:26 The Lord lift vp his countenance vpon thee, and giue thee peace.
text.NABRE: The Lord look upon you kindly and give you peace!

text.NRSV: So they shall put my name on the Israelites, and I will bless them.
text.Rotherham: Thus shall they put my name upon the sons of Israel,—and, I, myself, will bless them.
text.KJV: 6:27 And they shall put my Name vpon the children of Israel, and I will blesse them.
text.NABRE: So shall they invoke my name upon the Israelites, and I will bless them.

text.NRSV: On the day when Moses had finished setting up the tabernacle, and had anointed and consecrated it with all its furnishings, and had anointed and consecrated the altar with all its utensils,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on the day when Moses made an end of rearing up the habitation and anointing it, and hallowing it, and all the furniture thereof, and the altar, and all the utensils thereof,—both of anointing them, and hallowing them,
text.KJV: 7:1 And it came to passe on the day that Moses had fully set vp the Tabernacle, and had anointed it, and sanctified it, and all the instruments thereof, both the Altar, and all the vessels thereof, and had anointed them, and sanctified them:
text.NABRE: Chapter 7 Offerings of the Tribal Leaders. Now, when Moses had completed the erection of the tabernacle, he anointed and consecrated it with all its equipment, as well as the altar with all its equipment. After he anointed and consecrated them,

text.NRSV: the leaders of Israel, heads of their ancestral houses (the leaders of the tribes, those who were over the enrolled),
text.Rotherham: that the princes of Israel, the heads of their ancestral houses, made an offering,—they being princes of the tribes, they being the men who stood over them who were numbered:
text.KJV: 7:2 That the Princes of Israel, heads of the house of their fathers, (who were the Princes of the tribes, and were ouer them that were numbred) offered:
text.NABRE: an offering was made by the tribal leaders of Israel, who were heads of ancestral houses, the same leaders of the tribes who supervised those enrolled.

text.NRSV: brought their offerings before the Lord, six covered wagons and twelve oxen, a wagon for every two of the leaders, and for each one an ox; they presented them before the tabernacle.
text.Rotherham: yea then brought they in as their offering, before Yahweh—six covered waggons, and twelve oxen, a waggon for every two of the princes and an ox for each one,—yea they brought them near before the habitation.
text.KJV: 7:3 And they brought their offering before the Lord, sixe couered wagons, and twelue oxen: a wagon for two of the Princes, and for each one an oxe, and they brought them before the Tabernacle.
text.NABRE: The offering they brought before the Lord consisted of six wagons for baggage and twelve oxen, that is, a wagon for every two tribal leaders, and an ox for each. These they presented before the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 7:4 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord then said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Accept these from them, that they may be used in doing the service of the tent of meeting, and give them to the Levites, to each according to his service.
text.Rotherham: Take [them] of them, so shall they be for doing the laborious work of the tent of meeting,—and thou shalt give them unto the Levites, to every man according to the need of his laborious work.
text.KJV: 7:5 Take it of them, that they may be to doe the seruice of the Tabernacle of the Congregation, and thou shalt giue them vnto the Leuites, to euery man according to his seruice.
text.NABRE: Accept their offering, that these things may be put to use to maintain the tent of meeting. Assign them to the Levites, to each according to his duties.

text.NRSV: So Moses took the wagons and the oxen, and gave them to the Levites.
text.Rotherham: So Moses took the waggons, and the oxen,—and gave them unto the Levites:
text.KJV: 7:6 And Moses tooke the wagons, and the oxen, and gaue them vnto the Leuites.
text.NABRE: So Moses accepted the wagons and oxen, and assigned them to the Levites.

text.NRSV: Two wagons and four oxen he gave to the Gershonites, according to their service;
text.Rotherham: two of the waggons and four of the oxen, gave he unto the sons of Gershon,—according to the need of their laborious work;
text.KJV: 7:7 Two wagons and foure oxen he gaue vnto the sonnes of Gershon, according to their seruice.
text.NABRE: He gave two wagons and four oxen to the Gershonites according to their duties,

text.NRSV: and four wagons and eight oxen he gave to the Merarites, according to their service, under the direction of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
text.Rotherham: and four of the waggons, and eight of the oxen, gave he unto the sons of Merari,—according to the need of their laborious work, in the hand of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest;
text.KJV: 7:8 And foure wagons and eight oxen he gaue vnto the sonnes of Merari, according vnto their seruice, vnder the hand of Ithamar the sonne of Aaron the Priest.
text.NABRE: and four wagons and eight oxen to the Merarites according to their duties, under the supervision of Ithamar, son of Aaron the priest.

text.NRSV: But to the Kohathites he gave none, because they were charged with the care of the holy things that had to be carried on the shoulders.
text.Rotherham: but, unto the sons of Kohath, gave he none,—because as to the laborious work of the sanctuary which pertained unto them, upon their shoulders, were they to carry it.
text.KJV: 7:9 But vnto the sonnes of Kohath he gaue none: because the seruice of the Sanctuary belonging vnto them, was that they should beare vpon their shoulders.
text.NABRE: He gave none to the Kohathites, because they were responsible for maintenance of the sacred objects that had to be carried on their shoulders.

text.NRSV: The leaders also presented offerings for the dedication of the altar at the time when it was anointed; the leaders presented their offering before the altar.
text.Rotherham: So then the princes offered for the dedication of the altar, on the day it was anointed,—yea the princes offered their oblation before the altar,
text.KJV: 7:10 ¶ And the Princes offered for dedicating of the Altar, in the day that it was anointed, euen the Princes offered their offering before the Altar.
text.NABRE: For the dedication of the altar also, the tribal leaders brought offerings when it was anointed; the leaders presented their offering before the altar.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses: They shall present their offerings, one leader each day, for the dedication of the altar.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses: Each prince on his appointed day, thus shall they offer their oblation, for the dedication of the altar.
text.KJV: 7:11 And the Lord said vnto Moses, They shall offer their offering eche Prince on his day, for the dedicating of the Altar.
text.NABRE: But the Lord said to Moses: Let one leader each day present his offering for the dedication of the altar.

text.NRSV: The one who presented his offering the first day was Nahshon son of Amminadab, of the tribe of Judah;
text.Rotherham: And so it was, that, he who on the first day, offered his oblation,—was Nahshon son of Amminadab of the tribe of Judah;
text.KJV: 7:12 ¶ And he that offered his offring the first day, was Nahshon the sonne of Amminadab, of the tribe of Iudah.
text.NABRE: The one who presented his offering on the first day was Nahshon, son of Amminadab, of the tribe of Judah.

text.NRSV: his offering was one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin weighing seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of choice flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
text.Rotherham: and his offering, was—one charger of silver, a hundred and thirty shekels, the weight thereof, one tossing bowl of silver, seventy shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary,—both of them, full of fine meal overflowed with oil for a meal-offering;
text.KJV: 7:13 And his offering was one siluer charger, the weight thereof was an hundred and thirty shekels, one siluer bowle of seuentie shekels, after the shekel of the Sanctuary; both of them were full of fine flowre mingled with oile for a meat offering:
text.NABRE: His offering consisted of one silver plate weighing a hundred and thirty shekels and one silver basin weighing seventy shekels according to the sanctuary shekel, both filled with bran flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;

text.NRSV: one golden dish weighing ten shekels, full of incense;
text.Rotherham: one spoon of ten shekels of gold full of incense;
text.KJV: 7:14 One spoone of ten shekels of gold, full of incense:
text.NABRE: one gold cup of ten shekels’ weight filled with incense;

text.NRSV: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, for a burnt offering;
text.Rotherham: one choice young bullock one ram one he-lamb of the first year, as an ascending-sacrifice;
text.KJV: 7:15 One yong bullocke, one ramme, one lambe of the first yeere, for a burnt offering,
text.NABRE: one bull from the herd, one ram, and one yearling lamb for a burnt offering;

text.NRSV: one male goat for a sin offering;
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat, as a sin-bearer;
text.KJV: 7:16 One kid of the goats for a sinne offering:
text.NABRE: one goat for a purification offering;

text.NRSV: and for the sacrifice of well-being, two oxen, five rams, five male goats, and five male lambs a year old. This was the offering of Nahshon son of Amminadab.
text.Rotherham: and as a peace-offering, two oxen, five rams five he-goats, five he-lambs of the first year,—this, was the offering of Nashon, son of Amminadab.
text.KJV: 7:17 And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, fiue rammes, fiue hee goats, fiue lambes of the first yeere: this was the offering of Nahshon the sonne of Amminadab.
text.NABRE: and two bulls, five rams, five he-goats, and five yearling lambs for a communion sacrifice. This was the offering of Nahshon, son of Amminadab.

text.NRSV: On the second day Nethanel son of Zuar, the leader of Issachar, presented an offering;
text.Rotherham: On the second day, Nethanel, son of Zuar, prince of Issachar, brought his offering.
text.KJV: 7:18 ¶ On the second day Nethaneel the sonne of Zuar, Prince of Issachar did offer.
text.NABRE: On the second day Nethanel, son of Zuar, tribal leader of Issachar, made his offering.

text.NRSV: he presented for his offering one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin weighing seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of choice flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
text.Rotherham: He offered as his oblation—one charger of silver, a hundred and thirty shekels, the weight thereof, one tossing bowl of silver, seventy shekels by the shekel of the sanctuary,—both of them, full of fine meal overflowed with oil as a meal-offering;
text.KJV: 7:19 He offered for his offering one siluer charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirtie shekels, one siluer bowle of seuenty shekels, after the shekel of the Sanctuary, both of them full of fine flowre mingled with oile, for a meat offering:
text.NABRE: He presented as his offering one silver plate weighing a hundred and thirty shekels and one silver basin weighing seventy shekels according to the sanctuary shekel, both filled with bran flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;

text.NRSV: one golden dish weighing ten shekels, full of incense;
text.Rotherham: one spoon of ten [shekels] of gold full of incense;
text.KJV: 7:20 One spoone of gold of ten shekels, full of incense:
text.NABRE: one gold cup of ten shekels’ weight filled with incense;

text.NRSV: one young bull, one ram, one male lamb a year old, as a burnt offering;
text.Rotherham: one choice young bullock one ram one he-lamb of the first year, as an ascending-sacrifice;
text.KJV: 7:21 One yong bullocke, one ramme, one lambe of the first yeere for a burnt offering:
text.NABRE: one bull from the herd, one ram, and one yearling lamb for a burnt offering;

text.NRSV: one male goat as a sin offering;
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat, as a sin-bearer;
text.KJV: 7:22 One kid of the goats for a sinne offering:
text.NABRE: one goat for a purification offering;

text.Rotherham: and, as peace-offering two oxen, five rams, five he-goats, five he-lambs of the first year,—this, was the offering of Nethanel, son of Zuar.
text.KJV: 7:23 And for a sacrifice of peace offerings, two oxen, fiue rammes, fiue hee goats, fiue lambes of the first yeere: this was the offering of Nethaneel the sonne of Zuar.
text.NABRE: and two bulls, five rams, five he-goats, and five yearling lambs for a communion sacrifice. This was the offering of Nethanel, son of Zuar.

text.NABRE: On the third day it was the turn of the tribal leader of the Zebulunites, Eliab, son of Helon.

text.NABRE: and two bulls, five rams, five he-goats, and five yearling lambs for a communion sacrifice. This was the offering of Eliab, son of Helon.

text.NRSV: On the fourth day Elizur son of Shedeur, the leader of the Reubenites:
text.Rotherham: On the fourth day, the prince of he sons of Reuben,—Elizur, son of Shedeur:
text.KJV: 7:30 ¶ On the fourth day Elizur the sonne of Shedeur, Prince of the children of Reuben did offer.
text.NABRE: On the fourth day it was the turn of the tribal leader of the Reubenites, Elizur, son of Shedeur.

text.Rotherham: On the fifth day, the prince of the sons of Simeon,—Shelumiel, son of Zurishaddai:
text.KJV: 7:36 ¶ On the fifth day Shelumiel the sonne of Zurishaddai Prince of the children of Simeon, did offer.
text.NABRE: On the fifth day it was the turn of the tribal leader of the Simeonites, Shelumiel, son of Zurishaddai.

text.NABRE: His offering consisted of one silver plate weighing a hundred and thirty shekels and one silver basin weighing seventy shekels according to the sanctuary shekel, both filled with bran flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;
