text.Rotherham: On the sixth day, the prince of the sons of Gad,—Eliasaph, son of Deuel:
text.KJV: 7:42 ¶ On the sixt day, Eliasaph the sonne of Deuel, Prince of the children of Gad, offered:
text.NABRE: On the sixth day it was the turn of the tribal leader of the Gadites, Eliasaph, son of Reuel.

text.NABRE: and two bulls, five rams, five he-goats, and five yearling lambs for a communion sacrifice. This was the offering of Elishama, son of Ammihud.

text.NABRE: On the twelfth day it was the turn of the tribal leader of the Naphtalites, Ahira, son of Enan.

text.NRSV: his offering was one silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels, one silver basin weighing seventy shekels, according to the shekel of the sanctuary, both of them full of choice flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;

text.KJV: 7:79 His offering was one siluer charger, the weight whereof was an hundred and thirtie shekels, one siluer bowle of seuentie shekels, after the shekel of the Sanctuary, both of them full of fine flowre mingled with oyle, for a meate offering:
text.NABRE: His offering consisted of one silver plate weighing a hundred and thirty shekels and one silver basin weighing seventy shekels according to the sanctuary shekel, both filled with bran flour mixed with oil for a grain offering;

text.Rotherham: and, as a peace-offering, two oxen, five rams five he-goats, five he-lambs of the first year, this, was the offering of Ahira, son of Enan.
text.KJV: 7:83 And for a sacrifice of peace offrings, two oxen, fiue rammes, fiue hee goats, fiue lambs of the first yeere. This was the offering of Ahira the sonne of Enan.
text.NABRE: and two bulls, five rams, five he-goats, and five yearling lambs for a communion sacrifice. This was the offering of Ahira, son of Enan.

text.NRSV: This was the dedication offering for the altar, on the day when it was anointed, from the leaders of Israel: twelve silver plates, twelve silver basins, twelve golden dishes,
text.Rotherham: This, was the offering for the dedication of the altar in the day when it was anointed, from the princes of Israel,—twelve chargers of silver twelve tossing bowls of silver, twelve spoons of gold;
text.KJV: 7:84 This was the dedication of the Altar (in the day when it was annointed) by the Princes of Israel: twelue chargers of siluer, twelue siluer bowles, twelue spoones of gold:
text.NABRE: These were the offerings for the dedication of the altar, given by the tribal leaders of Israel on the occasion of its anointing: twelve silver plates, twelve silver basins, and twelve gold cups.

text.NRSV: each silver plate weighing one hundred thirty shekels and each basin seventy, all the silver of the vessels two thousand four hundred shekels according to the shekel of the sanctuary,
text.Rotherham: a hundred and thirty [shekels], each charger of silver, and seventy, each tossing bowl,—all the silver of the vessels, two thousand and four hundred [shekels], by the shekel of the sanctuary;
text.KJV: 7:85 Each charger of siluer weighing an hundred and thirtie shekels, each bowle seuentie: all the siluer vessels weighed two thousand and foure hundred shekels, after the shekel of the Sanctuary.
text.NABRE: Each silver plate weighed a hundred and thirty shekels, and each silver basin seventy, so that all the silver of these vessels amounted to two thousand four hundred shekels, according to the sanctuary shekel.

text.NRSV: the twelve golden dishes, full of incense, weighing ten shekels apiece according to the shekel of the sanctuary, all the gold of the dishes one hundred twenty shekels;
text.Rotherham: twelve spoons of gold full of incense, ten shekels each spoon by the shekel of the sanctuary,—all the gold of the spoons, a hundred and twenty [shekels] .
text.KJV: 7:86 The golden spoones were twelue, full of incense, weighing ten shekels a piece, after the shekel of the Sanctuary: all the gold of the spoones, was an hundred and twentie shekels.
text.NABRE: The twelve gold cups that were filled with incense weighed ten shekels apiece, according to the sanctuary shekel, so that all the gold of the cups amounted to one hundred and twenty shekels.

text.NRSV: all the livestock for the burnt offering twelve bulls, twelve rams, twelve male lambs a year old, with their grain offering; and twelve male goats for a sin offering;
text.Rotherham: All the herd for the ascending-sacrifice,—twelve bullocks twelve rams twelve he-lambs of the first year with their meal-offering,—twelve young he-goats for the bearing of sin;
text.KJV: 7:87 All the oxen for the burnt offering, were twelue bullocks, the rams twelue, the lambes of the first yeere twelue, with their meat offering: and the kids of the goats for sinne offering, twelue.
text.NABRE: The animals for the burnt offerings were, in all, twelve bulls, twelve rams, and twelve yearling lambs, with their grain offerings; those for the purification offerings were twelve goats.

text.NRSV: and all the livestock for the sacrifice of well-being twenty-four bulls, the rams sixty, the male goats sixty, the male lambs a year old sixty. This was the dedication offering for the altar, after it was anointed.
text.Rotherham: and all the herd for the peace-offering, twenty-four bullocks sixty rams, sixty he-goats sixty he-lambs of the first year,—this, was the offering for the dedication of the altar, after it was anointed.
text.KJV: 7:88 And all the oxen for the sacrifice of the peace offerings, were twenty and foure bullocks, the rammes sixtie, the hee goates sixtie, the lambes of the first yeere sixtie. This was the dedication of the Altar, after that it was anoynted.
text.NABRE: The animals for the communion sacrifices were, in all, twenty-four bulls, sixty rams, sixty he-goats, and sixty yearling lambs. These, then, were the offerings for the dedication of the altar after it was anointed.

text.NRSV: When Moses went into the tent of meeting to speak with the Lord, he would hear the voice speaking to him from above the mercy seat that was on the ark of the covenant from between the two cherubim; thus it spoke to him.
text.Rotherham: And when Moses entered into the tent of meeting, that He might speak with him, then heard he the Voice speaking unto him from off the propitiatory that was upon the ark of the testimony, from between the two cherubim,—so He spake unto him.
text.KJV: 7:89 And when Moses was gone into the Tabernacle of the Congregation, to speake with him, then he heard the voyce of one speaking vnto him, from off the Mercie seat, that was vpon the Arke of Testimony from betweene the two Cherubims: and he spake vnto him.
text.NABRE: The Voice. When Moses entered the tent of meeting to speak with God, he heard the voice addressing him from above the cover on the ark of the covenant, from between the two cherubim; and so it spoke to him.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses saying:
text.KJV: 8:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Chapter 8 The Menorah. The Lord said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to Aaron and say to him: When you set up the lamps, the seven lamps shall give light in front of the lampstand.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron, and thou shalt say unto him,—When thou lightest up the lamps, over against the front of the lampstand, shall the seven lamps give light.
text.KJV: 8:2 Speake vnto Aaron, and say vnto him, When thou lightest the lampes, the seuen lampes shall giue light, ouer against the candlesticke.
text.NABRE: Speak to Aaron and say: “When you set up the menorah-lamps, have the seven lamps throw their light in front of the menorah.”

text.NRSV: Aaron did so; he set up its lamps to give light in front of the lampstand, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And Aaron did so, over against the front of the lampstand, lighted he up the lamps thereof,—As Yahweh commanded Moses,
text.KJV: 8:3 And Aaron did so; he lighted the lampes therof, ouer against the candlestick, as the Lord comanded Moses.
text.NABRE: Aaron did so, setting up the menorah-lamps to face the area in front of the menorah, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: Now this was how the lampstand was made, out of hammered work of gold. From its base to its flowers, it was hammered work; according to the pattern that the Lord had shown Moses, so he made the lampstand.
text.Rotherham: Now, this, was the work of the lampstand—beaten work of gold both in the shaft thereof and in the flowers thereof, was it beaten work, according to the appearance which Yahweh caused to appear unto Moses, so, made he the lampstand.
text.KJV: 8:4 And this worke of the candlestick was of beaten gold, vnto the shaft thereof, vnto the flowres thereof was beaten worke: according vnto the paterne which the Lord had shewed Moses, so he made the candlesticke.
text.NABRE: This is the construction of the menorah: hammered gold, from its base to its bowls it was hammered; according to the pattern which the Lord had shown Moses, so he made the menorah.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 8:5 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Purification of the Levites. The Lord said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Separate the Levites from among the Israelites, and cleanse them.
text.Rotherham: Take the Levites, out of the midst of the sons of Israel,—and thou shalt purify them.
text.KJV: 8:6 Take the Leuites from among the children of Israel, and cleanse them.
text.NABRE: Take the Levites from among the Israelites and cleanse them.

text.NRSV: Thus you shall do to them, to cleanse them: sprinkle the water of purification on them, have them shave their whole body with a razor and wash their clothes, and so cleanse themselves.
text.Rotherham: And, thus, shalt thou do unto them, to purify them, sprinkle upon them sin-cleansing water,—then shall they, cause a razor to pass over all their flesh, and shall wash their clothes and so make themselves pure.
text.KJV: 8:7 And thus shalt thou doe vnto them, to cleanse them: sprinkle water of purifying vpon them, and let them shaue all their flesh, and let them wash their clothes, and so make themselues cleane.
text.NABRE: This is what you shall do to them to cleanse them. Sprinkle them with the water of purification, have them shave their whole bodies and wash their garments, and so cleanse themselves.

text.NRSV: Then let them take a young bull and its grain offering of choice flour mixed with oil, and you shall take another young bull for a sin offering.
text.Rotherham: Then shall they take a choice young bullock, with the meal-offering thereof even fine meal overflowed with oil,—and a second choice young bullock, shalt thou take. as a sin-bearer.
text.KJV: 8:8 Then let them take a yong bullocke with his meat offering, euen fine flowre mingled with oyle, and an other yong bullock shalt thou take for a sinne offering.
text.NABRE: Then they shall take a bull from the herd, along with its grain offering of bran flour mixed with oil; and you shall take another bull from the herd for a purification offering.

text.NRSV: You shall bring the Levites before the tent of meeting, and assemble the whole congregation of the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou bring the Levites near, before the tent of meeting,—and shalt call together all the assembly of the sons of Israel;
text.KJV: 8:9 And thou shalt bring the Leuites before the Tabernacle of the Congregation; and thou shalt gather the whole assembly of the children of Israel together.
text.NABRE: Bringing the Levites before the tent of meeting, you shall assemble also the whole community of the Israelites.

text.NRSV: When you bring the Levites before the Lord, the Israelites shall lay their hands on the Levites,
text.Rotherham: and shalt bring the Levites near before Yahweh,—and the sons of Israel shall lean their hands upon the Levites;
text.KJV: 8:10 And thou shalt bring the Leuites before the Lord, and the children of Israel shall put their hands vpon the Leuites.
text.NABRE: When you have brought the Levites before the Lord, the Israelites shall lay their hands upon them.

text.NRSV: and Aaron shall present the Levites before the Lord as an elevation offering from the Israelites, that they may do the service of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and Aaron shall offer the Levites as a wave-offering before Yahweh, from among the sons of Israel,—so shall they be for performing the laborious work of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 8:11 And Aaron shall offer the Leuites before the Lord for an offring of the children of Israel, that they may execute the seruice of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Aaron shall then present the Levites before the Lord as an elevated offering from the Israelites, that they may perform the service of the Lord.

text.NRSV: The Levites shall lay their hands on the heads of the bulls, and he shall offer the one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering to the Lord, to make atonement for the Levites.
text.Rotherham: And, the Levites, shall lean their hands upon the head of the bullocks; then make thou of the one a sin-bearer and of the other an ascending-sacrifice unto Yahweh, to put a propitiatory-covering over the Levites.
text.KJV: 8:12 And the Leuites shall lay their hands vpon the heads of the bullocks: and thou shalt offer the one for a sinne offering, and the other for a burnt offering vnto the Lord, to make an atonement for the Leuites.
text.NABRE: The Levites in turn shall lay their hands on the heads of the bulls, offering one as a purification offering and the other as a burnt offering to the Lord, to make atonement for the Levites.

text.NRSV: Then you shall have the Levites stand before Aaron and his sons, and you shall present them as an elevation offering to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thus shalt thou cause the Levites to stand before Aaron and before his sons,—thus shalt thou offer them as a wave-offering unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: 8:13 And thou shalt set the Leuites before Aaron, and before his sonnes, and offer them for an offering vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Then you shall have the Levites stand before Aaron and his sons, and you shall present them as an elevated offering to the Lord;

text.NRSV: Thus you shall separate the Levites from among the other Israelites, and the Levites shall be mine.
text.Rotherham: thus shalt thou separate the Levites out of the midst of the sons of Israel,—thus shall the Levites become mine.
text.KJV: 8:14 Thus shalt thou separate the Leuites from among the children of Israel: and the Leuites shalbe mine.
text.NABRE: thus you shall separate the Levites from the rest of the Israelites, and the Levites shall belong to me.

text.NRSV: Thereafter the Levites may go in to do service at the tent of meeting, once you have cleansed them and presented them as an elevation offering.
text.Rotherham: And after that, shall the Levites enter to do the labour of the tent of meeting,—so shalt thou purify them, and offer them as a wave-offering,
text.KJV: 8:15 And after that, shall the Leuites goe in; to doe the seruice of the Tabernacle of the Congregation: and thou shalt clense them, and offer them for an offering.
text.NABRE: Only then shall the Levites enter upon their service in the tent of meeting, when you have cleansed them and presented them as an elevated offering.

text.NRSV: For they are unreservedly given to me from among the Israelites; I have taken them for myself, in place of all that open the womb, the firstborn of all the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: For, given, given, they are unto me, out of the midst of the sons of Israel,—instead of every firstborn that a mother beareth from among the sons of Israel, have I taken them unto me.
text.KJV: 8:16 For they are wholly giuen vnto me, from among the children of Israel: in stead of such as open euery wombe, euen in stead of the first borne of all the children of Israel, haue I taken them vnto me.
text.NABRE: For they, among the Israelites, are totally dedicated to me; I have taken them for myself in place of everyone that opens the womb, the firstborn of all the Israelites.

text.NRSV: For all the firstborn among the Israelites are mine, both human and animal. On the day that I struck down all the firstborn in the land of Egypt I consecrated them for myself,
text.Rotherham: For mine, is every firstborn among the sons of Israel, among men and among beasts,—on the day when I smote every firstborn in the land of Egypt, did I hallow them unto me.
text.KJV: 8:17 For all the first borne of the children of Israel, are mine, both man and beast: on the day that I smote euery first borne in the land of Egypt, I sanctified them for my selfe.
text.NABRE: Indeed, all the firstborn among the Israelites, human being and beast alike, belong to me; I consecrated them to myself on the day I killed all the firstborn in the land of Egypt.

text.NRSV: but I have taken the Levites in place of all the firstborn among the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Therefore have I taken the Levites,—instead of every firstborn among the sons of Israel;
text.KJV: 8:18 And I haue taken the Leuites for all the first borne of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: But I have taken the Levites in place of all the firstborn Israelites;

text.NRSV: Moreover, I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and his sons from among the Israelites, to do the service for the Israelites at the tent of meeting, and to make atonement for the Israelites, in order that there may be no plague among the Israelites for coming too close to the sanctuary.
text.Rotherham: therefore have I given the Levites as a gift unto Aaron and unto his sons, out of the midst of the sons of Israel, to do the laborious work of the sons of Israel in the tent of meeting, and to put a propitiatory-covering over the sons of Israel,—so shall there be among the sons of Israel, no plague, by the coming nigh of the sons of Israel unto the sanctuary.
text.KJV: 8:19 And I haue giuen the Leuites as a gift to Aaron, and to his sonnes, from among the children of Israel, to do the seruice of the children of Israel, in the Tabernacle of the Congregation, and to make an atonement for the children of Israel: that there bee no plague among the children of Israel, when the children of Israel come nigh vnto the Sanctuarie.
text.NABRE: and from among the Israelites I have given to Aaron and his sons these Levites, who are to be dedicated, to perform the service of the Israelites in the tent of meeting and to make atonement for them, so that no plague may strike among the Israelites should they come too near the sanctuary.

text.NRSV: Moses and Aaron and the whole congregation of the Israelites did with the Levites accordingly; the Israelites did with the Levites just as the Lord had commanded Moses concerning them.
text.Rotherham: And Moses and Aaron and all the assembly of the sons of Israel did thus unto the Levites,—according to all that Yahweh commanded Moses as touching the Levites, so, did the sons of Israel unto them.
text.KJV: 8:20 And Moses and Aaron, and all the Congregation of the children of Israel did to the Leuites according vnto all that the Lord commanded Moses, concerning the Leuites, so did the children of Israel vnto them.
text.NABRE: This, then, is what Moses and Aaron and the whole community of the Israelites did with respect to the Levites; the Israelites did exactly as the Lord had commanded Moses concerning them.

text.NRSV: The Levites purified themselves from sin and washed their clothes; then Aaron presented them as an elevation offering before the Lord, and Aaron made atonement for them to cleanse them.
text.Rotherham: And the Levites accepted the cleansing from sin and washed their clothes, and Aaron offered them as a wave-offering before Yahweh,—and Aaron put a propitiatory-covering over them to make them pure.
text.KJV: 8:21 And the Leuites were purified, and they washed their clothes: and Aaron offered them as an offering before the Lord, and Aaron made an atonement for them to cleanse them.
text.NABRE: When the Levites had purified themselves and washed their garments, Aaron presented them as an elevated offering before the Lord, and made atonement for them to cleanse them.

text.NRSV: Thereafter the Levites went in to do their service in the tent of meeting in attendance on Aaron and his sons. As the Lord had commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so they did with them.
text.Rotherham: And after that, went the Levites in to do their laborious work in the tent of meeting, before Aaron and before his sons,—as Yahweh commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so, did they unto them.
text.KJV: 8:22 And after that, went the Leuites in, to do their seruice in the Tabernacle of the Congregation before Aaron and and before his sonnes: as the Lord had commanded Moses concerning the Leuites, so did they vnto them.
text.NABRE: Only then did they enter upon their service in the tent of meeting under the supervision of Aaron and his sons. Exactly as the Lord had commanded Moses concerning the Levites, so it was done with regard to them.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses saying;
text.KJV: 8:23 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Age Limits for Levitical Service. The Lord said to Moses:

text.NRSV: This applies to the Levites: from twenty-five years old and upward they shall begin to do duty in the service of the tent of meeting;
text.Rotherham: This is what concerneth the Levites,—from twenty-five years old and upwards, shall they enter to take rank in the host, for doing the laborious work of the tent of meeting;
text.KJV: 8:24 This is it that belongeth vnto the Leuites: from twentie and fiue yeeres old, and vpward, they shall goe in to waite vpon the seruice of the Tabernacle of the Congregation.
text.NABRE: This is the rule for the Levites. Everyone twenty-five years old or more shall join the personnel in the service of the tent of meeting.

text.NRSV: and from the age of fifty years they shall retire from the duty of the service and serve no more.
text.Rotherham: but from the age of fifty years, shall they retire from taking rank in the labour, and shall do laborious work no more;
text.KJV: 8:25 And from the age of fiftie yeeres they shall cease waiting vpon the seruice thereof, and shall serue no more:
text.NABRE: But everyone fifty on up shall retire from the work force and serve no more.

text.NRSV: They may assist their brothers in the tent of meeting in carrying out their duties, but they shall perform no service. Thus you shall do with the Levites in assigning their duties.
text.Rotherham: yet shall they wait upon their brethren in the tent of meeting, by keeping charge, but laborious work, shall they not perform,—thus, shalt thou do unto the Levites, as touching their charges.
text.KJV: 8:26 But shall minister with their brethren in the Tabernacle of the Congregation, to keepe the charge, and shall doe no seruice: thus shalt thou doe vnto the Leuites, touching their charge.
text.NABRE: They shall assist their fellow Levites in the tent of meeting in performing their duties, but they shall not do the work. This, then, is how you are to regulate the duties of the Levites.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the first month of the second year after they had come out of the land of Egypt, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses in the desert of Sinai, in the second year by their coming forth out of the land of Egypt in the first month, saying:
text.KJV: 9:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses in the wildernesse of Sinai, in the first moneth of the second yeere; after they were come out of the land of Egypt, saying,
text.NABRE: Chapter 9 Second Passover. The Lord said to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the first month of the second year following their departure from the land of Egypt:

text.NRSV: Let the Israelites keep the passover at its appointed time.
text.Rotherham: Let the sons of Israel therefore, keep the passover in its appointed season:
text.KJV: 9:2 Let the children of Israel also keepe the Passeouer, at his appointed season.
text.NABRE: Tell the Israelites to celebrate the Passover at the prescribed time.

text.NRSV: On the fourteenth day of this month, at twilight, you shall keep it at its appointed time; according to all its statutes and all its regulations you shall keep it.
text.Rotherham: on the fourteenth day of this month between the two evenings, shall ye keep it, in its appointed season,—according to all the statute thereof and according to all the regulations thereof, shall ye keep it.
text.KJV: 9:3 In the fourteenth day of this moneth at euen, ye shall keepe it in his appointed season: according to all the rites of it, and according to all the ceremonies thereof shall ye keepe it.
text.NABRE: In the evening twilight of the fourteenth day of this month you shall celebrate it at its prescribed time, in accord with all its statutes and regulations.

text.NRSV: So Moses told the Israelites that they should keep the passover.
text.Rotherham: Then spake Moses unto the sons of Israel that they should keep the passover.
text.KJV: 9:4 And Moses spake vnto the children of Israel that they should keepe the Passeouer.
text.NABRE: So Moses told the Israelites to celebrate the Passover,

text.NRSV: They kept the passover in the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, at twilight, in the wilderness of Sinai. Just as the Lord had commanded Moses, so the Israelites did.
text.Rotherham: So they kept the passover in the first [month], on the fourteenth day of the month between the two evenings, in the desert of Sinai,—according to all that Yahweh commanded Moses, so, did the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 9:5 And they kept the Passeouer on the fourteenth day of the first moneth at Euen, in the wildernesse of Sinai: according to all that the Lord commanded Moses, so did the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: and they did celebrate the Passover on the fourteenth day of the first month during the evening twilight in the wilderness of Sinai. Just as the Lord had commanded Moses, so the Israelites did.

text.NRSV: Now there were certain people who were unclean through touching a corpse, so that they could not keep the passover on that day. They came before Moses and Aaron on that day,
text.Rotherham: But so it was that there were, certain men who had become unclean by a dead person, and could not keep the passover on that day,—therefore came they near before Moses and before Aaron on that day.
text.KJV: 9:6 ¶ And there were certaine men who were defiled by the dead body of a man, that they could not keepe the Passeouer on that day: and they came before Moses, and before Aaron on that day.
text.NABRE: There were some, however, who were unclean because of a human corpse and so could not celebrate the Passover that day. These men came up to Moses and Aaron that same day

text.NRSV: and said to him, "Although we are unclean through touching a corpse, why must we be kept from presenting the Lord's offering at its appointed time among the Israelites?"
text.Rotherham: Then said those men unto him, We, are unclean by a dead person,—wherefore should we become of less esteem for want of offering the oblation of Yahweh in its appointed season, in the midst of the sons of Israel?
text.KJV: 9:7 And those men said vnto him, We are defiled by the dead body of a man: wherefore are we kept backe, that wee may not offer an offring of the Lord in his appointed season among the children of Israel?
text.NABRE: and they said to them, “Although we are unclean because of a human corpse, why should we be deprived of presenting the Lord ’s offering at its prescribed time along with other Israelites?”

text.NRSV: Moses spoke to them, "Wait, so that I may hear what the Lord will command concerning you."
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto them,—Stay, and let me hear, what Yahweh shall command concerning you!
text.KJV: 9:8 And Moses saide vnto them, Stand still, and I will heare what the Lord wil command concerning you.
text.NABRE: Moses answered them, “Wait so that I can learn what the Lord will command in your regard.”

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 9:9 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord then said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the Israelites, saying: Anyone of you or your descendants who is unclean through touching a corpse, or is away on a journey, shall still keep the passover to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, saying,—Though, any man, be unclean by a dead person or be on a journey afar off, whether in the case of yourselves or of your generations, yet shall he keep a passover unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 9:10 Speake vnto the children of Israel, saying, If any man of you, or of your posteritie shall be vncleane by reason of a dead body, or bee in a iourney afarre off, yet he shall keepe the Passeouer vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites: “If any one of you or of your descendants is unclean because of a human corpse, or is absent on a journey, you may still celebrate the Lord ’s Passover.

text.NRSV: In the second month on the fourteenth day, at twilight, they shall keep it; they shall eat it with unleavened bread and bitter herbs.
text.Rotherham: In the second month, on the fourteenth day between the two evenings, shall they keep it,—with unleavened cakes and bitter herbs, shall they eat it.
text.KJV: 9:11 The fourteenth day of the second moneth at Euen they shall keepe it, and eat it with vnleauened bread and bitter herbes.
text.NABRE: But you shall celebrate it in the second month, on the fourteenth day of that month during the evening twilight, eating it with unleavened bread and bitter herbs,

text.NRSV: They shall leave none of it until morning, nor break a bone of it; according to all the statute for the passover they shall keep it.
text.Rotherham: They shall not leave thereof until morning, and no bone, shall they break therein—according to all the statutes of the passover, shall they keep it.
text.KJV: 9:12 They shall leaue none of it vnto the morning, nor breake any bone of it: according to all the ordinances of the Passeouer they shall keepe it.
text.NABRE: and not leaving any of it over till morning, nor breaking any of its bones, but observing all the statutes of the Passover.

text.NRSV: But anyone who is clean and is not on a journey, and yet refrains from keeping the passover, shall be cut off from the people for not presenting the Lord's offering at its appointed time; such a one shall bear the consequences for the sin.
text.Rotherham: But as for the man who is, clean, and no a journey, doth not chance to be and yet faileth to keep the passover, that person shall he cut off from among his kinsfolk,—for the oblation of Yahweh, hath he not offered in its appointed season, his own sin, shall, that man, bear.
text.KJV: 9:13 But the man that is cleane, and is not in a iourney, and forbeareth to keep the Passeouer, euen the same soule shall be cut off from his people, because hee brought not the offering of the Lord in his appointed season: that man shall beare his sinne.
text.NABRE: However, anyone who is clean and not away on a journey, who yet fails to celebrate the Passover, shall be cut off from the people, for not presenting the Lord ’s offering at the prescribed time. That person shall bear the consequences of this sin.

text.NRSV: Any alien residing among you who wishes to keep the passover to the Lord shall do so according to the statute of the passover and according to its regulation; you shall have one statute for both the resident alien and the native.
text.Rotherham: When moreover there may sojourn with you a sojourner, who would keep a passover unto Yahweh, according to the statute of the passover, and according to the regulation thereof, so, must he keep [it],—one statute, shall there be for you, both for the sojourner and for the native of the land.
text.KJV: 9:14 And if a stranger shall soiourne among you, and will keepe the Passeouer vnto the Lord; according to the ordinance of the Passeouer, and according to the maner thereof, so shall he doe: ye shall haue one ordinance, both for the stranger, and for him that was borne in the land.
text.NABRE: “If an alien who lives among you would celebrate the Lord ’s Passover, it shall be celebrated according to the statutes and regulations for the Passover. You shall have the same law for the resident alien as for the native of the land.”

text.NRSV: On the day the tabernacle was set up, the cloud covered the tabernacle, the tent of the covenant; and from evening until morning it was over the tabernacle, having the appearance of fire.
text.Rotherham: Now, on the day the habitation was reared, the cloud covered the habitation, even the tent of the testimony,—and in the evening, it was over the habitation, like an appearance of fire, until morning,
text.KJV: 9:15 ¶ And on the day that the Tabernacle was reared vp, the cloud couered the Tabernacle, namely the Tent of the Testimony: and at Euen there was vpon the Tabernacle, as it were the appearance of fire, vntill the morning.
text.NABRE: The Fiery Cloud. On the day when the tabernacle was erected, the cloud covered the tabernacle, the tent of the covenant; but from evening until morning it took on the appearance of fire over the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: It was always so: the cloud covered it by day, and the appearance of fire by night.
text.Rotherham: Thus, came it to pass continually, the cloud, covered it,—and an appearance of fire, by night;
text.KJV: 9:16 So it was always: the cloud couered it by day, and the appearance of fire by night.
text.NABRE: It was always so: during the day the cloud covered the tabernacle and at night had the appearance of fire.

text.NRSV: Whenever the cloud lifted from over the tent, then the Israelites would set out; and in the place where the cloud settled down, there the Israelites would camp.
text.Rotherham: but at the bidding of the upgoings of the cloud from off the tent, then, after that, the sons of Israel set forward,—and in the place where the cloud abode, there the sons of Israel encamped:
text.KJV: 9:17 And when the cloud was taken vp from the Tabernacle, then after that, the children of Israel iourneyed, and in the place where the cloud abode, there the children of Israel pitched their tents.
text.NABRE: Whenever the cloud rose from the tent, the Israelites would break camp; wherever the cloud settled, the Israelites would pitch camp.

text.NRSV: At the command of the Lord the Israelites would set out, and at the command of the Lord they would camp. As long as the cloud rested over the tabernacle, they would remain in camp.
text.Rotherham: at the bidding of Yahweh, the sons of Israel set forward, and at the bidding of Yahweh, they encamped,—all the days that the cloud abode upon the habitation, they remained encamped.
text.KJV: 9:18 At the commandement of the Lord the children of Israel iourneied, and at the commandement of the Lord they pitched: as long as the cloud abode vpon the Tabernacle, they rested in the tents.
text.NABRE: At the direction of the Lord the Israelites broke camp, and at the Lord ’s direction they pitched camp. As long as the cloud stayed over the tabernacle, they remained in camp.

text.NRSV: Even when the cloud continued over the tabernacle many days, the Israelites would keep the charge of the Lord, and would not set out.
text.Rotherham: And when the cloud lengthened out its stay upon the habitation, many days, then would the sons of Israel keep the watch of Yahweh, and would not set forward.
text.KJV: 9:19 And when the cloud taried long vpon the Tabernacle many daies, then the children of Israel kept the charge of the Lord, and iourneyed not.
text.NABRE: Even when the cloud lingered many days over the tabernacle, the Israelites kept the charge of the Lord and would not move on.

text.NRSV: Sometimes the cloud would remain a few days over the tabernacle, and according to the command of the Lord they would remain in camp; then according to the command of the Lord they would set out.
text.Rotherham: And, so it was, when the cloud would be a few days upon the habitation, at the bidding of Yahweh, they remained encamped, and at the bidding of Yahweh, they set forward.
text.KJV: 9:20 And so it was when the cloude was a few daies vpon the Tabernacle, according to the commandement of the Lord, they abode in their tents, and according to the commandement of the Lord, they iourneyed.
text.NABRE: Yet if it happened the cloud was over the tabernacle only for a few days, at the direction of the Lord they stayed in camp; and at the Lord ’s direction they broke camp.

text.NRSV: Sometimes the cloud would remain from evening until morning; and when the cloud lifted in the morning, they would set out, or if it continued for a day and a night, when the cloud lifted they would set out.
text.Rotherham: And, so it was, when the cloud would be from evening until morning, as soon as the cloud went up in the morning, so soon did they set forward: whether by day or by night, as soon as the cloud went up, so soon did they set forward.
text.KJV: 9:21 And so it was when the cloude abode from Euen vnto the morning, and that the cloude was taken vp in the morning, then they iourneyed: whether it was by day or by night that the cloude was taken vp, they iourneyed.
text.NABRE: If it happened the cloud remained there only from evening until morning, when the cloud rose in the morning, they would break camp. Whether the cloud lifted during the day or the night they would then break camp.

text.NRSV: Whether it was two days, or a month, or a longer time, that the cloud continued over the tabernacle, remaining above it, the Israelites would remain in camp and would not set out; but when it lifted they would set out.
text.Rotherham: Whether for two days or a month or a year, the cloud lengthened out its stay upon the habitation abiding thereupon, the sons of Israel remained encamped and set not forward,—but at the upgoings thereof, they set forward.
text.KJV: 9:22 Or whether it were two dayes, or a moneth, or a yeere that the cloude taried vpon the Tabernacle, remayning thereon, the children of Israel abode in their tents, and iourneyed not: but when it was taken vp, they iourneyed.
text.NABRE: Whether the cloud lingered over the tabernacle for two days or for a month or longer, the Israelites remained in camp and did not break camp; but when it lifted, they broke camp.

text.NRSV: At the command of the Lord they would camp, and at the command of the Lord they would set out. They kept the charge of the Lord, at the command of the Lord by Moses.
text.Rotherham: At the bidding of Yahweh, they encamped, and at the bidding of Yahweh, they set forward,—the watch of Yahweh, they kept, at the bidding of Yahweh by the hand of Moses.
text.KJV: 9:23 At the commandement of the Lord they rested in the tents, and at the commaundement of the Lord they iourneyed: they kept the charge of the Lord, at the commandement of the Lord by the hand of Moses.
text.NABRE: At the direction of the Lord they pitched camp, and at the Lord ’s direction they broke camp; they kept the charge of the Lord, as the Lord directed them through Moses.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 10:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Chapter 10 The Silver Trumpets. The Lord said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Make two silver trumpets; you shall make them of hammered work; and you shall use them for summoning the congregation, and for breaking camp.
text.Rotherham: Make thee two trumpets of silver, of beaten work, shalt thou make them,—and they shall be unto thee for calling the assembly, and for setting forward the camps.
text.KJV: 10:2 Make thee two trumpets of siluer: of an whole piece shalt thou make them, that thou mayest vse them for the calling of the assembly, and for the iourneying of the campes.
text.NABRE: Make two trumpets of silver, making them of hammered silver, for you to use in summoning the community and in breaking camp.

text.NRSV: When both are blown, the whole congregation shall assemble before you at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: As soon as they blow therewith, so soon shall all the assembly assemble themselves unto thee, unto the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: 10:3 And when they shall blow with them, all the assembly shall assemble themselues to thee, at the doore of the Tabernacle of the Congregation.
text.NABRE: When both are blown, the whole community shall gather round you at the entrance of the tent of meeting;

text.NRSV: But if only one is blown, then the leaders, the heads of the tribes of Israel, shall assemble before you.
text.Rotherham: And, if, only once, they blow, then shall the princes, the heads of the thousands of Israel, gather themselves unto thee.
text.KJV: 10:4 And if they blow but with one trumpet, then the Princes, which are heads of the thousands of Israel, shall gather themselues vnto thee.
text.NABRE: but when one of them is blown, only the tribal leaders, the heads of the clans of Israel, shall gather round you.

text.NRSV: When you blow an alarm, the camps on the east side shall set out;
text.Rotherham: But when ye blow an alarm, then shall set forward the camps that are encamped eastwards;
text.KJV: 10:5 When ye blow an alarme, then the campes that lie on the East parts, shall goe forward.
text.NABRE: When you sound the signal, those encamped on the east side shall break camp;

text.NRSV: when you blow a second alarm, the camps on the south side shall set out. An alarm is to be blown whenever they are to set out.
text.Rotherham: and when ye blow a second alarm, then shall set forward the camps that are encamped southwards,—an alarm, shall they blow for setting them forward,
text.KJV: 10:6 When you blow an alarme the second time, then the campes that lye on the Southside, shall take their iourney: they shall blow an alarme for their iourneys.
text.NABRE: when you sound a second signal, those encamped on the south side shall break camp; when you sound a third signal, those encamped on the west side shall break camp; when you sound a fourth signal, those encamped on the north side shall break camp. Thus shall the signal be sounded for them to break camp.

text.NRSV: But when the assembly is to be gathered, you shall blow, but you shall not sound an alarm.
text.Rotherham: But in calling together the convocation, ye shall blow but shall not sound an alarm.
text.KJV: 10:7 But when the Congregation is to be gathered together, you shal blow: but you shall not sound an alarme.
text.NABRE: But in calling forth an assembly you are to blow a blast, without sounding the signal.

text.NRSV: The sons of Aaron, the priests, shall blow the trumpets; this shall be a perpetual institution throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Aaron the priests, shall blow with the trumpets,—and it shall be unto you for a statute age-abiding, unto your generations.
text.KJV: 10:8 And the sonnes of Aaron the Priests shall blow with the trumpets; and they shalbe to you for an ordinance for euer throughout your generations.
text.NABRE: The sons of Aaron, the priests, shall blow the trumpets; this is prescribed forever for you and your descendants.

text.NRSV: When you go to war in your land against the adversary who oppresses you, you shall sound an alarm with the trumpets, so that you may be remembered before the Lord your God, and be saved from your enemies.
text.Rotherham: And when ye go into war in your land against the assailant that assaileth you, then shall ye blow an alarm with the trumpets,—and bring yourselves to mind before Yahweh your God, and be saved, from your enemies,
text.KJV: 10:9 And if ye goe to warre in your land, against the enemie that oppresseth you, then ye shall blow an alarme with the trumpets, and ye shalbe remembred before the Lord your God, and yee shalbe saued from your enemies.
text.NABRE: When in your own land you go to war against an enemy that is attacking you, you shall sound the alarm on the trumpets, and you shall be remembered before the Lord, your God, and be saved from your foes.

text.NRSV: Also on your days of rejoicing, at your appointed festivals, and at the beginnings of your months, you shall blow the trumpets over your burnt offerings and over your sacrifices of well-being, and they shall serve as a reminder on your behalf before the Lord your God: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And in your day of rejoicing and in your appointed seasons and in the beginnings of your months, then shall ye blow with the trumpets, over your ascending-sacrifices, and over your peace-offerings,—so shall they be unto you for a memorial before your God. I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 10:10 Also in the day of your gladnesse, and in your solemne dayes, and in the beginnings of your monethes, ye shall blow with the trumpets ouer your burnt offerings, and ouer the sacrifices of your peace offerings, that they may bee to you for a memoriall before your God: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: And when you rejoice on your festivals, and your new-moon feasts, you shall blow the trumpets over your burnt offerings and your communion sacrifices, so that this serves as a reminder of you before your God. I, the Lord, am your God.

text.NRSV: In the second year, in the second month, on the twentieth day of the month, the cloud lifted from over the tabernacle of the covenant.
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass in the second year, in the second month on the twentieth of the month, that the cloud lifted itself up, from off the habitation of the testimony;
text.KJV: 10:11 ¶ And it came to passe on the twentieth day of the second moneth, in the second yeere, that the cloude was taken vp from off the Tabernacle of the Testimony.
text.NABRE: II. Departure, Rebellion, and Wandering in the Wilderness for Forty Years Departure from Sinai. In the second year, on the twentieth day of the second month, the cloud rose from the tabernacle of the covenant,

text.NRSV: Then the Israelites set out by stages from the wilderness of Sinai; and the cloud settled down in the wilderness of Paran.
text.Rotherham: and the sons of Israel set forward by their removals from the desert of Sinai, and then the cloud abode in the desert of Paran.
text.KJV: 10:12 And the children of Israel tooke their iourneys out of the wildernesse of Sinai; and the cloud rested in the wildernesse of Paran.
text.NABRE: and the Israelites moved on from the wilderness of Sinai by stages, until the cloud came to rest in the wilderness of Paran.

text.NRSV: They set out for the first time at the command of the Lord by Moses.
text.Rotherham: Thus then did they set forward for the first time,—at the bidding of Yahweh, by the hand of Moses.
text.KJV: 10:13 And they first tooke their iourney, according to the commandement of the Lord, by the hand of Moses.
text.NABRE: The first time that they broke camp at the direction of the Lord through Moses,

text.NRSV: The standard of the camp of Judah set out first, company by company, and over the whole company was Nahshon son of Amminadab.
text.Rotherham: So the standard of the camp of the sons of Judah set forward first, by their hosts, and over his own host, was Nahshon, son of Amminadab;
text.KJV: 10:14 ¶ In the first place went the standerd of the campe of the children of Iudah, according to their armies, and ouer his hoste was Nahshon the sonne of Amminadab.
text.NABRE: the divisional camp of the Judahites, arranged in companies, was the first to set out. Over its whole company was Nahshon, son of Amminadab,

text.NRSV: Over the company of the tribe of Issachar was Nethanel son of Zuar;
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Issachar, Nethanel, son of Zuar;
text.KJV: 10:15 And ouer the hoste of the tribe of the children of Issachar, was Nethaneel the sonne of Zuar.
text.NABRE: with Nethanel, son of Zuar, over the company of the tribe of Issacharites,

text.NRSV: and over the company of the tribe of Zebulun was Eliab son of Helon.
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Zebulun, Eliab, son of Helon.
text.KJV: 10:16 And ouer the hoste of the tribe of the children of Zebulun, was Eliab the sonne of Helon.
text.NABRE: and Eliab, son of Helon, over the company of the tribe of Zebulunites.

text.NRSV: Then the tabernacle was taken down, and the Gershonites and the Merarites, who carried the tabernacle, set out.
text.Rotherham: Then was taken down the habitation,—and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari set forward bearing the habitation.
text.KJV: 10:17 And the Tabernacle was taken downe, and the sonnes of Gershon, and the sonnes of Merari set forward, bearing the Tabernacle.
text.NABRE: Then, after the tabernacle was dismantled, the Gershonites and Merarites who carried the tabernacle set out.

text.NRSV: Next the standard of the camp of Reuben set out, company by company; and over the whole company was Elizur son of Shedeur.
text.Rotherham: Then set forward the standard of the camp of Reuben, by their hosts,—and over his own host, Elizur, son of Shedeur;
text.KJV: 10:18 ¶ And the standerd of the campe of Reuben set forward according to their armies: and ouer his hoste was Elizur the sonne of Shedeur.
text.NABRE: The divisional camp of the Reubenites, arranged in companies, was the next to set out. Over its whole company was Elizur, son of Shedeur,

text.NRSV: Over the company of the tribe of Simeon was Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai,
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Simeon, Shelumiel, son of Zurishaddai;
text.KJV: 10:19 And ouer the hoste of the tribe of the children of Simeon, was Shelumiel the sonne of Zurishaddai.
text.NABRE: with Shelumiel, son of Zurishaddai, over the company of the tribe of Simeonites,

text.NRSV: and over the company of the tribe of Gad was Eliasaph son of Deuel.
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Gad, Eliasaph, son of Deuel.
text.KJV: 10:20 And ouer the hoste of the tribe of the children of Gad, was Eliasaph the sonne of Deuel.
text.NABRE: and Eliasaph, son of Reuel, over the company of the tribe of Gadites.

text.NRSV: Then the Kohathites set out, carrying the holy things. (The tabernacle was set up before their arrival.)
text.Rotherham: Then set forward the Kohathites, bearing the sanctuary,—and so the habitation was reared by the time they came in.
text.KJV: 10:21 And the Kohathites set forward, bearing the Sanctuary, and the other did set vp the Tabernacle against they came.
text.NABRE: The Kohathites, who carried the sacred objects, then set out. Before their arrival the tabernacle would be erected.

text.NRSV: Next the standard of the Ephraimite camp set out, company by company, and over the whole company was Elishama son of Ammihud.
text.Rotherham: Then set forward the standard of the camp of the sons of Ephraim by their hosts,—and over his own host, was Elishama son of Ammihud;
text.KJV: 10:22 ¶ And the standerd of the campe of the children of Ephraim set forward, according to their armies, and ouer his hoste was Elishama the sonne of Ammiud.
text.NABRE: The divisional camp of the Ephraimites set out next, arranged in companies. Over its whole company was Elishama, son of Ammihud,

text.NRSV: Over the company of the tribe of Manasseh was Gamaliel son of Pedahzur,
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Manasseh, Gamaliel, son of Pedahzur;
text.KJV: 10:23 And ouer the hoste of the tribe of the children of Manasseh was Gamaliel the sonne of Pedazur.
text.NABRE: with Gamaliel, son of Pedahzur, over the company of the tribe of Manassites,

text.NRSV: and over the company of the tribe of Benjamin was Abidan son of Gideoni.
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Benjamin, Abidan, son of Gideoni.
text.KJV: 10:24 And ouer the hoste of the tribe of the children of Beniamin, was Abidan the soune of Gideoni.
text.NABRE: and Abidan, son of Gideoni, over the company of the tribe of Benjaminites.

text.NRSV: Then the standard of the camp of Dan, acting as the rear guard of all the camps, set out, company by company: Over the whole company was Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
text.Rotherham: Then set forward the standard of the camp of the sons of Dan, bringing up the rear of all the camps, by their hosts,—and over his own host, Ahiezur, son of Ammishaddai;
text.KJV: 10:25 ¶ And the standerd of the campe of the children of Dan set forward, which was the rere-ward of all the campes throughout their hostes: and ouer his hoste was Ahiezer the sonne of Ammishaddai.
text.NABRE: Finally, as rear guard for all the camps, the divisional camp of the Danites set out, arranged in companies. Over its whole company was Ahiezer, son of Ammishaddai,

text.NRSV: Over the company of the tribe of Asher was Pagiel son of Ochran,
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Asher, Pagiel, son of Ochran;
text.KJV: 10:26 And ouer the hoste of the tribe of the children of Asher, was Pagiel the sonne of Ocran.
text.NABRE: with Pagiel, son of Ochran, over the company of the tribe of Asherites,

text.NRSV: and over the company of the tribe of Naphtali was Ahira son of Enan.
text.Rotherham: and, over the host of the tribe of the sons of Naphtali, Ahira, son of Enan.
text.KJV: 10:27 And ouer the hoste of the tribe of the children of Naphtali was Ahira the sonne of Enan.
text.NABRE: and Ahira, son of Enan, over the company of the tribe of Naphtalites.

text.NRSV: This was the order of march of the Israelites, company by company, when they set out.
text.Rotherham: These, were the settings-forward of the sons of Israel by their hosts,—thus did they set forward.
text.KJV: 10:28 Thus were the iourneyings of the children of Israel, according to their armies, when they set forward.
text.NABRE: This was the order of march for the Israelites, company by company, when they set out.

text.NRSV: Moses said to Hobab son of Reuel the Midianite, Moses' father-in-law, "We are setting out for the place of which the Lord said, 'I will give it to you'; come with us, and we will treat you well; for the Lord has promised good to Israel."
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses to Hobab, son of Raguel the Midianite, father-in-law of Moses: Setting forward, are we unto the place of which Yahweh hath said, The same, will I give unto you,—Oh come with us and we will do thee good, for, Yahweh, hath spoken good, concerning Israel.
text.KJV: 10:29 ¶ And Moses said vnto Hobab the sonne of Raguel the Midianite Moses father in law, Wee are iourneying vnto the place of which the Lord said, I wil giue it you: come thou with vs, and we will doe thee good: for the Lord hath spoken good concerning Israel.
text.NABRE: Hobab as Guide. Moses said to Hobab, son of Reuel the Midianite, Moses’ father-in-law, “We are setting out for the place concerning which the Lord has said, ‘I will give it to you.’ Come with us, and we will be generous toward you, for the Lord has promised prosperity to Israel.”

text.NRSV: But he said to him, "I will not go, but I will go back to my own land and to my kindred."
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him, I will not go,—but unto my own land, and unto my own kindred, will I go.
text.KJV: 10:30 And he said vnto him, I will not goe, but I will depart to mine owne land, and to my kinred.
text.NABRE: But he answered, “No, I will not come. I am going instead to the land of my birth.”

text.NRSV: He said, "Do not leave us, for you know where we should camp in the wilderness, and you will serve as eyes for us.
text.Rotherham: Then said he: I beseech thee, do not forsake us,—for, on this account, hast thou come to know of our encamping in the desert, so shalt thou be unto us as eyes;
text.KJV: 10:31 And he said, Leaue vs not, I pray thee, forasmuch as thou knowest how we are to encampe in the wilderuesse, and thou mayest bee to vs in stead of eyes.
text.NABRE: Moses said, “Please, do not leave us; you know where we can camp in the wilderness, and you can serve as our guide.

text.NRSV: Moreover, if you go with us, whatever good the Lord does for us, the same we will do for you."
text.Rotherham: and it shall be if thou wilt go with us,—yea it shall be that with the very good wherewith Yahweh shall do us good, will we do good unto thee.
text.KJV: 10:32 And it shall bee if thou goe with vs, yea it shall be, that what goodnesse the Lord shall doe vnto vs, the same will we doe vnto thee.
text.NABRE: If you come with us, we will share with you the prosperity the Lord will bestow on us.”

text.NRSV: So they set out from the mount of the Lord three days' journey with the ark of the covenant of the Lord going before them three days' journey, to seek out a resting place for them,
text.Rotherham: So then they went forward, from the mountain of Yahweh, a journey of three days,—and the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, was going before them a journey of three days, to search out for them a resting-place,
text.KJV: 10:33 ¶ And they departed from the Mount of the Lord three dayes iourney: and the Arke of the Couenant of the Lord went before them in the three dayes iourney, to search out a resting place for them.
text.NABRE: Into the Wilderness. From the mountain of the Lord they made a journey of three days, and the ark of the covenant of the Lord went before them for the three-day journey to seek out a resting place for them.

text.NRSV: the cloud of the Lord being over them by day when they set out from the camp.
text.Rotherham: And the cloud of Yahweh, was over them by day,—when they set forward out of the camp.
text.KJV: 10:34 And the cloude of the Lord was vpon them by day, when they went out of the campe.
text.NABRE: And the cloud of the Lord was over them by day when they set out from camp.

text.NRSV: Whenever the ark set out, Moses would say, "Arise, O Lord, let your enemies be scattered, and your foes flee before you."
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when the ark set forward, that Moses said: Arise, O Yahweh! and scattered be thy foes, Let them that hate thee flee before thee;
text.KJV: 10:35 And it came to passe when the Arke set forward, that Moses said, Rise vp Lord, and let thine enemies be scattered, and let them that hate thee, flee before thee.
text.NABRE: Whenever the ark set out, Moses would say, “Arise, O Lord, may your enemies be scattered, and may those who hate you flee before you.”

text.NRSV: And whenever it came to rest, he would say, "Return, O Lord of the ten thousand thousands of Israel."
text.Rotherham: But when it rested, he said,—Return, O Yahweh! unto the myriads of the thousands of Israel.
text.KJV: 10:36 And when it rested, he said, Returne, O Lord, vnto the many thousands of Israel.
text.NABRE: And when it came to rest, he would say, “Bring back, O Lord, the myriads of Israel’s troops!”

text.NRSV: Now when the people complained in the hearing of the Lord about their misfortunes, the Lord heard it and his anger was kindled. Then the fire of the Lord burned against them, and consumed some outlying parts of the camp.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that when the people were giving themselves up to murmuring, it was grievous in the ears of Yahweh,—so Yahweh hearkened, and kindled was his anger, and the fire of Yahweh burned among them, and consumed the uttermost part of the camp.
text.KJV: 11:1 And when the people complained, it displeased the Lord: and the Lord heard it: and his anger was kindled, and the fire of the Lord burnt among them, and consumed them that were in the vttermost parts of the campe.
text.NABRE: Chapter 11 Discontent of the People. Now the people complained bitterly in the hearing of the Lord; and when he heard it his wrath flared up, so that the Lord ’s fire burned among them and consumed the outskirts of the camp.

text.NRSV: But the people cried out to Moses; and Moses prayed to the Lord, and the fire abated.
text.Rotherham: Then did the people make outcry unto Moses,—and Moses prayed unto Yahweh, and the fire sank down.
text.KJV: 11:2 And the people cried vnto Moses, and when Moses prayed vnto the Lord, the fire was quenched.
text.NABRE: But when the people cried out to Moses, he prayed to the Lord and the fire died out.

text.NRSV: So that place was called Taberah, because the fire of the Lord burned against them.
text.Rotherham: So he called the name of that place, Taberah [="A Burning"] because there burned among them the fire of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 11:3 And hee called the name of the place Taberah: because the fire of the Lord burnt among them.
text.NABRE: Hence that place was called Taberah, because there the fire of the Lord burned among them.

text.NRSV: The rabble among them had a strong craving; and the Israelites also wept again, and said, "If only we had meat to eat!
text.Rotherham: Moreover the mixed multitude that was in their midst concealed not their lusting,—and so even the sons of Israel, fell away and wept, and said: Who will grant us to eat flesh?
text.KJV: 11:4 ¶ And the mixt multitude that was among them, fell a lusting, and the children of Israel also wept againe, and said, Who shal giue vs flesh to eate?
text.NABRE: The riffraff among them were so greedy for meat that even the Israelites lamented again, “If only we had meat for food!

text.NRSV: We remember the fish we used to eat in Egypt for nothing, the cucumbers, the melons, the leeks, the onions, and the garlic;
text.Rotherham: We remember the fish that we did eat in Egypt, without money,—the cucumbers, and the water-melons, and the leeks and the onions, and the garlick.
text.KJV: 11:5 We remember the fish which wee did eate in Egypt freely: the cucumbers and the melons, and the leekes, and the onions, and the garlicke.
text.NABRE: We remember the fish we used to eat without cost in Egypt, and the cucumbers, the melons, the leeks, the onions, and the garlic.

text.NRSV: but now our strength is dried up, and there is nothing at all but this manna to look at."
text.Rotherham: But, now, our soul is dried up, there is nothing at all,—unless, unto the manna, [we turn] our eyes.
text.KJV: 11:6 But now our soule is dried away, there is nothing at all, besides this Manna, before our eyes.
text.NABRE: But now we are famished; we have nothing to look forward to but this manna.”

text.NRSV: Now the manna was like coriander seed, and its color was like the color of gum resin.
text.Rotherham: Now, the manna, was like coriander seed, and the appearance thereof like the appearance of bdellium,
text.KJV: 11:7 And the Manna was as Coriander seed, and the colour thereof as the colour of Bdelium:
text.NABRE: Manna was like coriander seed and had the appearance of bdellium.

text.NRSV: The people went around and gathered it, ground it in mills or beat it in mortars, then boiled it in pots and made cakes of it; and the taste of it was like the taste of cakes baked with oil.
text.Rotherham: The people used to go about and pick it up, and grind it with a pair of mill-stones, or pound it in a mortar, and boil it in a pot, and make it into round cakes,—then was the taste thereof like the taste of a sweet cake made with oil.
text.KJV: 11:8 And the people went about, and gathered it, and ground it in milles, or beat it in a morter, and baked it in pans, and made cakes of it: and the taste of it was as the taste of fresh oyle.
text.NABRE: When they had gone about and gathered it up, the people would grind it between millstones or pound it in a mortar, then cook it in a pot and make it into loaves, with a rich creamy taste.

text.NRSV: When the dew fell on the camp in the night, the manna would fall with it.
text.Rotherham: When the dew came down upon the camp by night, the manna came down thereupon.
text.KJV: 11:9 And when the dew fell vpon the campe in the night, the Manna fell vpon it.
text.NABRE: At night, when the dew fell upon the camp, the manna also fell.

text.NRSV: Moses heard the people weeping throughout their families, all at the entrances of their tents. Then the Lord became very angry, and Moses was displeased.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses heard the people weeping by their families, every one at the entrance of his tent,—then kindled the anger of Yahweh fiercely, and in the eyes of Moses, it was grievous.
text.KJV: 11:10 ¶ Then Moses heard the people weepe throughout their families, euery man in the doore of his tent, and the anger of the Lord was kindled greatly, Moses also was displeased.
text.NABRE: When Moses heard the people, family after family, crying at the entrance of their tents, so that the Lord became very angry, he was grieved.

text.NRSV: So Moses said to the Lord, "Why have you treated your servant so badly? Why have I not found favor in your sight, that you lay the burden of all this people on me?
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto Yahweh—Wherefore hast thou let thy servant come to grief, and wherefore have I not found favour in thine eyes,—that thou shouldest lay the burden of all this people upon me?
text.KJV: 11:11 And Moses said vnto the Lord, Wherefore hast thou afflicted thy seruant? and wherefore haue I not found fauour in thy sight, that thou layest the burden of all this people vpon me?
text.NABRE: “Why do you treat your servant so badly?” Moses asked the Lord. “Why are you so displeased with me that you burden me with all this people?

text.NRSV: Did I conceive all this people? Did I give birth to them, that you should say to me, 'Carry them in your bosom, as a nurse carries a sucking child,' to the land that you promised on oath to their ancestors?
text.Rotherham: Did, I, conceive all this people, or, I, beget them,—that thou shouldest say unto me, Carry them in thy bosom, as a nursing father carrieth a suckling, unto the soil which thou didst swear unto their fathers?
text.KJV: 11:12 Haue I conceiued all this people? haue I begotten them, that thou shouldest say vnto me, Cary them in thy bosome (as a nursing father beareth the sucking child) vnto the land which thou swarest vnto their fathers?
text.NABRE: Was it I who conceived all this people? or was it I who gave them birth, that you tell me to carry them at my breast, like a nurse carrying an infant, to the land you have promised under oath to their fathers?

text.NRSV: Where am I to get meat to give to all this people? For they come weeping to me and say, 'Give us meat to eat!'
text.Rotherham: Whence should, I, have flesh to give to all this people,—for they keep weeping by me saying, Oh give us flesh that we may eat!
text.KJV: 11:13 Whence should I haue flesh to giue vnto all this people? for they weep vnto me, saying, Giue vs flesh, that we may eate.
text.NABRE: Where can I get meat to give to all this people? For they are crying to me, ‘Give us meat for our food.’

text.NRSV: I am not able to carry all this people alone, for they are too heavy for me.
text.Rotherham: Unable, am, I, by myself, to carry all this people,—for they are too heavy for me.
text.KJV: 11:14 I am not able to beare all this people alone, because it is too heauie for mee.
text.NABRE: I cannot carry all this people by myself, for they are too heavy for me.

text.NRSV: If this is the way you are going to treat me, put me to death at once—if I have found favor in your sight—and do not let me see my misery."
text.Rotherham: But if, in this way, thou art going to deal with me, slay me, I beseech thee, slay, if I have found favour in thine eyes,—and let me not see my grief,
text.KJV: 11:15 And if thou deale thus with mee, kill me, I pray thee out of hand, if I haue found fauour in thy sight, and let me not see my wretchednesse.
text.NABRE: If this is the way you will deal with me, then please do me the favor of killing me at once, so that I need no longer face my distress.”

text.NRSV: So the Lord said to Moses, "Gather for me seventy of the elders of Israel, whom you know to be the elders of the people and officers over them; bring them to the tent of meeting, and have them take their place there with you.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses—Gather thou unto me seventy men, from among the elders of Israel, of whom thou knowest that they are elders of the people and their overseers,—then shalt thou take them unto the tent of meeting, and they shall station themselves there with thee.
text.KJV: 11:16 ¶ And the Lord said vnto Moses, Gather vnto me seuentie men, of the Elders of Israel, whome thou knowest to be the elders of the people, and officers ouer them: and bring them vnto the Tabernacle of the Congregation, that they may stand there with thee.
text.NABRE: The Seventy Elders. Then the Lord said to Moses: Assemble for me seventy of the elders of Israel, whom you know to be elders and authorities among the people, and bring them to the tent of meeting. When they are in place beside you,

text.NRSV: I will come down and talk with you there; and I will take some of the spirit that is on you and put it on them; and they shall bear the burden of the people along with you so that you will not bear it all by yourself.
text.Rotherham: Then will I come down, and speak with thee there, and will take of the spirit that is upon thee and put upon them,—and they shall carry, with thee, the burden of the people, and, thou, shalt not carry it by thyself.
text.KJV: 11:17 And I will come downe and talke with thee there, and I will take of the spirit which is vpon thee, and wil put it vpon them, and they shall beare the burden of the people with thee, that thou beare it not thy selfe alone.
text.NABRE: I will come down and speak with you there. I will also take some of the spirit that is on you and will confer it on them, that they may share the burden of the people with you. You will then not have to bear it by yourself.

text.NRSV: And say to the people: Consecrate yourselves for tomorrow, and you shall eat meat; for you have wailed in the hearing of the Lord, saying, 'If only we had meat to eat! Surely it was better for us in Egypt.' Therefore the Lord will give you meat, and you shall eat.
text.Rotherham: And, unto the people, shalt thou say: Hallow yourselves by to-morrow, and ye shall eat flesh, for ye have wept in the ears of Yahweh saying—Who will grant us to eat flesh? for it was well with us in Egypt,—so then Yahweh will give you flesh and ye shall eat.
text.KJV: 11:18 And say thou vnto the people, Sanctifie your selues against to morrow, and yee shall eate flesh: (for you haue wept in the eares of the Lord, saying, Who shall giue vs flesh to eate? for it was well with vs in Egypt:) therfore the Lord wil giue you flesh, and ye shall eate.
text.NABRE: To the people, however, you shall say: “Sanctify yourselves for tomorrow, when you shall have meat to eat. For in the hearing of the Lord you have cried, ‘If only we had meat for food! Oh, how well off we were in Egypt!’ Therefore the Lord will give you meat to eat,

text.NRSV: You shall eat not only one day, or two days, or five days, or ten days, or twenty days,
text.Rotherham: Not one day, shall ye eat nor two days,—nor five days nor ten days, nor twenty days:
text.KJV: 11:19 Ye shall not eate one day, nor two dayes, nor fiue dayes, neither ten dayes, nor twentie dayes:
text.NABRE: and you will eat it, not for one day, or two days, or five, or ten, or twenty days,

text.NRSV: but for a whole month—until it comes out of your nostrils and becomes loathsome to you—because you have rejected the Lord who is among you, and have wailed before him, saying, 'Why did we ever leave Egypt?'"
text.Rotherham: for a month of days until it cometh forth out of your nostrils, so shall it become to you a loathsome thing,—because ye have refused Yahweh who is in your midst, and have wept before him, saying, Wherefore now came we forth out of Egypt?
text.KJV: 11:20 But euen a whole moneth, vntill it come out at your nostrels, and it bee loathsome vnto you, because that yee haue despised the Lord which is among you, and haue wept before him, saying, Why came we foorth out of Egypt?
text.NABRE: but for a whole month—until it comes out of your very nostrils and becomes loathsome to you. For you have rejected the Lord who is in your midst, and in his presence you have cried, ‘Why did we ever leave Egypt?’”

text.NRSV: But Moses said, "The people I am with number six hundred thousand on foot; and you say, 'I will give them meat, that they may eat for a whole month'!
text.Rotherham: And Moses said, Six hundred thousand footmen, are the people in whose midst am, I, yet thou hast said—Flesh, will I give unto them, and they shall eat for a month of days.
text.KJV: 11:21 And Moses said, The people amongst whome I am, are sixe hundred thousand footmen, and thou hast said, I will giue them flesh, that they may eate a whole moneth.
text.NABRE: But Moses said, “The people around me include six hundred thousand soldiers; yet you say, ‘I will give them meat to eat for a whole month.’

text.NRSV: Are there enough flocks and herds to slaughter for them? Are there enough fish in the sea to catch for them?"
text.Rotherham: Shall the flocks and herds, be slaughtered for them, that it may suffice for them? Shall all the fishes of the sea, be gathered together unto them that it may suffice for them?
text.KJV: 11:22 Shall the flockes and the herds be slaine for them to suffice them? or shal all the fish of the sea bee gathered together for them, to suffice them?
text.NABRE: Can enough sheep and cattle be slaughtered for them? If all the fish of the sea were caught for them, would they have enough?”

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, "Is the Lord's power limited? Now you shall see whether my word will come true for you or not."
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, Shall the hand of Yahweh, fail? Now, shalt thou see whether my word come to pass unto thee or not.
text.KJV: 11:23 And the Lord said vnto Moses, Is the Lords hand waxed short? thou shalt see now whether my word shall come to passe vnto thee, or not.
text.NABRE: The Lord answered Moses: Is this beyond the Lord ’s reach? You shall see now whether or not what I have said to you takes place.

text.NRSV: So Moses went out and told the people the words of the Lord; and he gathered seventy elders of the people, and placed them all around the tent.
text.Rotherham: And Moses went forth and spake unto the people the words of Yahweh,—and gathered together seventy men from among the elders of the people, and caused them to stand round about the tent.
text.KJV: 11:24 ¶ And Moses went out, and tolde the people the wordes of the Lord, and gathered the seuenty men of the Elders of the people, and set them round about the Tabernacle.
text.NABRE: The Spirit on the Elders. So Moses went out and told the people what the Lord had said. Gathering seventy elders of the people, he had them stand around the tent.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord came down in the cloud and spoke to him, and took some of the spirit that was on him and put it on the seventy elders; and when the spirit rested upon them, they prophesied. But they did not do so again.
text.Rotherham: Then Yahweh came down, in the cloud, and spake unto him, and took of the spirit that was upon him, and gave unto the seventy men—the elders. And it came to pass that when the spirit rested upon them, they prophesied, and then did so no more.
text.KJV: 11:25 And the Lord came downe in a cloude, and spake vnto him, and tooke of the spirit that was vpon him, and gaue it vnto the seuentie Elders: and it came to passe that when the spirit rested vpon them, they prophesied, and did not cease.
text.NABRE: The Lord then came down in the cloud and spoke to him. Taking some of the spirit that was on Moses, he bestowed it on the seventy elders; and as the spirit came to rest on them, they prophesied but did not continue.

text.NRSV: Two men remained in the camp, one named Eldad, and the other named Medad, and the spirit rested on them; they were among those registered, but they had not gone out to the tent, and so they prophesied in the camp.
text.Rotherham: Now there were two men left behind in the camp—the name of the one, was Eldad and the name of the other, Medad, so then the spirit, rested on them—they, being among them who were written, though they had not gone forth unto the tent,—but they prophesied in the camp.
text.KJV: 11:26 But there remained two of the men in the campe, the name of the one was Eldad, & the name of the other Medad: and the Spirit rested vpon them, (and they were of them that were written, but went not out vnto the Tabernacle) and they prophesied in the campe.
text.NABRE: Now two men, one named Eldad and the other Medad, had remained in the camp, yet the spirit came to rest on them also. They too had been on the list, but had not gone out to the tent; and so they prophesied in the camp.

text.NRSV: And a young man ran and told Moses, "Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp."
text.Rotherham: And there ran a young man, and told Moses and said,—Eldad and Medad, are prophesying in the camp!
text.KJV: 11:27 And there ranne a yong man, and tolde Moses, and said, Eldad and Medad doe prophesie in the campe.
text.NABRE: So, when a young man ran and reported to Moses, “Eldad and Medad are prophesying in the camp,”

text.NRSV: And Joshua son of Nun, the assistant of Moses, one of his chosen men, said, "My lord Moses, stop them!"
text.Rotherham: Then responded Joshua son of Nun the attendant of Moses from his youth, and said,—My lord Moses forbid them!
text.KJV: 11:28 And Ioshua the sonne of Nun the seruant of Moses, one of his yong men, answered and said, My lord Moses, Forbid them.
text.NABRE: Joshua, son of Nun, who from his youth had been Moses’ aide, said, “My lord, Moses, stop them.”

text.NRSV: But Moses said to him, "Are you jealous for my sake? Would that all the Lord's people were prophets, and that the Lord would put his spirit on them!"
text.Rotherham: But Moses said unto him, Art, thou, jealous for, me? Oh would that, all the people of Yahweh, were prophets! Yea let Yahweh put his spirit upon them!
text.KJV: 11:29 And Moses said vnto him, Enuiest thou for my sake? Would God that all the Lords people were Prophets, and that the Lord would put his Spirit vpon them.
text.NABRE: But Moses answered him, “Are you jealous for my sake? If only all the people of the Lord were prophets! If only the Lord would bestow his spirit on them!”

text.NRSV: And Moses and the elders of Israel returned to the camp.
text.Rotherham: Then was Moses received back into the camp,—he and the elders of Israel.
text.KJV: 11:30 And Moses gate him into the campe, he, and the Elders of Israel.
text.NABRE: Then Moses retired to the camp, along with the elders of Israel.

text.NRSV: Then a wind went out from the Lord, and it brought quails from the sea and let them fall beside the camp, about a day's journey on this side and a day's journey on the other side, all around the camp, about two cubits deep on the ground.
text.Rotherham: Now, a wind, had sprung up, from Yahweh, and cut off quails from the sea, and let them lie over the camp—as it were a days journey here and a days journey there, round about the camp,—and as it were two cubits on the face of the land.
text.KJV: 11:31 ¶ And there went forth a winde from the Lord, and brought quailes from the sea, and let them fall by the campe, as it were a dayes iourney on this side, and as it were a dayes iourney on the other side round about the campe, and as it were two cubits high vpon the face of the earth.
text.NABRE: The Quail. There arose a wind from the Lord that drove in quail from the sea and left them all around the camp site, to a distance of a day’s journey and at a depth of two cubits upon the ground.
