text.NRSV: So the people worked all that day and night and all the next day, gathering the quails; the least anyone gathered was ten homers; and they spread them out for themselves all around the camp.
text.Rotherham: And the people rose up all that day and all the night and all the next day, and gathered the quails, he that did least, gathered ten homers,—and they spread them out for themselves spreading away, round about the camp.
text.KJV: 11:32 And the people stood vp all that day, and all that night, and all the next day, and they gathered the quailes: he that gathered least, gathered ten homers: and they spread them all abroad for themselues round about the campe.
text.NABRE: So all that day, all night, and all the next day the people set about to gather in the quail. Even the one who got the least gathered ten homers of them. Then they spread them out all around the camp.

text.NRSV: But while the meat was still between their teeth, before it was consumed, the anger of the Lord was kindled against the people, and the Lord struck the people with a very great plague.
text.Rotherham: The flesh, was yet between their teeth, not yet chewed,—when the anger of Yahweh, had kindled upon the people, and Yahweh smote the people, with an exceeding great plague.
text.KJV: 11:33 And while the flesh was yet betweene their teeth, yer it was chewed, the wrath of the Lord was kindled against the people, and the Lord smote the people with a very great plague.
text.NABRE: But while the meat was still between their teeth, before it could be chewed, the Lord ’s wrath flared up against the people, and the Lord struck them with a very great plague.

text.NRSV: So that place was called Kibroth-hattaavah, because there they buried the people who had the craving.
text.Rotherham: And the name of that place was called Kibroth-hattaavah—because there, they buried the people who had lusted,
text.KJV: 11:34 And he called the name of that place, Kibroth-Hattaauah: because there they buried the people that lusted.
text.NABRE: So that place was named Kibroth-hattaavah, because it was there that the greedy people were buried.

text.NRSV: From Kibroth-hattaavah the people journeyed to Hazeroth.
text.Rotherham: From Kibroth-hattaavah, the people set forward to Hazeroth,—and they remained in Hazeroth.
text.KJV: 11:35 And the people iourneyed from Kibroth-Hattaauah, vnto Hazeroth: and abode at Hazeroth.
text.NABRE: From Kibroth-hattaavah the people set out for Hazeroth, where they stayed.

text.NRSV: While they were at Hazeroth, Miriam and Aaron spoke against Moses because of the Cushite woman whom he had married (for he had indeed married a Cushite woman);
text.Rotherham: And Miriam and Aaron spake against Moses, on account of the Cushite woman whom he had taken,—for, a Cushite woman, had he taken.
text.KJV: 12:1 And Miriam and Aaron spake against Moses, because of the Ethiophian woman, whom hee had married: for he had married an Ethiopian woman.
text.NABRE: Chapter 12 Jealousy of Aaron and Miriam. Miriam and Aaron spoke against Moses on the pretext of the Cushite woman he had married; for he had in fact married a Cushite woman.

text.NRSV: and they said, "Has the Lord spoken only through Moses? Has he not spoken through us also?" And the Lord heard it.
text.Rotherham: And they said: Is it only and solely with Moses, that Yahweh hath spoken? With us also, hath he not spoken? And Yahweh heard it.
text.KJV: 12:2 And they said, Hath the Lord indeed spoken onely by Moses? Hath hee not spoken also by vs? And the Lord heard it.
text.NABRE: They complained, “Is it through Moses alone that the Lord has spoken? Has he not spoken through us also?” And the Lord heard this.

text.NRSV: Now the man Moses was very humble, more so than anyone else on the face of the earth.
text.Rotherham: Now, the man Moses, was patient, exceedingly,—above all the sons of earth, who were on the face of the ground.
text.KJV: 12:3 (Now the man Moses was very meeke, aboue all the men which were vpon the face of the earth.)
text.NABRE: Now the man Moses was very humble, more than anyone else on earth.

text.NRSV: Suddenly the Lord said to Moses, Aaron, and Miriam, "Come out, you three, to the tent of meeting." So the three of them came out.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh suddenly, unto Moses, and unto Aaron, and unto Miriam, Come out ye three unto the tent of meeting. And they three went out.
text.KJV: 12:4 And the Lord spake suddenly vnto Moses, and vnto Aaron, and vnto Miriam, Come out ye three vnto the Tabernacle of the Congregation: and they three came out.
text.NABRE: So at once the Lord said to Moses and Aaron and Miriam: Come out, you three, to the tent of meeting. And the three of them went.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord came down in a pillar of cloud, and stood at the entrance of the tent, and called Aaron and Miriam; and they both came forward.
text.Rotherham: Then Yahweh came down in a pillar of cloud, and stood at the entrance of the tent,—and called Aaron and Miriam, and they both went forth.
text.KJV: 12:5 And the Lord came downe in the pillar of the cloude, and stood in the doore of the Tabernacle, and called Aaron and Miriam: and they both came foorth.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord came down in a column of cloud, and standing at the entrance of the tent, called, “Aaron and Miriam.” When both came forward,

text.NRSV: And he said, "Hear my words: When there are prophets among you, I the Lord make myself known to them in visions; I speak to them in dreams.
text.Rotherham: Then said he Hear, I beseech you my words. When ye have your prophet, As Yahweh in a vision, will I make myself known, unto him; In a dream, will I speak with him.
text.KJV: 12:6 And hee saide, Heare now my words: If there be a Prophet among you, I the Lord will make my selfe knowen vnto him in a vision, and will speake vnto him in a dreame:
text.NABRE: the Lord said: Now listen to my words: If there are prophets among you, in visions I reveal myself to them, in dreams I speak to them;

text.NRSV: Not so with my servant Moses; he is entrusted with all my house.
text.Rotherham: Not so, my servant Moses,—In all my house, trusty, is he:
text.KJV: 12:7 My seruant Moses is not so, who is faithfull in all mine house.
text.NABRE: Not so with my servant Moses! Throughout my house he is worthy of trust:

text.NRSV: With him I speak face to face—clearly, not in riddles; and he beholds the form of the Lord. Why then were you not afraid to speak against my servant Moses?"
text.Rotherham: Mouth to mouth, do I speak with him And plainly—not in dark sayings, And the form of Yahweh, doth he discern,—Wherefore then, were ye not afraid to speak against my servant—against Moses?
text.KJV: 12:8 With him will I speake mouth to mouth euen apparantly, and not in darke speeches, and the similitude of the Lord shall hee behold: wherefore then were yee not afraid to speake against my seruant Moses?
text.NABRE: face to face I speak to him, plainly and not in riddles. The likeness of the Lord he beholds. Why, then, do you not fear to speak against my servant Moses?

text.NRSV: And the anger of the Lord was kindled against them, and he departed.
text.Rotherham: And the anger of Yahweh kindled upon them and he departed.
text.KJV: 12:9 And the anger of the Lord was kindled against them, and he departed.
text.NABRE: And so the Lord ’s wrath flared against them, and he departed.

text.NRSV: When the cloud went away from over the tent, Miriam had become leprous, as white as snow. And Aaron turned towards Miriam and saw that she was leprous.
text.Rotherham: And, when, the cloud, removed from off the tent,—lo! Miriam, leprous, like snow! And Aaron turned unto Miriam and lo! …leprous!
text.KJV: 12:10 And the cloud departed from off the Tabernacle, and behold, Miriam became leprous, white as snow: and Aaron looked vpon Miriam, and behold, she was leprous.
text.NABRE: Miriam’s Punishment. Now the cloud withdrew from the tent, and there was Miriam, stricken with a scaly infection, white as snow! When Aaron turned toward Miriam and saw her stricken with snow-white scales,

text.NRSV: Then Aaron said to Moses, "Oh, my lord, do not punish us for a sin that we have so foolishly committed.
text.Rotherham: Then said Aaron unto Moses,—Oh my lord, do not I beseech thee lay upon us sin, although we have made ourselves foolish, and although we have sinned.
text.KJV: 12:11 And Aaron said vnto Moses, Alas my lord, I beseech thee, lay not the sinne vpon vs, wherein we haue done foolishly, and wherein we haue sinned:
text.NABRE: he said to Moses, “Ah, my lord! Please do not charge us with the sin that we have foolishly committed!

text.NRSV: Do not let her be like one stillborn, whose flesh is half consumed when it comes out of its mother's womb."
text.Rotherham: Let her not, I beseech thee, remain like the still-born,—which when it is born of its mother, the half of its flesh is consumed.
text.KJV: 12:12 Let her not bee as one dead, of whom the flesh is halfe consumed, when he commeth out of his mothers wombe.
text.NABRE: Do not let her be like the stillborn baby that comes forth from its mother’s womb with its flesh half consumed.”

text.NRSV: And Moses cried to the Lord, "O God, please heal her."
text.Rotherham: Then Moses made outcry unto Yahweh, saying,—O GOD, I beseech thee grant healing. I beseech thee unto her.
text.KJV: 12:13 And Moses cryed vnto the Lord, saying, Heale her now, O God, I beseech thee.
text.NABRE: Then Moses cried to the Lord, “Please, not this! Please, heal her!”

text.NRSV: But the Lord said to Moses, "If her father had but spit in her face, would she not bear her shame for seven days? Let her be shut out of the camp for seven days, and after that she may be brought in again."
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses: If, her own father, had, but spat, in her face, would she not, have acknowledged the shame for seven days? Let her shut herself up for seven days, outside the camp, and afterwards, let her be received back.
text.KJV: 12:14 ¶ And the Lord said vnto Moses, If her father had but spit in her face, should she not bee ashamed seuen dayes? let her be shut out from the campe seuen dayes, and after that let her be receiued in againe.
text.NABRE: But the Lord answered Moses: Suppose her father had spit in her face, would she not bear her shame for seven days? Let her be confined outside the camp for seven days; afterwards she may be brought back.

text.NRSV: So Miriam was shut out of the camp for seven days; and the people did not set out on the march until Miriam had been brought in again.
text.Rotherham: So Miriam shut herself up, outside the camp, for seven days,—and, the people, set not forward, until Miriam had been received back.
text.KJV: 12:15 And Miriam was shut out from the campe seuen dayes: and the people iourneied not, til Miriam was brought in againe.
text.NABRE: So Miriam was confined outside the camp for seven days, and the people did not start out again until she was brought back.

text.NRSV: After that the people set out from Hazeroth, and camped in the wilderness of Paran.
text.Rotherham: Then afterwards, did the people set forward from Hazeroth, and encamped in the desert of Paran.
text.KJV: 12:16 And afterward the people remoued from Hazeroth, and pitched in the wildernesse of Paran.
text.NABRE: After that the people set out from Hazeroth and encamped in the wilderness of Paran.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses,
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 13:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Chapter 13 The Twelve Scouts. The Lord said to Moses:

text.NRSV: "Send men to spy out the land of Canaan, which I am giving to the Israelites; from each of their ancestral tribes you shall send a man, every one a leader among them."
text.Rotherham: Send for thee men and let them spy out the land of Canaan, which I am giving unto the sons of Israel,—one man each for the tribe of his fathers, shalt thou send, each one a prince among them.
text.KJV: 13:2 Send thou men, that they may search the lande of Canaan, which I giue vnto the children of Israel: of euery tribe of their fathers shal ye send a man, euery one a ruler among them.
text.NABRE: Send men to reconnoiter the land of Canaan, which I am giving the Israelites. You shall send one man from each ancestral tribe, every one a leader among them.

text.NRSV: So Moses sent them from the wilderness of Paran, according to the command of the Lord, all of them leading men among the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses sent them out of the desert of Paran, at the bidding of Yahweh,—all of them, great men, heads of the sons of Israel, were they.
text.KJV: 13:3 And Moses by the commaundement of the Lord, sent them from the wildernes of Paran: all those men were heads of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: So Moses sent them from the wilderness of Paran, at the direction of the Lord. All of them were leaders among the Israelites.

text.NRSV: These were their names: From the tribe of Reuben, Shammua son of Zaccur;
text.Rotherham: And, these, are their names,—Of the tribe of Reuben, Shammua, son of Zaccur;
text.KJV: 13:4 And these were their names. Of the tribe of Reuben, Shammua the sonne of Zaccur.
text.NABRE: These were their names: from the tribe of Reuben, Shammua, son of Zaccur;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat son of Hori;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat, son of Hori;
text.KJV: 13:5 Of the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat the sonne of Hori.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat, son of Hori;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Judah, Caleb, son of Jephunneh;
text.KJV: 13:6 Of the tribe of Iudah, Caleb the sonne of Iephunneh.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Judah, Caleb, son of Jephunneh;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Issachar, Igal son of Joseph;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Issachar, Igal, son of Joseph;
text.KJV: 13:7 Of the tribe of Issachar, Igal the sonne of Ioseph.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Issachar, Igal;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea son of Nun;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea, son of Nun;
text.KJV: 13:8 Of the tribe of Ephraim, Oshea the sonne of Nun.
text.NABRE: for the Josephites, from the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea, son of Nun;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Benjamin, Palti son of Raphu;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Benjamin, Palti, son of Raphu;
text.KJV: 13:9 Of the tribe of Beniamin, Palti the sonne of Raphu.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Benjamin, Palti, son of Raphu;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel son of Sodi;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel, son of Sodi;
text.KJV: 13:10 Of the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel the sonne of Sodi.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel, son of Sodi;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Joseph (that is, from the tribe of Manasseh), Gaddi son of Susi;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Joseph—of the tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi son of Susi;
text.KJV: 13:11 Of the tribe of Ioseph, namely of the tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi the sonne of Susi.
text.NABRE: for the Josephites, from the tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi, son of Susi;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Dan, Ammiel son of Gemalli;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Dan, Ammiel, son of Gemalli;
text.KJV: 13:12 Of the tribe of Dan, Ammiel the sonne of Gemalli.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Dan, Ammiel, son of Gemalli;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Asher, Sethur son of Michael;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Asher, Sethur, son of Michael;
text.KJV: 13:13 Of the tribe of Asher, Sethur the sonne of Michael.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Asher, Sethur, son of Michael;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi son of Vophsi;
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi, son of Vophsi;
text.KJV: 13:14 Of the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi the sonne of Uophsi.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi, son of Vophsi;

text.NRSV: from the tribe of Gad, Geuel son of Machi.
text.Rotherham: Of the tribe of Gad, Geuel, son of Machi.
text.KJV: 13:15 Of the tribe of Gad, Geuel the sonne of Machi.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Gad, Geuel, son of Machi.

text.NRSV: These were the names of the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land. And Moses changed the name of Hoshea son of Nun to Joshua.
text.Rotherham: These, are the names of the men, whom Moses sent to spy out the land,—but Moses called Hoshea son of Nun, Joshua.
text.KJV: 13:16 These are the names of the men which Moses sent to spie out the land: and Moses called Oshea the sonne of Nun, Iehoshua.
text.NABRE: These are the names of the men whom Moses sent to reconnoiter the land. But Hoshea, son of Nun, Moses called Joshua.

text.NRSV: Moses sent them to spy out the land of Canaan, and said to them, "Go up there into the Negeb, and go up into the hill country,
text.Rotherham: So Moses sent them, to spy out the land of Canaan,—and said unto them—Go up, here in the South, Yea ye shall go up the mountain;
text.KJV: 13:17 ¶ And Moses sent them to spie out the land of Canaan, and said vnto them, Get you by this way Southward, and goe vp into the mountaine:
text.NABRE: In sending them to reconnoiter the land of Canaan, Moses said to them, “Go up there in the Negeb, up into the highlands,

text.NRSV: and see what the land is like, and whether the people who live in it are strong or weak, whether they are few or many,
text.Rotherham: And shall see the land, what it is,—And the people that dwell thereupon, Whether they are, strong, or weak, Whether they are, few or many;
text.KJV: 13:18 And see the lande what it is, and the people that dwelleth therein, whether they bee strong or weake, fewe or many:
text.NABRE: and see what kind of land it is and whether the people living there are strong or weak, few or many.

text.NRSV: and whether the land they live in is good or bad, and whether the towns that they live in are unwalled or fortified,
text.Rotherham: And what, the land, is, wherein they dwell, Whether it is, good, or, bad,—And the cities within which they dwell, Whether in camps or in strongholds;
text.KJV: 13:19 And what the lande is that they dwell in, whether it be good or bad, and what cities they bee that they dwell in, whether in tents, or in strong holds:
text.NABRE: Is the country in which they live good or bad? Are the towns in which they dwell open or fortified?

text.NRSV: and whether the land is rich or poor, and whether there are trees in it or not. Be bold, and bring some of the fruit of the land." Now it was the season of the first ripe grapes.
text.Rotherham: And what the land is, Whether, fat, or, lean, Whether there is timber therein or is not, Then shall ye pluck up courage and take of the fruit of the land. Now the days, were the days of the first-fruits of grapes.
text.KJV: 13:20 And what the land is, whether it be fat or leane, whether there be wood therein, or not. And be ye of good courage, and bring of the fruit of the land: (Now the time was the time of the first ripe grapes)
text.NABRE: Is the soil fertile or barren, wooded or clear? And do your best to get some of the fruit of the land.” It was then the season for early grapes.

text.NRSV: So they went up and spied out the land from the wilderness of Zin to Rehob, near Lebo-hamath.
text.Rotherham: So they went up, and spied out the land,—from the wilderness of Zin unto Rehob at the entering in of Hamath.
text.KJV: 13:21 ¶ So they went vp, and searched the land, from the wildernesse of Zin, vnto Rehob, as men come to Hamath.
text.NABRE: So they went up and reconnoitered the land from the wilderness of Zin as far as where Rehob adjoins Lebo-hamath.

text.NRSV: They went up into the Negeb, and came to Hebron; and Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, the Anakites, were there. (Hebron was built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.)
text.Rotherham: Yea they went up in the South and entered as far as Hebron, and there, were Ahiman Sheshai and Talmai descendants of the Anak. Now, Hebron, was built seven years, before Zoan of Egypt.
text.KJV: 13:22 And they ascended by the South, and came vnto Hebron: where Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, the children of Anak were: Now Hebron was built seuen yeeres before Zoan in Egypt.
text.NABRE: Going up by way of the Negeb, they reached Hebron, where Ahiman, Sheshai and Talmai, descendants of the Anakim, were. (Now Hebron had been built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.)

text.NRSV: And they came to the Wadi Eshcol, and cut down from there a branch with a single cluster of grapes, and they carried it on a pole between two of them. They also brought some pomegranates and figs.
text.Rotherham: And they entered as far as the ravine of Eschol, and cut down, from thence a branch with one cluster of grapes, and bare it on a pole between two,—also of the pomegranates and of the figs.
text.KJV: 13:23 And they came vnto the brooke of Eshcol, and cut downe from thence a branch with one cluster of grapes, and they bare it betweene two vpon a staffe, and they brought of the pomegranates and of the figs.
text.NABRE: They also reached the Wadi Eshcol, where they cut down a branch with a single cluster of grapes on it, which two of them carried on a pole, as well as some pomegranates and figs.

text.NRSV: That place was called the Wadi Eshcol, because of the cluster that the Israelites cut down from there.
text.Rotherham: That place, was called, The Ravine of Eschol [="Cluster-ravine"]—on account of the cluster, which was cut down from thence by the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 13:24 The place was called the brooke Eshcol, because of the cluster of grapes which the children of Israel cut downe from thence.
text.NABRE: It was because of the cluster the Israelites cut there that they called the place Wadi Eshcol.

text.NRSV: At the end of forty days they returned from spying out the land.
text.Rotherham: And they returned from spying out the land,—at the end of forty days.
text.KJV: 13:25 And they returned from searching of the land after fourty dayes.
text.NABRE: Their Report. They returned from reconnoitering the land forty days later.

text.NRSV: And they came to Moses and Aaron and to all the congregation of the Israelites in the wilderness of Paran, at Kadesh; they brought back word to them and to all the congregation, and showed them the fruit of the land.
text.Rotherham: So they took their journey, and came in unto Moses and unto Aaron and unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel into the wilderness of Paran, towards Kadesh,—and brought back word—unto them and unto all the assembly, and showed them the fruit of the land.
text.KJV: 13:26 ¶ And they went and came to Moses, and to Aaron, and to all the Congregation of the children of Israel vnto the wildernesse of Paran, to Kadesh, and brought backe word vnto them, and vnto all the Congregation, and shewed them the fruit of the land.
text.NABRE: Proceeding directly to Moses and Aaron and the whole community of the Israelites in the wilderness of Paran at Kadesh, they made a report to them and to the whole community, showing them the fruit of the land.

text.NRSV: And they told him, "We came to the land to which you sent us; it flows with milk and honey, and this is its fruit.
text.Rotherham: And they recounted unto him, and said, We entered into the land whither thou didst send us, and surely it doth flow with milk and honey, and, this, is the fruit thereof.
text.KJV: 13:27 And they told him, and said, We came vnto the land whither thou sentest vs, & surely it floweth with milke and honie; and this is the fruit of it.
text.NABRE: They told Moses: “We came to the land to which you sent us. It does indeed flow with milk and honey, and here is its fruit.

text.NRSV: Yet the people who live in the land are strong, and the towns are fortified and very large; and besides, we saw the descendants of Anak there.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless surely mighty, are the people that dwell in the land,—and, the cities, walled in, exceeding great, moreover also, the descendants of the Anak, saw we there.
text.KJV: 13:28 Neuerthelesse, the people bee strong that dwell in the land, and the cities are walled and very great: and moreouer, we saw the children of Anak there.
text.NABRE: However, the people who are living in the land are powerful, and the towns are fortified and very large. Besides, we saw descendants of the Anakim there.

text.NRSV: The Amalekites live in the land of the Negeb; the Hittites, the Jebusites, and the Amorites live in the hill country; and the Canaanites live by the sea, and along the Jordan."
text.Rotherham: Amalek, dwelleth in the land of the South; And, the Hittite, and the Jebusite and the Amorite, dwell in the mountain And the Canaanite, dwelleth by the sea, and by the side of the Jordan.
text.KJV: 13:29 The Amalekites dwell in the land of the South: and the Hittites, and the Iebusites, and the Amorites dwell in the mountaines: and the Canaanites dwell by the sea, and by the coast of Iordane.
text.NABRE: Amalekites live in the region of the Negeb; Hittites, Jebusites and Amorites dwell in the highlands, and Canaanites along the sea and the banks of the Jordan.”

text.NRSV: But Caleb quieted the people before Moses, and said, "Let us go up at once and occupy it, for we are well able to overcome it."
text.Rotherham: And Caleb stilled the people unto Moses,—and said unto them—Let us go straight up, and possess it, for we shall, surely prevail, against it.
text.KJV: 13:30 And Caleb stilled the people before Moses, and said, Let vs goe vp at once, and possesse it, for we are well able to ouercome it.
text.NABRE: Caleb, however, quieted the people before Moses and said, “We ought to go up and seize the land, for we can certainly prevail over it.”

text.NRSV: Then the men who had gone up with him said, "We are not able to go up against this people, for they are stronger than we."
text.Rotherham: But the men who went up with him said, We cannot go up against the people,—for stronger, are they than we.
text.KJV: 13:31 But the men that went vp with him, said, Wee be not able to goe vp against the people, for they are stronger then we.
text.NABRE: But the men who had gone up with him said, “We cannot attack these people; they are too strong for us.”

text.NRSV: So they brought to the Israelites an unfavorable report of the land that they had spied out, saying, "The land that we have gone through as spies is a land that devours its inhabitants; and all the people that we saw in it are of great size.
text.Rotherham: So they sent forth a rumour of the land which they had spied out, among the sons of Israel, saying,—As touching the land through which we passed to spy it out, it is, land that eateth up them who dwell therein, And all the people that we saw in the midst thereof, were men of great stature.
text.KJV: 13:32 And they brought vp an euill report of the land which they had searched, vnto the children of Israel, saying, The land through which we haue gone, to search it, is a land that eateth vp the inhabitants thereof, and all the people that we saw in it, are men of a great stature.
text.NABRE: They spread discouraging reports among the Israelites about the land they had reconnoitered, saying, “The land that we went through and reconnoitered is a land that consumes its inhabitants. And all the people we saw there are huge.

text.NRSV: There we saw the Nephilim (the Anakites come from the Nephilim); and to ourselves we seemed like grasshoppers, and so we seemed to them."
text.Rotherham: And, there, saw we the giants, sons of Anak [descended] of the giants,—And we were in our own eyes, as grass-hoppers, And, so, were we in their eyes.
text.KJV: 13:33 And there we saw the giants, the sonnes of Anak, which come of the giants: and wee were in our owne sight as grashoppers, and so wee were in their sight.
text.NABRE: There we saw the Nephilim (the Anakim are from the Nephilim); in our own eyes we seemed like mere grasshoppers, and so we must have seemed to them.”

text.NRSV: Then all the congregation raised a loud cry, and the people wept that night.
text.Rotherham: Then did all the assembly lift up, and give forth their voice,—and the people wept throughout that night.
text.KJV: 14:1 And all the Congregation lifted vp their voyce and cried; and the people wept that night.
text.NABRE: Chapter 14 Threats of Revolt. At this, the whole community broke out with loud cries, and the people wept into the night.

text.NRSV: And all the Israelites complained against Moses and Aaron; the whole congregation said to them, "Would that we had died in the land of Egypt! Or would that we had died in this wilderness!
text.Rotherham: And all the sons of Israel, murmured against Moses and against Aaron,—and all the assembly said unto them—Oh that we had died in the land of Egypt, Or in this wilderness, oh that we had died!
text.KJV: 14:2 And all the children of Israel murmured against Moses, and against Aaron: and the whole Congregation said vnto them, Would God that we had died in the land of Egypt, or would God we had died in this wildernesse.
text.NABRE: All the Israelites grumbled against Moses and Aaron, the whole community saying to them, “If only we had died in the land of Egypt,” or “If only we would die here in the wilderness!

text.NRSV: Why is the Lord bringing us into this land to fall by the sword? Our wives and our little ones will become booty; would it not be better for us to go back to Egypt?"
text.Rotherham: Wherefore then is Yahweh bringing us into this land, to fall by the sword? Our wives and little ones, will become a prey! Would it not be well for us to go back towards Egypt?
text.KJV: 14:3 And wherefore hath the Lord brought vs vnto this land, to fall by the sword, that our wiues, and our children should be a pray? Were it not better for vs to returne into Egypt?
text.NABRE: Why is the Lord bringing us into this land only to have us fall by the sword? Our wives and little ones will be taken as spoil. Would it not be better for us to return to Egypt?”

text.NRSV: So they said to one another, "Let us choose a captain, and go back to Egypt."
text.Rotherham: And they said, one to another,—Let us appoint a head and let us go back towards Egypt.
text.KJV: 14:4 And they saide one to another, Let vs make a captaine, and let vs returne into Egypt.
text.NABRE: So they said to one another, “Let us appoint a leader and go back to Egypt.”

text.NRSV: Then Moses and Aaron fell on their faces before all the assembly of the congregation of the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Then did Moses and Aaron fall upon their faces,—before all the convocation of the assembly of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 14:5 Then Moses and Aaron fell on their faces before all the assembly of the Congregation of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: But Moses and Aaron fell prostrate before the whole assembled community of the Israelites;

text.NRSV: And Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh, who were among those who had spied out the land, tore their clothes
text.Rotherham: But, Joshua son of Nun, and Caleb son of Jephunneh, of them who spied out the land, rent their clothes;
text.KJV: 14:6 ¶ And Ioshua the sonne of Nun, and Caleb the sonne of Iephunneh, which were of them that searched the land, rent their clothes.
text.NABRE: while Joshua, son of Nun, and Caleb, son of Jephunneh, who had been among those that reconnoitered the land, tore their garments

text.NRSV: and said to all the congregation of the Israelites, "The land that we went through as spies is an exceedingly good land.
text.Rotherham: and spake unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel, saying,—As for the land through which we passed to spy it out, Good, is the land, exceedingly,
text.KJV: 14:7 And they spake vnto all the company of the children of Israel, saying. The land which wee passed thorow to search it, is an exceeding good land.
text.NABRE: and said to the whole community of the Israelites, “The land which we went through and reconnoitered is an exceedingly good land.

text.NRSV: If the Lord is pleased with us, he will bring us into this land and give it to us, a land that flows with milk and honey.
text.Rotherham: If Yahweh delight in us, then will he bring us into this land, and give it unto us,—A land which doth flow with milk and honey.
text.KJV: 14:8 If the Lord delight in vs, then he will bring vs into this land, and giue it vs, a land which floweth with milke and hony.
text.NABRE: If the Lord is pleased with us, he will bring us in to this land and give it to us, a land which flows with milk and honey.

text.NRSV: Only, do not rebel against the Lord; and do not fear the people of the land, for they are no more than bread for us; their protection is removed from them, and the Lord is with us; do not fear them."
text.Rotherham: Only against Yahweh, do not rebel. Do not, ye, fear the people of the land, For our food, they are,—Their shade hath departed, from over them. And Yahweh is with us, Do not fear them!
text.KJV: 14:9 Onely rebell not yee against the Lord, neither feare yee the people of the land, for they are bread for vs: their defence is departed from them, and the Lord is with vs: feare them not.
text.NABRE: Only do not rebel against the Lord! You need not be afraid of the people of the land, for they are but food for us! Their protection has left them, but the Lord is with us. Do not fear them.”

text.NRSV: But the whole congregation threatened to stone them. Then the glory of the Lord appeared at the tent of meeting to all the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: But when all the assembly bade stone them with stones, the glory of Yahweh, appeared, in the tent of meeting, unto all the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 14:10 But all the Congregation bade stone them with stones: and the glory of the Lord appeared in the Tabernacle of the Congregation, before all the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: The Lord’s Sentence. The whole community threatened to stone them. But the glory of the Lord appeared at the tent of meeting to all the Israelites.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said to Moses, "How long will this people despise me? And how long will they refuse to believe in me, in spite of all the signs that I have done among them?"
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses How long! must this people, despise me? And how long can they not believe in me. in view of all the signs which I have done in their midst?
text.KJV: 14:11 ¶ And the Lord said vnto Moses, How long will this people provoke me? and how long will it bee, yer they beleeue me, for all the signes which I haue shewed among them?
text.NABRE: And the Lord said to Moses: How long will this people spurn me? How long will they not trust me, despite all the signs I have performed among them?

text.NRSV: I will strike them with pestilence and disinherit them, and I will make of you a nation greater and mightier than they."
text.Rotherham: Let me smite them with pestilence, and disinherit them,—And make thee into a nation greater and more mighty than they!
text.KJV: 14:12 I will smite them with the pestilence, and disinherite them, and will make of thee a greater nation, and mightier then they.
text.NABRE: I will strike them with pestilence and disown them. Then I will make of you a nation greater and mightier than they.

text.NRSV: But Moses said to the Lord, "Then the Egyptians will hear of it, for in your might you brought up this people from among them,"
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses unto Yahweh,—So shall the Egyptians hear, For thou hast brought up. in thy might, this people out of their midst;
text.KJV: 14:13 ¶ And Moses said vnto the Lord, Then the Egyptians shall heare it, (for thou broughtest vp this people in thy might from among them:)
text.NABRE: But Moses said to the Lord: “The Egyptians will hear of this, for by your power you brought out this people from among them.

text.NRSV: and they will tell the inhabitants of this land. They have heard that you, O Lord, are in the midst of this people; for you, O Lord, are seen face to face, and your cloud stands over them and you go in front of them, in a pillar of cloud by day and in a pillar of fire by night.
text.Rotherham: And will tell it unto the inhabitants of this land: They have heard, That, thou, Yahweh, art in the midst of this people,—That eye to eye, is he seen—thou, Yahweh, And thy cloud, is standing over them, And in a pillar of cloud, thou thyself, art going on before them by day, And in a pillar of fire, by night.
text.KJV: 14:14 And they will tell it to the inhabitants of this land: for they haue heard that thou Lord art among this people, that thou Lord art seene face to face, and that thy cloud standeth ouer them, and that thou goest before them, by day time in a pillar of a cloud, and in a pillar of fire by night.
text.NABRE: They will tell the inhabitants of this land, who have heard that you, Lord, are in the midst of this people; you, Lord, who directly revealed yourself! Your cloud stands over them, and you go before them by day in a column of cloud and by night in a column of fire.

text.NRSV: Now if you kill this people all at one time, then the nations who have heard about you will say,
text.Rotherham: As soon, therefore, as thou hast put to death this people as one man, so soon will the nations who have heard thy fame speak saying:
text.KJV: 14:15 ¶ Now if thou shalt kill all this people, as one man, then the nations which haue heard the fame of thee, will speake, saying,
text.NABRE: If now you slay this people all at once, the nations who have heard such reports of you will say,

text.NRSV: 'It is because the Lord was not able to bring this people into the land he swore to give them that he has slaughtered them in the wilderness.'
text.Rotherham: Because Yahweh, was not able, to bring in this people into the land which he had sworn unto them, therefore did he slay them in the desert.
text.KJV: 14:16 Because the Lord was not able to bring this people into the lande which he sware vnto them, therefore he hath slaine them in the wildernesse.
text.NABRE: ‘The Lord was not able to bring this people into the land he swore to give them; that is why he slaughtered them in the wilderness.’

text.NRSV: And now, therefore, let the power of the Lord be great in the way you promised when you spoke, saying,
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, I beseech thee let the might of My Lord, be magnified,—according as thou didst speak saying:
text.KJV: 14:17 And now, I beseech thee, let the power of my Lord be great, according as thou hast spoken, saying,
text.NABRE: Now then, may my Lord’s forbearance be great, even as you have said,

text.NRSV: 'The Lord is slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love, forgiving iniquity and transgression, but by no means clearing the guilty, visiting the iniquity of the parents upon the children to the third and the fourth generation.'
text.Rotherham: Yahweh—slow to anger and great in lovingkindness, Forgiving iniquity and transgression: Though he will not leave, wholly unpunished, Visiting the iniquity of fathers upon sons, Upon a third generation, and upon a fourth.
text.KJV: 14:18 The Lord is long suffering, and of great mercie, forgiuing iniquitie and transgression, and by no meanes clearing the guiltie, visiting the iniquity of the fathers vpon the chldren, vnto the third and fourth generation.
text.NABRE: ‘The Lord is slow to anger and abounding in kindness, forgiving iniquity and rebellion; yet certainly not declaring the guilty guiltless, but punishing children to the third and fourth generation for their parents’ iniquity.’

text.NRSV: Forgive the iniquity of this people according to the greatness of your steadfast love, just as you have pardoned this people, from Egypt even until now."
text.Rotherham: Pardon I beseech thee, the iniquity of this people, According to the greatness of thy lovingkindness,—And according as thou hast forgiven this people, from Egypt even thus far.
text.KJV: 14:19 Pardon, I beseech thee, the iniquitie of this people, according vnto the greatnesse of thy mercie, and as thou hast forgiuen this people, from Egypt, euen vntill now.
text.NABRE: Pardon, then, the iniquity of this people in keeping with your great kindness, even as you have forgiven them from Egypt until now.”

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said, "I do forgive, just as you have asked;
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said, I have pardoned according to thy word;
text.KJV: 14:20 And the Lord said, I haue pardoned, according to thy word.
text.NABRE: The Lord answered: I pardon them as you have asked.

text.NRSV: nevertheless—as I live, and as all the earth shall be filled with the glory of the Lord—
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless as I live,—and that all the earth may be filled with the glory of Yahweh,
text.KJV: 14:21 But as truly as I liue, all the earth shalbe filled with the glory of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Yet, by my life and the Lord ’s glory that fills the whole earth,

text.NRSV: none of the people who have seen my glory and the signs that I did in Egypt and in the wilderness, and yet have tested me these ten times and have not obeyed my voice,
text.Rotherham: Verily, none of the men who have been beholding my glory and my signs, which I have done in Egypt and in the desert,—and have put me to the proof these ten times, and have not hearkened unto my voice,
text.KJV: 14:22 Because all those men which haue seene my glory, and my miracles which I did in Egypt, and in the wildernesse, and haue tempted mee now these ten times, and haue not hearkened to my voice,
text.NABRE: of all the people who have seen my glory and the signs I did in Egypt and in the wilderness, and who nevertheless have put me to the test ten times already and have not obeyed me,

text.NRSV: shall see the land that I swore to give to their ancestors; none of those who despised me shall see it.
text.Rotherham: shall see the land, which I sware to their fathers,—yea, none of my despisers, shall see it.
text.KJV: 14:23 Surely they shall not see the land which I sware vnto their fathers, neither shall any of them that prouoked me, see it.
text.NABRE: not one shall see the land which I promised on oath to their ancestors. None of those who have spurned me shall see it.

text.NRSV: But my servant Caleb, because he has a different spirit and has followed me wholeheartedly, I will bring into the land into which he went, and his descendants shall possess it.
text.Rotherham: But my servant Caleb—because there was another spirit with him, and he followed me fully, therefore will I bring him into the land whereinto he entered, and his seed, shall possess it.
text.KJV: 14:24 But my seruant Caleb, because hee had another spirit with him, (and hath followed mee fully) him will I bring into the land, whereinto he went, and his seed shall possesse it.
text.NABRE: But as for my servant Caleb, because he has a different spirit and follows me unreservedly, I will bring him into the land which he entered, and his descendants shall possess it.

text.NRSV: Now, since the Amalekites and the Canaanites live in the valleys, turn tomorrow and set out for the wilderness by the way to the Red Sea."
text.Rotherham: Now the Amalekites and the Canaanites, are dwelling in the valley,—to-morrow, turn ye away; and set yourselves forward towards the desert by the way of the Red Sea.
text.KJV: 14:25 (Now the Amalekites, and the Canaanites dwelt in the valley) to morrow turne you and get you into the wildernesse, by the way of the Red sea.
text.NABRE: But now, since the Amalekites and Canaanites are living in the valleys, turn away tomorrow and set out into the wilderness by way of the Red Sea road.

text.NRSV: And the Lord spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying:
text.KJV: 14:26 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, and vnto Aaron, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord also said to Moses and Aaron:

text.NRSV: How long shall this wicked congregation complain against me? I have heard the complaints of the Israelites, which they complain against me.
text.Rotherham: How long, as regardeth this evil assembly, are they to be murmuring against me? The murmuring of the sons of Israel which, they, have been murmuring against me, have I heard,
text.KJV: 14:27 How long shall I beare with this euil congregation which murmure against mee? I haue heard the murmurings of the children of Israel, which they murmure against mee.
text.NABRE: How long will this wicked community grumble against me? I have heard the grumblings of the Israelites against me.

text.NRSV: Say to them, "As I live," says the Lord, "I will do to you the very things I heard you say:
text.Rotherham: Say unto them: As I live, is the oracle of Yahweh: Surely as ye have spoken in mine ears, so, will I do unto you:—
text.KJV: 14:28 Say vnto them, As truly as I liue, saith the Lord, as ye haue spoken in mine eares, so will I doe to you:
text.NABRE: Tell them: “By my life”—oracle of the Lord —“I will do to you just what I have heard you say.

text.NRSV: your dead bodies shall fall in this very wilderness; and of all your number, included in the census, from twenty years old and upward, who have complained against me,
text.Rotherham: In this desert, shall your dead bodies fall And none of you who were numbered in all your counting, from twenty years old and upwards,—who have murmured against me,
text.KJV: 14:29 Your carcases shall fall in this wildernesse, and all that were numbred of you, according to your whole number from twentie yeeres old and vpward, which haue murmured against mee,
text.NABRE: Here in the wilderness your dead bodies shall fall. Of all your men of twenty years or more, enrolled in your registration, who grumbled against me,

text.NRSV: not one of you shall come into the land in which I swore to settle you, except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun.
text.Rotherham: none of you, shall enter into the land, as to which I uplifted my hand to give you an abode therein,—save Caleb son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, son of Nun:
text.KJV: 14:30 Doubtlesse ye shall not come into the land concerning which I sware to make you dwell therein, saue Caleb the sonne of Iephunneh, and Ioshua the sonne of Nun.
text.NABRE: not one of you shall enter the land where I solemnly swore to settle you, except Caleb, son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, son of Nun.

text.NRSV: But your little ones, who you said would become booty, I will bring in, and they shall know the land that you have despised.
text.Rotherham: whereas, your little ones, of whom ye said, they should become, a prey, I will bring them in, and they shall acknowledge the land which ye refused.
text.KJV: 14:31 But your little ones, which yee said should be a pray, them will I bring in, and they shall know the land which ye haue despised.
text.NABRE: Your little ones, however, who you said would be taken as spoil, I will bring in, and they shall know the land you rejected.

text.NRSV: But as for you, your dead bodies shall fall in this wilderness.
text.Rotherham: But, your own dead bodies, shall fall in this desert;
text.KJV: 14:32 But as for you, your carkases, they shall fall in this wildernesse.
text.NABRE: But as for you, your bodies shall fall here in the wilderness,

text.NRSV: And your children shall be shepherds in the wilderness for forty years, and shall suffer for your faithlessness, until the last of your dead bodies lies in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: and your sons, shall be shepherds in the desert forty years, and shall bear your unchastities,—until your dead bodies are consumed in the desert.
text.KJV: 14:33 And your children shall wander in the wildernes forty yeres, and beare your whoredomes, vntill your carkases be wasted in the wildernesse.
text.NABRE: while your children will wander for forty years, suffering for your infidelity, till the last of you lies dead in the wilderness.

text.NRSV: In accordance with the number of the days in which you spied out the land, forty days, for every day a year, you shall bear your iniquity, forty years, and you shall know my displeasure."
text.Rotherham: By the number of the days wherein ye spied out the land—forty days, each day for a year, shall they bear your iniquities—forty years,—so shall ye know my opposition.
text.KJV: 14:34 After the number of the dayes in which ye searched the land, euen fortie dayes (each day for a yeere) shall yee beare your iniquities, euen forty yeeres, and yee shall know my breach of promise.
text.NABRE: Corresponding to the number of days you spent reconnoitering the land—forty days—you shall bear your punishment one year for each day: forty years. Thus you will realize what it means to oppose me.

text.NRSV: I the Lord have spoken; surely I will do thus to all this wicked congregation gathered together against me: in this wilderness they shall come to a full end, and there they shall die.
text.Rotherham: I—Yahweh, have spoken, Verily this, will I do to all this evil assembly, who have conspired against me,—In this desert, shall they be consumed And there, shall they die.
text.KJV: 14:35 I the Lord haue said, I will surely doe it vnto all this euill Congregation, that are gathered together against mee: in this wildernesse they shalbe consumed, & there they shall die.
text.NABRE: I, the Lord, have spoken; and I will surely do this to this entire wicked community that conspired against me: here in the wilderness they shall come to their end and there they will die.”

text.NRSV: And the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land, who returned and made all the congregation complain against him by bringing a bad report about the land—
text.Rotherham: Now as for the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land,—who returned and caused all the assembly to murmur against him by giving out a rumour against the land,
text.KJV: 14:36 And the men which Moses sent to search the land, who returned, and made all the Congregation to murmure against him, by bringing vp a slander vpon the land,
text.NABRE: And the men whom Moses had sent to reconnoiter the land and who on returning had set the whole community grumbling against him by spreading discouraging reports about the land—

text.NRSV: the men who brought an unfavorable report about the land died by a plague before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: they died—the men who gave out an evil rumour of the land,—by the plague, before Yahweh.
text.KJV: 14:37 Euen those men that did bring vp the euill report vpon the land, died by the plague, before the Lord.
text.NABRE: these men who had spread discouraging reports about the land were struck down by the Lord and died.

text.NRSV: But Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh alone remained alive, of those men who went to spy out the land.
text.Rotherham: But, Joshua son of Nun, and Caleb son of Jephunneh! remained alive—of those men who went to spy out the land.
text.KJV: 14:38 But Ioshua the sonne of Nun, and Caleb the sonne of Iephunneh, which were of the men that went to search the land, liued still.
text.NABRE: Only Joshua, son of Nun, and Caleb, son of Jephunneh, survived of all the men who had gone to reconnoiter the land.

text.NRSV: When Moses told these words to all the Israelites, the people mourned greatly.
text.Rotherham: And Moses spake these words unto all the sons of Israel, and the people were greatly depressed.
text.KJV: 14:39 And Moses told these sayings vnto all the children of Israel, and the people mourned greatly.
text.NABRE: Unsuccessful Invasion. When Moses repeated these words to all the Israelites, the people mourned greatly.

text.NRSV: They rose early in the morning and went up to the heights of the hill country, saying, "Here we are. We will go up to the place that the Lord has promised, for we have sinned."
text.Rotherham: So they arose early in the morning, and went up into the top of the mountain, saying,—We are here—therefore will we go up into the place as to which Yahweh hath said that we have sinned.
text.KJV: 14:40 ¶ And they rose vp early in the morning, and gate them vp into the top of the mountaine, saying, Loe, we be here, and will goe vp vnto the place which the Lord hath promised: for we haue sinned.
text.NABRE: Early the next morning they started up high into the hill country, saying, “Here we are, ready to go up to the place that the Lord spoke of: for we did wrong.”

text.NRSV: But Moses said, "Why do you disobey the command of the Lord? It will not succeed.
text.Rotherham: But Moses said, Wherefore is it that, ye, are going beyond the bidding of Yahweh,—since that cannot prosper?
text.KJV: 14:41 And Moses said, Wherefore now doe you transgresse the commaundement of the Lord ? but it shall not prosper.
text.NABRE: But Moses said, “Why are you now transgressing the Lord ’s order? This cannot succeed.

text.NRSV: Do not go up, for the Lord is not with you; do not let yourselves be struck down before your enemies.
text.Rotherham: Do not go up, For Yahweh is not in your midst,—Lest ye be smitten down before your enemies.
text.KJV: 14:42 Goe not vp, for the Lord is not among you, that ye be not smitten before your enemies.
text.NABRE: Do not go up, because the Lord is not in your midst; do not allow yourself to be struck down by your enemies.

text.NRSV: For the Amalekites and the Canaanites will face you there, and you shall fall by the sword; because you have turned back from following the Lord, the Lord will not be with you."
text.Rotherham: For, the Amalekites and the Canaanites, are there before you, So shall ye fall by the sword,—For inasmuch as ye have turned back from following Yahweh, Therefore will Yahweh not be with you.
text.KJV: 14:43 For the Amalekites, and the Canaanites are there before you, and yee shall fall by the sword, because yee are turned away from the Lord; therefore the Lord will not bee with you.
text.NABRE: For there the Amalekites and Canaanites will face you, and you will fall by the sword. You have turned back from following the Lord; therefore the Lord will not be with you.”

text.NRSV: But they presumed to go up to the heights of the hill country, even though the ark of the covenant of the Lord, and Moses, had not left the camp.
text.Rotherham: But they presumptuously ascended into the top of the mountain,—though, neither, the ark of the covenant of Yahweh nor Moses moved out of the midst of the camp.
text.KJV: 14:44 But they presumed to go vp vnto the hill top: neuertheles the Arke of the Couenant of the Lord, and Moses departed not out of the campe.
text.NABRE: Yet they dared to go up high into the hill country, even though neither the ark of the covenant of the Lord nor Moses left the camp.

text.NRSV: Then the Amalekites and the Canaanites who lived in that hill country came down and defeated them, pursuing them as far as Hormah.
text.Rotherham: So ten the Amalekites and the Canaanites, that dwelt in that mountain, came down,—and smote them and routed them even unto Hormah.
text.KJV: 14:45 Then the Amalekites came downe, and the Canaanites which dwelt in that hill, and smote them, and discomfited them, euen vnto Hormah.
text.NABRE: And the Amalekites and Canaanites who dwelt in that hill country came down and defeated them, beating them back as far as Hormah.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 15:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Chapter 15 Secondary Offerings. The Lord spoke to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When you come into the land you are to inhabit, which I am giving you,
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shall say unto them,—When ye shall enter into the land of your dwelling-places, which, I, am giving unto you;
text.KJV: 15:2 Speake vnto the children of Israel, and say vnto them, When ye be come into the land of your habitations, which I giue vnto you,
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When you enter the land that I am giving you for your settlements,

text.NRSV: and you make an offering by fire to the Lord from the herd or from the flock—whether a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow or as a freewill offering or at your appointed festivals—to make a pleasing odor for the Lord,
text.Rotherham: and ye would offer, as an altar-flame unto Yahweh an ascending-offering or a sacrifice, for celebrating a vow or as a freewill-offering, or in your appointed seasons,—by way of offering a satisfying odour unto Yahweh, from the herd or from the flock,
text.KJV: 15:3 And will make an offering by fire vnto the Lord, a burnt offering or a sacrifice in performing a vow, or in a free will offering, or in your solemne feasts, to make a sweet sauour vnto the Lord, of the herd or of the flocke:
text.NABRE: if you make to the Lord an oblation from the herd or from the flock—either a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow, or as a voluntary offering, or for one of your festivals—to produce a pleasing aroma for the Lord,

text.NRSV: then whoever presents such an offering to the Lord shall present also a grain offering, one-tenth of an ephah of choice flour mixed with one-fourth of a hin of oil.
text.Rotherham: then shall he that bringeth near his oblation bring near unto Yahweh a meal-offering, of fine meal a tenth part [of an ephah] overflowed with the fourth part of a hin of oil;
text.KJV: 15:4 Then shall he that offereth his offering vnto the Lord, bring a meat offring of a tenth deale of flowre, mingled with the fourth part of an Hyn of oyle.
text.NABRE: the one presenting the offering shall also present to the Lord a grain offering, a tenth of a measure of bran flour mixed with a fourth of a hin of oil,

text.NRSV: Moreover, you shall offer one-fourth of a hin of wine as a drink offering with the burnt offering or the sacrifice, for each lamb.
text.Rotherham: wine also for a drink-offering, the fourth part of a hin, shalt thou offer with the ascending-offering or with the sacrifice,—for each he-lamb.
text.KJV: 15:5 And the fourth part of an Hyn of wine for a drinke offring shalt thou prepare, with the burnt offering or sacrifice for one lambe.
text.NABRE: as well as wine for a libation, a fourth of a hin. You will do this with the burnt offering or the sacrifice, for each lamb.

text.NRSV: For a ram, you shall offer a grain offering, two-tenths of an ephah of choice flour mixed with one-third of a hin of oil;
text.Rotherham: Or, with a ram, thou shalt offer a meal-offering, of fine meal, two tenths,—overflowed with oil, the third of a hin;
text.KJV: 15:6 Or for a ramme, thou shalt prepare for a meate offering two tenth deales of flowre mingled with the third part of an Hyn of oyle.
text.NABRE: Alternatively for a ram you shall make a grain offering of two tenths of a measure of bran flour mixed with a third of a hin of oil,

text.NRSV: and as a drink offering you shall offer one-third of a hin of wine, a pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: wine also, for a drink-offering, the third of a hin, shalt thou bring near as a satisfying odour unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 15:7 And for a drinke offering, thou shalt offer the third part of an Hyn of wine, for a sweete sauour vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: and for a libation, a third of a hin of wine, thereby presenting a pleasing aroma to the Lord.

text.NRSV: When you offer a bull as a burnt offering or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow or as an offering of well-being to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: And when thou wouldest offer a choice young bullock as an ascending-offering or sacrifice,—for celebrating a vow or as a peace-offering unto Yahweh,
text.KJV: 15:8 And when thou preparest a bullocke for a burnt offering, or for a sacrifice in performing a vow, or peace offerings vnto the Lord:
text.NABRE: If you make an offering from the herd—either a burnt offering, or a sacrifice, to fulfill a vow, or as a communion offering to the Lord,

text.NRSV: then you shall present with the bull a grain offering, three-tenths of an ephah of choice flour mixed with half a hin of oil,
text.Rotherham: then shall he bring near with his choice young bullock, a meal-offering, of fine meal three tenths,—overflowed with half a hin of oil;
text.KJV: 15:9 Then shall hee bring with a bullocke a meate offering of three tenth deales of flowre, mingled with halfe an Hyn of oyle.
text.NABRE: with it a grain offering of three tenths of a measure of bran flour mixed with half a hin of oil will be presented;

text.NRSV: and you shall present as a drink offering half a hin of wine, as an offering by fire, a pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: wine also, shalt thou bring near for a drink-offering, half a hin,—for an altar-flame of satisfying odour unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 15:10 And thou shalt bring for a drinke offering halfe an Hyn of wine, for an offering made by fire of a sweet sauour vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: and you will present for a libation, half a hin of wine—a sweet-smelling oblation to the Lord.

text.NRSV: Thus it shall be done for each ox or ram, or for each of the male lambs or the kids.
text.Rotherham: Thus and thus, shall it be done, for each ox, and for each ram,—and for each young one among the sheep, or among the goats:
text.KJV: 15:11 Thus shall it be done for one bullocke, or for one ramme, or for a lambe, or a kidde.
text.NABRE: The same is to be done for each ox, ram, lamb or goat.

text.NRSV: According to the number that you offer, so you shall do with each and every one.
text.Rotherham: according to the number which ye shall offer, thus and thus, shall ye do for each one according to their number.
text.KJV: 15:12 According to the number that yee shall prepare, so shall yee doe to euery one, according to their number.
text.NABRE: Whatever the number you offer, do the same for each of them.

text.NRSV: Every citizen shall do thus in presenting an offering by fire, a pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Every one born in the land, thus and thus, shall offer these things,—when bringing near an altar-flame of a satisfying odour unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 15:13 All that are borne of the countrey shall doe these things after this maner, in offering an offering made by fire of a sweet sauour, vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: All the native-born shall make these offerings in this way, whenever they present a sweet-smelling oblation to the Lord.

text.NRSV: An alien who lives with you, or who takes up permanent residence among you, and wishes to offer an offering by fire, a pleasing odor to the Lord, shall do as you do.
text.Rotherham: And when there shall sojourn with you a sojourner, or one who is in your midst to your generations, and he would offer an altar-flame of a satisfying odour unto Yahweh, as ye offer, so, shall he offer,
text.KJV: 15:14 And if a stranger soiourne with you, or whosoeuer bee among you in your generations, and will offer an offering made by fire of a sweete sauour vnto the Lord: as ye doe, so hee shall doe.
text.NABRE: Likewise, in any future generation, any alien residing with you or anyone else in your midst, who presents an oblation of pleasing aroma to the Lord, must do as you do.

text.NRSV: As for the assembly, there shall be for both you and the resident alien a single statute, a perpetual statute throughout your generations; you and the alien shall be alike before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: In the convocation, one statute, shall there be for you and for the sojourner who sojourneth,—an age-abiding statute unto your generations, as ye are, so, the sojourner, shall be before Yahweh:
text.KJV: 15:15 One ordinance shall be both for you of the Congregation, and also for the stranger that soiourneth with you, an ordinance for euer in your generations: as ye are, so shall the stranger bee, before the Lord.
text.NABRE: There is but one statute for you and for the resident alien, a perpetual statute throughout your generations. You and the resident alien will be alike before the Lord;

text.NRSV: You and the alien who resides with you shall have the same law and the same ordinance.
text.Rotherham: one law and one regulation, shall there be for yourselves and for the sojourner that sojourneth with you.
text.KJV: 15:16 One law, and one maner shall be for you, and for the stranger that soiourneth with you.
text.NABRE: you and the alien residing with you will have the same rule and the same application of it.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 15:17 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord spoke to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When you come into the land to which I bring you,
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them,—When ye enter into the land whereinto I, am bringing you,
text.KJV: 15:18 Speake vnto the children of Israel, and say vnto them, When ye come into the land whither I bring you,
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When you enter the land into which I am bringing you

text.NRSV: and when you eat of the bread of the land, you shall present a donation to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: then shall it be, when ye eat the bread of the land, that ye shall heave up a heave-offering unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 15:19 Then it shall be that when ye eate of the bread of the land, yee shall offer vp an heaue offring vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: and eat of the bread of the land, you shall offer the Lord a contribution.

text.NRSV: From your first batch of dough you shall present a loaf as a donation; you shall present it like the donation from the threshing floor.
text.Rotherham: Of the first of your meal, shall ye heave up, a cake, as a heave-offering,—like the heave-offering of grain, so shall ye heave it.
text.KJV: 15:20 Ye shall offer vp a cake of the first of your dough, for an heaue offring: as ye doe the heaue offering of the threshing floore, so shall ye heaue it.
text.NABRE: A round loaf from your first batch of dough you shall offer as a contribution. Just like a contribution from the threshing floor you shall offer it.

text.NRSV: Throughout your generations you shall give to the Lord a donation from the first of your batch of dough.
text.Rotherham: Of the first of your meal, shall ye give unto Yahweh a heave-offering,—unto your generations.
text.KJV: 15:21 Of the first of your dough ye shal giue vnto the Lord, an heaue offering in your generations.
text.NABRE: Throughout your generations you shall give a contribution to the Lord from your first batch of dough. Purification Offerings.

text.NRSV: But if you unintentionally fail to observe all these commandments that the Lord has spoken to Moses—
text.Rotherham: And when ye shall make a mistake, and not observe all these commandments,—which Yahweh hath spoken unto Moses;
text.KJV: 15:22 ¶ And if yee haue erred, and not obserued all these Commaundements which the Lord hath spoken vnto Moses,
text.NABRE: If through inadvertence you fail to do any of these commandments which the Lord has given to Moses—

text.NRSV: everything that the Lord has commanded you by Moses, from the day the Lord gave commandment and thereafter, throughout your generations—
text.Rotherham: even all that Yahweh hath given unto you in command, by the hand of Moses,—from the day that Yahweh gave command, and onward unto your generations,
text.KJV: 15:23 Euen all that the Lord hath commanded you, by the hand of Moses from the day that the Lord commanded Moses, and henceforward among your generations:
text.NABRE: anything the Lord commanded you through Moses from the time the Lord first gave the command down through your generations—

text.NRSV: if it was done unintentionally without the knowledge of the congregation, the whole congregation shall offer one young bull for a burnt offering, a pleasing odor to the Lord, together with its grain offering and its drink offering, according to the ordinance, and one male goat for a sin offering.
text.Rotherham: then shall it be—if, any from the eyes of the assembly, it hath been done by mistake, that all the assembly shall offer one choice young bullock for an ascending-sacrifice as a satisfying odour unto Yahweh, with the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offering thereof, according to the regulation,—and one young he-goat as a sin-bearer.
text.KJV: 15:24 Then it shalbe, if ought be committed by ignorance without the knowledge of the Congregation, that all the Congregation shall offer one yong bullocke for a burnt offering, for a sweet sauour vnto the Lord, with his meate offering, and his drinke offering, according to the manner, and one kid of the goats for a sinne offering.
text.NABRE: if it was done inadvertently without the community’s knowledge, the whole community shall sacrifice one bull from the herd as a burnt offering of pleasing aroma to the Lord, along with its prescribed grain offering and libation, as well as one he-goat as a purification offering.

text.NRSV: The priest shall make atonement for all the congregation of the Israelites, and they shall be forgiven; it was unintentional, and they have brought their offering, an offering by fire to the Lord, and their sin offering before the Lord, for their error.
text.Rotherham: So shall the priest put a propitiatory, covering over all the assembly of the sons of Israel and pardon shall be granted unto them,—because a mistake, it was, they, therefore have brought in their offering—an altar-flame unto Yahweh and their sin-bearer before Yahweh for their mistake:
text.KJV: 15:25 And the Priest shall make an atonement for all the Congregation of the children of Israel, and it shal be forgiuen them, for it is ignorance: and they shall bring their offring, a sacrifice made by fire vnto the Lord, and their sinne offering before the Lord, for their ignorance.
text.NABRE: Then the priest shall make atonement for the whole Israelite community; and they will be forgiven, since it was inadvertence, and for their inadvertence they have brought their offering: an oblation to the Lord as well as their purification offering before the Lord.

text.NRSV: All the congregation of the Israelites shall be forgiven, as well as the aliens residing among them, because the whole people was involved in the error.
text.Rotherham: and pardon shall be granted unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel and unto the sojourner that sojourneth in their midst,—for, in the case of all the people, it was, by mistake.
text.KJV: 15:26 And it shall bee forgiuen all the Congregation of the children of Israel, and the stranger that soiourneth among them, seeing all the people were in ignorance.
text.NABRE: Not only the whole Israelite community but also the aliens residing among you shall be forgiven, since the inadvertent fault affects all the people.

text.NRSV: An individual who sins unintentionally shall present a female goat a year old for a sin offering.
text.Rotherham: And if, any one person, shall sin by mistake, then shall he bring near a she-goat of the first year as a sin-bearer;
text.KJV: 15:27 ¶ And if any soule sinne through ignorance, then hee shall bring a shee goat of the first yeere for a sinne offring.
text.NABRE: If it is an individual who sins inadvertently, this person shall bring a yearling she-goat as a purification offering.

text.NRSV: And the priest shall make atonement before the Lord for the one who commits an error, when it is unintentional, to make atonement for the person, who then shall be forgiven.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall put a propitiatory-covering over the person who hath made the mistake, when he hath sinned by mistake before Yahweh, even put a propitiatory-covering over him, and pardon shall be granted him.
text.KJV: 15:28 And the Priest shall make an atonement for the soule that sinneth ignorantly, when he sinneth by ignorance before the Lord, to make an atonement for him, & it shalbe forgiuen him.
text.NABRE: And the priest shall make atonement before the Lord for the one who erred, since the sin was inadvertent, making atonement for the person to secure forgiveness.

text.NRSV: For citizen and alien who reside among you there shall be one law for anyone who does anything in error.
text.Rotherham: For the native born among the sons of Israel, and for the sojourner that sojourneth in their midst, one law, shall there be unto you—for acting by mistake.
text.KJV: 15:29 You shall haue one law for him that sinneth through ignorance, both for him that is borne amongst the children of Israel, and for the stranger that soiourneth among them.
text.NABRE: You shall have but one rule for the person who sins inadvertently, whether a native-born Israelite or an alien residing among you.

text.NRSV: But whoever acts high-handedly, whether a native or an alien, affronts the Lord, and shall be cut off from among the people.
text.Rotherham: But, as for the person who acteth with a high hand—of the native born or of the sojourners, Yahweh himself, he, reproacheth,—therefore shall that person be cut off out of the midst of his people.
text.KJV: 15:30 ¶ But the soule that doeth ought presumptuously, whether he be borne in the land, or a stranger, the same reprocheth the Lord: and that soule shall be cut off from among his people.
text.NABRE: But anyone who acts defiantly, whether a native or an alien, reviles the Lord, and shall be cut off from among the people.

text.NRSV: Because of having despised the word of the Lord and broken his commandment, such a person shall be utterly cut off and bear the guilt.
text.Rotherham: Because the word of Yahweh, hath he despised, and his commandment, hath he broken, that person shall be surely cut off, his iniquity, is in himself.
text.KJV: 15:31 Because he hath despised the word of the Lord, and hath broken his commandement, that soule shall vtterly be cut off: his iniquitie shall be vpon him.
text.NABRE: For having despised the word of the Lord and broken his commandment, he must be cut off entirely and bear the punishment.

text.NRSV: When the Israelites were in the wilderness, they found a man gathering sticks on the sabbath day.
text.Rotherham: While the sons of Israel were in the desert, they found a man gathering sticks on the sabbath day.
text.KJV: 15:32 ¶ And while the children of Israel were in the wildernes, they found a man that gathered stickes vpon the Sabbath day.
text.NABRE: The Sabbath-breaker. While the Israelites were in the wilderness, a man was discovered gathering wood on the sabbath day.

text.NRSV: Those who found him gathering sticks brought him to Moses, Aaron, and to the whole congregation.
text.Rotherham: And they who found him gathering sticks, brought him near, unto Moses and unto Aaron and unto all the assembly.
text.KJV: 15:33 And they that found him gathering sticks, brought him vnto Moses and Aaron, and vnto all the Congregation.
text.NABRE: Those who caught him at it brought him to Moses and Aaron and the whole community.

text.NRSV: They put him in custody, because it was not clear what should be done to him.
text.Rotherham: And they put him in ward,—because it was not clear what they should do unto him.
text.KJV: 15:34 And they put him in ward, because it was not declared what should be done to him.
text.NABRE: But they put him in custody, for there was no clear decision as to what should be done with him.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses, "The man shall be put to death; all the congregation shall stone him outside the camp."
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses, The man shall be, put to death,—all the assembly, stoning him with stones, outside the camp.
text.KJV: 15:35 And the Lord said vnto Moses, The man shall bee surely put to death: all the Congregation shall stone him with stones without the campe.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to Moses: This man shall be put to death; let the whole community stone him outside the camp.

text.NRSV: The whole congregation brought him outside the camp and stoned him to death, just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: So then all the assembly, put him forth, outside the camp, and stoned him with stones, and he died,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: 15:36 And all the Cogregation brought him without the campe, and stoned him with stones, and he died, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: So the whole community led him outside the camp and stoned him to death, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses saying:
text.KJV: 15:37 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Tassels on the Cloak. The Lord said to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the Israelites, and tell them to make fringes on the corners of their garments throughout their generations and to put a blue cord on the fringe at each corner.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them, That they shall make them fringes on the corners of their garments unto their generations—and shall put upon the fringe of the corner a cord of blue:
text.KJV: 15:38 Speake vnto the children of Israel, and bidde them that they make them fringes in the borders of their garments, throughout their generations, and that they put vpon the fringe of the borders a ribband of blew.
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and tell them that throughout their generations they are to make tassels for the corners of their garments, fastening a violet cord to each corner.

text.NRSV: You have the fringe so that, when you see it, you will remember all the commandments of the Lord and do them, and not follow the lust of your own heart and your own eyes.
text.Rotherham: so shall they serve you as fringes, and when ye see then, then shall ye call to mind all the commandments of Yahweh, and shall do them,—and shall not spy out, after your own hearts, and after your own eyes for things, after which, ye, are ready to go unchastely away:
text.KJV: 15:39 And it shall bee vnto you for a fringe, that ye may looke vpon it, and remember all the commandements of the Lord, and doe them: and that ye seeke not after your owne heart, and your owne eyes, after which ye vse to goe a whoring:
text.NABRE: When you use these tassels, the sight of the cord will remind you of all the commandments of the Lord and you will do them, without prostituting yourself going after the desires of your hearts and your eyes.

text.NRSV: So you shall remember and do all my commandments, and you shall be holy to your God.
text.Rotherham: to the intent ye may call to mind, and do all my commandments, and be holy unto your God.
text.KJV: 15:40 That ye may remember, and doe all my commandements, and be holy vnto your God.
text.NABRE: Thus you will remember to do all my commandments and you will be holy to your God.

text.NRSV: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: I—Yahweh, am your God—who brought you forth out of the land of Egypt, to become your God,—I,—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: 15:41 I am the Lord your God, which brought you out of the land of Egypt, to bee your God: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: I, the Lord, am your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God: I, the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: Now Korah son of Izhar son of Kohath son of Levi, along with Dathan and Abiram sons of Eliab, and On son of Peleth—descendants of Reuben—took
text.Rotherham: Then took Korah, son of Izhar son of Kohath son of Levi,—and Dathan and Abiram sons of Eliab, and On, son of Peleth sons of Reuben;
text.KJV: 16:1 Now Korah the sonne of Izhar, the sonne of Kohath, the sonne of Leui, and Dathan, and Abiram the sonnes of Eliab, and On the sonne of Peleth, sonnes of Reuben, tooke men.
text.NABRE: Chapter 16 Rebellion of Korah. Korah, son of Izhar, son of Kohath, son of Levi, and the Reubenites Dathan and Abiram, sons of Eliab, and On, son of Peleth, son of Reuben took
