text.NRSV: Take the staff, and assemble the congregation, you and your brother Aaron, and command the rock before their eyes to yield its water. Thus you shall bring water out of the rock for them; thus you shall provide drink for the congregation and their livestock.
text.Rotherham: Take the staff and gather together the assembly, thou, and Aaron thy brother, then shall ye speak unto the cliff before their eyes and it shall give forth its waters,—so shalt thou bring forth unto them water, out of the cliff, and shalt cause the assembly, and their cattle to drink.
text.KJV: 20:8 Take the rodde, and gather thou the assembly together, thou and Aaron thy brother, and speake yee vnto the rocke before their eyes, and it shall giue foorth his water, and thou shalt bring foorth to them, water out of the rocke: so thou shalt giue the Congregation, and their beasts drinke.
text.NABRE: Take the staff and assemble the community, you and Aaron your brother, and in their presence command the rock to yield its waters. Thereby you will bring forth water from the rock for them, and supply the community and their livestock with water.

text.NRSV: So Moses took the staff from before the Lord, as he had commanded him.
text.Rotherham: And Moses took the staff from before Yahweh,—as he commanded him.
text.KJV: 20:9 And Moses tooke the rod from before the Lord, as he commanded him.
text.NABRE: So Moses took the staff from its place before the Lord, as he was ordered.

text.NRSV: Moses and Aaron gathered the assembly together before the rock, and he said to them, "Listen, you rebels, shall we bring water for you out of this rock?"
text.Rotherham: And Moses and Aaron gathered together the convocation before the cliff,—and he said unto them: Hear I pray you ye rebels! Out of this cliff, must we bring forth for you water?
text.KJV: 20:10 And Moses and Aaron gathered the Congregation together before the rocke, and hee said vnto them, Heare now, ye rebels; must we fetch you water out of this rocke?
text.NABRE: Then Moses and Aaron gathered the assembly in front of the rock, where he said to them, “Just listen, you rebels! Are we to produce water for you out of this rock?”

text.NRSV: Then Moses lifted up his hand and struck the rock twice with his staff; water came out abundantly, and the congregation and their livestock drank.
text.Rotherham: And Moses lifted high his hand and smote the cliff with his staff, twice,—and there came forth water in abundance, and the assembly and their cattle, did drink.
text.KJV: 20:11 And Moses lift vp his hand, and with his rod he smote the rocke twice: and the water came out abundantly, and the Congregation dranke, and their beasts also.
text.NABRE: Then, raising his hand, Moses struck the rock twice with his staff, and water came out in abundance, and the community and their livestock drank.

text.NRSV: But the Lord said to Moses and Aaron, "Because you did not trust in me, to show my holiness before the eyes of the Israelites, therefore you shall not bring this assembly into the land that I have given them."
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses and unto Aaron, Because ye believed not in me, to hallow me in the eyes of the sons of Israel, therefore, shall ye not bring in this convocation into the land which I have given unto them.
text.KJV: 20:12 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses and Aaron, Because ye beleeue me not, to sanctifie me in the eyes of the children of Israel, therefore ye shall not bring this Congregation into the land which I haue giuen them.
text.NABRE: But the Lord said to Moses and Aaron: Because you did not have confidence in me, to acknowledge my holiness before the Israelites, therefore you shall not lead this assembly into the land I have given them.

text.NRSV: These are the waters of Meribah, where the people of Israel quarreled with the Lord, and by which he showed his holiness.
text.Rotherham: The same, were the waters of Meribah [that is "Contention"] in that the sons of Israel contended with Yahweh,—and so he hallowed himself among them.
text.KJV: 20:13 This is the water of Meribah, because the children of Israel stroue with the Lord; and he was sanctified in them.
text.NABRE: These are the waters of Meribah, where the Israelites quarreled with the Lord, and through which he displayed his holiness.

text.NRSV: Moses sent messengers from Kadesh to the king of Edom, "Thus says your brother Israel: You know all the adversity that has befallen us:
text.Rotherham: And Moses sent messengers from Kadesh, unto the king of Edom,—Thus, saith thy brother Israel, Thou thyself, knowest all the distress which befell us;
text.KJV: 20:14 ¶ And Moses sent messengers from Kadesh, vnto the King of Edom; Thus saith thy brother Israel, Thou knowest all the trauaile that hath befallen vs:
text.NABRE: Edom’s Refusal. From Kadesh Moses sent messengers to the king of Edom: “Thus says your brother Israel: You know of all the hardships that have befallen us,

text.NRSV: how our ancestors went down to Egypt, and we lived in Egypt a long time; and the Egyptians oppressed us and our ancestors;"
text.Rotherham: and how our fathers went down to Egypt, and we abode in Egypt, many days, and that the Egyptians ill-treated us, and our fathers;
text.KJV: 20:15 How our fathers went downe into Egypt, and we haue dwelt in Egypt a long time: and the Egyptians vexed vs, and our fathers.
text.NABRE: how our ancestors went down to Egypt, and we stayed in Egypt a long time, and the Egyptians treated us and our ancestors harshly.

text.NRSV: and when we cried to the Lord, he heard our voice, and sent an angel and brought us out of Egypt; and here we are in Kadesh, a town on the edge of your territory.
text.Rotherham: and we made outcry unto Yahweh, and he heard our voice, and sent a messenger, and brought us forth out of Egypt,—lo! then, we, are in Kadesh, a city at the extremity of thy boundary,
text.KJV: 20:16 And when wee cryed vnto the Lord, he heard our voyce, and sent an Angel, and hath brought vs foorth out of Egypt: and behold, wee are in Kadesh, a citie in the vttermost of thy border.
text.NABRE: When we cried to the Lord, he heard our cry and sent an angel who led us out of Egypt. Now here we are at Kadesh, a town at the edge of your territory.

text.NRSV: Now let us pass through your land. We will not pass through field or vineyard, or drink water from any well; we will go along the King's Highway, not turning aside to the right hand or to the left until we have passed through your territory."
text.Rotherham: Let us, we pray thee, pass through thy land—we will not pass through field or vineyard, nor will we drink the waters of a well,—by the king’s road, will we go—we will turn neither to the right hand nor to the left, until we get through thy boundary.
text.KJV: 20:17 Let vs passe, I pray thee, thorow thy countrey: we will not passe thorow the fields, or thorow the Uineyards, neither will we drinke of the water of the wells: wee will goe by the Kings high-way, we wil not turne to the right hand nor to the left, vntill wee haue passed thy borders.
text.NABRE: Please let us pass through your land. We will not cross any fields or vineyards, nor drink any well water, but we will go straight along the King’s Highway without turning to the right or to the left, until we have passed through your territory.”

text.NRSV: But Edom said to him, "You shall not pass through, or we will come out with the sword against you."
text.Rotherham: And Edom said unto him, Thou shalt not pass through my land,—lest with the sword, I come out to meet thee.
text.KJV: 20:18 And Edom said vnto him, Thou shalt not passe by me, lest I come out against thee with the sword.
text.NABRE: But Edom answered him, “You shall not pass through here; if you do, I will advance against you with the sword.”

text.NRSV: The Israelites said to him, "We will stay on the highway; and if we drink of your water, we and our livestock, then we will pay for it. It is only a small matter; just let us pass through on foot."
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel said unto him—By the highway, will we go up, and if thy waters, we should drink—I, and my cattle then would I give the price thereof,—only—it is nothing!—with my feet, would I pass through.
text.KJV: 20:19 And the children of Israel said vnto him, We will goe by the high-way: and if I and my cattell drinke of thy water, then I will pay for it: I will onely (without doing any thing else) go thorow on my feet.
text.NABRE: The Israelites said to him, “We will go up along the highway. If we or our livestock drink any of your water, we will pay for it. It is nothing—just let us pass through on foot.”

text.NRSV: But he said, "You shall not pass through." And Edom came out against them with a large force, heavily armed.
text.Rotherham: And he said, Thou shalt not pass through. And Edom came out to meet him, with a strong people, and with a firm hand.
text.KJV: 20:20 And he said, Thou shalt not goe thorow. And Edom came out against him with much people, and with a strong hand.
text.NABRE: But Edom replied, “You shall not pass through,” and advanced against them with a large and heavily armed force.

text.NRSV: Thus Edom refused to give Israel passage through their territory; so Israel turned away from them.
text.Rotherham: Thus did Edom refuse to give Israel passage through his boundary,—and Israel turned away from him.
text.KJV: 20:21 Thus Edom refused to giue Israel passage thorow his border: wherefore Israel turned away from him.
text.NABRE: Therefore, since Edom refused to let Israel pass through their territory, Israel turned away from them.

text.NRSV: They set out from Kadesh, and the Israelites, the whole congregation, came to Mount Hor.
text.Rotherham: Then set they forward from Kadesh,—and the sons of Israel. all the assembly, came in to Mount Hor.
text.KJV: 20:22 ¶ And the children of Israel, euen the whole Congregation, iourneyed from Kadesh, and came vnto mount Hor.
text.NABRE: Death of Aaron. Setting out from Kadesh, the Israelites, the whole community, came to Mount Hor.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron at Mount Hor, on the border of the land of Edom,
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses and unto Aaron in Mount Hor,—near the boundary of the land of Edom saying:
text.KJV: 20:23 And the Lord spake vnto Moses and Aaron in mount Hor, by the coast of the land of Edom, saying;
text.NABRE: There at Mount Hor, on the border of the land of Edom, the Lord said to Moses and Aaron:

text.NRSV: "Let Aaron be gathered to his people. For he shall not enter the land that I have given to the Israelites, because you rebelled against my command at the waters of Meribah."
text.Rotherham: Aaron shall be withdrawn unto his kinsfolk, for he shall not enter into the land. which I have given unto the sons of Israel,—because ye spurned my bidding, at the waters of Meribah.
text.KJV: 20:24 Aaron shall bee gathered vnto his people: for hee shall not enter into the land which I haue giuen vnto the children of Israel, because yee rebelled against my word at the water of Meribah.
text.NABRE: Let Aaron be gathered to his people, for he shall not enter the land I have given to the Israelites, because you both rebelled against my directions at the waters of Meribah.

text.NRSV: Take Aaron and his son Eleazar, and bring them up Mount Hor;
text.Rotherham: Take thou Aaron, and Eleazar his son,—and bring them up Mount Hor;
text.KJV: 20:25 Take Aaron, and Eleazar his sonne, and bring them vp vnto mount Hor.
text.NABRE: Take Aaron and Eleazar his son and bring them up on Mount Hor.

text.NRSV: strip Aaron of his vestments, and put them on his son Eleazar. But Aaron shall be gathered to his people, and shall die there."
text.Rotherham: and strip thou Aaron of his garments, and put them upon Eleazar his son,—and, Aaron, shall be withdrawn and shall die there.
text.KJV: 20:26 And strippe Aaron of his garments, and put them vpon Eleazar his sonne, and Aaron shall be gathered vnto his people, and shall die there.
text.NABRE: Then strip Aaron of his garments and put them on Eleazar, his son; but there Aaron shall be gathered up in death.

text.NRSV: Moses did as the Lord had commanded; they went up Mount Hor in the sight of the whole congregation.
text.Rotherham: And Moses did as Yahweh commanded,—and they went up into, Mount Hor, in sight of all the assembly.
text.KJV: 20:27 And Moses did as the Lord commaunded: and they went vp into mount Hor, in the sight of all the Congregation.
text.NABRE: Moses did as the Lord commanded. When they had climbed Mount Hor in view of the whole community,

text.NRSV: Moses stripped Aaron of his vestments, and put them on his son Eleazar; and Aaron died there on the top of the mountain. Moses and Eleazar came down from the mountain.
text.Rotherham: And Moses stripped Aaron of his garments and put them upon Eleazar his son, and Aaron died there, in the top of the mount,—and Moses and Eleazar came down out of the mount.
text.KJV: 20:28 And Moses stripped Aaron of his garments, and put them vpon Eleazar his sonne, and Aaron died there in the top of the mount: and Moses and Eleazar came downe from the mount.
text.NABRE: Moses stripped Aaron of his garments and put them on Eleazar his son. Then Aaron died there on top of the mountain. When Moses and Eleazar came down from the mountain,

text.NRSV: When all the congregation saw that Aaron had died, all the house of Israel mourned for Aaron thirty days.
text.Rotherham: And all the assembly saw that Aaron was dead; and they bewailed Aaron thirty days, even all the house of Israel.
text.KJV: 20:29 And when all the Congregation saw that Aaron was dead, they mourned for Aaron thirty dayes, euen all the house of Israel.
text.NABRE: all the community understood that Aaron had breathed his last; and for thirty days the whole house of Israel mourned Aaron.

text.NRSV: When the Canaanite, the king of Arad, who lived in the Negeb, heard that Israel was coming by the way of Atharim, he fought against Israel and took some of them captive.
text.Rotherham: And the Canaanite, the king of Arad who dwelt in the South, heard that Israel had entered by the way of Atharim,—so he made war with Israel, and took some of them captive.
text.KJV: 21:1 And when king Arad the Canaanite, which dwelt in the South, heard tell that Israel came by the way of the spies, then hee fought against Israel, and tooke some of them prisoners.
text.NABRE: Chapter 21 Victory over Arad. When the Canaanite, the king of Arad, who ruled over the Negeb, heard that the Israelites were coming along the way of Atharim, he engaged Israel in battle and took some of them captive.

text.NRSV: Then Israel made a vow to the Lord and said, "If you will indeed give this people into our hands, then we will utterly destroy their towns."
text.Rotherham: Then Israel vowed vow unto Yahweh, and said,—If thou wilt, surely deliver up, this people into my hand, then will I utterly destroy their cities.
text.KJV: 21:2 And Israel vowed a vow vnto the Lord, and said, If thou wilt in deed deliuer this people into my hand, then I wil vtterly destroy their cities.
text.NABRE: Israel then made this vow to the Lord: “If you deliver this people into my hand, I will put their cities under the ban.”

text.NRSV: The Lord listened to the voice of Israel, and handed over the Canaanites; and they utterly destroyed them and their towns; so the place was called Hormah.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh hearkened unto the voice of Israel and delivered up the Canaanite into his hand, so he utterly destroyed them, and their cities, and called the name of the place Hormah [that is, "Utter Destruction"].
text.KJV: 21:3 And the Lord hearkened to the voyce of Israel, and deliuered vp the Canaanites: and they vtterly destroyed them, and their cities, and hee called the name of the place Hormah.
text.NABRE: The Lord paid attention to Israel and delivered up the Canaanites, and they put them and their cities under the ban. Hence that place was named Hormah.

text.NRSV: From Mount Hor they set out by the way to the Red Sea, to go around the land of Edom; but the people became impatient on the way.
text.Rotherham: Then brake they up from Mount Hor, by way of the Red Sea, to go round the land of Edom,—and the soul of the people became impatient because of the way,
text.KJV: 21:4 ¶ And they iourneyed from mount Hor, by the way of the red sea, to compasse the land of Edom: and the soule of the people was much discouraged because of the way.
text.NABRE: The Bronze Serpent. From Mount Hor they set out by way of the Red Sea, to bypass the land of Edom, but the people’s patience was worn out by the journey;

text.NRSV: The people spoke against God and against Moses, "Why have you brought us up out of Egypt to die in the wilderness? For there is no food and no water, and we detest this miserable food."
text.Rotherham: So then the people spake against God and against Moses, Wherefore have ye brought us up out of Egypt, to die in the desert? for there is neither bread nor water, and our soul, doth loathe this contemptible bread!
text.KJV: 21:5 And the people spake against God and against Moses, Wherefore haue ye brought vs vp out of Egypt, to die in the wildernesse? for there is no bread, neither is there any water, and our soule loatheth this light bread.
text.NABRE: so the people complained against God and Moses, “Why have you brought us up from Egypt to die in the wilderness, where there is no food or water? We are disgusted with this wretched food!”

text.NRSV: Then the Lord sent poisonous serpents among the people, and they bit the people, so that many Israelites died.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh sent among the people the poisonous serpents, and they bit the people,—and much people out of Israel died.
text.KJV: 21:6 And the Lord sent fierie serpents among the people, and they bit the people, and much people of Israel died.
text.NABRE: So the Lord sent among the people seraph serpents, which bit the people so that many of the Israelites died.

text.NRSV: The people came to Moses and said, "We have sinned by speaking against the Lord and against you; pray to the Lord to take away the serpents from us." So Moses prayed for the people.
text.Rotherham: Then came the people of Israel unto Moses and said—We have sinned in that we spake against Yahweh and against thee, Pray thou unto Yahweh, that he may take from us the serpent. So Moses prayed for the people.
text.KJV: 21:7 ¶ Therefore the people came to Moses, and said, We haue sinned: for wee haue spoken against the Lord, and against thee: pray vnto the Lord that hee take away the serpents from vs: and Moses prayed for the people.
text.NABRE: Then the people came to Moses and said, “We have sinned in complaining against the Lord and you. Pray to the Lord to take the serpents from us.” So Moses prayed for the people,

text.NRSV: And the Lord said to Moses, "Make a poisonous serpent, and set it on a pole; and everyone who is bitten shall look at it and live."
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses—Make thee a like serpent, and put it upon standard,—and it shall come to pass, that, any one who is bitten, as soon as he seeth it, shall live.
text.KJV: 21:8 And the Lord said vnto Moses, Make thee a fierie serpent, and set it vpon a pole: and it shall come to passe, that euery one that is bitten, when hee looketh vpon it, shall liue.
text.NABRE: and the Lord said to Moses: Make a seraph and mount it on a pole, and everyone who has been bitten will look at it and recover.

text.NRSV: So Moses made a serpent of bronze, and put it upon a pole; and whenever a serpent bit someone, that person would look at the serpent of bronze and live.
text.Rotherham: So Moses made a serpent of bronze, and put it upon the standard,—and it came to pass—that if the serpent had bitten any man, as soon as he directed his look unto the serpent of bronze, he lived.
text.KJV: 21:9 And Moses made a serpent of brasse, and put it vpon a pole, and it came to passe, that if a serpent had bitten any man, when hee beheld the serpent of brasse, he liued.
text.NABRE: Accordingly Moses made a bronze serpent and mounted it on a pole, and whenever the serpent bit someone, the person looked at the bronze serpent and recovered.

text.NRSV: The Israelites set out, and camped in Oboth.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel brake up,—and encamped in Oboth.
text.KJV: 21:10 ¶ And the children of Israel set forward, and pitched in Oboth.
text.NABRE: Journey Around Moab. The Israelites moved on and encamped in Oboth.

text.NRSV: They set out from Oboth, and camped at Iye-abarim, in the wilderness bordering Moab toward the sunrise.
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Oboth,—and encamped in Ije-Abarim, in the desert which is over against Moab, towards the rising of the sun.
text.KJV: 21:11 And they iourneyed from Oboth, and pitched at Iie-Abarim, in the wildernes which is before Moab, toward the Sunne rising.
text.NABRE: Then they moved on from Oboth and encamped in Iye-abarim in the wilderness facing Moab on the east.

text.NRSV: From there they set out, and camped in the Wadi Zered.
text.Rotherham: From thence, brake they up,—and encamped in the ravine of Zered.
text.KJV: 21:12 ¶ From thence they remooued, and pitched in the valley of Zared.
text.NABRE: Moving on from there, they encamped in the Wadi Zered.

text.NRSV: From there they set out, and camped on the other side of the Arnon, in the wilderness that extends from the boundary of the Amorites; for the Arnon is the boundary of Moab, between Moab and the Amorites.
text.Rotherham: From thence, brake they up, and encamped on the other side of Arnon which is in the desert, that cometh forth out of the boundary of the Amorites,—for, Arnon, is the boundary of Moab, between Moab and the Amorites.
text.KJV: 21:13 From thence they remooued, and pitched on the other side of Arnon, which is in the wildernesse that commeth out of the coasts of the Amorites: for Arnon is the border of Moab, betweene Moab and the Amorites.
text.NABRE: Moving on from there, they encamped on the other side of the Arnon, in the wilderness that extends from the territory of the Amorites; for the Arnon forms Moab’s boundary, between Moab and the Amorites.

text.NRSV: Wherefore it is said in the Book of the Wars of the Lord, "Waheb in Suphah and the wadis. The Arnon"
text.Rotherham: For this cause is it said, in the Book of the Wars of Yahweh,— …Waheb with a hurricane, And the ravines of Arnon;
text.KJV: 21:14 Wherefore it is said in the booke of the warres of the Lord, what he did in the Red sea, and in the brookes of Arnon,
text.NABRE: Hence it is said in the “Book of the Wars of the Lord ”: “Waheb in Suphah and the wadies,

text.NRSV: and the slopes of the wadis that extend to the seat of Ar, and lie along the border of Moab."
text.Rotherham: And the bottom of the ravines, that extendeth toward the dwelling of Ar,—And adjoineth to the boundary of Moab,
text.KJV: 21:15 And at the streame of the brookes that goeth downe to the dwelling of Ar, & lieth vpon the border of Moab.
text.NABRE: Arnon and the wadi gorges That reach back toward the site of Ar and lean against the border of Moab.”

text.NRSV: From there they continued to Beer; that is the well of which the Lord said to Moses, "Gather the people together, and I will give them water."
text.Rotherham: And from thence, towards Beer,—the same, is the well whereof Yahweh said unto Moses, Gather together the people, that I may give them water.
text.KJV: 21:16 And from thence they went to Beer: that is the well whereof the Lord spake vnto Moses, Gather the people together, and I will giue them water.
text.NABRE: From there they went to Beer, which is the well of which the Lord said to Moses, Gather the people together so that I may give them water.

text.NRSV: Then Israel sang this song: "Spring up, O well!—Sing to it!—"
text.Rotherham: Then, sang Israel this song,—Spring thou up, O well! Respond ye thereunto;
text.KJV: 21:17 ¶ Then Israel sang this song, Spring vp O well, Sing ye vnto it:
text.NABRE: Then Israel sang this song: Spring up, O well!—so sing to it—

text.NRSV: the well that the leaders sank, that the nobles of the people dug, with the scepter, with the staff." And from the wilderness to Mattanah,
text.Rotherham: A well!—princes digged it, Nobles of the people, delved it, With a sceptre, With their staves. And, from the desert, to Mattanah;
text.KJV: 21:18 The Princes digged the well, the nobles of the people digged it, by the direction of the Law-giuer, with their staues. And from the wildernesse they went to Mattanah:
text.NABRE: The well that the princes sank, that the nobles of the people dug, With their scepters and their staffs— from the wilderness, a gift.

text.NRSV: from Mattanah to Nahaliel, from Nahaliel to Bamoth,
text.Rotherham: and from Mattanah, to Nahaliel; and from Nahaliel, to Bamoth;
text.KJV: 21:19 And from Mattanah, to Nahaliel, and from Nahaliel to Bamoth:
text.NABRE: From Beer to Nahaliel, from Nahaliel to Bamoth,

text.NRSV: and from Bamoth to the valley lying in the region of Moab by the top of Pisgah that overlooks the wasteland.
text.Rotherham: and, from Bamoth of the valley, which is in the field-country of Moab, to the top of Pisgah,—which overlooketh Jeshimon.
text.KJV: 21:20 And from Bamoth in the valley, that is in the countrey of Moab, to the toppe of Pisgah, which looketh toward Ieshimon.
text.NABRE: from Bamoth to the valley in the country of Moab at the headland of Pisgah that overlooks Jeshimon.

text.NRSV: Then Israel sent messengers to King Sihon of the Amorites, saying,
text.Rotherham: Then sent Israel messengers, unto Sihon king of the Amorites, saying:
text.KJV: 21:21 ¶ And Israel sent messengers vnto Sihon king of the Amorites, saying,
text.NABRE: Victory over Sihon. Now Israel sent messengers to Sihon, king of the Amorites, with the message,

text.NRSV: "Let me pass through your land; we will not turn aside into field or vineyard; we will not drink the water of any well; we will go by the King's Highway until we have passed through your territory."
text.Rotherham: Let me pass through thy land we will not turn aside into field or into vineyard, nor will we drink the water of a well,—by the king’s road, will we go, until we get through thy boundary.
text.KJV: 21:22 Let me passe thorow thy land, we will not turne into the fields, or into the vineyards, we will not drinke of the waters of the well: but we will goe along by the kings high way, vntill wee be past thy borders.
text.NABRE: “Let us pass through your land. We will not turn aside into any field or vineyard, nor will we drink any well water, but we will go straight along the King’s Highway until we have passed through your territory.”

text.NRSV: But Sihon would not allow Israel to pass through his territory. Sihon gathered all his people together, and went out against Israel to the wilderness; he came to Jahaz, and fought against Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Sihon suffered not Israel to pass through his boundary, but Sihon gathered together all his people, and came forth to meet Israel, towards the desert, and entered Jahaz, and fought with Israel.
text.KJV: 21:23 And Sihon would not suffer Israel to passe thorow his border: but Sihon gathered all his people together, and went out against Israel into the wildernes: and he came to Iahaz, and fought against Israel.
text.NABRE: Sihon, however, would not permit Israel to pass through his territory, but mustered all his forces and advanced against Israel into the wilderness. When he reached Jahaz, he engaged Israel in battle.

text.NRSV: Israel defeated him with the edge of the sword, and took possession of his land from the Arnon to the Jabbok, as far as to the Ammonites; for the boundary of the Ammonites was strong.
text.Rotherham: And Israel smote him with the edge of the sword,—and took possession of his land, from Arnon unto Jabbok, unto the sons of Ammon, for strong, was the boundary of the sons of Ammon.
text.KJV: 21:24 And Israel smote him with the edge of the sword, and possessed his land from Arnon vnto Iabok, euen vnto the children of Ammon: for the border of the children of Ammon was strong.
text.NABRE: But Israel put him to the sword, and took possession of his land from the Arnon to the Jabbok and as far as Jazer of the Ammonites, for Jazer is the boundary of the Ammonites.

text.NRSV: Israel took all these towns, and Israel settled in all the towns of the Amorites, in Heshbon, and in all its villages.
text.Rotherham: So Israel took all these cities,—and Israel dwelt in all the cities of the Amorites, in Heshbon and in all her villages;
text.KJV: 21:25 And Israel tooke all these cities: and Israel dwelt in all the cities of the Amorites, in Heshbon, and in all the villages thereof.
text.NABRE: Israel seized all the towns here, and Israel settled in all the towns of the Amorites, in Heshbon and all its dependencies.

text.NRSV: For Heshbon was the city of King Sihon of the Amorites, who had fought against the former king of Moab and captured all his land as far as the Arnon.
text.Rotherham: for, as for Heshbon, the city of Sihon king of the Amorites, it was, he, having fought with the former king of Moab, and taken all his land out of his hand, unto Arnon.
text.KJV: 21:26 For Heshbon was the citie of Sihon the King of the Amorites, who had fought against the former King of Moab, and taken all his land out of his hand, euen vnto Arnon.
text.NABRE: For Heshbon was the city of Sihon, king of the Amorites, who had fought against the former king of Moab and had taken all his land from him as far as the Arnon.

text.NRSV: Therefore the ballad singers say, "Come to Heshbon, let it be built; let the city of Sihon be established."
text.Rotherham: For this cause, say the poets—Enter ye Heshbon,—Built and prepared be the city of Sihon;
text.KJV: 21:27 Wherefore they that speake in prouerbes, say, Come into Heshbon: let the citie of Sihon bee built and prepared.
text.NABRE: That is why the poets say: “Come to Heshbon, let it be rebuilt, let Sihon’s city be firmly constructed.

text.NRSV: For fire came out from Heshbon, flame from the city of Sihon. It devoured Ar of Moab, and swallowed up the heights of the Arnon.
text.Rotherham: For, a fire, hath come forth out of Heshbon, A flame, out of the stronghold of Sihon; It hath consumed Ar of Moab, The lords of the high places of Arnon.
text.KJV: 21:28 For there is a fire gone out of Heshbon, a flame from the citie of Sihon: it hath consumed Ar of Moab, and the lordes of the high places of Arnon.
text.NABRE: For fire went forth from Heshbon and a blaze from the city of Sihon; It consumed Ar of Moab and swallowed up the high places of the Arnon.

text.NRSV: Woe to you, O Moab! You are undone, O people of Chemosh! He has made his sons fugitives, and his daughters captives, to an Amorite king, Sihon.
text.Rotherham: Woe to thee, Moab, Thou art lost, O people of Chemosh,—He hath given up his sons as fugitives, And his daughters into captivity, Unto the king of the Amorites, Sihon.
text.KJV: 21:29 Woe to thee, Moab, thou art vndone, O people of Chemosh: he hath giuen his sonnes that escaped, and his daughters, into captiuitie vnto Sihon King of the Amorites.
text.NABRE: Woe to you, Moab! You are no more, people of Chemosh! He let his sons become fugitives and his daughters be taken captive by the Amorite king Sihon.

text.NRSV: So their posterity perished from Heshbon to Dibon, and we laid waste until fire spread to Medeba."
text.Rotherham: Then we shot them—Heshbon is destroyed, as far as Dibon,—Then laid we waste as far as Nophah, A fire reacheth unto Medeba.
text.KJV: 21:30 We haue shot at them; Heshbon is perished euen vnto Dibon, and we haue layde them waste euen vnto Nophah, which reacheth vnto Medeba.
text.NABRE: From Heshbon to Dibon their dominion is no more; Ar is laid waste; fires blaze as far as Medeba.”

text.NRSV: Thus Israel settled in the land of the Amorites.
text.Rotherham: So then Israel dwelt in the land of the Amorites.
text.KJV: 21:31 ¶ Thus Israel dwelt in the land of the Amorites.
text.NABRE: So Israel settled in the land of the Amorites.

text.NRSV: Moses sent to spy out Jazer; and they captured its villages, and dispossessed the Amorites who were there.
text.Rotherham: And Moses sent to spy out Jazer, and they captured the villages thereof,—and dispossessed the Amorites that were there.
text.KJV: 21:32 And Moses sent to spy out Iaazer, and they tooke the villages thereof, and droue out the Amorites that were there.
text.NABRE: Moses sent spies to Jazer; and the Israelites captured it with its dependencies and dispossessed the Amorites who were there.

text.NRSV: Then they turned and went up the road to Bashan; and King Og of Bashan came out against them, he and all his people, to battle at Edrei.
text.Rotherham: Then turned they, and went up by the way of Bashan,—and Og, king of Bashan, came forth to meet them—he and all his people, to give battle at Edrei.
text.KJV: 21:33 ¶ And they turned and went vp by the way of Bashan: and Og the King of Bashan went out against them, he, and all his people, to the battell at Edrei.
text.NABRE: Victory over Og. Then they turned and went up along the road to Bashan. But Og, king of Bashan, advanced against them with all his forces to give battle at Edrei.

text.NRSV: But the Lord said to Moses, "Do not be afraid of him; for I have given him into your hand, with all his people, and all his land. You shall do to him as you did to King Sihon of the Amorites, who ruled in Heshbon."
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Moses—Do not fear him, for into thy hand, have I delivered him and all his people, and his land,—Therefore shalt thou do unto him, as thou didst unto Sihon king of the Amorites, who was dwelling in Heshbon.
text.KJV: 21:34 And the Lord said vnto Moses, Feare him not: for I haue deliuered him into thy hand, and all his people, and his land, and thou shalt doe to him as thou didst vnto Sihon King of the Amorites, which dwelt at Heshbon.
text.NABRE: The Lord, however, said to Moses: Do not fear him; for into your hand I deliver him with all his forces and his land. You will do to him as you did to Sihon, king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon.

text.NRSV: So they killed him, his sons, and all his people, until there was no survivor left; and they took possession of his land.
text.Rotherham: So then they smote him and his sons and all his people, until there was not left him a remnant,—and took possession of his land.
text.KJV: 21:35 So they smote him & his sonnes, and all his people, vntill there was none left him aliue, and they possessed his land.
text.NABRE: So they struck him down with his sons and all his forces, until not a survivor was left to him, and they took possession of his land.

text.NRSV: Then the Israelites journeyed on and camped in the plains of Moab beyond the Jordan opposite Jericho.
text.Rotherham: Then did the sons of Israel break up,—and encamp in the waste plains of Moab, on the other side of the Jordan by Jericho.
text.KJV: 22:1 And the children of Israel set forward, and pitched in the plaines of Moab, on this side Iordane by Iericho.
text.NABRE: Chapter 22 Then the Israelites moved on and encamped in the plains of Moab on the other side of the Jordan opposite Jericho.

text.NRSV: Now Balak son of Zippor saw all that Israel had done to the Amorites.
text.Rotherham: And Balak son of Zippor saw all that Israel had done unto the Amorites;
text.KJV: 22:2 ¶ And Balak the sonne of Zippor, saw all that Israel had done to the Amorites.
text.NABRE: Balaam Summoned. Now Balak, son of Zippor, saw all that Israel did to the Amorites,

text.NRSV: Moab was in great dread of the people, because they were so numerous; Moab was overcome with fear of the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: and Moab shrank with great fear from the presence of the people because, many, they were,—and Moab was alarmed at the presence of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 22:3 And Moab was sore afraid of the people, because they were many, and Moab was distressed, because of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: and Moab feared the Israelites greatly because they were numerous. Moab was in dread of the Israelites.

text.NRSV: And Moab said to the elders of Midian, "This horde will now lick up all that is around us, as an ox licks up the grass of the field." Now Balak son of Zippor was king of Moab at that time.
text.Rotherham: So Moab said unto the elders of Midian—Now, shall the gathered host lick up all that are round about us, as the ox doth lick up the verdure of the field. But, Balak son of Zippor, was king unto Moab at that time.
text.KJV: 22:4 And Moab said vnto the elders of Midian; Now shall this company licke vp all that are round about vs, as the oxe licketh vp the grasse of the field. And Balak the sonne of Zippor, was King of the Moabites at that time.
text.NABRE: So Moab said to the elders of Midian, “Now this horde will devour everything around us as an ox devours the grass of the field.” At that time Balak, son of Zippor, was king of Moab;

text.NRSV: He sent messengers to Balaam son of Beor at Pethor, which is on the Euphrates, in the land of Amaw, to summon him, saying, "A people has come out of Egypt; they have spread over the face of the earth, and they have settled next to me."
text.Rotherham: So he sent messengers unto Balaam son of Beor, to Pethor which was by the river of the land of the sons of his people to call him,—saying Lo! a people, hath come forth out of Egypt Lo! he hath covered the eye of the land, Yea he, is tarrying over against me.
text.KJV: 22:5 He sent messengers therefore vnto Balaam the sonne of Beor, to Pethor, which is by the riuer of the land of the children of his people, to call him, saying, Behold, there is a people come out from Egypt: beholde, they couer the face of the earth, and they abide ouer against me.
text.NABRE: and he sent messengers to Balaam, son of Beor, at Pethor on the river, in the land of the Ammonites, to summon him with these words, “A people has come out of Egypt! They have covered up the earth and are settling down opposite me!

text.NRSV: Come now, curse this people for me, since they are stronger than I; perhaps I shall be able to defeat them and drive them from the land; for I know that whomever you bless is blessed, and whomever you curse is cursed."
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, do come, I pray thee curse me this people. For stronger, he is than I, If peradventure I prevail, we shall smite him, That I may drive him out of the land,—For I know that, he whom thou dost bless, is to be blessed, And, he whom thou dost curse, is to be cursed.
text.KJV: 22:6 Come now therefore, I pray thee, curse mee this people, for they are too mightie for me: peraduenture I shall preuaile, that we may smite them, and that I may driue them out of the land: for I wot that he whom thou blessest, is blessed, and hee whom thou cursest, is cursed.
text.NABRE: Now come, curse this people for me, since they are stronger than I am. Perhaps I may be able to defeat them and drive them out of the land. For I know that whoever you bless is blessed and whoever you curse is cursed.”

text.NRSV: So the elders of Moab and the elders of Midian departed with the fees for divination in their hand; and they came to Balaam, and gave him Balak's message.
text.Rotherham: And the elders of Moab and the elders of Midian went their way, with the rewards of divination in their hand,—so they came in unto Balaam, and spake unto him the words of Balak.
text.KJV: 22:7 And the elders of Moab, and the elders of Midian departed, with the rewards of diuination in their hand; and they came vnto Balaam, and spake vnto him the words of Balak.
text.NABRE: So the elders of Moab and the elders of Midian, themselves experts in divination, left and went to Balaam, to whom they gave Balak’s message.

text.NRSV: He said to them, "Stay here tonight, and I will bring back word to you, just as the Lord speaks to me"; so the officials of Moab stayed with Balaam.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—Tarry ye here for the night, and I will bring you back word, as Yahweh shall speak unto me. So the princes of Moab abode with Balaam.
text.KJV: 22:8 And hee said vnto them, Lodge here this night, and I will bring you word againe as the Lord shal speake vnto mee: and the Princes of Moab abode with Balaam.
text.NABRE: He said to them, “Stay here overnight, and I will give you whatever answer the Lord gives me.” So the princes of Moab lodged with Balaam.

text.NRSV: God came to Balaam and said, "Who are these men with you?"
text.Rotherham: And God came in unto Balaam,—and said, Who are these men with thee?
text.KJV: 22:9 And God came vnto Balaam, and said, What men are these with thee?
text.NABRE: Then God came to Balaam and said: Who are these men with you?

text.NRSV: Balaam said to God, "King Balak son of Zippor of Moab has sent me this message:"
text.Rotherham: Then said Balaam unto God,—Balak, son of Zippor king of Moab, hath sent unto me:
text.KJV: 22:10 And Balaam said vnto God, Balak the sonne of Zippor, King of Moab, hath sent vnto me, saying;
text.NABRE: Balaam answered God, “Balak, son of Zippor, king of Moab, sent me the message:

text.NRSV: "A people has come out of Egypt and has spread over the face of the earth; now come, curse them for me; perhaps I shall be able to fight against them and drive them out."
text.Rotherham: Lo! the people that hath come forth out of Egypt—he covereth the eye of the land. Now, do come, do revile me him, Peradventure I shall prevail in making war with him, And shall drive him out.
text.KJV: 22:11 Behold, there is a people come out of Egypt, which couereth the face of the earth: Come now, curse me them; peraduenture I shalbe able to ouercome them, and driue them out.
text.NABRE: ‘This people that has come out of Egypt has covered up the earth. Now come, lay a curse on them for me; perhaps I may be able to fight them and drive them out.’”

text.NRSV: God said to Balaam, "You shall not go with them; you shall not curse the people, for they are blessed."
text.Rotherham: And God said unto Balaam, Thou shalt not go with them,—Thou shalt not curse the people, For blessed, he is.
text.KJV: 22:12 And God saide vnto Balaam; Thou shalt not goe with them, thou shalt not curse the people: for they are blessed.
text.NABRE: But God said to Balaam: Do not go with them and do not curse this people, for they are blessed.

text.NRSV: So Balaam rose in the morning, and said to the officials of Balak, "Go to your own land, for the Lord has refused to let me go with you."
text.Rotherham: And Balaam rose up in the morning, and said unto the princes of Balak, Get you into your own land,—For Yahweh hath refused to let me go with you.
text.KJV: 22:13 And Balaam rose vp in the morning, and said vnto the Princes of Balak, Get you into your land: for the Lord refuseth to giue mee leaue to goe with you.
text.NABRE: The next morning Balaam arose and told the princes of Balak, “Go back to your own country, for the Lord has refused to let me go with you.”

text.NRSV: So the officials of Moab rose and went to Balak, and said, "Balaam refuses to come with us."
text.Rotherham: So the princes of Moab arose, and came in unto Balak,—and said, Balaam refused to come with us.
text.KJV: 22:14 And the Princes of Moab rose vp, and they went vnto Balak, and said, Balaam refuseth to come with vs.
text.NABRE: So the princes of Moab went back to Balak with the report, “Balaam refused to come with us.”

text.NRSV: Once again Balak sent officials, more in number and more distinguished than these.
text.Rotherham: Then added Balak again,—to send princes, more and weightier than these.
text.KJV: 22:15 ¶ And Balak sent yet againe Princes, moe, and more honourable then they.
text.NABRE: Second Appeal to Balaam. Balak yet again sent princes, who were more numerous and more distinguished than the others.

text.NRSV: They came to Balaam and said to him, "Thus says Balak son of Zippor: 'Do not let anything hinder you from coming to me;"
text.Rotherham: And they came in unto Balaam,—and said unto him—Thus, said Balak son of Zippor, Do not be withheld, I pray thee from coming unto me:
text.KJV: 22:16 And they came to Balaam, and said to him, Thus saith Balak the son of Zippor; Let nothing, I pray thee, hinder thee from comming vnto me:
text.NABRE: On coming to Balaam they told him, “Thus says Balak, son of Zippor: Please do not refuse to come to me.

text.NRSV: for I will surely do you great honor, and whatever you say to me I will do; come, curse this people for me.'"
text.Rotherham: For I will honour, thee, exceedingly, and all that thou shalt say unto me, will I do. Do come therefore I pray thee oh revile me this people.
text.KJV: 22:17 For I wil promote thee vnto very great honour, and I will do whatsoeuer thou saiest vnto me: Come therefore, I pray thee, curse me this people.
text.NABRE: I will reward you very handsomely and will do anything you ask of me. Come, lay a curse on this people for me.”

text.NRSV: But Balaam replied to the servants of Balak, "Although Balak were to give me his house full of silver and gold, I could not go beyond the command of the Lord my God, to do less or more."
text.Rotherham: And Balaam responded and said unto the servants of Balak, Though Balak would give me his house full of silver and gold, I could not go beyond the bidding of Yahweh my God, to do less or more.
text.KJV: 22:18 And Balaam answered and said vnto the seruants of Balak, If Balak would giue me his house full of siluer and gold, I cannot goe beyond the word of the Lord my God, to doe lesse or more.
text.NABRE: But Balaam replied to Balak’s servants, “Even if Balak gave me his house full of silver and gold, I could not do anything, small or great, contrary to the command of the Lord, my God.

text.NRSV: You remain here, as the others did, so that I may learn what more the Lord may say to me."
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore abide here, I pray you even ye,—for the night,—that I may get to know what further Yahweh may be speaking with me.
text.KJV: 22:19 Now therefore, I pray you, tarie yee also here this night, that I may know what the Lord will say vnto me more.
text.NABRE: But, you too stay here overnight, so that I may learn what else the Lord may say to me.”

text.NRSV: That night God came to Balaam and said to him, "If the men have come to summon you, get up and go with them; but do only what I tell you to do."
text.Rotherham: And God came in unto Balaam, by night, and said unto him—If, to call thee, the men have come in, arise go with them,—But only, the thing that I shall speak unto thee, that, shalt thou do.
text.KJV: 22:20 And God came vnto Balaam at night, and said vnto him, If the men come to call thee, rise vp, and goe with them: but yet the word which I shall say vnto thee, that shalt thou doe.
text.NABRE: That night God came to Balaam and said to him: If these men have come to summon you, go back with them; yet only on the condition that you do exactly as I tell you.

text.NRSV: So Balaam got up in the morning, saddled his donkey, and went with the officials of Moab.
text.Rotherham: So Balaam arose in the morning, and saddled his ass,—and went with the princes of Moab.
text.KJV: 22:21 And Balaam rose vp in the morning, and sadled his asse, and went with the princes of Moab.
text.NABRE: So the next morning when Balaam arose, he saddled his donkey, and went off with the princes of Moab.

text.NRSV: God's anger was kindled because he was going, and the angel of the Lord took his stand in the road as his adversary. Now he was riding on the donkey, and his two servants were with him.
text.Rotherham: Then kindled the anger of God, because he was going, and the messenger of Yahweh stationed himself in the way, to withstand him,—as he was riding upon his ass, his two young men being with him.
text.KJV: 22:22 ¶ And Gods anger was kindled, because he went: and the Angel of the Lord stood in the way for an aduersarie against him: Now he was riding vpon his asse, and his two seruants were with him.
text.NABRE: The Talking Donkey. But now God’s anger flared up at him for going, and the angel of the Lord took up a position on the road as his adversary. As Balaam was riding along on his donkey, accompanied by two of his servants,

text.NRSV: The donkey saw the angel of the Lord standing in the road, with a drawn sword in his hand; so the donkey turned off the road, and went into the field; and Balaam struck the donkey, to turn it back onto the road.
text.Rotherham: And the ass saw the messenger of Yahweh stationed in the road with his drawn sword in his hand, so the ass turned aside out of the road and went into the field,—and Balaam smote the ass, to make her turn back into the road.
text.KJV: 22:23 And the Asse sawe the Angel of the Lord standing in the way, and his sword drawen in his hand: and the asse turned aside out of the way, and went into the field: and Balaam smote the asse, to turne her into the way.
text.NABRE: the donkey saw the angel of the Lord standing in the road with sword drawn. The donkey turned off the road and went into the field, and Balaam beat the donkey to bring her back on the road.

text.NRSV: Then the angel of the Lord stood in a narrow path between the vineyards, with a wall on either side.
text.Rotherham: But the messenger of Yahweh took his stand, in a hollow pass of the vineyards,—with a fence on this side, and a fence on that side.
text.KJV: 22:24 But the Angel of the Lord stood in a path of the vineyards, a wall being on this side, & a wall on that side.
text.NABRE: Then the angel of the Lord stood in a narrow lane between vineyards with a stone wall on each side.

text.NRSV: When the donkey saw the angel of the Lord, it scraped against the wall, and scraped Balaam's foot against the wall; so he struck it again.
text.Rotherham: And when the ass saw the messenger of Yahweh, she squeezed herself against the wall, and squeezed the foot of Balaam against the wall,—and again he smote her.
text.KJV: 22:25 And when the asse saw the Angel of the Lord, she thrust her selfe vnto the wall, and crusht Balaams foote against the wall: and hee smote her againe.
text.NABRE: When the donkey saw the angel of the Lord there, she pressed against the wall; and since she squeezed Balaam’s leg against the wall, he beat her again.

text.NRSV: Then the angel of the Lord went ahead, and stood in a narrow place, where there was no way to turn either to the right or to the left.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh went on further, and took his stand in a narrow place, where there was no way to turn to the right hand or to the left.
text.KJV: 22:26 And the Angel of the Lord went further, and stood in a narrowe place, where was no way to turne, either to the right hand, or to the left.
text.NABRE: Then the angel of the Lord again went ahead, and stood next in a passage so narrow that there was no room to move either to the right or to the left.

text.NRSV: When the donkey saw the angel of the Lord, it lay down under Balaam; and Balaam's anger was kindled, and he struck the donkey with his staff.
text.Rotherham: And, when the ass saw the messenger of Yahweh, she sank down under Balaam,—then was Balaam’s anger kindled, and he smote the ass with the staff.
text.KJV: 22:27 And when the asse sawe the Angel of the Lord, shee fell downe vnder Balaam, and Balaams anger was kindled, and hee smote the asse with a staffe.
text.NABRE: When the donkey saw the angel of the Lord there, she lay down under Balaam. Balaam’s anger flared up and he beat the donkey with his stick.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord opened the mouth of the donkey, and it said to Balaam, "What have I done to you, that you have struck me these three times?"
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh opened the mouth of the ass,—and she said unto Balaam—What have I done to thee, that thou hast smitten me, these three times?
text.KJV: 22:28 And the Lord opened the mouth of the asse, and shee saide vnto Balaam, What haue I done vnto thee, that thou hast smitten mee these three times?
text.NABRE: Then the Lord opened the mouth of the donkey, and she asked Balaam, “What have I done to you that you beat me these three times?”

text.NRSV: Balaam said to the donkey, "Because you have made a fool of me! I wish I had a sword in my hand! I would kill you right now!"
text.Rotherham: And Balaam said unto the ass, Surely thou hast been making sport of me,—Would there had been a sword in my hand, for, now, would I have slain thee.
text.KJV: 22:29 And Balaam said vnto the asse, Because thou hast mocked mee: I would there were a sword in mine hand, for now would I kill thee.
text.NABRE: “You have acted so willfully against me,” said Balaam to the donkey, “that if I only had a sword at hand, I would kill you here and now.”

text.NRSV: But the donkey said to Balaam, "Am I not your donkey, which you have ridden all your life to this day? Have I been in the habit of treating you this way?" And he said, "No."
text.Rotherham: Then said the ass unto Balaam—Am not I thine own ass on which thou hast ridden all thy life, until this day? Have I, been wont, to do unto thee, thus? And he said, Nay!
text.KJV: 22:30 And the asse said vnto Balaam, Am not I thine asse, vpon which thou hast ridden euer since I was thine, vnto this day? was I euer wont to do so vnto thee? And he said, Nay.
text.NABRE: But the donkey said to Balaam, “Am I not your donkey, on which you have always ridden until now? Have I been in the habit of treating you this way before?” “No,” he replied.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord opened the eyes of Balaam, and he saw the angel of the Lord standing in the road, with his drawn sword in his hand; and he bowed down, falling on his face.
text.Rotherham: Then did Yahweh unveil the eyes of Balaam, and he saw the messenger of Yahweh stationed in the road, with his sword drawn in his hand,—so he bent his head and bowed down before him.
text.KJV: 22:31 Then the Lord opened the eyes of Balaam, and hee saw the Angel of the Lord standing in the way, and his sword drawen in his hand: and hee bowed downe his head, and fell flat on his face.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord opened Balaam’s eyes, so that he saw the angel of the Lord standing on the road with sword drawn; and he knelt and bowed down to the ground.

text.NRSV: The angel of the Lord said to him, "Why have you struck your donkey these three times? I have come out as an adversary, because your way is perverse before me."
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh said unto him, Wherefore hast thou smitten thine ass, these three times? Lo! I, myself, am come forth to withstand thee, because headlong was thy way before me.
text.KJV: 22:32 And the Angel of the Lord said vnto him, Wherefore hast thou smitten thine asse these three times? Behold, I went out to withstand thee, because thy way is peruerse before me.
text.NABRE: But the angel of the Lord said to him: “Why have you beaten your donkey these three times? I have come as an adversary because this rash journey of yours is against my will.

text.NRSV: The donkey saw me, and turned away from me these three times. If it had not turned away from me, surely just now I would have killed you and let it live."
text.Rotherham: And the ass hath seen me, and hath turned aside at my presence these three times: Unless she had turned aside from my presence, yea now even thee, had I slain, but, her, had I suffered to live.
text.KJV: 22:33 And the asse saw me, and turned from me these three times: vnlesse shee had turned from me, surely now also I had slaine thee, and saued her aliue.
text.NABRE: When the donkey saw me, she turned away from me these three times. If she had not turned away from me, you are the one I would have killed, though I would have spared her.”

text.NRSV: Then Balaam said to the angel of the Lord, "I have sinned, for I did not know that you were standing in the road to oppose me. Now therefore, if it is displeasing to you, I will turn back."
text.Rotherham: Then said Balaam unto the messenger of Yahweh—I have sinned, for I knew not, that, thou, wast stationed to meet me in the road,—Now, therefore, if it be displeasing in thine eyes, I must get me back again.
text.KJV: 22:34 And Balaam said vnto the Angel of the Lord, I haue sinned: for I knew not that thou stoodest in the way against mee: Now therefore if it displease thee, I will get mee backe againe.
text.NABRE: Then Balaam said to the angel of the Lord, “I have sinned. Yet I did not know that you took up a position to oppose my journey. Since it has displeased you, I will go back home.”

text.NRSV: But the angel of the Lord said to Balaam, "Go with the men; but speak only what I tell you to speak." So Balaam went on with the officials of Balak.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh said unto Balaam—Go with the men: Only the word that I shall speak unto thee, that, shalt thou speak. So Balaam went his way with the princes of Balak.
text.KJV: 22:35 And the Angel of the Lord said vnto Balaam, Goe with the men: but onely the word that I shall speake vnto thee, that thou shalt speake: So Balaam went with the princes of Balak.
text.NABRE: But the angel of the Lord said to Balaam: “Go with the men; but you may say only what I tell you.” So Balaam went on with the princes of Balak.

text.NRSV: When Balak heard that Balaam had come, he went out to meet him at Ir-moab, on the boundary formed by the Arnon, at the farthest point of the boundary.
text.Rotherham: And Balak heard that Balaam, was coming,—so he went forth to meet him unto a city of Moab which was upon the boundary of Arnon, which was in the utmost part of the boundary.
text.KJV: 22:36 ¶ And when Balak heard that Balaam was come, hee went out to meete him, vnto a citie of Moab, which is in the border of Arnon, which is in the vtmost coast.
text.NABRE: When Balak heard that Balaam was coming, he went out to meet him at Ar-Moab on the border formed by the Arnon, at its most distant point.

text.NRSV: Balak said to Balaam, "Did I not send to summon you? Why did you not come to me? Am I not able to honor you?"
text.Rotherham: And Balak said unto Balaam: Did I not, send, unto thee, to call thee? Wherefore didst thou not come unto me? Am I not in very deed, able to honour thee?
text.KJV: 22:37 And Balak said vnto Balaam, Did I not earnestly send vnto thee to call thee? wherefore camest thou not vnto me? Am I not able indeed to promote thee to honour?
text.NABRE: And Balak said to Balaam, “Did I not send an urgent summons to you? Why did you not come to me? Did you think I could not reward you?”

text.NRSV: Balaam said to Balak, "I have come to you now, but do I have power to say just anything? The word God puts in my mouth, that is what I must say."
text.Rotherham: And Balaam said unto Balak—Lo! I am come unto thee, Now, can, I, possibly, promise anything? The word that God shall put in my mouth, that, must I speak.
text.KJV: 22:38 And Balaam saide vnto Balak, Loe, I am come vnto thee: haue I now any power at all to say any thing? the worde that God putteth in my mouth, that shall I speake.
text.NABRE: Balaam answered Balak, “Well, I have come to you after all. But what power have I to say anything? I can speak only what God puts in my mouth.”

text.NRSV: Then Balaam went with Balak, and they came to Kiriath-huzoth.
text.Rotherham: So Balaam went with Balak,—and they entered Kiriath-huzoth.
text.KJV: 22:39 And Balaam went with Balak, and they came vnto Kiriath-Huzoth.
text.NABRE: Then Balaam went with Balak, and they came to Kiriath-huzoth.

text.NRSV: Balak sacrificed oxen and sheep, and sent them to Balaam and to the officials who were with him.
text.Rotherham: And Balak sacrificed herd and flock,—and sent unto Balaam, and unto the princes that were with him.
text.KJV: 22:40 And Balak offered oxen, and sheepe, and sent to Balaam, and to the princes that were with him.
text.NABRE: Here Balak sacrificed oxen and sheep, and sent portions to Balaam and to the princes who were with him.

text.NRSV: On the next day Balak took Balaam and brought him up to Bamoth-baal; and from there he could see part of the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in the morning, that Balak took Balaam, and brought him up the high places of Baal,—and he saw from thence the utmost part of the people.
text.KJV: 22:41 And it came to passe on the morrow, that Balak tooke Balaam, and brought him vp into the high places of Baal, that thence hee might see the vtmost part of the people.
text.NABRE: The First Oracle. The next morning Balak took Balaam up on Bamoth-baal, and from there he could see some of the people.

text.NRSV: Then Balaam said to Balak, "Build me seven altars here, and prepare seven bulls and seven rams for me."
text.Rotherham: Then said Balaam unto Balak: Build me here seven altars,—and prepare me here, seven bullocks and seven rams.
text.KJV: 23:1 And Balaam saide vnto Balak, Build me here seuen Altars, and prepare mee here seuen oxen, and seuen rammes.
text.NABRE: Chapter 23 Then Balaam said to Balak, “Build me seven altars here, and here prepare seven bulls and seven rams for me.”

text.NRSV: Balak did as Balaam had said; and Balak and Balaam offered a bull and a ram on each altar.
text.Rotherham: And Balak did as Balaam had spoken,—and Balak and Balaam caused to ascend a bullock and a ram on each altar.
text.KJV: 23:2 And Balak did as Balaam had spoken, and Balak & Balaam offered on euery altar a bullocke and a ramme.
text.NABRE: So Balak did as Balaam had ordered, and Balak and Balaam offered a bull and a ram on each altar.

text.NRSV: Then Balaam said to Balak, "Stay here beside your burnt offering, while I go aside. Perhaps the Lord will come to meet me. Whatever he shows me I will tell you." And he went to a bare height.
text.Rotherham: Then said Balaam unto Balak: Station thyself by thine ascending-sacrifice, and let me go my way—peradventure Yahweh may come and meet me, and what thing soever he may show me, I will tell thee. So he went his way to a bare height.
text.KJV: 23:3 And Balaam said vnto Balak, Stand by thy burnt offring, and I wil goe: peraduenture the Lord will come to meete mee; and whatsoeuer he sheweth me, I will tell thee. And he went to an high place.
text.NABRE: Balaam said to Balak, “Stand here by your burnt offering while I go over there. Perhaps the Lord will meet me, and then I will tell you whatever he lets me see.” And so he went out on the barren height.

text.NRSV: Then God met Balaam; and Balaam said to him, "I have arranged the seven altars, and have offered a bull and a ram on each altar."
text.Rotherham: And God came unto Balaam,—so he said unto him The seven altars, have I built in a row, and have caused to ascend, bullock and a ram on each altar.
text.KJV: 23:4 And God met Balaam, and he said vnto him, I haue prepared seuen altars, and I haue offered vpon euery altar a bullocke and a ramme.
text.NABRE: Then God met Balaam, and Balak said to him: “I have erected the seven altars, and have offered a bull and a ram on each altar.”

text.NRSV: The Lord put a word in Balaam's mouth, and said, "Return to Balak, and this is what you must say."
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh put a word into the mouth of Balaam,—and said—Return unto Balak, and, thus, shalt thou speak.
text.KJV: 23:5 And the Lord put a word in Balaams mouth, and said, Returne vnto Balak, & thus thou shalt speake.
text.NABRE: The Lord put an utterance in Balaam’s mouth, and said: Go back to Balak, and speak accordingly.

text.NRSV: So he returned to Balak, who was standing beside his burnt offering with all the officials of Moab.
text.Rotherham: So he returned unto him, and lo! he was stationed by his ascending-sacrifice,—he, and all the princes of Moab.
text.KJV: 23:6 And he returned vnto him, and loe, he stood by his burnt sacrifice, hee, and all the Princes of Moab.
text.NABRE: So he went back to Balak, who was still standing by his burnt offering together with all the princes of Moab.

text.NRSV: Then Balaam uttered his oracle, saying: "Balak has brought me from Aram, the king of Moab from the eastern mountains: 'Come, curse Jacob for me; Come, denounce Israel!'
text.Rotherham: So he took up his parable and said,—From Aram, doth Balak conduct me, The king of Moab, from the mountains of the East, Come thou! curse me Jacob, Yea, come thou! rage on Israel.
text.KJV: 23:7 And he tooke vp his parable, and said, Balak the King of Moab hath brought mee from Aram, out of the mountaines of the East, saying, Come, curse me Iacob, and come, defie Israel.
text.NABRE: Then Balaam recited his poem: From Aram Balak has led me here, Moab’s king, from the mountains of Qedem: “Come, curse for me Jacob, come, denounce Israel.”

text.NRSV: How can I curse whom God has not cursed? How can I denounce those whom the Lord has not denounced?"
text.Rotherham: How can I revile one whom GOD hath not reviled? Yea, how can I rage on one on whom Yahweh hath not raged?
text.KJV: 23:8 How shall I curse, whom God hath not cursed? or how shall I defie, whom the Lord hath not defied?
text.NABRE: How can I lay a curse on the one whom God has not cursed? How denounce the one whom the Lord has not denounced?

text.NRSV: For from the top of the crags I see him, from the hills I behold him; Here is a people living alone, and not reckoning itself among the nations!"
text.Rotherham: Surely from the head of the rocks, do I see him, Yea, from the hills, do I observe him,—Lo! a people, who alone doth dwell, And among the nations, he doth not reckon himself.
text.KJV: 23:9 For from the top of the rockes I see him, and from the hilles I behold him: loe, the people shall dwell alone, and shall not bee reckoned among the nations.
text.NABRE: For from the top of the crags I see him, from the heights I behold him. Here is a people that lives apart and does not reckon itself among the nations.

text.NRSV: Who can count the dust of Jacob, or number the dust-cloud of Israel? Let me die the death of the upright, and let my end be like his!"
text.Rotherham: Who hath counted the dust of Jacob? Or who hath numbered the fourth part of Israel? Let me die the death of the upright, And let my hereafter be like his!
text.KJV: 23:10 Who can count the dust of Iacob, and the number of the fourth part of Israel? Let mee die the death of the righteous, & let my last end be like his.
text.NABRE: Who has ever counted the dust of Jacob, who numbered Israel’s dust-cloud? May I die the death of the just, may my end be like theirs!

text.NRSV: Then Balak said to Balaam, "What have you done to me? I brought you to curse my enemies, but now you have done nothing but bless them."
text.Rotherham: Then said Balak unto Balaam, What hast thou done for me? To revile mine enemies, I summoned thee, and lo! thou hast kept on blessing.
text.KJV: 23:11 And Balak saide vnto Balaam, What hast thou done vnto me? I tooke thee to curse mine enemies, and behold, thou hast blessed them altogether.
text.NABRE: “What have you done to me?” cried Balak to Balaam. “It was to lay a curse on my foes that I brought you here; but instead, you have blessed them!”

text.NRSV: He answered, "Must I not take care to say what the Lord puts into my mouth?"
text.Rotherham: And he answered and said,—Was it not to be so that whatsoever Yahweh should put into my mouth, the same, should I take heed to speak?
text.KJV: 23:12 And he answered, and said, Must I not take heede to speake that which the Lord hath put in my mouth?
text.NABRE: Balaam replied, “Is it not what the Lord puts in my mouth that I take care to repeat?”

text.NRSV: So Balak said to him, "Come with me to another place from which you may see them; you shall see only a part of them, and shall not see them all; then curse them for me from there."
text.Rotherham: Then said Balak unto him—Come, I pray thee, with me unto another place, from whence thou mayest see him, nothing but his utmost part, shalt thou see, but all of him, shalt thou not see,—and so revile me him from thence.
text.KJV: 23:13 And Balak said vnto him, Come, I pray thee, with me, vnto another place, from whence thou mayest see them: thou shalt see but the vtmost part of them, and shalt not see them all: and curse me them from thence.
text.NABRE: The Second Oracle. Then Balak said to him, “Please come with me to another place from which you can see them; but you will see only some, not all of them, and from there lay a curse on them for me.”

text.NRSV: So he took him to the field of Zophim, to the top of Pisgah. He built seven altars, and offered a bull and a ram on each altar.
text.Rotherham: And he took him to the field of Zophim, unto the head of Pisgah,—and built seven altars, and caused to ascend a bullock and a ram on each altar.
text.KJV: 23:14 ¶ And hee brought him into the fielde of Zophim, to the toppe of Pisgah, and built seuen altars, and offered a bullocke and a ramme on euery altar.
text.NABRE: So he brought him to a lookout post on the top of Pisgah, where he built seven altars and offered a bull and a ram on each of them.

text.NRSV: Balaam said to Balak, "Stand here beside your burnt offering, while I meet the Lord over there."
text.Rotherham: Then said he to Balak, Station thyself here by thine ascending-sacrifice,—while, I, meet him yonder.
text.KJV: 23:15 And he said vnto Balak, Stand here by thy burnt offering, while I meete the Lord yonder.
text.NABRE: Balaam then said to Balak, “Stand here by your burnt offering, while I seek a meeting over there.”

text.NRSV: The Lord met Balaam, put a word into his mouth, and said, "Return to Balak, and this is what you shall say."
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh met Balaam, and put a word into his mouth,—and said—Return unto Balak and thus shalt thou speak.
text.KJV: 23:16 And the Lord met Balaam, and put a word in his mouth, and saide, Goe againe vnto Balak, and say thus.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord met Balaam, and, having put an utterance in his mouth, said to him: Return to Balak, and speak accordingly.

text.NRSV: When he came to him, he was standing beside his burnt offering with the officials of Moab. Balak said to him, "What has the Lord said?"
text.Rotherham: So he came in unto him and, there he was-stationed by his ascending-sacrifice, and the princes of Moab with him. And Balak said unto him, What hath Yahweh spoken?
text.KJV: 23:17 And when hee came to him, behold, he stood by his burnt offring, and the Princes of Moab with him. And Balak said vnto him, What hath the Lord spoken?
text.NABRE: So he went to Balak, who was still standing by his burnt offering together with the princes of Moab. When Balak asked him, “What did the Lord say?”

text.NRSV: Then Balaam uttered his oracle, saying: "Rise, Balak, and hear; listen to me, O son of Zippor:"
text.Rotherham: Then took he up his parable and said,—Rise, Balak, and hear, Give ear unto me son of Zippor!
text.KJV: 23:18 And he tooke vp his parable, and said, Rise vp Balak, & heare; hearken vnto me, thou sonne of Zippor:
text.NABRE: Balaam recited his poem: Rise, Balak, and listen; give ear to my testimony, son of Zippor!

text.NRSV: God is not a human being, that he should lie, or a mortal, that he should change his mind. Has he promised, and will he not do it? Has he spoken, and will he not fulfill it?"
text.Rotherham: GOD is, not a man, that he should lie, Nor a son of Adam, that he should repent,—Hath, he, said, and will not perform? Yea spoken, and will not make it stand fast?
text.KJV: 23:19 God is not a man that he should lie, neither the sonne of man, that hee should repent: hath he said, and shall he not doe it? or, hath hee spoken, and shall he not make it good?
text.NABRE: God is not a human being who speaks falsely, nor a mortal, who feels regret. Is God one to speak and not act, to decree and not bring it to pass?

text.NRSV: See, I received a command to bless; he has blessed, and I cannot revoke it."
text.Rotherham: Lo! to bless, have I received [command],—And I will bless, nor will I take it back.
text.KJV: 23:20 Behold, I haue receiued commandement to blesse: and hee hath blessed, and I cannot reuerse it.
text.NABRE: I was summoned to bless; I will bless; I cannot revoke it!

text.NRSV: He has not beheld misfortune in Jacob; nor has he seen trouble in Israel. The Lord their God is with them, acclaimed as a king among them."
text.Rotherham: He hath not discovered trouble in Jacob, Neither hath he seen wretchedness in Israel,—Yahweh, his God, is with him, And, the acclaim of a king, in his midst:
text.KJV: 23:21 Hee hath not beheld iniquitie in Iacob, neither hath he seene peruersenesse in Israel: the Lord his God is with him, and the shoute of a King is among them.
text.NABRE: Misfortune I do not see in Jacob, nor do I see misery in Israel. The Lord, their God, is with them; among them is the war-cry of their King.

text.NRSV: God, who brings them out of Egypt, is like the horns of a wild ox for them."
text.Rotherham: GOD having brought them forth out of Egypt, The very horns of the buffalo, are his:
text.KJV: 23:22 God brought them out of Egypt; he hath as it were the strength of an Unicorne.
text.NABRE: They have the like of a wild ox’s horns: God who brought them out of Egypt.

text.NRSV: Surely there is no enchantment against Jacob, no divination against Israel; now it shall be said of Jacob and Israel, 'See what God has done!'
text.Rotherham: Surely, there is no enchantment against Jacob, Nor divination against Israel,—Now, shall it be said—Of Jacob and of Israel, What hath GOD wrought!
text.KJV: 23:23 Surely there is no inchantment against Iacob, neither is there any diuination against Israel: according to this time it shalbe said of Iacob, and of Israel, What hath God wrought!
text.NABRE: No, there is no augury against Jacob, nor divination against Israel. Now it is said of Jacob, of Israel, “Look what God has done!”

text.NRSV: Look, a people rising up like a lioness, and rousing itself like a lion! It does not lie down until it has eaten the prey and drunk the blood of the slain."
text.Rotherham: Lo, a people, like a lioness, shall rise, And like a strong lion, shall rouse himself up; He will not lie down till he eat the torn, And the blood of the slain, he drink.
text.KJV: 23:24 Beholde, the people shall rise vp as a great Lion, and lift vp himselfe as a yong Lion: hee shall not lie downe vntill he eate of the pray, and drinke the blood of the slaine.
text.NABRE: Here is a people that rises up like a lioness, and gets up like a lion; It does not rest till it has devoured its prey and has drunk the blood of the slain.

text.NRSV: Then Balak said to Balaam, "Do not curse them at all, and do not bless them at all."
text.Rotherham: Then said Balak unto Balaam, Thou shalt neither revile, him,—nor bless him.
text.KJV: 23:25 ¶ And Balak said vnto Balaam, Neither curse them at all, nor blesse them at all.
text.NABRE: “Neither lay a curse on them nor bless them,” said Balak to Balaam.

text.NRSV: But Balaam answered Balak, "Did I not tell you, 'Whatever the Lord says, that is what I must do'?"
text.Rotherham: And Balaam responded, and said unto Balak,—Spake I not unto thee, saying, All that Yahweh shall speak, that, must I do?
text.KJV: 23:26 But Balaam answered, and said vnto Balak, Told not I thee, saying, All that the Lord speaketh, that I must doe?
text.NABRE: But Balaam answered Balak, “Did I not tell you, ‘Everything the Lord tells me I must do’?”

text.NRSV: So Balak said to Balaam, "Come now, I will take you to another place; perhaps it will please God that you may curse them for me from there."
text.Rotherham: And Balak said unto Balaam, Come, I pray thee! let me take thee unto another place,—peradventure, it may be right in the eyes of God, that thou revile me him. from thence.
text.KJV: 23:27 ¶ And Balak saide vnto Balaam, Come, I pray thee, I will bring thee vnto another place, peraduenture it will please God, that thou mayest curse me them from thence.
text.NABRE: The Third Oracle. Then Balak said to Balaam, “Come, let me bring you to another place; perhaps God will approve of your laying a curse on them for me from there.”

text.NRSV: So Balak took Balaam to the top of Peor, which overlooks the wasteland.
text.Rotherham: So Balak took Balaam,—unto the head of Peor, that looketh over the face of the Jeshimon.
text.KJV: 23:28 And Balak brought Balaam vnto the top of Peor, that looketh toward Ieshimon.
text.NABRE: So he took Balaam to the top of Peor, that overlooks Jeshimon.

text.NRSV: Balaam said to Balak, "Build me seven altars here, and prepare seven bulls and seven rams for me."
text.Rotherham: And Balaam said unto Balak, Build me here, seven altars,—and prepare me here, seven bullocks and seven rams.
text.KJV: 23:29 And Balaam saide vnto Balak, Build mee here seuen altars, and prepare me here seuen bullocks, and seuen rammes.
text.NABRE: Balaam then said to Balak, “Build me seven altars here; and here prepare for me seven bulls and seven rams.”

text.NRSV: Balak did as Balaam had said, and offered a bull and a ram on each altar.
text.Rotherham: And Balak did as Balaam said,—and caused to ascend a bullock and a ram on each altar.
text.KJV: 23:30 And Balak did as Balaam had said, and offred a bullocke and a ramme on euery altar.
text.NABRE: And Balak did as Balaam had ordered, offering a bull and a ram on each altar.

text.NRSV: Now Balaam saw that it pleased the Lord to bless Israel, so he did not go, as at other times, to look for omens, but set his face toward the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: And when Balaam saw that it was good in the eyes of Yahweh to bless Israel, he went not as at other times to invoke enchantments,—but set towards the desert, his face.
text.KJV: 24:1 And when Balaam sawe that it pleased the Lord to blesse Israel, hee went not, as at other times to seeke for inchantments, but hee set his face toward the wildernesse.
text.NABRE: Chapter 24 Balaam, however, perceiving that the Lord was pleased to bless Israel, did not go aside as before to seek omens, but turned his gaze toward the wilderness.

text.NRSV: Balaam looked up and saw Israel camping tribe by tribe. Then the spirit of God came upon him,
text.Rotherham: So Balaam lifted up his eyes and saw Israel, dwelling, according to his tribes,—then came upon him the Spirit of God;
text.KJV: 24:2 And Balaam lift vp his eyes, and he saw Israel abiding in his tents, according to their Tribes: and the Spirit of God came vpon him.
text.NABRE: When Balaam looked up and saw Israel encamped, tribe by tribe, the spirit of God came upon him,

text.NRSV: and he uttered his oracle, saying: "The oracle of Balaam son of Beor, the oracle of the man whose eye is clear,"
text.Rotherham: and he took up his parable, and said,—The oracle of Balaam, son of Beor, Yea the oracle of the man, of opened eye;
text.KJV: 24:3 And he tooke vp his parable, and said, Balaam the sonne of Beor hath said, and the man whose eyes are open hath said:
text.NABRE: and he recited his poem: The oracle of Balaam, son of Beor, the oracle of the man whose eye is true,

text.NRSV: the oracle of one who hears the words of God, who sees the vision of the Almighty, who falls down, but with eyes uncovered:"
text.Rotherham: The oracle of one hearing sayings of GOD, Who the sight of the Almighty, receiveth in vision, Who falleth down but hath unveiled eyes:—
text.KJV: 24:4 Hee hath said, which heard the words of God, which saw the vision of the Almightie, falling into a trance, but hauing his eyes open:
text.NABRE: The oracle of one who hears what God says, and knows what the Most High knows, Of one who sees what the Almighty sees, in rapture and with eyes unveiled:

text.NRSV: How fair are your tents, O Jacob, your encampments, O Israel!"
text.Rotherham: How pleasing are thy tents, O Jacob,—Thy habitations, O Israel:
