text.KJV: 28:14 And their drinke offerings shal be halfe an Hin of wine vnto a bullocke, and the third part of an Hin vnto a ramme, and a fourth part of an Hin vnto a lambe: This is the burnt offring of euery moneth, throughout the moneths of the yeere.
text.NABRE: Their libations will consist of a half a hin of wine for each bull, a third of a hin for the ram, and a fourth of a hin for each lamb. This is the burnt offering for the new moon, for every new moon through the months of the year.

text.NRSV: And there shall be one male goat for a sin offering to the Lord; it shall be offered in addition to the regular burnt offering and its drink offering.
text.Rotherham: Also one he-goat as a sin-bearer unto Yahweh,—besides the continual ascending-sacrifice, shall it be offered, with the drink-offering thereof.
text.KJV: 28:15 And one kidde of the goates for a sinne offering vnto the Lord shalbe offered, besides the continuall burnt offring and his drinke offering.
text.NABRE: Moreover, there will be one goat for a purification offering to the Lord; it will be offered in addition to the regular burnt offering and its libation.

text.NRSV: In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, is the passover of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, on the first month on the fourteenth day of the month, shall be a passover unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: 28:16 And in the fourteenth day of the first moneth, is the Passeouer of the Lord.
text.NABRE: At the Passover. The fourteenth day of the first month is the Passover of the Lord,

text.NRSV: On the fifteenth day of this month there shall be a festival; seven days shall unleavened bread be eaten.
text.Rotherham: and, on the fifteenth day of this month, a festival,—seven days, unleavened cakes, shall be eaten.
text.KJV: 28:17 And in the fifteenth day of this moneth is the feast: seuen dayes shall vnleauened bread be eaten.
text.NABRE: and the fifteenth day of this month is the pilgrimage feast. For seven days unleavened bread is to be eaten.

text.NRSV: On the first day there shall be a holy convocation. You shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: On the first day, a holy convocation,—no laborious work, shall ye do;
text.KJV: 28:18 In the first day shall bee an holy conuocation, yee shall doe no maner of seruile worke therein.
text.NABRE: On the first day you will declare a holy day, and you shall do no heavy work.

text.NRSV: You shall offer an offering by fire, a burnt offering to the Lord: two young bulls, one ram, and seven male lambs a year old; see that they are without blemish.
text.Rotherham: but ye shall bring near—as an altar-flame—an ascending-sacrifice unto Yahweh,—two choice bullocks and one ram,—and seven he-lambs a year old, without defect, must they be for you;
text.KJV: 28:19 But ye shall offer a sacrifice made by fire for a burnt offering vnto the Lord, two yong bullockes, and one ramme, and seuen lambes of the first yeere: they shall be vnto you without blemish.
text.NABRE: You will offer an oblation, a burnt offering to the Lord: two bulls of the herd, one ram, and seven yearling lambs that you are sure are unblemished.

text.NRSV: Their grain offering shall be of choice flour mixed with oil: three-tenths of an ephah shall you offer for a bull, and two-tenths for a ram;
text.Rotherham: and, for their meal-offering, fine meal, overflowed with oil,—three-tenths for a bullock, and two-tenths for a ram, shall ye offer;
text.KJV: 28:20 And their meate offering shall be of flowre mingled with oyle: three tenth deales shall ye offer for a bullocke, and two tenth deales for a ramme.
text.NABRE: Their grain offerings will be of bran flour mixed with oil; you will offer three tenths of an ephah for each bull and two tenths for the ram.

text.NRSV: one-tenth you shall offer for each of the seven lambs;
text.Rotherham: a tenth, severally, shalt thou offer for each lamb,—for the seven lambs,;
text.KJV: 28:21 A seuerall tenth deale shalt thou offer for euery lambe, throughout the seuen lambes:
text.NABRE: You will offer one tenth for each of the seven lambs;

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, to make atonement for you.
text.Rotherham: also one he-goat for bearing sin, to put a propitiatory-covering over you:
text.KJV: 28:22 And one goat for a sinne offering, to make an atonement for you.
text.NABRE: and one goat as a purification offering to make atonement for yourselves.

text.NRSV: You shall offer these in addition to the burnt offering of the morning, which belongs to the regular burnt offering.
text.Rotherham: in addition to the ascending-sacrifice of the morning, which is for the continual ascending-sacrifice, shall ye offer these.
text.KJV: 28:23 Ye shal offer these beside the burnt offering in the morning, which is for a continuall burnt offering.
text.NABRE: These offerings you will make in addition to the morning burnt offering which is part of the regular burnt offering.

text.NRSV: In this way you shall offer daily, for seven days, the food of the offering by fire, a pleasing odor to the Lord; it shall be offered in addition to the regular burnt offering and its drink offering.
text.Rotherham: Like these, shall ye offer, daily, for seven days, as the food of the altar-flame of a satisfying odour, unto Yahweh,—besides the continual ascending-sacrifice, shall it be offered, with the drink-offering thereof.
text.KJV: 28:24 After this maner yee shall offer dayly throughout the seuen dayes, the meat of the sacrifice made by fire, of a sweet sauour vnto the Lord: it shal be offred beside the continuall burnt offring, and his drinke offering.
text.NABRE: You will make exactly the same offerings each day for seven days as food offerings, oblations of pleasing aroma to the Lord; they will be offered in addition to the regular burnt offering with its libation.

text.NRSV: And on the seventh day you shall have a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: And on the seventh day, shall ye have, a holy convocation,—no laborious work, shall ye do.
text.KJV: 28:25 And on the seuenth day yee shall haue an holy conuocation: yee shall doe no seruile worke.
text.NABRE: On the seventh day you will declare a holy day: you shall do no heavy work.

text.NRSV: On the day of the first fruits, when you offer a grain offering of new grain to the Lord at your festival of weeks, you shall have a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: And on the day of firstfruits when ye bring near a new meal-offering unto Yahweh, in your weeks, a holy convocation shall there be unto you, no laborious work, shall ye do;
text.KJV: 28:26 ¶ Also in the day of the first fruits when ye bring a new meat offering vnto the Lord, after your weekes bee out: ye shall haue an holy conuocation, ye shall doe no seruile worke.
text.NABRE: At Pentecost. On the day of first fruits, on your feast of Weeks, when you present to the Lord an offering of new grain, you will declare a holy day: you shall do no heavy work.

text.NRSV: You shall offer a burnt offering, a pleasing odor to the Lord: two young bulls, one ram, seven male lambs a year old.
text.Rotherham: but ye shall bring near as an ascending-sacrifice, for a satisfying odour unto Yahweh, two choice bullocks one ram,—seven he-lambs a year old;
text.KJV: 28:27 But yee shall offer the burnt offering for a sweete sauour vnto the Lord, two yong bullockes, one ramme, seuen lambes of the first yeere.
text.NABRE: You will offer burnt offering for a pleasing aroma to the Lord: two bulls of the herd, one ram, and seven yearling lambs that you are sure are unblemished.

text.NRSV: Their grain offering shall be of choice flour mixed with oil, three-tenths of an ephah for each bull, two-tenths for one ram,
text.Rotherham: also their meal-offering, fine meal, overflowed with oil,—three-tenths for each bullock, two-tenths for the one ram;
text.KJV: 28:28 And their meat offering of flowre mingled with oyle, three tenth deales vnto one bullocke, two tenth deales vnto one ramme,
text.NABRE: Their grain offerings will be of bran flour mixed with oil: three tenths of an ephah for each bull, two tenths for the ram,

text.NRSV: one-tenth for each of the seven lambs;
text.Rotherham: a tenth severally, for each lamb,—of the seven lambs;
text.KJV: 28:29 A seuerall tenth deale vnto one lambe, thorowout the seuen lambes,
text.NABRE: and one tenth for each of the seven lambs.

text.NRSV: with one male goat, to make atonement for you.
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat,—for putting a propitiatory-covering over you:
text.KJV: 28:30 And one kidde of the goates, to make an atonement for you.
text.NABRE: One goat will be for a purification offering to make atonement for yourselves.

text.NRSV: In addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering, you shall offer them and their drink offering. They shall be without blemish.
text.Rotherham: in addition to, the continual ascending-sacrifice, with the meal-offering thereof, shall ye offer them,—without defect, shall they be for you, with their drink-offerings.
text.KJV: 28:31 Ye shall offer them besides the continuall burnt offering, and his meat offering, (they shall be vnto you without blemish) and their drinke offerings.
text.NABRE: You will make these offerings, together with their libations, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering.

text.NRSV: On the first day of the seventh month you shall have a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations. It is a day for you to blow the trumpets.
text.Rotherham: And in the seventh month, on the first of the month, a holy convocation, shall there be unto you, no laborious work, shall ye do,—a day of loud acclamation, shall it be unto you.
text.KJV: 29:1 And in the seuenth moneth, on the first day of the moneth, ye shall haue an holy conuocation, yee shall doe no seruile worke: it is a day of blowing the trumpets vnto you.
text.NABRE: Chapter 29 On New Year’s Day. In the seventh month on the first day you will declare a holy day, and do no heavy work; it shall be a day on which you sound the trumpet.

text.NRSV: You shall offer a burnt offering as a pleasing odor to the Lord: one young bull, one ram, seven male lambs a year old without blemish;
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye offer, as an ascending-sacrifice for a satisfying odour unto Yahweh, one choice bullock one ram,—seven he-lambs a year old without defect;
text.KJV: 29:2 And ye shall offer a burnt offering for a sweet sauour vnto the Lord, one yong bullocke, one ramme, and seuen lambes of the first yeere without blemish.
text.NABRE: You will offer a burnt offering for a pleasing aroma to the Lord: one bull of the herd, one ram, and seven unblemished yearling lambs.

text.NRSV: their grain offering of choice flour mixed with oil: three-tenths of one ephah for the bull, two-tenths for the ram,
text.Rotherham: and as their meal-offering, fine meal, overflowed with oil,—three-tenths to a bullock two-tenths to a ram;
text.KJV: 29:3 And their meat offering shall be of floure mingled with oyle, three tenth deales for a bullocke, and two tenth deales for a ramme:
text.NABRE: Their grain offerings will be of bran flour mixed with oil: three tenths of an ephah for the bull, two tenths for the ram,

text.NRSV: and one-tenth for each of the seven lambs;
text.Rotherham: and one-tenth to each lamb,—of the seven lambs;
text.KJV: 29:4 And one tenth deale for one lambe thorowout the seuen lambes:
text.NABRE: and one tenth for each of the seven lambs.

text.NRSV: with one male goat for a sin offering, to make atonement for you.
text.Rotherham: and one young he-goat as a sin-bearer,—for putting a propitiatory-covering over you:
text.KJV: 29:5 And one kidde of the goats for a sinne offering to make an atonement for you:
text.NABRE: One goat will be a purification offering to make atonement for yourselves.

text.NRSV: These are in addition to the burnt offering of the new moon and its grain offering, and the regular burnt offering and its grain offering, and their drink offerings, according to the ordinance for them, a pleasing odor, an offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: in addition to the monthly ascending-sacrifice with the meal-offering thereof and the continual ascending sacrifice, with the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offering thereof according to their regulation,—for a satisfying odour, an altar-flame, unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 29:6 Beside the burnt offering of the moneth, and his meat offering, and the dayly burnt offering, and his meat offering, and their drinke offerings, according vnto their maner, for a sweet sauour, a sacrifice made by fire vnto the Lord.
text.NABRE: These are in addition to the burnt offering for the new moon with its grain offering, and in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering, together with the libations prescribed for them, for a pleasing aroma, an oblation to the Lord.

text.NRSV: On the tenth day of this seventh month you shall have a holy convocation, and deny yourselves; you shall do no work.
text.Rotherham: And on the tenth of this seventh month, a holy convocation, shall there be unto you,—when ye shall humble your souls,—no work, shall ye do;
text.KJV: 29:7 ¶ And ye shall haue on the tenth day of this seuenth moneth an holy conuocation; and yee shall afflict your soules: yee shall not doe any worke therein.
text.NABRE: On the Day of Atonement. On the tenth day of this seventh month you will declare a holy day, humble yourselves, and do no sort of work.

text.NRSV: You shall offer a burnt offering to the Lord, a pleasing odor: one young bull, one ram, seven male lambs a year old. They shall be without blemish.
text.Rotherham: but ye shall bring near, as an ascending-sacrifice unto Yahweh a satisfying odour, one choice bullock one ram,—seven he-lambs a year old, without defect, shall they be for you;
text.KJV: 29:8 But ye shall offer a burnt offering vnto the Lord for a sweet sauour, one yong bullock, one ramme, and seuen lambes of the first yeere, they shall bee vnto you without blemish.
text.NABRE: You will offer a burnt offering to the Lord, a pleasing aroma: one bull of the herd, one ram, and seven yearling lambs that you are sure are unblemished.

text.NRSV: Their grain offering shall be of choice flour mixed with oil, three-tenths of an ephah for the bull, two-tenths for the one ram,
text.Rotherham: and, as their meal-offering, fine meal overflowed with oil,—three-tenths to a bullock, two-tenths to the one ram;
text.KJV: 29:9 And their meate offering shall be of floure mingled with oyle, three tenth deales to a bullocke, and two tenth deales to one ramme:
text.NABRE: Their grain offerings of bran flour mixed with oil: three tenths of an ephah for the bull, two tenths for the one ram,

text.NRSV: one-tenth for each of the seven lambs;
text.Rotherham: a tenth severally to each lamb,—of the seven lambs;
text.KJV: 29:10 A seuerall tenth deale for one lambe, thorowout the seuen lambes;
text.NABRE: and one tenth for each of the seven lambs.

text.NRSV: with one male goat for a sin offering—in addition to the sin offering of atonement, and the regular burnt offering and its grain offering, and their drink offerings.
text.Rotherham: one young he-goat as a sin-bearer,—in addition to, the propitiatory sin-bearer, and the continual ascending-sacrifice, with its meal-offering and their drink-offerings.
text.KJV: 29:11 One kidde of the goats for a sinne offering, beside the sinne offering of atonement, and the continuall burnt offering, and the meat offering of it, and their drinke offerings.
text.NABRE: One goat will be a purification offering. These are in addition to the purification offering for purging, the regular burnt offering with its grain offering, and their libations.

text.NRSV: On the fifteenth day of the seventh month you shall have a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations. You shall celebrate a festival to the Lord seven days.
text.Rotherham: And on the fifteenth day of the seventh month, a holy convocation shall there be unto you, no laborious work, shall ye do, but ye shall celebrate a festival unto Yahweh, seven days.
text.KJV: 29:12 ¶ And on the fifteenth day of the seuenth moneth, yee shall haue an holy conuocation, yee shall doe no seruile worke, and ye shall keepe a feast vnto the Lord seuen dayes.
text.NABRE: On the Feast of Booths. On the fifteenth day of the seventh month you will declare a holy day: you shall do no heavy work. For the following seven days you will celebrate a pilgrimage feast to the Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall offer a burnt offering, an offering by fire, a pleasing odor to the Lord: thirteen young bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old. They shall be without blemish.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye bring near as an ascending-sacrifice—an altar-flame of a satisfying odour unto Yahweh,—thirteen choice bullocks two rams,—fourteen he-lambs a year old without defect, shall they be;
text.KJV: 29:13 And ye shall offer a burnt offring, a sacrifice made by fire, of a sweet sauour vnto the Lord, thirteene yong bullocks, two rammes, and fourteene lambes of the first yeere: They shall be without blemish.
text.NABRE: You will offer a burnt offering, an oblation of pleasing aroma to the Lord: thirteen bulls of the herd, two rams, and fourteen yearling lambs that are unblemished.

text.NRSV: Their grain offering shall be of choice flour mixed with oil, three-tenths of an ephah for each of the thirteen bulls, two-tenths for each of the two rams,
text.Rotherham: and, as their meal-offering, fine-meal overflowed with oil, three-tenths to each bullock of the thirteen bullocks, two-tenths to each ram, of the two rams;
text.KJV: 29:14 And their meat offering shall be of floure mingled with oyle, three tenth deales vnto euery bullocke of the thirteene bullocks, two tenth deales to each ramme of the two rammes:
text.NABRE: Their grain offerings will be of bran flour mixed with oil: three tenths of an ephah for each of the thirteen bulls, two tenths for each of the two rams,

text.NRSV: and one-tenth for each of the fourteen lambs;
text.Rotherham: and a tenth severally, to each lamb,—of the fourteen lambs;
text.KJV: 29:15 And a seuerall tenth deale to each lambe of the foureteene lambes:
text.NABRE: and one tenth for each of the fourteen lambs.

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering and its drink offering.
text.Rotherham: also one young he-goat, as a sin-bearer,—in addition to the continual ascending-sacrifice, the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offering thereof.
text.KJV: 29:16 And one kidde of the goats for a sinne offring, beside the continual burnt offering, his meate offering, and his drinke offering.
text.NABRE: One goat will be a purification offering. These are in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and libation.

text.NRSV: On the second day: twelve young bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish,
text.Rotherham: And on the second day, twelve choice bullocks two rams,—fourteen he-lambs a year old without defect;
text.KJV: 29:17 ¶ And on the second day ye shal offer twelue yong bullocks, two rammes, foureteene lambes of the first yeere without spot.
text.NABRE: On the second day: twelve bulls of the herd, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs,

text.NRSV: with the grain offering and the drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, as prescribed in proportion to their number;
text.Rotherham: with their meal-offerings and their drink-offerings—to the bullocks, to the rams, and to the lambs, by their number according to the regulation;
text.KJV: 29:18 And their meat offring, and their drinke offerings for the bullockes, for the rammes, and for the lambes, shall be according to their number, after the maner:
text.NABRE: with the grain offerings and libations for the bulls, rams and lambs in their prescribed number,

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering, and their drink offerings.
text.Rotherham: also one young he-goat as a sin-bearer,—in addition to the continual ascending-sacrifice, with its meal-offering, and their drink-offerings.
text.KJV: 29:19 And one kidde of the goats for a sinne offering, beside the continuall burnt offering, and the meate offering thereof, and their drinke offerings.
text.NABRE: as well as one goat as a purification offering, besides the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and libation.

text.NRSV: On the third day: eleven bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish,
text.Rotherham: And on the third day, eleven bullocks two rams,—and fourteen he-lambs a year old without defect;
text.KJV: 29:20 ¶ And on the third day eleuen bullocks, two rammes, foureteene lambs of the first yere without blemish.
text.NABRE: On the third day: eleven bulls, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs,

text.NRSV: with the grain offering and the drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, as prescribed in proportion to their number;
text.Rotherham: with their meal-offering and their drink-offerings, to the bullocks, to the rams, and to the lambs, by their number, according to the regulation;
text.KJV: 29:21 And their meate offering, and their drinke offerings for the bullocks, for the rammes, and for the lambes, shall be according to their number after the maner:
text.NABRE: with the grain offerings and libations for the bulls, rams and lambs in their prescribed number,

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering.
text.Rotherham: also one young he-goat as a sin-bearer, in addition to the continual ascending-sacrifice, with the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offering thereof.
text.KJV: 29:22 And one goat for a sinne offering, beside the continuall burnt offering, and his meate offering, and his drinke offering.
text.NABRE: as well as one goat for a purification offering, besides the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and libation.

text.NRSV: On the fourth day: ten bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish,
text.Rotherham: And on the fourth day, ten bullocks, two rams,—fourteen he-lambs a year old without defect;
text.KJV: 29:23 ¶ And on the fourth day ten bullocks, two rammes, and foureteene lambs of the first yere without blemish.
text.NABRE: On the fourth day: ten bulls, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs,

text.NRSV: with the grain offering and the drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, as prescribed in proportion to their number;
text.Rotherham: their meal-offering, and their drink-offerings, to the bullocks, to the rams, and to the lambs, by their number, according to the regulation;
text.KJV: 29:24 Their meat offering, and their drinke offerings, for the bullocks, for the rammes, and for the lambes, shall be according to their number after the maner:
text.NABRE: the grain offerings and libations for the bulls, rams and lambs in their prescribed number,

text.Rotherham: also one he-goat as a sin-bearer,—in addition to the continual ascending-sacrifice, the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offering thereof.

text.NABRE: as well as one goat as a purification offering, besides the regular burnt offering, its grain offering and libation.

text.NRSV: On the fifth day: nine bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish,
text.Rotherham: And on the fifth day, nine bullocks two rams,—fourteen he-lambs, a year old without defect;
text.KJV: 29:26 ¶ And on the fift day, nine bullockes, two rammes, and fourteene lambes of the first yeere, without spot:
text.NABRE: On the fifth day: nine bulls, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs,

text.NRSV: with the grain offering and the drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, as prescribed in proportion to their number;
text.Rotherham: with their meal-offering and their drink-offerings,—to the bullocks to the rams, and to the lambs, by their number, according to the regulation:
text.KJV: 29:27 And their meat offring and their drinke offerings, for the bullockes, for the rammes, and for the lambes, shall be according to their number after the maner:
text.NABRE: with the grain offerings and libations for the bulls, rams and lambs in their prescribed number,

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering.
text.Rotherham: also one young he-goat as a sin-bearer,—in addition to the continual ascending-sacrifice, with the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offering thereof.
text.KJV: 29:28 And one goate for a sinne offring, beside the continuall burnt offring, and his meate offering and his drinke offering.
text.NABRE: as well as one goat as a purification offering, besides the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and libation.

text.NRSV: On the sixth day: eight bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish,
text.Rotherham: And on the sixth day, eight bullocks, two rams, fourteen he-lambs a year old without defect;
text.KJV: 29:29 ¶ And on the sixt day eight bullockes, two rammes, and fourteene lambes of the first yeere without blemish:
text.NABRE: On the sixth day: eight bulls, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs,

text.NRSV: with the grain offering and the drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, as prescribed in proportion to their number;
text.Rotherham: with their meal-offering and their drink-offerings to the bullocks, to the rams, and to the lambs, by their number, according to the regulation;
text.KJV: 29:30 And their meat offring, and their drinke offerings, for the bullockes, for the rammes, and for the lambes, shall be according to their number, after the maner:
text.NABRE: with the grain offerings and libations for the bulls, rams and lambs in their prescribed number,

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offerings.
text.Rotherham: also one young he-goat as a sin-bearer,—in addition to the continual ascending-sacrifice, the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offerings thereof,
text.KJV: 29:31 And one goat for a sinne offering, beside the continuall burnt offering, his meate offering and his drinke offering.
text.NABRE: as well as one goat as a purification offering, besides the regular burnt offering, its grain offering and libation.

text.NRSV: On the seventh day: seven bulls, two rams, fourteen male lambs a year old without blemish,
text.Rotherham: And on the seventh day, seven bullocks, two rams, fourteen he-lambs a year old, with-out defect:
text.KJV: 29:32 ¶ And on the seuenth day, seuen bullockes, two rammes, and fourteene lambes of the first yeere without blemish.
text.NABRE: On the seventh day: seven bulls, two rams, and fourteen unblemished yearling lambs,

text.NRSV: with the grain offering and the drink offerings for the bulls, for the rams, and for the lambs, as prescribed in proportion to their number;
text.Rotherham: with their meal-offering and their drink-offering, to the bullock, to the rams, and to the lambs by their number, according to their regulation;
text.KJV: 29:33 And their meate offring, and their drinke offerings, for the bullockes, for the rammes, and for the lambes, shall be according to their number, after the maner:
text.NABRE: with the grain offerings and libations for the bulls, rams and lambs in their prescribed number,

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering, its grain offering, and its drink offering.
text.Rotherham: also one young he-goat as a sin-bearer, in addition to the continual ascending-sacrifice, the meal-offering thereof and the drink-offering thereof
text.KJV: 29:34 And one goat for a sinne offring, beside the continuall burnt offering, his meate offering, and his drinke offring.
text.NABRE: as well as one goat as a purification offering, besides the regular burnt offering, its grain offering and libation.

text.NRSV: On the eighth day you shall have a solemn assembly; you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: [And] on the eighth day, a closing feast shall there be unto you, no laborious work, shall ye do;
text.KJV: 29:35 ¶ On the eight day, ye shall haue a solemne assembly: ye shall do no seruile worke therein:
text.NABRE: On the eighth day you will hold a public assembly: you shall do no heavy work.

text.NRSV: You shall offer a burnt offering, an offering by fire, a pleasing odor to the Lord: one bull, one ram, seven male lambs a year old without blemish,
text.Rotherham: but ye shall bring near—as an ascending-sacrifice, an altar-flame of a satisfying odour, unto Yahweh—one bullock, one ram,—seven he-lambs a year old, without defect:
text.KJV: 29:36 But ye shal offer a burnt offring, a sacrifice made by fire, of a sweet sauour vnto the Lord, one bullocke, one ramme, seuen lambes of the first yeere without blemish:
text.NABRE: You will offer a burnt offering, an oblation of pleasing aroma to the Lord: one bull, one ram, and seven unblemished yearling lambs,

text.NRSV: and the grain offering and the drink offerings for the bull, for the ram, and for the lambs, as prescribed in proportion to their number;
text.Rotherham: [with] their meal-offering and their drink-offerings—to the bullock to the ram, and to the lambs—by their number, according to the regulation;
text.KJV: 29:37 Their meate offering, and their drinke offrings, for the bullocke, for the ramme, and for the lambes shall be according to their number, after the maner:
text.NABRE: with the grain offerings and libations for the bulls, rams and lambs in their prescribed number,

text.NRSV: also one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering and its drink offering.
text.Rotherham: also one young he-goat as a sin-bearer,—in addition to the continual ascending-sacrifice, with the meal-offering thereof, and the drink-offering thereof.
text.KJV: 29:38 And one goat for a sinne offering, beside the continuall burnt offring, and his meate offering, and his drinke offering.
text.NABRE: as well as one goat as a purification offering, besides the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and libation.

text.NRSV: These you shall offer to the Lord at your appointed festivals, in addition to your votive offerings and your freewill offerings, as your burnt offerings, your grain offerings, your drink offerings, and your offerings of well-being.
text.Rotherham: These, shall ye offer unto Yahweh in your appointed seasons,—besides your vow-offerings and your freewill offerings—as your ascending-sacrifices, and as your meal-offerings, and as your drink-offerings and as your peace-offerings.
text.KJV: 29:39 These things ye shall doe vnto the Lord in your set feasts, besides your vowes, and your free will offerings, for your burnt offerings, and for your meate offerings, and for your drinke offerings, and for your peace offerings.
text.NABRE: These are the offerings you will make to the Lord on your festivals, besides your votive or voluntary offerings of burnt offerings, grain offerings, libations, and communion offerings.

text.NRSV: So Moses told the Israelites everything just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: So Moses told the sons of Israel,—According to all that Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: 29:40 And Moses tolde the children of Israel, according to all that the Lord commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: Then Moses said to the heads of the tribes of the Israelites: This is what the Lord has commanded.
text.Rotherham: And Moses spake unto the heads of the tribes of the sons of Israel saying: This, is the thing which Yahweh hath commanded-
text.KJV: 30:1 And Moses spake vnto the heads of the tribes, concerning the children of Israel, saying, This is the thing which the Lord hath commanded.
text.NABRE: Chapter 30 So Moses instructed the Israelites exactly as the Lord had commanded him.

text.NRSV: When a man makes a vow to the Lord, or swears an oath to bind himself by a pledge, he shall not break his word; he shall do according to all that proceeds out of his mouth.
text.Rotherham: When, a man, shall vow a vow unto Yahweh, or swear an oath to bind a bond upon his soul, he shall not break his word,—according to all that proceedeth out of his mouth, shall he do.
text.KJV: 30:2 If a man vowe a vow vnto the Lord, or sweare an othe to bind his soule with a bond: he shall not breake his word, hee shall doe according to all that proceedeth out of his mouth.
text.NABRE: Validity and Annulment of Vows. Moses said to the heads of the Israelite tribes, “This is what the Lord has commanded:

text.NRSV: When a woman makes a vow to the Lord, or binds herself by a pledge, while within her father's house, in her youth,"
text.Rotherham: And, when, a woman, shall vow a vow unto Yahweh, and bind a bond, in the house of her father, in her youth;
text.KJV: 30:3 If a woman also vow a vow vnto the Lord, and binde her selfe by a bond, being in her fathers house in her youth;
text.NABRE: When a man makes a vow to the Lord or binds himself under oath to a pledge, he shall not violate his word, but must fulfill exactly the promise he has uttered.

text.NRSV: and her father hears of her vow or her pledge by which she has bound herself, and says nothing to her; then all her vows shall stand, and any pledge by which she has bound herself shall stand."
text.Rotherham: and her father shall hear her vow or her bond which she bindeth upon her soul, and her father shall hold his peace at her, then shall all her vows stand, and every bond which she hath bound upon her soul shall stand.
text.KJV: 30:4 And her father heare her vow, and her bond wherewith shee hath bound her soule, and her father shall holde his peace at her: then all her vowes shall stand, and euery bond wherewith shee hath bound her soule, shall stand.
text.NABRE: “When a woman makes a vow to the Lord, or binds herself to a pledge, while still in her father’s house in her youth,

text.NRSV: But if her father expresses disapproval to her at the time that he hears of it, no vow of hers, and no pledge by which she has bound herself, shall stand; and the Lord will forgive her, because her father had expressed to her his disapproval."
text.Rotherham: But, if her father forbade her, in the day when he heard, none of her vows or her bonds which she hath bound upon her soul, shall stand,—and, Yahweh, will pardon her, because her father forbade her.
text.KJV: 30:5 But if her father disallow her in the day that he heareth; not any of her vowes or of her bonds, wherewith she hath bound her soule, shall stand: and the Lord shall forgiue her, because her father disallowed her.
text.NABRE: and her father learns of her vow or the pledge to which she bound herself and says nothing to her about it, then any vow or any pledge to which she bound herself remains valid.

text.NRSV: If she marries, while obligated by her vows or any thoughtless utterance of her lips by which she has bound herself,"
text.Rotherham: But, if she, belonged to a husband, when her vows were taken upon her,—or a rash utterance fell from her lips, wherewith she put a bond upon her soul;
text.KJV: 30:6 And if she had at all an husband when she vowed, or vttered ought out of her lips, wherewith shee bound her soule,
text.NABRE: But if on the day he learns of it her father opposes her, then any vow or any pledge to which she bound herself becomes invalid; and the Lord will release her from it, since her father opposed her.

text.NRSV: and her husband hears of it and says nothing to her at the time that he hears, then her vows shall stand, and her pledges by which she has bound herself shall stand."
text.Rotherham: and her husband heard it, and on the day that he heard, he held his peace at her, then shall her vows stand and her bonds which she hath bound upon her soul, shall stand,
text.KJV: 30:7 And her husband heard it, and held his peace at her in the day that hee heard it: then her vowes shall stand, and her bonds wherewith shee bound her soule, shall stand.
text.NABRE: “If she marries while under a vow or under a rash pledge to which she bound herself,

text.NRSV: But if, at the time that her husband hears of it, he expresses disapproval to her, then he shall nullify the vow by which she was obligated, or the thoughtless utterance of her lips, by which she bound herself; and the Lord will forgive her."
text.Rotherham: But if, on the day her husband heard, he forbade her, then shall he have made of none effect her vow that is upon her, or the rash utterance of her lips, wherewith she put a bond upon her soul,—and, Yahweh, will pardon her.
text.KJV: 30:8 But if her husband disallowe her on the day that he heard it, then he shall make her vowe which she vowed, and that which she vttered with her lippes wherewith shee bound her soule, of none effect, and the Lord shall forgiue her.
text.NABRE: and her husband learns of it, yet says nothing to her on the day he learns it, then the vows or the pledges to which she bound herself remain valid.

text.NRSV: (But every vow of a widow or of a divorced woman, by which she has bound herself, shall be binding upon her.)"
text.Rotherham: But as for the vow of a widow, or of a woman divorced, whatsoever she hath bound on her soul, shall stand against her.
text.KJV: 30:9 But euery vow of a widow, and of her that is diuorced, wherewith they haue bound their soules, shall stand against her.
text.NABRE: But if on the day her husband learns of it he opposes her, he thereby annuls the vow she had made or the rash pledge to which she had bound herself, and the Lord will release her from it.

text.NRSV: If she made a vow in her husband's house, or bound herself by a pledge with an oath,"
text.Rotherham: But, if in the house of her husband, she vowed, or bound a bond upon her soul. with an oath;
text.KJV: 30:10 And if she vowed in her husbands house, or bound her soule by a bond with an oath;
text.NABRE: (The vow of a widow or of a divorced woman, however, any pledge to which such a woman binds herself, is valid.)

text.NRSV: and her husband heard it and said nothing to her, and did not express disapproval to her, then all her vows shall stand, and any pledge by which she bound herself shall stand."
text.Rotherham: and her husband heard, and held his peace at her, did not forbid her, then shall all her vows stand, and every bond which she hath bound upon her soul, shall stand.
text.KJV: 30:11 And her husband heard it, and held his peace at her, and disallowed her not: then all her vowes shall stand, and euery bond wherewith shee bound her soule, shall stand.
text.NABRE: “If it is in her husband’s house that she makes a vow or binds herself under oath to a pledge,

text.NRSV: But if her husband nullifies them at the time that he hears them, then whatever proceeds out of her lips concerning her vows, or concerning her pledge of herself, shall not stand. Her husband has nullified them, and the Lord will forgive her."
text.Rotherham: But if her husband, did make, them, of none effect, on the day he heard, nothing which came forth out of her lips—of her vows, or of the bond of her soul, shall stand,—her husband, made it of none effect, and Yahweh, will pardon her:
text.KJV: 30:12 But if her husband hath vtterly made them voyd on the day hee heard them: then whatsoeuer proceeded out of her lips concerning her vowes, or concerning the bond of her soule, shall not stand: her husband hath made them voyd, and the Lord shal forgiue her.
text.NABRE: and her husband learns of it yet says nothing to her to oppose her, then all her vows remain valid or any pledge to which she has bound herself.

text.NRSV: Any vow or any binding oath to deny herself, her husband may allow to stand, or her husband may nullify."
text.Rotherham: As for any vow, or any oath of binding, to humble one’s soul, her husband, may make it stand, or, her husband, may make it of none effect.
text.KJV: 30:13 Euery vow, and euery binding othe to afflict the soule, her husband may establish it, or her husband may make it voyd.
text.NABRE: But if on the day he learns of them her husband annuls them, then whatever she has expressly promised in her vows or in her pledge becomes invalid; since her husband has annulled them, the Lord will release her from them.

text.NRSV: But if her husband says nothing to her from day to day, then he validates all her vows, or all her pledges, that are upon her; he has validated them, because he said nothing to her at the time that he heard of them."
text.Rotherham: But if her husband do hold his peace, at her, from day to day, then shall he cause all her vows to stand, or all her bonds which are upon her,—cause them to stand, because he held his peace at her, on the day when he heard.
text.KJV: 30:14 But if her husband altogether hold his peace at her, from day to day, then he establisheth all her vowes, or all her bonds which are vpon her: hee confirmeth them, because hee held his peace at her, in the day that hee heard them.
text.NABRE: “Any vow or any pledge that she makes under oath to humble herself, her husband may either confirm or annul.

text.NRSV: But if he nullifies them some time after he has heard of them, then he shall bear her guilt."
text.Rotherham: And, if he, do make them of none effect, after that he hath heard them, then shall he bear her iniquity.
text.KJV: 30:15 But if hee shall any wayes make them voyd after that he hath heard them, then he shall beare her iniquitie.
text.NABRE: But if her husband, day after day, says nothing at all to her, he thereby confirms all her vows or all the pledges incumbent upon her; he has confirmed them, because on the day he learned of them he said nothing to her.

text.NRSV: These are the statutes that the Lord commanded Moses concerning a husband and his wife, and a father and his daughter while she is still young and in her father's house.
text.Rotherham: These are the statutes which Yahweh commanded Moses, between a man and his wife, between a father and his daughter, in her youth in the house of her father.
text.KJV: 30:16 These are the statutes which the Lord commanded Moses betweene a man and his wife, betweene the father and his daughter, being yet in her youth, in her fathers house.
text.NABRE: If, however, he annuls them some time after he first learned of them, he will be responsible for her guilt.”

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 31:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Chapter 31 Campaign Against the Midianites. The Lord said to Moses:

text.NRSV: "Avenge the Israelites on the Midianites; afterward you shall be gathered to your people."
text.Rotherham: Exact thou the avenging of the sons of Israel, from the Midianites,—and, afterwards, shalt thou be withdrawn unto thy kinsfolk.
text.KJV: 31:2 Auenge the children of Israel of the Midianites: afterward shalt thou be gathered vnto thy people.
text.NABRE: Avenge the Israelites on the Midianites, and then you will be gathered to your people.

text.NRSV: So Moses said to the people, "Arm some of your number for the war, so that they may go against Midian, to execute the Lord's vengeance on Midian."
text.Rotherham: So Moses spake unto the people saying, Arm ye from among you men, for the war, and let them go against Midian, to render the avenging of Yahweh upon Midian.
text.KJV: 31:3 And Moses spake vnto the people, saying, Arme some of your selues vnto the warre, and let them goe against the Midianites, and auenge the Lord of Midian.
text.NABRE: So Moses told the people, “Arm some men among you for the campaign, to attack Midian and to execute the Lord ’s vengeance on Midian.

text.NRSV: You shall send a thousand from each of the tribes of Israel to the war."
text.Rotherham: A thousand from each tribe,—of all the tribes of Israel, shall ye send forth unto the war.
text.KJV: 31:4 Of euery tribe a thousand, throughout all the tribes of Israel, shall ye send to the warre.
text.NABRE: From each of the tribes of Israel you will send a thousand men to the campaign.”

text.NRSV: So out of the thousands of Israel, a thousand from each tribe were conscripted, twelve thousand armed for battle.
text.Rotherham: And there volunteered out of the thousands of Israel, a thousand of each tribe,—twelve thousand, armed for war.
text.KJV: 31:5 So there were deliuered out of the thousands of Israel, a thousand of euery tribe, twelue thousand armed for warre.
text.NABRE: From the contingents of Israel, therefore, a thousand men of each tribe were levied, so that there were twelve thousand men armed for war.

text.NRSV: Moses sent them to the war, a thousand from each tribe, along with Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest, with the vessels of the sanctuary and the trumpets for sounding the alarm in his hand.
text.Rotherham: And Moses sent them a thousand of each tribe to the war,—them, and Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest, to the war, with the vessels of the sanctuary, and the alarm trumpets, in his hand.
text.KJV: 31:6 And Moses sent them to the warre, a thousand of euery tribe, them and Phinehas the sonne of Eleazar the Priest, to the warre, with the holy instruments, and the trumpets to blow, in his hand.
text.NABRE: Moses sent them out on the campaign, a thousand from each tribe, with Phinehas, son of Eleazar, the priest for the campaign, who had with him the sacred vessels and the trumpets for sounding the alarm.

text.NRSV: They did battle against Midian, as the Lord had commanded Moses, and killed every male.
text.Rotherham: So they made war upon Midian, as Yahweh had commanded Moses, and slew every male:
text.KJV: 31:7 And they warred against the Midianites, as the Lord commanded Moses, and they slew all the males.
text.NABRE: They waged war against the Midianites, as the Lord had commanded Moses, and killed every male.

text.NRSV: They killed the kings of Midian: Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur, and Reba, the five kings of Midian, in addition to others who were slain by them; and they also killed Balaam son of Beor with the sword.
text.Rotherham: and the kings of Midian, slew they besides their other slain namely, Evi, and Rekem, and Zur, and Hur, and Reba, five kings of Midian,—Balaam also, son of Beor, slew they with the sword.
text.KJV: 31:8 And they slew the Kings of Midian, beside the rest of them that were slaine; namely Eui, and Rekem, and Zur, and Hur, and Reba, fiue Kings of Midian; Balaam also the sonne of Beor they slew with the sword.
text.NABRE: Besides those slain in battle, they killed the kings of Midian: Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur and Reba, the five kings of Midian; and they also killed Balaam, son of Beor, with the sword.

text.NRSV: The Israelites took the women of Midian and their little ones captive; and they took all their cattle, their flocks, and all their goods as booty.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Israel took captive the women of Midian and their little ones,—all their cattle also and all their flocks and all their substance, carried they off as a prey;
text.KJV: 31:9 And the children of Israel tooke all the women of Midian captiues, and their little ones, and tooke the spoile of all their cattell, and all their flocks, and all their goods.
text.NABRE: But the Israelites took captive the women of the Midianites with their children, and all their herds and flocks and wealth as loot,

text.NRSV: All their towns where they had settled, and all their encampments, they burned,
text.Rotherham: all their cities also in their sites, and all their encampments, burned they up with fire.
text.KJV: 31:10 And they burnt all their cities wherein they dwelt, and all their goodly castles with fire:
text.NABRE: while they set on fire all the towns where they had settled and all their encampments.

text.NRSV: but they took all the spoil and all the booty, both people and animals.
text.Rotherham: Then took they all the spoil, and all the booty,—both of man and beast;
text.KJV: 31:11 And they tooke all the spoile, and all the pray, both of men and of beasts.
text.NABRE: Then they took all the plunder, with the people and animals they had captured, and brought the captives, together with the spoils and plunder,

text.NRSV: Then they brought the captives and the booty and the spoil to Moses, to Eleazar the priest, and to the congregation of the Israelites, at the camp on the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho.
text.Rotherham: and brought in unto Moses and unto Eleazar the priest and unto the assembly of the sons of Israel—the captives and the booty, and the spoil unto the camp,—unto the waste plains of Moab, which are by Jordan, near Jericho.
text.KJV: 31:12 And they brought the captiues, and the pray, and the spoile vnto Moses and Eleazar the Priest, and vnto the Congregation of the children of Israel, vnto the campe at the plaines of Moab, which are by Iordan neere Iericho.
text.NABRE: to Moses and Eleazar the priest and to the Israelite community at their camp on the plains of Moab by the Jordan opposite Jericho.

text.NRSV: Moses, Eleazar the priest, and all the leaders of the congregation went to meet them outside the camp.
text.Rotherham: And Moses, and Eleazar the priest and all the princes of the assembly, went forth to meet them,—unto the outside of the camp.
text.KJV: 31:13 ¶ And Moses and Eleazar the Priest, and all the Princes of the Congregation went foorth to meete them without the campe.
text.NABRE: Treatment of the Captives. When Moses and Eleazar the priest, with all the leaders of the community, went outside the camp to meet them,

text.NRSV: Moses became angry with the officers of the army, the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, who had come from service in the war.
text.Rotherham: Then was Moses sore displeased with the officers of the force,—the princes of thousands, and the princes of hundreds who were coming in from the warring host.
text.KJV: 31:14 And Moses was wroth with the officers of the hoste, with the captaines ouer thousands, and captaines ouer hundreds, which came from the battel.
text.NABRE: Moses became angry with the officers of the army, the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, who were returning from the military campaign.

text.NRSV: Moses said to them, "Have you allowed all the women to live?"
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto them,—Have ye saved alive every female?
text.KJV: 31:15 And Moses said vnto them, Haue ye saued all the women aliue?
text.NABRE: “So you have spared all the women!” he exclaimed.

text.NRSV: These women here, on Balaam's advice, made the Israelites act treacherously against the Lord in the affair of Peor, so that the plague came among the congregation of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Lo! they, became unto the sons of Israel, by the advice of Balaam, the cause of daring acts of treachery against Yahweh, over the affair of Peor,—and then came the plague against the assembly of Yahweh!
text.KJV: 31:16 Behold, these caused the children of Israel, through the counsell of Balaam, to commit trespasse against the Lord in the matter of Peor, and there was a plague among the Congregation of the Lord.
text.NABRE: “These are the very ones who on Balaam’s advice were behind the Israelites’ unfaithfulness to the Lord in the affair at Peor, so that plague struck the Lord ’s community.

text.NRSV: Now therefore, kill every male among the little ones, and kill every woman who has known a man by sleeping with him.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, slay ye every male among the young,—every woman also that hath cohabited with man, slay ye.
text.KJV: 31:17 Now therefore kill euery male among the little ones, and kill euery woman that hath knowen man, by lying with him.
text.NABRE: Now kill, therefore, every male among the children and kill every woman who has had sexual relations with a man.

text.NRSV: But all the young girls who have not known a man by sleeping with him, keep alive for yourselves.
text.Rotherham: But all the young of womankind that have, not cohabited with man, preserve alive for yourselves.
text.KJV: 31:18 But all the women children that haue not knowen a man by lying with him, keepe aliue for your selues.
text.NABRE: But you may spare for yourselves all the girls who have not had sexual relations.

text.NRSV: Camp outside the camp seven days; whoever of you has killed any person, or touched a corpse, purify yourselves and your captives on the third and on the seventh day.
text.Rotherham: Ye, then, pitch outside the camp, for seven days,—whosoever hath killed a person and whoever hath touched the slain, cleanse yourselves (from sin) on the third day and on the seventh day ye and your captives,.
text.KJV: 31:19 And doe yee abide without the campe seuen dayes: whosoeuer hath killed any person, and whosoeuer hath touched any slaine, purifie both your selues, and your captiues, on the third day, and on the seuenth day.
text.NABRE: Purification After Combat. “Moreover, remain outside the camp for seven days; every one of you who has killed anyone or touched someone killed will purify yourselves on the third and on the seventh day—both you and your captives.

text.NRSV: You shall also purify every garment, every article of skin, everything made of goats' hair, and every article of wood.
text.Rotherham: Every garment also, and every article of skin and every thing made of goat’s-hair, and every article of wood, shall ye cleanse (from sin).
text.KJV: 31:20 And purifie all your raiment, and all that is made of skinnes, and all worke of goates haire, and all things made of wood.
text.NABRE: You will also purify every garment, every article of leather, everything made of goats’ hair, and every article of wood.”

text.NRSV: Eleazar the priest said to the troops who had gone to battle: "This is the statute of the law that the Lord has commanded Moses:"
text.Rotherham: Then said Eleazar the priest unto the men of the host, who had been to the war: This, is the statute of the law, which Yahweh hath commanded Moses:
text.KJV: 31:21 ¶ And Eleazar the Priest said vnto the men of warre which went to the battell, This is the ordinance of the law which the Lord commaunded Moses.
text.NABRE: Eleazar the priest told the soldiers who had taken part in the battle: “This is the prescribed ritual which the Lord has commanded Moses:

text.NRSV: gold, silver, bronze, iron, tin, and lead—"
text.Rotherham: Surely the gold and the silver,—the bronze the iron, the tin and the lead,
text.KJV: 31:22 Onely the gold, and the siluer, the brasse, the yron, the tinne, and the lead,
text.NABRE: gold, silver, bronze, iron, tin and lead—

text.NRSV: everything that can withstand fire, you shall pass through fire, and it shall be clean. Nevertheless it shall also be purified with the water for purification; and whatever cannot withstand fire, you shall pass through the water."
text.Rotherham: whatsoever thing can go into fire, ye shall pass through fire and it shall be clean, only with the water of separation, shall ye cleanse it (from sin). But whatsoever cannot go into fire, ye shall pass through water.
text.KJV: 31:23 Euery thing that may abide the fire, yee shall make it goe through the fire, and it shall be cleane: neuerthelesse, it shall be purified with the water of separation: and all that abideth not the fire, yee shall make goe through the water.
text.NABRE: whatever can stand fire—you shall put into the fire, that it may become clean; however, it must also be purified with water of purification. But whatever cannot stand fire you must put into the water.

text.NRSV: You must wash your clothes on the seventh day, and you shall be clean; afterward you may come into the camp.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall wash your clothes on the seventh day, and be clean,—and after-wards, shall ye come into the camp.
text.KJV: 31:24 And ye shall wash your clothes on the seuenth day, and ye shalbe cleane, and afterward yee shall come into the campe.
text.NABRE: On the seventh day you will wash your garments, and then you will again be clean. After that you may enter the camp.”

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 31:25 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Division of the Spoils. The Lord said to Moses:

text.NRSV: "You and Eleazar the priest and the heads of the ancestral houses of the congregation make an inventory of the booty captured, both human and animal."
text.Rotherham: Reckon thou up the sum of the booty that was captured, both of man and of beast,—thou and Eleazar the priest, and the ancestral heads of the assembly;
text.KJV: 31:26 Take the summe of the pray, that was taken, both of man and of beast, thou and Eleazar the Priest, and the chiefe fathers of the Congregation:
text.NABRE: With the help of Eleazar the priest and of the heads of the ancestral houses of the community, inventory all the spoils captured, human being and beast alike;

text.NRSV: Divide the booty into two parts, between the warriors who went out to battle and all the congregation.
text.Rotherham: and divide the booty into two parts, between them who took upon them the war, who went out in the host,—and all the [rest of the] assembly.
text.KJV: 31:27 And diuide the pray into two parts, betweene them that tooke the warre vpon them, who went out to battell, and betweene all the Congregation.
text.NABRE: then divide the spoils between the warriors who went on the campaign and the whole community.

text.NRSV: From the share of the warriors who went out to battle, set aside as tribute for the Lord, one item out of every five hundred, whether persons, oxen, donkeys, sheep, or goats.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou levy a tribute unto Yahweh—from the men of war, who went forth in the host, one living thing, out of five hundred,—of the human beings, and of the herd, and of the asses and of the flock:
text.KJV: 31:28 And leuie a tribute vnto the Lord of the men of warre which went out to battell: one soule of fiue hundred, both of the persons, and of the beeues, and of the asses, and of the sheepe.
text.NABRE: You will levy a tax for the Lord on the soldiers who went on the campaign: one out of every five hundred persons, oxen, donkeys, and sheep.

text.NRSV: Take it from their half and give it to Eleazar the priest as an offering to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: out of their half, shall ye take [them],—and thou shall give [them] unto Eleazar the priest as a heave-offering unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 31:29 Take it of their halfe, and giue it vnto Eleazar the Priest, for an heaue offering of the Lord.
text.NABRE: From their half you will take it and give it to Eleazar the priest as a contribution to the Lord.

text.NRSV: But from the Israelites' half you shall take one out of every fifty, whether persons, oxen, donkeys, sheep, or goats—all the animals—and give them to the Levites who have charge of the tabernacle of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And out of the half allotted to the sons of Israel, shalt thou take one allotted portion out of fifty of the human beings, of the herd, of the asses and of the flock of all the cattle,—and shalt give them unto the Levites, who keep the charge of the habitation of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 31:30 And of the children of Israels halfe, thou shalt take one portion of fiftie, of the persons, of the beeues, of the asses, and of the flockes, of all maner of beasts, and giue them vnto the Leuites, which keepe the charge of the Tabernacle of the Lord.
text.NABRE: From the Israelites’ half you will take one captive from every fifty human beings, oxen, donkeys, and sheep—all the animals—and give them to the Levites, who perform the duties of the Lord ’s tabernacle.

text.NRSV: Then Moses and Eleazar the priest did as the Lord had commanded Moses:
text.Rotherham: And Moses and Eleazar the priest did,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: 31:31 And Moses and Eleazar the Priest did as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: So Moses and Eleazar the priest did this, as the Lord had commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: The booty remaining from the spoil that the troops had taken totaled six hundred seventy-five thousand sheep,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that the booty, over and above the prey which the people of the host had seized, was,—of the flock, six hundred and seventy-five thousand;
text.KJV: 31:32 And the bootie being the rest of the pray which the men of war had caught, was six hundred thousand, and seuenty thousand, and fiue thousand sheepe,
text.NABRE: Amount of the Plunder. This plunder, what was left of the loot which the troops had taken, amounted to six hundred and seventy-five thousand sheep,

text.NRSV: seventy-two thousand oxen,
text.Rotherham: and, of the herd, seventy-two thousand;
text.KJV: 31:33 And threescore and twelue thousand beeues,
text.NABRE: seventy-two thousand oxen,

text.NRSV: sixty-one thousand donkeys,
text.Rotherham: and, of the asses, sixty-one thousand;
text.KJV: 31:34 And threescore and one thousand asses:
text.NABRE: sixty-one thousand donkeys,

text.NRSV: and thirty-two thousand persons in all, women who had not known a man by sleeping with him.
text.Rotherham: and, of the human persons, even of the woman-kind who had not cohabited with man,—all the persons, thirty-two thousand.
text.KJV: 31:35 And thirtie and two thousand persons in all, of women that had not knowen man by lying with him.
text.NABRE: and thirty-two thousand women who had not had sexual relations.

text.NRSV: The half-share, the portion of those who had gone out to war, was in number three hundred thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep and goats,
text.Rotherham: And the half, the share of them who had gone forth in the host, was,—the number of the flock—three hundred and thirty-seven thousand and five hundred;
text.KJV: 31:36 And the halfe which was the portion of them that went out to warre, was in number three hundred thousand, and seuen and thirtie thousand, and fiue hundred sheepe.
text.NABRE: The half-share that fell to those who had gone out on the campaign was in number: three hundred and thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep,

text.NRSV: and the Lord's tribute of sheep and goats was six hundred seventy-five.
text.Rotherham: and so, the tribute unto Yahweh, out of the flock, was—six hundred and seventy-five;
text.KJV: 31:37 And the Lords tribute of the sheepe was sixe hundred and threescore and fifteene.
text.NABRE: of which six hundred and seventy-five fell as tax to the Lord;

text.NRSV: The oxen were thirty-six thousand, of which the Lord's tribute was seventy-two.
text.Rotherham: and the herd, thirty-six thousand,—and the tribute of them unto Yahweh—seventy-two;
text.KJV: 31:38 And the beeues were thirtie and sixe thousand, of which the Lords tribute was threescore and twelue.
text.NABRE: thirty-six thousand oxen, of which seventy-two fell as tax to the Lord;

text.NRSV: The donkeys were thirty thousand five hundred, of which the Lord's tribute was sixty-one.
text.Rotherham: and the asses, thirty thousand and five hundred,—and the tribute of them unto Yahweh, sixty-one;
text.KJV: 31:39 And the asses were thirtie thousand and fiue hundred, of which the Lords tribute was threescore and one.
text.NABRE: thirty thousand five hundred donkeys, of which sixty-one fell as tax to the Lord;

text.NRSV: The persons were sixteen thousand, of which the Lord's tribute was thirty-two persons.
text.Rotherham: and the human persons, sixteen thousand,—and the tribute of them unto Yahweh, thirty-two persons.
text.KJV: 31:40 And the persons were sixteene thousand, of which the Lords tribute was thirtie and two persons.
text.NABRE: and sixteen thousand persons, of whom thirty-two persons fell as tax to the Lord.

text.NRSV: Moses gave the tribute, the offering for the Lord, to Eleazar the priest, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And Moses gave the tribute—the heave-offering of Yahweh, unto Eleazar the priest,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: 31:41 And Moses gaue the tribute which was the Lords heaue offering, vnto Eleazar the Priest, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: Moses gave the taxes contributed to the Lord to Eleazar the priest, exactly as the Lord had commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: As for the Israelites' half, which Moses separated from that of the troops,
text.Rotherham: And, of the half, allotted unto the sons of Israel,—which Moses halved away from the men who had gone out in the host,
text.KJV: 31:42 And of the children of Israels halfe, which Moses diuided from the men that warred:
text.NABRE: As for the Israelites’ half, which Moses had taken from the men who had fought—

text.NRSV: the congregation's half was three hundred thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep and goats,
text.Rotherham: the half allotted unto the assembly, was—of the flock, three hundred and thirty-seven thousand, and five hundred;
text.KJV: 31:43 (Now the halfe that perteined vnto the Congregation, was three hundred thousand, and thirtie thousand, and seuen thousand, and fiue hundred sheepe:
text.NABRE: the community’s half was three hundred and thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep,

text.NRSV: thirty-six thousand oxen,
text.Rotherham: and, of the herd, six and thirty thousand;
text.KJV: 31:44 And thirtie and sixe thousand beeues:
text.NABRE: thirty-six thousand oxen,

text.NRSV: thirty thousand five hundred donkeys,
text.Rotherham: and, of asses, thirty thousand and five hundred;
text.KJV: 31:45 And thirtie thousand asses, and fiue hundred:
text.NABRE: thirty thousand five hundred donkeys,

text.NRSV: and sixteen thousand persons.
text.Rotherham: and, human persons, sixteen thousand.
text.KJV: 31:46 And sixteene thousand persons)
text.NABRE: and sixteen thousand persons.

text.NRSV: From the Israelites' half Moses took one of every fifty, both of persons and of animals, and gave them to the Levites, who had charge of the tabernacle of the Lord; as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses took—out of the half belonging to the sons of Israel the allotted portion one out of fifty, of the human beings and of the beasts,—and gave them unto the Levites, the keepers of the charge of the habitation of Yahweh, As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: 31:47 Euen of the children of Israels halfe, Moses tooke one portion of fiftie, both of man and of beast, and gaue them vnto the Leuites, which kept the charge of the Tabernacle of the Lord, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: From the Israelites’ half, Moses took one captive from every fifty, from human being and beast alike, and gave them to the Levites, who performed the duties of the Lord ’s tabernacle, exactly as the Lord had commanded Moses.

text.NRSV: Then the officers who were over the thousands of the army, the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, approached Moses,
text.Rotherham: Then came near unto Moses the officers who belonged unto the thousands of the host,—the princes of thousands and the princes of hundreds;
text.KJV: 31:48 ¶ And the officers which were ouer thousands of the hoste, the captaines of thousands, and captaines of hundreds came neere vnto Moses.
text.NABRE: Gifts of the Officers. Then those who were officers over the contingents of the army, commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds, came up to Moses

text.NRSV: and said to Moses, "Your servants have counted the warriors who are under our command, and not one of us is missing."
text.Rotherham: and said unto Moses, Thy servants have reckoned up the sum of the men of war who are in our hand,—and there is not missed from among us, a man!
text.KJV: 31:49 And they said vnto Moses, Thy seruants haue taken the summe of the men of warre which are vnder our charge, and there lacketh not one man of vs.
text.NABRE: and said to him, “Your servants have counted the soldiers under our command, and not one of us is missing.

text.NRSV: So we have brought as an offering to the Lord what each of us found, articles of gold, armlets and bracelets, signet rings, earrings, and pendants, to make atonement for ourselves before the Lord."
text.Rotherham: Therefore have we brought near an offering unto Yahweh, what, each man, hath found—articles of gold, ankle chains and bracelets, rings earrings and buckles,—to put a propitiatory-covering over our souls before Yahweh.
text.KJV: 31:50 Wee haue therefore brought an oblation for the Lord, what euerie man hath gotten, of iewels of golde chaines, and bracelets, rings, earerings, and tablets, to make an atonement for our soules before the Lord.
text.NABRE: So, we have brought as an offering to the Lord articles of gold that each of us has picked up—anklets, bracelets, rings, earrings, or pendants—to make atonement for ourselves before the Lord.”

text.NRSV: Moses and Eleazar the priest received the gold from them, all in the form of crafted articles.
text.Rotherham: So Moses, and Eleazar the priest took the gold of them,—all the wrought articles.
text.KJV: 31:51 And Moses and Eleazar the Priest tooke the gold of them: euen all wrought iewels.
text.NABRE: Moses and Eleazar the priest accepted the gold from them, all fashioned pieces.

text.NRSV: And all the gold of the offering that they offered to the Lord, from the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds, was sixteen thousand seven hundred fifty shekels.
text.Rotherham: And all the gold of the heave-offering which they offered up unto Yahweh, was—sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels,—of the princes of thousands, and of the princes of hundreds.
text.KJV: 31:52 And all the gold of the offring that they offered vp to the Lord, of the captaines of thousands, and of the captaines of hundreds, was sixteene thousand, seuen hundred and fiftie shekels.
text.NABRE: The gold that was given as a contribution to the Lord —from the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds—amounted in all to sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels.

text.NRSV: (The troops had all taken booty for themselves.)"
text.Rotherham: The men of the host, had taken prey, each man for himself.
text.KJV: 31:53 (For the men of warre had taken spoile, euery man for himselfe.)
text.NABRE: What the common soldiers had looted each one kept for himself.

text.NRSV: So Moses and Eleazar the priest received the gold from the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and brought it into the tent of meeting as a memorial for the Israelites before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold of the princes of thousands, and hundreds,—and brought it into the tent of meeting, as a memorial for the sons of Israel before Yahweh.
text.KJV: 31:54 And Moses and Eleazar the Priest tooke the gold of the captaines, of thousands, and of hundreds, and brought it into the Tabernacle of the Congregation, for a memoriall for the children of Israel before the Lord.
text.NABRE: So Moses and Eleazar the priest accepted the gold from the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and put it in the tent of meeting as a reminder on behalf of the Israelites before the Lord.

text.NRSV: Now the Reubenites and the Gadites owned a very great number of cattle. When they saw that the land of Jazer and the land of Gilead was a good place for cattle,
text.Rotherham: Now, much cattle, had come into the possession of the sons of Reuben and of the sons of Gad, an exceeding mighty number. And when they saw the land of Jazer and the land of Gilead, lo! the place, was a place for cattle.
text.KJV: 32:1 Now the children of Reuben, and the children of Gad, had a very great multitude of cattell: and when they saw the land of Iazer, and the land of Gilead, that behold, the place was a place for cattell;
text.NABRE: Chapter 32 Request of Gad and Reuben. Now the Reubenites and Gadites had a very large number of livestock. Noticing that the land of Jazer and of Gilead was a place suited to livestock,

text.NRSV: the Gadites and the Reubenites came and spoke to Moses, to Eleazar the priest, and to the leaders of the congregation, saying,"
text.Rotherham: So the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben came in,—and spake unto Moses and unto Eleazar the priest, and unto the princes of assembly, saying,
text.KJV: 32:2 The children of Gad, and the children of Reuben, came and spake vnto Moses, and to Eleazar the Priest, and vnto the Princes of the Congregation, saying,
text.NABRE: the Gadites and Reubenites came to Moses and Eleazar the priest and to the leaders of the community and said,

text.NRSV: Ataroth, Dibon, Jazer, Nimrah, Heshbon, Elealeh, Sebam, Nebo, and Beon—"
text.Rotherham: As touching Ataroth and Dibon, and Jazer and Nimrah, and Heshbon, and Elealeh,—and Sebam and Nebo, and Beon-
text.KJV: 32:3 Ataroth, and Dibon, and Iazer, and Nimrah, and Heshbon, and Elealeh, and Shebam, and Nebo, and Beon,
text.NABRE: “The region of Ataroth, Dibon, Jazer, Nimrah, Heshbon, Elealeh, Sebam, Nebo and Baal-meon—

text.NRSV: the land that the Lord subdued before the congregation of Israel—is a land for cattle; and your servants have cattle."
text.Rotherham: the land which Yahweh smote before the assembly of Israel, a land for cattle, it is,—and, thy servants, have cattle.
text.KJV: 32:4 Euen the countrey which the Lord smote before the Congregation of Israel, is a land for cattell, and thy seruants haue cattell.
text.NABRE: the land which the Lord has laid low before the community of Israel, is a land for livestock, and your servants have livestock.”

text.NRSV: They continued, "If we have found favor in your sight, let this land be given to your servants for a possession; do not make us cross the Jordan."
text.Rotherham: And they said—If we have found favour in thine eyes, let this land be given unto thy servants, for a possession,—do not take us over the Jordan.
text.KJV: 32:5 Wherefore, said they, if wee haue found grace in thy sight, let this lande be giuen vnto thy seruants for a possession, and bring vs not ouer Iordane.
text.NABRE: They continued, “If we find favor with you, let this land be given to your servants as their possession. Do not make us cross the Jordan.”

text.NRSV: But Moses said to the Gadites and to the Reubenites, "Shall your brothers go to war while you sit here?"
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses unto the sons of Gad and unto the sons of Reuben,—Shall, your brethren, go into the war, and ye, settle down here?
text.KJV: 32:6 ¶ And Moses said vnto the children of Gad, and to the children of Reuben, Shall your brethren goe to warre, and shall ye sit here?
text.NABRE: Moses’ Rebuke. But Moses answered the Gadites and Reubenites: “Are your kindred, then, to go to war, while you remain here?

text.NRSV: Why will you discourage the hearts of the Israelites from going over into the land that the Lord has given them?"
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, then, should ye dissuade the heart of the sons of Israel, from passing over into the land which Yahweh hath given to them?
text.KJV: 32:7 And wherefore discourage yee the heart of the children of Israel from going ouer into the lande, which the Lord hath giuen them?
text.NABRE: Why do you wish to discourage the Israelites from crossing to the land the Lord has given them?

text.NRSV: Your fathers did thus when I sent them from Kadesh-barnea to see the land."
text.Rotherham: Thus, did your fathers,—when I sent them from Kadesh-barnea, to view the land,
text.KJV: 32:8 Thus did your fathers, when I sent them from Kadesh Barnea to see the land.
text.NABRE: That is just what your ancestors did when I sent them from Kadesh-barnea to reconnoiter the land.

text.NRSV: When they went up to the Wadi Eshcol and saw the land, they discouraged the hearts of the Israelites from going into the land that the Lord had given them."
text.Rotherham: and they went up as far as the ravine of Eschol, and viewed the land, and then dissuaded the heart of the sons of Israel from entering into the land, which Yahweh had given them.
text.KJV: 32:9 For when they went vp vnto the valley of Eshcol, and saw the land, they discouraged the heart of the children of Israel, that they should not goe into the land which the Lord had giuen them.
text.NABRE: They went up to the Wadi Eshcol and reconnoitered the land, then so discouraged the Israelites that they would not enter the land the Lord had given them.

text.NRSV: The Lord's anger was kindled on that day and he swore, saying,"
text.Rotherham: Then was the anger of Yahweh kindled on that day,—and he sware, saying:
text.KJV: 32:10 And the Lords anger was kindled the same time, and hee sware, saying,
text.NABRE: At that time the anger of the Lord flared up, and he swore:

text.NRSV: 'Surely none of the people who came up out of Egypt, from twenty years old and upward, shall see the land that I swore to give to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, because they have not unreservedly followed me—"
text.Rotherham: Surely none of the men that came up out of Egypt from twenty years old and upwards, shall see the soil, which I sware unto Abraham, unto Isaac and unto Jacob, because they have not followed after me fully;
text.KJV: 32:11 Surely none of the men that came vp out of Egypt, from twentie yeeres old and vpward, shall see the lande which I sware vnto Abraham, vnto Isaac, and vnto Iacob, because they haue not wholly followed me:
text.NABRE: None of the men twenty years old or more who have come up from Egypt will see the land I promised under oath to Abraham and Isaac and Jacob, because they have not followed me unreservedly—

text.NRSV: none except Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite and Joshua son of Nun, for they have unreservedly followed the Lord.'"
text.Rotherham: save Caleb son of Jephunneh, the Kenezite, and Joshua, son of Nun,—for they followed after Yahweh, fully.
text.KJV: 32:12 Saue Caleb the sonne of Iephunneh the Kenezite, and Ioshua the sonne of Nun: for they haue wholly followed the Lord.
text.NABRE: except the Kenizzite Caleb, son of Jephunneh, the Kenizzite, and Joshua, son of Nun, since they have followed the Lord unreservedly.

text.NRSV: And the Lord's anger was kindled against Israel, and he made them wander in the wilderness for forty years, until all the generation that had done evil in the sight of the Lord had disappeared."
text.Rotherham: So the anger of Yahweh kindled upon Israel, and he made them wander in the desert, forty years,—until all the generation who had done the evil in the eyes of Yahweh, had wasted away!
text.KJV: 32:13 And the Lords anger was kindled against Israel, and hee made them wander in the wildernesse fourty yeeres, vntill all the generation that had done euill in the sight of the Lord was consumed.
text.NABRE: So the anger of the Lord flared up against the Israelites and he made them wander in the wilderness forty years, until the whole generation that had done evil in the sight of the Lord had disappeared.

text.NRSV: And now you, a brood of sinners, have risen in place of your fathers, to increase the Lord's fierce anger against Israel!"
text.Rotherham: Lo! therefore, ye have arisen in the room of your fathers, a brood of sinful men,—to heap up yet more upon the heat of the anger of Yahweh, towards Israel.
text.KJV: 32:14 And beholde, ye are risen vp in your fathers stead, an increase of sinfull men, to augment yet the fierce anger of the Lord toward Israel.
text.NABRE: And now here you are, offspring of sinful stock, rising up in your ancestors’ place to add still more to the Lord ’s blazing anger against the Israelites.
