text.NRSV: If you turn away from following him, he will again abandon them in the wilderness; and you will destroy all this people."
text.Rotherham: If ye do turn away from following him, then will he yet again leave them behind in the desert,—so shall ye bring destruction upon all this people.
text.KJV: 32:15 For if yee turne away from after him, hee will yet againe leaue them in the wildernesse, and ye shall destroy all this people.
text.NABRE: If you turn away from following him, he will again abandon them in the wilderness, and you will bring about the ruin of this entire people.”

text.NRSV: Then they came up to him and said, "We will build sheepfolds here for our flocks, and towns for our little ones,"
text.Rotherham: Then came they near unto him, and said, Folds, for flocks, would we build for our cattle, here,—and cities for our little ones;
text.KJV: 32:16 ¶ And they came neere vnto him, and said, Wee will build sheepfoldes here for our cattell, and cities for our litle ones.
text.NABRE: Counter Proposal. But they approached him and said: “We will only build sheepfolds here for our flocks and towns for our families;

text.NRSV: but we will take up arms as a vanguard before the Israelites, until we have brought them to their place. Meanwhile our little ones will stay in the fortified towns because of the inhabitants of the land."
text.Rotherham: but, we, would arm ourselves promptly, before the sons of Israel, until that we have brought them into their place,—so should our little ones remain in the fortified cities, because of the inhabitants of the land.
text.KJV: 32:17 But we ourselues will goe ready armed before the children of Israel, vntill wee haue brought them vnto their place: and our litle ones shall dwell in the fenced cities, because of the inhabitants of the land.
text.NABRE: but we ourselves will march as troops in the vanguard before the Israelites, until we have led them to their destination. Meanwhile our families will remain in the fortified towns because of the land’s inhabitants.

text.NRSV: We will not return to our homes until all the Israelites have obtained their inheritance."
text.Rotherham: We would not return unto our houses,—until the sons of Israel have possessed themselves each man, of his inheritance.
text.KJV: 32:18 Wee will not returne vnto our houses, vntill the children of Israel haue inherited euery man his inheritance:
text.NABRE: We will not return to our homes until all the Israelites have taken possession of their heritage.

text.NRSV: We will not inherit with them on the other side of the Jordan and beyond, because our inheritance has come to us on this side of the Jordan to the east."
text.Rotherham: For we would not inherit with them, over the Jordan, and onwards,—because our inheritance hath come unto us on this side the Jordan towards sunrise.
text.KJV: 32:19 For wee will not inherite with them on yonder side Iordane, or forward, because our inheritance is fallen to vs on this side Iordane Eastward.
text.NABRE: But we will not claim any heritage with them across the Jordan and beyond, because we have received a heritage for ourselves on the eastern side of the Jordan.”

text.NRSV: Moses said to them: "If you do this—if you take up arms to go before the Lord for the war,"
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto them, If ye will indeed do this thing,—if ye will arm yourselves, before Yahweh, for the war;
text.KJV: 32:20 ¶ And Moses said vnto them, If ye will doe this thing, if ye will goe armed before the Lord to warre,
text.NABRE: Agreement Reached. Moses said to them in reply: “If you do this—if you march as troops before the Lord into battle

text.NRSV: and all those of you who bear arms cross the Jordan before the Lord, until he has driven out his enemies from before him"
text.Rotherham: and every armed man of you pass over the Jordan before Yahweh,—until he hath dispossessed his enemies, from before him;
text.KJV: 32:21 And will goe all of you armed ouer Iordane before the Lord, vntill he hath driuen out his enemies from before him,
text.NABRE: and cross the Jordan in full force before the Lord until he has driven his enemies out of his way

text.NRSV: and the land is subdued before the Lord—then after that you may return and be free of obligation to the Lord and to Israel, and this land shall be your possession before the Lord."
text.Rotherham: and the land be subdued before Yahweh then afterwards, shall ye return, and shall be acquitted by Yahweh and by Israel,—and this land shall be yours for a possession before Yahweh.
text.KJV: 32:22 And the land bee subdued before the Lord: then afterward ye shall returne, and bee guiltlesse before the Lord, and before Israel; and this land shall be your possession before the Lord.
text.NABRE: and the land is subdued before the Lord, then you may return here, free from every obligation to the Lord and to Israel, and this land will be your possession before the Lord.

text.NRSV: But if you do not do so, you have sinned against the Lord; and be sure your sin will find you out."
text.Rotherham: But, if ye shall not do this, lo! ye will have sinned against Yahweh,—and take note of your sin, that it will find you out.
text.KJV: 32:23 But if ye will not doe so, behold, yee haue sinned against the Lord: and bee sure your sinne will finde you out.
text.NABRE: But if you do not do this, you will have sinned against the Lord, and you can be sure that the consequences of your sin will overtake you.

text.NRSV: Build towns for your little ones, and folds for your flocks; but do what you have promised."
text.Rotherham: Build you cities for your little ones, and folds for your flocks,—and then that which hath gone forth from your mouth, ye shall do.
text.KJV: 32:24 Build ye cities for your litle ones, and folds for your sheepe, and doe that which hath proceeded out of your mouth.
text.NABRE: Build the towns, then, for your families, and the folds for your flocks, but fulfill what you have promised.”

text.NRSV: Then the Gadites and the Reubenites said to Moses, "Your servants will do as my lord commands."
text.Rotherham: Then spake the sons of Gad, and the sons of Reuben unto Moses, saying,—thy servants, will do, as, my lord, is giving command.
text.KJV: 32:25 And the children of Gad, and the children of Reuben spake vnto Moses, saying, Thy seruants will doe as my lord commandeth.
text.NABRE: The Gadites and Reubenites answered Moses, “Your servants will do as my lord commands.

text.NRSV: Our little ones, our wives, our flocks, and all our livestock shall remain there in the towns of Gilead;"
text.Rotherham: Our little ones, our wives, our cattle, and all our beasts, shall remain there in the cities of Gilead;
text.KJV: 32:26 Our little ones, our wiues, our flocks, and all our cattell shall be there in the cities of Gilead.
text.NABRE: While our wives and children, our livestock and other animals remain there in the towns of Gilead,

text.NRSV: but your servants will cross over, everyone armed for war, to do battle for the Lord, just as my lord orders."
text.Rotherham: and thy servants, will pass over every one armed for war, before Yahweh to battle,—as, my lord is speaking.
text.KJV: 32:27 But thy seruants will passe ouer, euery man armed for warre, before the Lord to battell, as my lord saith.
text.NABRE: all your servants will go across as armed troops before the Lord to battle, just as my lord says.”

text.NRSV: So Moses gave command concerning them to Eleazar the priest, to Joshua son of Nun, and to the family heads of the tribes of the Israelites."
text.Rotherham: So Moses gave command concerning them, unto Eleazar the priest and unto Joshua son of Nun, and unto the heads of the fathers of the tribes, of the sons of Israel;
text.KJV: 32:28 So concerning them Moses commaunded Eleazar the Priest, and Ioshua the sonne of Nun, and the chiefe fathers of the tribes of the children of Israel:
text.NABRE: So Moses gave this command in their regard to Eleazar the priest, to Joshua, son of Nun, and to the heads of the ancestral houses of the Israelite tribes:

text.NRSV: And Moses said to them, "If the Gadites and the Reubenites, everyone armed for battle before the Lord, will cross over the Jordan with you and the land shall be subdued before you, then you shall give them the land of Gilead for a possession;"
text.Rotherham: and Moses said unto them—If the sons of Gad and the son of Reuben will pass with you over the Jordan, every one armed for the war before Yahweh, and the land be subdued before you, then shall ye give unto them the land of Gilead, for a possession.
text.KJV: 32:29 And Moses said vnto them, If the children of Gad, and the children of Reuben will passe with you ouer Iordane, euery man armed to battell before the Lord, and the land shall be subdued before you, then ye shall giue them the land of Gilead for a possession:
text.NABRE: He said to them, “If all the Gadites and Reubenites cross the Jordan with you in full force before the Lord into battle, the land will be subdued before you, and you will give them Gilead as a possession.

text.NRSV: but if they will not cross over with you armed, they shall have possessions among you in the land of Canaan."
text.Rotherham: But, if they will not pass over armed, with you, then shall they accept a possession in your midst, in the land of Canaan.
text.KJV: 32:30 But if they will not passe ouer with you armed, they shall haue possessions among you in the land of Canaan.
text.NABRE: But if they will not go across in force with you before the Lord, you will bring their wives and children and livestock across before you into Canaan, and they will possess a holding among you in the land of Canaan.”

text.NRSV: The Gadites and the Reubenites answered, saying, "As the Lord has spoken to your servants, so we will do."
text.Rotherham: Then responded the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben, saying,—That which Yahweh had spoken unto thy servants, so, will we do.
text.KJV: 32:31 And the children of Gad, and the children of Reuben answered, saying, As the Lord hath said vnto thy seruants, so will we doe.
text.NABRE: To this the Gadites and Reubenites replied, “We will do what the Lord has ordered your servants.

text.NRSV: We will cross over armed before the Lord into the land of Canaan, but the possession of our inheritance shall remain with us on this side of the Jordan."
text.Rotherham: We, will pass over armed, before Yahweh into the land of Canaan,—then shall be ours, the possession of our inheritance across the Jordan.
text.KJV: 32:32 Wee will passe ouer armed before the Lord into the land of Canaan, that the possession of our inheritance on this side Iordane may be ours.
text.NABRE: We ourselves will go across in force before the Lord into the land of Canaan, but we will retain our hereditary property on this side of the Jordan.”

text.NRSV: Moses gave to them—to the Gadites and to the Reubenites and to the half-tribe of Manasseh son of Joseph—the kingdom of King Sihon of the Amorites and the kingdom of King Og of Bashan, the land and its towns, with the territories of the surrounding towns."
text.Rotherham: So then Moses gave unto them even unto the sons of Gad and unto the sons of Reuben and unto the half tribe of Manasseh son of Joseph the kingdom of sihon, king of the Amorites, and the kingdom of Og, the king of Bashan, the land by her cities with boundaries, even the cities of the land round about.
text.KJV: 32:33 And Moses gaue vnto them, euen to the children of Gad, and to the children of Reuben; and vnto halfe the tribe of Manasseh the sonne of Ioseph, the kingdome of Sihon King of the Amorites, and the kingdome of Og King of Bashan, the land with the cities thereof, in the coastes, euen the cities of the countrey round about.
text.NABRE: So Moses gave them— the Gadites and Reubenites, as well as half the tribe of Manasseh, son of Joseph—the kingdom of Sihon, king of the Amorites, and the kingdom of Og, king of Bashan, the land with its towns, and the districts of the surrounding towns.

text.NRSV: The Gadites rebuilt Dibon, Ataroth, Aroer,"
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Gad built Dibon, and Ataroth,—and Aroer;
text.KJV: 32:34 ¶ And the children of Gad built Dibon, and Ataroth, and Aroer,
text.NABRE: The Gadites rebuilt the cities of Dibon, Ataroth, Aroer,

text.NRSV: Atroth-shophan, Jazer, Jogbehah,"
text.Rotherham: and Atroth-shophan and Jazer, and Jogbehah;
text.KJV: 32:35 And Atroth, Shophan, and Iaazer, and Iogbehah,
text.NABRE: Atroth-shophan, Jazer, Jogbehah,

text.NRSV: Beth-nimrah, and Beth-haran, fortified cities, and folds for sheep."
text.Rotherham: and Beth-nimrah, and Beth-haran,—fortified cities and folds for flocks.
text.KJV: 32:36 And Bethnimrah, and Bethharan, fenced cities: and foldes for sheepe.
text.NABRE: Beth-nimrah and Beth-haran—fortified cities—and sheepfolds.

text.NRSV: The Reubenites rebuilt Heshbon, Elealeh, Kiriathaim,"
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Reuben built Heshbon, and Elealeh,—and Kiriathaim,
text.KJV: 32:37 And the children of Reuben built Heshbon, and Elealeh, and Kiriathaim,
text.NABRE: The Reubenites rebuilt Heshbon, Elealeh, Kiriathaim,

text.NRSV: Nebo, and Baal-meon (some names being changed), and Sibmah; and they gave names to the towns that they rebuilt."
text.Rotherham: and Nebo and Baal-meon—their names being changed, and Sibmah,—and they gave names to the cities which they built.
text.KJV: 32:38 And Nebo, and Baalmeon (their names being changed) and Shibmah: and gaue other names vnto the cities which they builded.
text.NABRE: Nebo, Baal-meon (names to be changed!), and Sibmah. These towns, which they rebuilt, they called by their old names.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Machir son of Manasseh went to Gilead, captured it, and dispossessed the Amorites who were there."
text.Rotherham: Then went the sons of Machir son of Manasseh to Gilead, and captured it, and dispossessed the Amorites who were therein,
text.KJV: 32:39 And the children of Machir, the sonne of Manasseh, went to Gilead, and tooke it, and dispossessed the Amorite which was in it.
text.NABRE: Other Conquests. The descendants of Machir, son of Manasseh, went to Gilead and captured it, dispossessing the Amorites who were there.

text.NRSV: So Moses gave Gilead to Machir son of Manasseh, and he settled there."
text.Rotherham: So Moses gave Gilead unto Machir son of Manasseh, and he dwelt therein.
text.KJV: 32:40 And Moses gaue Gilead vnto Machir the sonne of Manasseh, and he dwelt therein.
text.NABRE: (Moses gave Gilead to Machir, son of Manasseh, and he settled there.)

text.NRSV: Jair son of Manasseh went and captured their villages, and renamed them Havvoth-jair."
text.Rotherham: And Jair, son of Manasseh, went, and captured their encampments,—and called them Havvoth-jair [="The encampments of Jair"].
text.KJV: 32:41 And Iair the sonne of Manasseh went and tooke the small townes thereof, and called them Hauoth-Iair.
text.NABRE: Jair, a descendant of Manasseh, went and captured their tent villages, and called them Havvoth-jair.

text.NRSV: And Nobah went and captured Kenath and its villages, and renamed it Nobah after himself.
text.Rotherham: And, Nobah, went, and captured Kenath, with the villages thereof,—and called it Nobah, after his own name.
text.KJV: 32:42 And Nobah went and tooke Kenath, and the villages thereof, and called it Nobah, after his owne name.
text.NABRE: Nobah went and captured Kenath with its dependencies and called it Nobah after his own name.

text.NRSV: These are the stages by which the Israelites went out of the land of Egypt in military formation under the leadership of Moses and Aaron."
text.Rotherham: These, are the departures of the sons of Israel whereby they came forth out of the land of Egypt by their hosts,—in the hand of Moses and Aaron.
text.KJV: 33:1 These are the iourneyes of the children of Israel, which went foorth out of the land of Egypt, with their armies, vnder the hand of Moses and Aaron.
text.NABRE: Chapter 33 Stages of the Journey. The following are the stages by which the Israelites went out by companies from the land of Egypt under the guidance of Moses and Aaron.

text.NRSV: Moses wrote down their starting points, stage by stage, by command of the Lord; and these are their stages according to their starting places."
text.Rotherham: And Moses wrote their comings forth by their departures, at the bidding of Yahweh,—and these, are their departures by their comings forth.
text.KJV: 33:2 And Moses wrote their goings out according to their iourneyes, by the commandement of the Lord: and these are their iourneyes according to their goings out.
text.NABRE: Moses recorded the starting points of the various stages at the direction of the Lord. These are the stages according to their starting points:

text.NRSV: They set out from Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month; on the day after the passover the Israelites went out boldly in the sight of all the Egyptians,"
text.Rotherham: So then they brake up from Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month, on the morrow of the passover, came forth the sons of Israel with an uplifted hand, in the sight of all the Egyptians;
text.KJV: 33:3 And they departed from Rameses in the first moneth, on the fifteenth day of the first moneth: on the morrow after the Passeouer, the children of Israel went out with an high hand in the sight of all the Egyptians.
text.NABRE: They set out from Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month. On the day after the Passover the Israelites went forth in triumph, in view of all Egypt,

text.NRSV: while the Egyptians were burying all their firstborn, whom the Lord had struck down among them. The Lord executed judgments even against their gods."
text.Rotherham: when the Egyptians, were burying them whom Yahweh had smitten among them, every firstborn,—when, upon their gods, Yahweh had executed judgments,
text.KJV: 33:4 (For the Egyptians buried all their first borne, which the Lord had smitten among them: vpon their gods also the Lord executed iudgements.)
text.NABRE: while the Egyptians buried those whom the Lord had struck down, every firstborn; on their gods, too, the Lord executed judgments.

text.NRSV: So the Israelites set out from Rameses, and camped at Succoth."
text.Rotherham: Thus then the sons of Israel brake up from Rameses,—and encamped in Succoth.
text.KJV: 33:5 And the children of Israel remoued from Rameses, and pitched in Succoth.
text.NABRE: From Egypt to Sinai. Setting out from Rameses, the Israelites camped at Succoth.

text.NRSV: They set out from Succoth, and camped at Etham, which is on the edge of the wilderness."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Succoth,—and encamped in Etham, which is at the edge of the desert.
text.KJV: 33:6 And they departed from Succoth, and pitched in Etham, which is in the edge of the wildernesse.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Succoth, they camped at Etham near the edge of the wilderness.

text.NRSV: They set out from Etham, and turned back to Pi-hahiroth, which faces Baal-zephon; and they camped before Migdol."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Etham, and turned upon Pi-hahiroth, which is over against Baal-zephon,—and encamped before Migdol.
text.KJV: 33:7 And they remoued from Etham, and turned againe vnto Pihahiroth, which is before Baal-zephon: and they pitched before Migdol.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Etham, they turned back to Pi-hahiroth, which is opposite Baal-zephon, and they camped opposite Migdol.

text.NRSV: They set out from Pi-hahiroth, passed through the sea into the wilderness, went a three days' journey in the wilderness of Etham, and camped at Marah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from before Hahiroth, and passed through the midst of the sea towards the desert,—and went their way a journey of three days in the desert of Etham, and encamped in Marah.
text.KJV: 33:8 And they departed from before Pihahiroth, and passed thorow the midst of the sea, into the wildernes, and went three dayes iourney in the wildernesse of Etham, and pitched in Marah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Pi-hahiroth, they crossed over through the sea into the wilderness, and after they traveled a three days’ journey in the wilderness of Etham, they camped at Marah.

text.NRSV: They set out from Marah and came to Elim; at Elim there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees, and they camped there."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Marah, and came in towards Elim; there being, in Elim, twelve fountains of water, and seventy palm-trees, so they encamped there.
text.KJV: 33:9 And they remoued from Marah, and came vnto Elim, and in Elim were twelue fountaines of water, and three score and ten palme trees, and they pitched there.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Marah, they came to Elim; at Elim there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees, and they camped there.

text.NRSV: They set out from Elim and camped by the Red Sea."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Elim,—and encamped by the Red Sea.
text.KJV: 33:10 And they remooued from Elim, and encamped by the red sea.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Elim, they camped beside the Red Sea.

text.NRSV: They set out from the Red Sea and camped in the wilderness of Sin."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from the Red Sea,—and encamped in the desert of Sin.
text.KJV: 33:11 And they remooued from the red sea, and encamped in the wildernesse of Sin.
text.NABRE: Setting out from the Red Sea, they camped in the wilderness of Sin.

text.NRSV: They set out from the wilderness of Sin and camped at Dophkah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from the desert of Sin,—and encamped in Dophkah.
text.KJV: 33:12 And they tooke their iourney out of the wildernesse of Sin, and encamped in Dophkah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from the wilderness of Sin, they camped at Dophkah.

text.NRSV: They set out from Dophkah and camped at Alush."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Dophkah,—and encamped in Alush.
text.KJV: 33:13 And they departed from Dophkah, and encamped in Alush.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Dophkah, they camped at Alush.

text.NRSV: They set out from Alush and camped at Rephidim, where there was no water for the people to drink."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Alush,—and encamped in Rephidim, where was no water for the people to drink.
text.KJV: 33:14 And they remoued from Alush, and encamped at Rephidim, where was no water for the people to drinke.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Alush, they camped at Rephidim, where there was no water for the people to drink.

text.NRSV: They set out from Rephidim and camped in the wilderness of Sinai."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Rephidim,—and encamped in the desert of Sinai.
text.KJV: 33:15 And they departed from Rephidim, and pitched in the wildernesse of Sinai.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Rephidim, they camped in the wilderness of Sinai.

text.NRSV: They set out from the wilderness of Sinai and camped at Kibroth-hattaavah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from the desert of Sinai,—and encamped in Kibroth-hattaavah.
text.KJV: 33:16 And they remoued from the desert of Sinai, and pitched at Kibroth Hattaauah.
text.NABRE: From Sinai to Kadesh. Setting out from the wilderness of Sinai, they camped at Kibroth-hattaavah.

text.NRSV: They set out from Kibroth-hattaavah and camped at Hazeroth."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Kibroth-hattaavah,—and encamped in Hazeroth.
text.KJV: 33:17 And they departed from Kibroth Hattaauah, and encamped at Hazeroth.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Kibroth-hattaavah, they camped at Hazeroth.

text.NRSV: They set out from Hazeroth and camped at Rithmah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Hazeroth,—and encamped in Rithmah.
text.KJV: 33:18 And they departed from Hazeroth, and pitched in Rithmah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Hazeroth, they camped at Rithmah.

text.NRSV: They set out from Rithmah and camped at Rimmon-perez."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Rithmah,—and encamped in Rimmon-perez.
text.KJV: 33:19 And they departed from Rithmah, and pitched at Rimmon Parez.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Rithmah, they camped at Rimmon-perez.

text.NRSV: They set out from Rimmon-perez and camped at Libnah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Rimmon-perez,—and encamped in Libnah.
text.KJV: 33:20 And they departed from Rimmon Parez, and pitched in Libnah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Rimmon-perez, they camped at Libnah.

text.NRSV: They set out from Libnah and camped at Rissah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Libnah,—and encamped in Rissah.
text.KJV: 33:21 And they remoued from Libnah, and pitched at Rissah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Libnah, they camped at Rissah.

text.NRSV: They set out from Rissah and camped at Kehelathah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Rissah,—and encamped in Kehelathah.
text.KJV: 33:22 And they iourneyed from Rissah, and pitched in Kehelathah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Rissah, they camped at Kehelathah.

text.NRSV: They set out from Kehelathah and camped at Mount Shepher."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Kehelathah,—and encamped in Mount Shepher.
text.KJV: 33:23 And they went from Kehelathah, and pitched in mount Shapher.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Kehelathah, they camped at Mount Shepher.

text.NRSV: They set out from Mount Shepher and camped at Haradah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Mount Shepher,—and encamped in Haradah.
text.KJV: 33:24 And they remoued from mount Shapher, and encamped in Haradah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Mount Shepher, they camped at Haradah.

text.NRSV: They set out from Haradah and camped at Makheloth."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Haradah,—and encamped in Makheloth.
text.KJV: 33:25 And they remooued from Haradah, and pitched in Makheloth.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Haradah, they camped at Makheloth.

text.NRSV: They set out from Makheloth and camped at Tahath."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Makheloth,—and encamped in Tahath.
text.KJV: 33:26 And they remooued from Makheloth, and encamped at Tahath.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Makheloth, they camped at Tahath.

text.NRSV: They set out from Tahath and camped at Terah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Tahath,—and encamped in Terah.
text.KJV: 33:27 And they departed from Tahath, and pitched at Tarah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Tahath, they camped at Terah.

text.NRSV: They set out from Terah and camped at Mithkah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Terah,—and encamped in Mithkah.
text.KJV: 33:28 And they remoued from Tarah, and pitched in Mithcah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Terah, they camped at Mithkah.

text.NRSV: They set out from Mithkah and camped at Hashmonah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Mithkah,—and encamped in Hashmonah.
text.KJV: 33:29 And they went from Mithcah, and pitched in Hashmonah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Mithkah, they camped at Hashmonah.

text.NRSV: They set out from Hashmonah and camped at Moseroth."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Hashmonah,—and encamped in Moseroth.
text.KJV: 33:30 And they departed from Hashmonah, and encamped at Moseroth.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Hashmonah, they camped at Moseroth.

text.NRSV: They set out from Moseroth and camped at Bene-jaakan."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Moseroth, and encamped in Bene-jaakan.
text.KJV: 33:31 And they departed from Moseroth, and pitched in Bene-Iaakan.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Moseroth, they camped at Bene-jaakan.

text.NRSV: They set out from Bene-jaakan and camped at Hor-haggidgad."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Bene-jaakan, and encamped in Hor-haggidgad.
text.KJV: 33:32 And they remooued from Bene-Iaakan, & encamped at Horhagidgad.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Bene-jaakan, they camped at Mount Gidgad.

text.NRSV: They set out from Hor-haggidgad and camped at Jotbathah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Hor-haggidgad,—and encamped in Jotbathah,—
text.KJV: 33:33 And they went from Horhagidgad, and pitched in Iotbathah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Mount Gidgad, they camped at Jotbathah.

text.NRSV: They set out from Jotbathah and camped at Abronah."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Jotbathah,—and encamped in Abronah.
text.KJV: 33:34 And they remooued from Iotbathah, and encamped at Ebronah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Jotbathah, they camped at Abronah.

text.NRSV: They set out from Abronah and camped at Ezion-geber."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Abronah,—and encamped in Eziongeber.
text.KJV: 33:35 And they departed from Ebronah, and encamped at Ezion-gaber.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Abronah, they camped at Ezion-geber.

text.NRSV: They set out from Ezion-geber and camped in the wilderness of Zin (that is, Kadesh)."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Eziongeber,—and encamped in the desert of Zin, the same, is Kadesh.
text.KJV: 33:36 And they remoued from Ezion-gaber, and pitched in the wildernes of Zin, which is Kadesh.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Ezion-geber, they camped in the wilderness of Zin, that is, Kadesh.

text.NRSV: They set out from Kadesh and camped at Mount Hor, on the edge of the land of Edom."
text.Rotherham: And they brake, up from Kadesh,—and encamped in Mount Hor, on the outskirts of the land of Edom;
text.KJV: 33:37 And they remooued from Kadesh, and pitched in mount Hor, in the edge of the land of Edom.
text.NABRE: From Kadesh to the Plains of Moab. Setting out from Kadesh, they camped at Mount Hor on the border of the land of Edom.

text.NRSV: Aaron the priest went up Mount Hor at the command of the Lord and died there in the fortieth year after the Israelites had come out of the land of Egypt, on the first day of the fifth month."
text.Rotherham: and Aaron the priest went up into Mount Hor, at the bidding of Yahweh, and died there, in the fortieth year, by the coming forth of the sons of Israel out of the land of Egypt, in the fifth month, on the first of the month.
text.KJV: 33:38 And Aaron the Priest went vp into mount Hor, at the commandement of the Lord, and died there in the fourtieth yeere, after the children of Israel were come out of the lande of Egypt, in the first day of the fift moneth.
text.NABRE: Aaron the priest ascended Mount Hor at the Lord ’s direction, and there he died in the fortieth year after the departure of the Israelites from the land of Egypt, on the first day of the fifth month.

text.NRSV: Aaron was one hundred twenty-three years old when he died on Mount Hor."
text.Rotherham: Now, Aaron, was a hundred and twenty-three years old, when he died in Mount Hor.
text.KJV: 33:39 And Aaron was an hundred and twentie and three yeeres old, when hee died in mount Hor.
text.NABRE: Aaron was a hundred and twenty-three years old when he died on Mount Hor.

text.NRSV: The Canaanite, the king of Arad, who lived in the Negeb in the land of Canaan, heard of the coming of the Israelites."
text.Rotherham: And a Canaanite king of Arad, who was dwelling in the South, in the land of Canaan, heard of the coming in of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 33:40 And King Arad the Canaanite (which dwelt in the South, in the land of Canaan) heard of the comming of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: When the Canaanite, the king of Arad, who ruled over the Negeb in the land of Canaan, heard that the Israelites were coming….

text.NRSV: They set out from Mount Hor and camped at Zalmonah."
text.Rotherham: And, they brake up from Mount Hor,—and encamped in Zalmonah.
text.KJV: 33:41 And they departed from mount Hor, and pitched in Zalmonah.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Mount Hor, they camped at Zalmonah.

text.NRSV: They set out from Zalmonah and camped at Punon."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Zalmonah,—and encamped in Punon.
text.KJV: 33:42 And they departed from Zalmonah, and pitched in Punon.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Zalmonah, they camped at Punon.

text.NRSV: They set out from Punon and camped at Oboth."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Punon, and encamped in Oboth.
text.KJV: 33:43 And they departed from Punon, and pitched in Oboth.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Punon, they camped at Oboth.

text.NRSV: They set out from Oboth and camped at Iye-abarim, in the territory of Moab."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Oboth,—and encamped in Iye-abarim, within the bounds of Moab.
text.KJV: 33:44 And they departed from Oboth, and pitched in Iie-Abarim, in the border of Moab.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Oboth, they camped at Iye-abarim on the border of Moab.

text.NRSV: They set out from Iyim and camped at Dibon-gad."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Iyim,—and encamped in Dibon-gad.
text.KJV: 33:45 And they departed from Iim, and pitched in Dibon Gad.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Iye-abarim, they camped at Dibon-gad.

text.NRSV: They set out from Dibon-gad and camped at Almon-diblathaim."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Dibon-gad, and encamped in Almon-diblathaim.
text.KJV: 33:46 And they remoued from Dibon Gad, and encamped in Almon-Diblathaim.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Dibon-gad, they camped at Almon-diblathaim.

text.NRSV: They set out from Almon-diblathaim and camped in the mountains of Abarim, before Nebo."
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from Almon-diblathaim,—and encamped among the mountains of Abarim, before Nebo.
text.KJV: 33:47 And they remooued from Almon-Diblathaim, and pitched in the mountaines of Abarim, before Nebo.
text.NABRE: Setting out from Almon-diblathaim, they camped in the Abarim range opposite Nebo.

text.NRSV: They set out from the mountains of Abarim and camped in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho;"
text.Rotherham: And they brake up from the mountains of Abarim,—and encamped in the waste plains of Moab, by Jordan near Jericho.
text.KJV: 33:48 And they departed from the mountaines of Abarim, and pitched in the plaines of Moab, by Iordan neere Iericho.
text.NABRE: Setting out from the Abarim range, they camped on the plains of Moab by the Jordan opposite Jericho.

text.NRSV: they camped by the Jordan from Beth-jeshimoth as far as Abel-shittim in the plains of Moab."
text.Rotherham: Thus did they encamp by the Jordan from Beth-jeshimoth unto Abel-Shittim [that is, "The acacia—meadows"]—in the waste plains of Moab.
text.KJV: 33:49 And they pitched by Iordane from Beth-Iesimoth, euen vnto Abel Shittim, in the plaines of Moab.
text.NABRE: They camped by the Jordan on the plains of Moab extended from Beth-jeshimoth to Abel-shittim.

text.NRSV: In the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho, the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:"
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, in the waste plains of Moab, by Jordan near Jericho saying:
text.KJV: 33:50 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, in the plaines of Moab by Iordane, neere Iericho, saying,
text.NABRE: Conquest and Division of Canaan. The Lord spoke to Moses on the plains of Moab by the Jordan opposite Jericho:

text.NRSV: Speak to the Israelites, and say to them: When you cross over the Jordan into the land of Canaan,"
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them,—When ye do pass over the Jordan into the land of Canaan,
text.KJV: 33:51 Speake vnto the children of Israel, and say vnto them, When ye are passed ouer Iordane into the land of Canaan;
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When you go across the Jordan into the land of Canaan,

text.NRSV: you shall drive out all the inhabitants of the land from before you, destroy all their figured stones, destroy all their cast images, and demolish all their high places."
text.Rotherham: then shall ye dispossess all the inhabitants of the land from before you, and shall destroy all their figured stones,—all their molten images, also shall ye destroy, and all their high places, shall ye lay waste.
text.KJV: 33:52 Then ye shall driue out all the inhabitants of the land from before you, and destroy all their pictures, and destroy all their molten images, and quite plucke downe all their high places.
text.NABRE: dispossess all the inhabitants of the land before you; destroy all their stone figures, destroy all their molten images, and demolish all their high places.

text.NRSV: You shall take possession of the land and settle in it, for I have given you the land to possess."
text.Rotherham: So shall ye possess the land, and settle down therein,—for unto you, have I given the land to possess it.
text.KJV: 33:53 And ye shall dispossesse the inhabitants of the land, and dwell therein: for I haue giuen you the land to possesse it.
text.NABRE: You will take possession of the land and settle in it, for I have given you the land to possess.

text.NRSV: You shall apportion the land by lot according to your clans; to a large one you shall give a large inheritance, and to a small one you shall give a small inheritance; the inheritance shall belong to the person on whom the lot falls; according to your ancestral tribes you shall inherit."
text.Rotherham: And ye shall take your inheritance in the land by lot by your families—for the large one, ye shall make large his inheritance, and for the small one, make small his inheritance, whithersoever the lot cometh out to him, his, shall, it be, by the tribes of your fathers, shall ye take your inheritance.
text.KJV: 33:54 And ye shall diuide the land by lot, for an inheritance among your families, and to the moe ye shall giue the more inheritance, and to the fewer yee shall giue the lesse inheritance: euery mans inheritance shall bee in the place where his lot falleth, according to the tribes of your fathers, ye shall inherite.
text.NABRE: You will apportion the land among yourselves by lot, clan by clan, assigning a large heritage to a large clan and a small heritage to a small clan. Wherever anyone’s lot falls, there will his possession be; you will apportion these shares within your ancestral tribe.

text.NRSV: But if you do not drive out the inhabitants of the land from before you, then those whom you let remain shall be as barbs in your eyes and thorns in your sides; they shall trouble you in the land where you are settling."
text.Rotherham: But if ye do not dispossess the inhabitants of the land from before you, then shall it be. that they whom ye leave remaining of them will become pricks in your eyes, and thorns in your sides, and will harass you, concerning the land, wherein, ye are settling down.
text.KJV: 33:55 But if ye will not driue out the inhabitants of the land from before you, then it shall come to passe that those which ye let remaine of them, shall be prickes in your eyes, and thornes in your sides, and shal vexe you in the land wherein ye dwell.
text.NABRE: But if you do not dispossess the inhabitants of the land before you, those whom you allow to remain will become barbs in your eyes and thorns in your sides, and they will harass you in the land where you live,

text.NRSV: And I will do to you as I thought to do to them.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be, that as I thought to do unto them, I will do unto you.
text.KJV: 33:56 Moreouer, it shall come to passe, that I shall do vnto you, as I thought to doe vnto them.
text.NABRE: and I will treat you as I had intended to treat them.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 34:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Chapter 34 The Boundaries. The Lord spoke to Moses:

text.NRSV: Command the Israelites, and say to them: When you enter the land of Canaan (this is the land that shall fall to you for an inheritance, the land of Canaan defined by its boundaries),"
text.Rotherham: Command the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them, When, ye, are coming into the land of Canaan, this is the land which shall fall unto you, as an inheritance, even the land of Canaan, by the boundaries thereof.
text.KJV: 34:2 Command the children of Israel, and say vnto them, When ye come into the land of Canaan, (this is the land that shall fall vnto you for an inheritance, euen the land of Canaan, with the coasts thereof.)
text.NABRE: Give the Israelites this order: When you enter the land of Canaan, this is the territory that shall fall to you as your heritage—the land of Canaan with its boundaries:

text.NRSV: your south sector shall extend from the wilderness of Zin along the side of Edom. Your southern boundary shall begin from the end of the Dead Sea on the east;"
text.Rotherham: Ye shall therefore have a south corner, from the desert of Zin, on the side of Edom,—so shall ye have a south boundary, from the end of the salt sea, eastward:
text.KJV: 34:3 Then your South quarter shall be from the wildernesse of Zin, along by the coast of Edom, and your South border shall be the outmost coast of the salt Sea Eastward.
text.NABRE: Your southern boundary will be at the wilderness of Zin along the border of Edom; on the east your southern boundary will begin at the end of the Salt Sea.

text.NRSV: your boundary shall turn south of the ascent of Akrabbim, and cross to Zin, and its outer limit shall be south of Kadesh-barnea; then it shall go on to Hazar-addar, and cross to Azmon;"
text.Rotherham: and the boundary shall go round for you from the south towards the cliffs of Akrabbim, then cross over towards Zin, and the extension thereof shall be from the south to Kadesh-barnea, then shall it reach out to Hazar-addar, and cross over towards Azmon;
text.KJV: 34:4 And your border shal turne from the South to the ascent of Akrabbim, and passe on to Zin: and the going foorth thereof shall be from the South to Kadesh-Barnea, and shall goe on to Hazar-Addar, and passe on to Azmon.
text.NABRE: Then your boundary will turn south of the Akrabbim Pass and cross Zin. Terminating south of Kadesh-barnea, it extends to Hazar-addar and crosses to Azmon.

text.NRSV: the boundary shall turn from Azmon to the Wadi of Egypt, and its termination shall be at the Sea."
text.Rotherham: then shall the boundary turn round from Azmon towards the ravine of Egypt,—and the extension thereof shall be towards the sea.
text.KJV: 34:5 And the border shall fetch a comcompasse from Azmon vnto the riuer of Egypt, and the goings out of it shall be at the sea.
text.NABRE: Then the boundary will turn from Azmon to the Wadi of Egypt and terminate at the Sea.

text.NRSV: For the western boundary, you shall have the Great Sea and its coast; this shall be your western boundary."
text.Rotherham: And, for a west boundary,—ye shall have the great sea, even a boundary, this, shall serve you as west boundary.
text.KJV: 34:6 And as for the Westerne border, you shall euen haue the great sea for a border: this shall be your West border.
text.NABRE: For your western boundary you will have the Great Sea with its coast; this will be your western boundary.

text.NRSV: This shall be your northern boundary: from the Great Sea you shall mark out your line to Mount Hor;"
text.Rotherham: And, this, shall serve you as a north boundary,—From the great sea, ye shall draw a line for you to Mount Hor:
text.KJV: 34:7 And this shall be your North border: from the great sea, you shall point out for you, mount Hor.
text.NABRE: This will be your boundary on the north: from the Great Sea you will draw a line to Mount Hor,

text.NRSV: from Mount Hor you shall mark it out to Lebo-hamath, and the outer limit of the boundary shall be at Zedad;"
text.Rotherham: From Mount Hor, ye shall draw a line to the entering in of Hamath,—and the extension of the boundary shall be towards Zedad:
text.KJV: 34:8 From mount Hor, ye shall point out your border vnto the entrance of Hamath: and the goings foorth of the border shall be to Zedad.
text.NABRE: and draw it from Mount Hor to Lebo-hamath, with the boundary terminating at Zedad.

text.NRSV: then the boundary shall extend to Ziphron, and its end shall be at Hazar-enan; this shall be your northern boundary."
text.Rotherham: then shall the boundary reach out towards Ziphron, and the extension thereof, be to Hazar-enan. This, shall serve you as a north, boundary.
text.KJV: 34:9 ¶ And the border shall goe on to Ziphron, and the goings out of it shall bee at Hazar Enan: this shall be your North border.
text.NABRE: Then the boundary extends to Ziphron and terminates at Hazar-enan. This will be your northern boundary.

text.NRSV: You shall mark out your eastern boundary from Hazar-enan to Shepham;"
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye draw for yourselves a line, for an east boundary,—from Hazar-enan towards Shepham;
text.KJV: 34:10 And ye shall point out your East border, from Hazar Enan to Shepham.
text.NABRE: For your eastern boundary you will draw a line from Hazar-enan to Shepham.

text.NRSV: and the boundary shall continue down from Shepham to Riblah on the east side of Ain; and the boundary shall go down, and reach the eastern slope of the sea of Chinnereth;"
text.Rotherham: and the boundary shall go down from Shepham towards Riblah, on the east of Ain—then shall the boundary go down and strike on the side of the Sea of Chinnereth, eastward:
text.KJV: 34:11 And the coast shall goe downe from Shepham to Riblah, on the East side of Ain: and the border shall descend and shall reach vnto the side of the sea of Chinnereth Eastward.
text.NABRE: From Shepham the boundary will go down to Riblah, east of Ain, and descending further, the boundary will strike the ridge on the east side of the Sea of Chinnereth;

text.NRSV: and the boundary shall go down to the Jordan, and its end shall be at the Dead Sea. This shall be your land with its boundaries all around."
text.Rotherham: then shall the boundary go down towards the Jordan, and the extension thereof be to the salt sea. This shall be your land by the boundaries thereof round about.
text.KJV: 34:12 And the border shall goe downe to Iordane, and the goings out of it shall be at the salt sea: this shall be your land with the coastes thereof round about.
text.NABRE: then the boundary will descend along the Jordan and terminate with the Salt Sea. This will be your land, with the boundaries that surround it.

text.NRSV: Moses commanded the Israelites, saying: This is the land that you shall inherit by lot, which the Lord has commanded to give to the nine tribes and to the half-tribe;"
text.Rotherham: And Moses commanded the sons of Israel saying,—This, is the land, which ye shall inherit by lot, which Yahweh had commanded to be given to the nine tribes and the half tribe.
text.KJV: 34:13 And Moses commanded the children of Israel, saying, This is the land which ye shall inherite by lot, which the Lord commanded to giue vnto the nine tribes, and to the halfe tribe.
text.NABRE: Moses also gave this order to the Israelites: “This is the land, to be apportioned among you by lot, which the Lord has commanded to be given to the nine and a half tribes.

text.NRSV: for the tribe of the Reubenites by their ancestral houses and the tribe of the Gadites by their ancestral houses have taken their inheritance, and also the half-tribe of Manasseh;"
text.Rotherham: For the tribe of the sons of the Reubenites by their ancestral house, and the tribe of the sons of Gad, by their ancestral house, have received, and, the half tribe of Manasseh have received, their inheritance:
text.KJV: 34:14 For the tribe of the children of Reuben, according to the house of their fathers, and the tribe of the children of Gad, according to the house of their fathers, haue receiued their inheritance, and halfe the tribe of Manasseh haue receiued their inheritance.
text.NABRE: For the tribe of the Reubenites according to their ancestral houses, and the tribe of the Gadites according to their ancestral houses, as well as half of the tribe of Manasseh, have already received their heritage;

text.NRSV: the two tribes and the half-tribe have taken their inheritance beyond the Jordan at Jericho eastward, toward the sunrise."
text.Rotherham: the two tribes, and the half tribe, have received their inheritance—on this side Jordan near Jericho, eastwards, towards sunrise.
text.KJV: 34:15 The two tribes, and the halfe tribe haue receiued their inheritance on this side Iordane neere Iericho, Eastward, toward the Sunne rising.
text.NABRE: these two and a half tribes have received their heritage across the Jordan opposite Jericho, in the east, toward the sunrise.”

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 34:16 And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Supervisors of the Allotment. The Lord spoke to Moses:

text.NRSV: These are the names of the men who shall apportion the land to you for inheritance: the priest Eleazar and Joshua son of Nun."
text.Rotherham: These, are the names of the men who shall receive for you the land, as an inheritance, Eleazar, the priest, and Joshua, son of Nun.
text.KJV: 34:17 These are the names of the men which shall diuide the land vnto you: Eleazar the Priest, and Ioshua the sonne of Nun.
text.NABRE: These are the names of the men who shall apportion the land among you: Eleazar the priest, and Joshua, son of Nun;

text.NRSV: You shall take one leader of every tribe to apportion the land for inheritance."
text.Rotherham: Also, one prince from each tribe, shall ye take to receive the land, as an inheritance.
text.KJV: 34:18 And yee shall take one Prince of euery tribe, to diuide the land by inheritance.
text.NABRE: and you will designate one leader from each of the tribes to apportion the land.

text.NRSV: These are the names of the men: Of the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh."
text.Rotherham: These, therefore, are the names of the men,—For the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh;
text.KJV: 34:19 And the names of the men are these: Of the tribe of Iudah, Caleb the sonne of Iephunneh.
text.NABRE: These are the names of the men: from the tribe of Judah: Caleb, son of Jephunneh,

text.NRSV: Of the tribe of the Simeonites, Shemuel son of Ammihud."
text.Rotherham: And, for the tribe of the sons of Simeon, Shemuel son of Ammihud:
text.KJV: 34:20 And of the tribe of the children of Simeon, Shemuel the sonne of Ammihud.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of the Simeonites: Samuel, son of Ammihud;

text.NRSV: Of the tribe of Benjamin, Elidad son of Chislon."
text.Rotherham: For the tribe of Benjamin, Elidad son of Chislon;
text.KJV: 34:21 Of the tribe of Beniamin, Elidad the sonne of Chislon.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of Benjamin: Elidad, son of Chislon;

text.NRSV: Of the tribe of the Danites a leader, Bukki son of Jogli."
text.Rotherham: And, for the tribe of the sons of Dan, a prince—Bukki, son of Jogli:
text.KJV: 34:22 And the Prince of the tribe of the children of Dan, Bukki the sonne of Iogli.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of the Danites: a leader, Bukki, son of Jogli;

text.NRSV: Of the Josephites: of the tribe of the Manassites a leader, Hanniel son of Ephod,"
text.Rotherham: For the sons of Joseph, For the tribe of the sons of Manasseh, a prince,—Hanniel son of Ephod;
text.KJV: 34:23 The Prince of the children of Ioseph: for the tribe of the children of Manasseh, Hanniel the sonne of Ephod.
text.NABRE: for the descendants of Joseph: from the tribe of the Manassites: a leader, Hanniel, son of Ephod; and

text.NRSV: and of the tribe of the Ephraimites a leader, Kemuel son of Shiphtan."
text.Rotherham: And, for the tribe of the sons of Ephraim, a prince,—Kemuel, son of Shiphtan;
text.KJV: 34:24 And the Prince of the tribe of the children of Ephraim, Kemuel the sonne of Shiphtan.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of the Ephraimites: a leader, Kemuel, son of Shiphtan;

text.NRSV: Of the tribe of the Zebulunites a leader, Eli-zaphan son of Parnach."
text.Rotherham: And, for the tribe of the sons of Zebulun, a prince,—Elizaphan, son of Parnach;
text.KJV: 34:25 And the Prince of the tribe of the children of Zebulun, Elizaphan the sonne of Parnach.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of the Zebulunites: a leader, Elizaphan, son of Parnach;

text.NRSV: Of the tribe of the Issacharites a leader, Paltiel son of Azzan."
text.Rotherham: And, for the tribe of the sons of Issachar, a prince,—Paltiel son of Azzan;
text.KJV: 34:26 And the Prince of the tribe of the children of Issachar, Paltiel the sonne of Azzan.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of the Issacharites: a leader, Paltiel, son of Azzan;

text.NRSV: Of the tribe of the Asherites a leader, Ahihud son of Shelomi."
text.Rotherham: And, for the tribe of the sons of Asher, a prince,—Ahihud son of Shelomi:
text.KJV: 34:27 And the Prince of the tribe of the children of Asher, Ahihud the sonne of Shelomi.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of the Asherites: a leader, Ahihud, son of Shelomi;

text.NRSV: Of the tribe of the Naphtalites a leader, Pedahel son of Ammihud."
text.Rotherham: And, for the tribe of the sons of Naphtali, a prince,—Pedahel, son of Ammihud.
text.KJV: 34:28 And the Prince of the tribe of the children of Naphtali, Pedahel the sonne of Ammihud.
text.NABRE: from the tribe of the Naphtalites: a leader, Pedahel, son of Ammihud.

text.NRSV: These were the ones whom the Lord commanded to apportion the inheritance for the Israelites in the land of Canaan.
text.Rotherham: These, are they whom Yahweh hath commanded to receive for the sons of Israel their inheritance, in the land of Canaan.
text.KJV: 34:29 These are they whom the Lord commaunded to diuide the inheritance vnto the children of Israel in the land of Canaan.
text.NABRE: These are the ones whom the Lord commanded to apportion to the Israelites their heritage in the land of Canaan.

text.NRSV: In the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho, the Lord spoke to Moses, saying:"
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses in the waste plains of Moab,—by Jordan, near Jericho, saying:
text.KJV: 35:1 And the Lord spake vnto Moses in the plaines of Moab by Iordane, neere Iericho, saying,
text.NABRE: Chapter 35 Cities for the Levites. The Lord spoke to Moses on the plains of Moab by the Jordan opposite Jericho:

text.NRSV: Command the Israelites to give, from the inheritance that they possess, towns for the Levites to live in; you shall also give to the Levites pasture lands surrounding the towns."
text.Rotherham: Command the sons of Israel, that they give unto the Levites—out of the inheritance which they possess—cities to dwell in,—pasture land also unto the cities, round about them, shall ye give unto the Levites.
text.KJV: 35:2 Command the children of Israel, that they giue vnto the Leuites of the inheritance of their possession, cities to dwell in: and yee shall giue also vnto the Leuites suburbs for the cities round about them.
text.NABRE: Command the Israelites out of the heritage they possess to give the Levites cities to dwell in; you will also give the Levites the pasture lands around the cities.

text.NRSV: The towns shall be theirs to live in, and their pasture lands shall be for their cattle and for their livestock and for all their animals."
text.Rotherham: So shall the cities be theirs, to dwell in,—and their pasture lands, shall, be for their cattle and for their goods and for all their beasts.
text.KJV: 35:3 And the cities shall they haue to dwell in, and the suburbs of them shall be for their cattell, and for their goods, and for all their beasts.
text.NABRE: The cities will be for them to dwell in, and the pasture lands will be for their cattle, their flocks, and all their other animals.

text.NRSV: The pasture lands of the towns, which you shall give to the Levites, shall reach from the wall of the town outward a thousand cubits all around."
text.Rotherham: And, the pasture lands of the cities which ye shall give unto the Levite, shall be,—from the wall of the city, and outwards, a thousand cubits round about
text.KJV: 35:4 And the suburbs of the cities, which yee shall giue vnto the Leuites, shall reach from the wall of the citie, and outward, a thousand cubites round about.
text.NABRE: The pasture lands of the cities to be assigned the Levites shall extend a thousand cubits out from the city walls in every direction.

text.NRSV: You shall measure, outside the town, for the east side two thousand cubits, for the south side two thousand cubits, for the west side two thousand cubits, and for the north side two thousand cubits, with the town in the middle. This shall belong to them as pasture land for their towns."
text.Rotherham: So ye shall measure—on the outside of the city—the eastward quarter two thousand by the cubit, and the south quarter two thousand by the cubit, and the west quarter two thousand by the cubit and the north quarter two thousand by the cubit, with, the city, in the midst. This, shall be unto them the pasture lands of the cities,
text.KJV: 35:5 And ye shall measure from without the city on the Eastside two thousand cubites, and on the Southside two thousand cubites, and on the Westside two thousand cubites, & on the Northside two thousand cubites: and the citie shall be in the midst; this shalbe to them the suburbs of the cities.
text.NABRE: You will measure out two thousand cubits outside the city along the east side, two thousand cubits along the south side, two thousand cubits along the west side, and two thousand cubits along the north side, with the city lying in the center. These will be the pasture lands of their cities.

text.NRSV: The towns that you give to the Levites shall include the six cities of refuge, where you shall permit a slayer to flee, and in addition to them you shall give forty-two towns."
text.Rotherham: And, among the cities which ye shall give unto the Levites, shall be the six cities of refuge, which ye shall give that the manslayer may flee thither,—and besides them, ye shall give, forty-two cities,
text.KJV: 35:6 And among the cities which yee shal giue vnto the Leuites, there shalbe sixe cities for refuge, which ye shall appoint for the manslayer, that hee may flee thither: And to them ye shall adde fourty and two cities.
text.NABRE: Now these are the cities you will give to the Levites: the six cities of asylum which you must establish for the homicide to run to, and in addition forty-two other cities—

text.NRSV: The towns that you thus give shall be forty-eight in all, together with their pasture lands."
text.Rotherham: All the cities which ye shall give unto the Levites, shall be forty-eight cities,—them, and their pasture lands,
text.KJV: 35:7 So all the cities which ye shall giue to the Leuites, shall be fourty and eight cities: them shall yee giue with their suburbs.
text.NABRE: a total of forty-eight cities with their pasture lands which you will assign to the Levites.

text.NRSV: And as for the towns that you shall give from the possession of the Israelites, from the larger tribes you shall take many, and from the smaller tribes you shall take few; each, in proportion to the inheritance that it obtains, shall give of its towns to the Levites."
text.Rotherham: And as touching the cities which ye shall give out of the possession of the sons of Israel from the many, shall ye take many, and from the few, shall ye take few,—each, according to his inheritance, which they shall inherit, shall give of his cities unto the Levites.
text.KJV: 35:8 And the cities which ye shal giue, shalbe of the possession of the children of Israel: from them that haue many ye shall giue many; but from them that haue few, ye shall giue few. Euery one shal giue of his cities vnto the Leuites, according to his inheritance which he inheriteth.
text.NABRE: In assigning the cities from what the Israelites possess, take more from a larger group and fewer from a smaller one, so that each will cede cities to the Levites in proportion to the heritage which it receives.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:"
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: 35:9 ¶ And the Lord spake vnto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Cities of Asylum. The Lord spoke to Moses:

text.NRSV: Speak to the Israelites, and say to them: When you cross the Jordan into the land of Canaan,"
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them,—When ye are passing over the Jordan unto the land of Canaan,
text.KJV: 35:10 Speake vnto the children of Israel, and say vnto them, When ye bee come ouer Iordane, into the land of Canaan:
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When you go across the Jordan into the land of Canaan,

text.NRSV: then you shall select cities to be cities of refuge for you, so that a slayer who kills a person without intent may flee there."
text.Rotherham: then shall ye find for you, convenient cities, cities of refuge, shall they be unto you,—and the manslayer, who hath slain a person, by mistake, shall flee thither.
text.KJV: 35:11 Then ye shall appoint you cities, to be cities of refuge for you; that the slayer may flee thither which killeth any person at vnawares.
text.NABRE: select for yourselves cities to serve as cities of asylum, where a homicide who has killed someone inadvertently may flee.

text.NRSV: The cities shall be for you a refuge from the avenger, so that the slayer may not die until there is a trial before the congregation."
text.Rotherham: So shall the cities serve you for places of refuge from the redeemer,—that the manslayer may not die, until he has stood before the assembly, for judgment,
text.KJV: 35:12 And they shall be vnto you cities for refuge from the auenger, that the man-slayer die not, vntill he stand before the Congregation in iudgement.
text.NABRE: These cities will serve you as places of asylum from the avenger of blood, so that a homicide will not be put to death until tried before the community.

text.NRSV: The cities that you designate shall be six cities of refuge for you:"
text.Rotherham: And as touching the cities which ye shall give six cities of refuge, shall there be unto you.
text.KJV: 35:13 And of these cities which ye shall giue, sixe cities shall ye haue for refuge.
text.NABRE: As for the cities you assign, you will have six cities of asylum:

text.NRSV: you shall designate three cities beyond the Jordan, and three cities in the land of Canaan, to be cities of refuge."
text.Rotherham: Three of the cities, shall ye give, on this side the Jordan, and three of the cities, shall ye give in the land of Canaan,—cities of refuge, shall they be.
text.KJV: 35:14 Yee shall giue three cities on this side Iordane, and three cities shall yee giue in the land of Canaan, which shall be cities of refuge.
text.NABRE: you will designate three cities beyond the Jordan, and you will designate three cities in the land of Canaan. These will be cities of asylum.

text.NRSV: These six cities shall serve as refuge for the Israelites, for the resident or transient alien among them, so that anyone who kills a person without intent may flee there."
text.Rotherham: For the sons of Israel and for the sojourner. and for the settler in your midst, shall these six cities serve, as places of refuge,—that any one may flee thither who hath slain a person, by mistake.
text.KJV: 35:15 These sixe cities shall be a refuge, both for the children of Israel, and for the stranger, and for the soiourner among them: that euery one that killeth any person vnawares, may flee thither.
text.NABRE: These six cities will serve as places of asylum for the Israelites, and for the resident or transient aliens among them, so that anyone who has killed a person inadvertently may flee there.

text.NRSV: But anyone who strikes another with an iron object, and death ensues, is a murderer; the murderer shall be put to death."
text.Rotherham: If, then, with an instrument of iron, he smote him, and he died, a manslayer, he is,—a manslayer, shall surely die.
text.KJV: 35:16 And if he smite him with an instrument of Iron, (so that he die,) hee is a murderer: the murderer shall surely be put to death.
text.NABRE: Murder and Manslaughter. If someone strikes another with an iron instrument and causes death, that person is a murderer, and the murderer must be put to death.

text.NRSV: Or anyone who strikes another with a stone in hand that could cause death, and death ensues, is a murderer; the murderer shall be put to death."
text.Rotherham: Or if with a stone thrown by hand, where-by one might die, he smote him and he died, a manslayer, he is,—the man-slayer, shall surely die,
text.KJV: 35:17 And if he smite him with throwing a stone, (wherewith hee may die) and he die, he is a murderer: the murderer shall surely be put to death.
text.NABRE: If someone strikes another with a death-dealing stone in the hand and death results, that person is a murderer, and the murderer must be put to death.

text.NRSV: Or anyone who strikes another with a weapon of wood in hand that could cause death, and death ensues, is a murderer; the murderer shall be put to death."
text.Rotherham: Or, if with hand-weapon of wood, wherewith one might die, he smote him and he died a manslayer, he is,—the manslayer shall surely die.
text.KJV: 35:18 Or if he smite him with an handweapon of wood, (wherewith he may die) and he die, hee is a murderer: the murderer shall surely be put to death.
text.NABRE: Or if someone strikes another with a death-dealing club in the hand and death results, that person is a murderer, and the murderer must be put to death.

text.NRSV: The avenger of blood is the one who shall put the murderer to death; when they meet, the avenger of blood shall execute the sentence."
text.Rotherham: The blood-redeemer, he, shall put to death the man-slayer,—when he lighteth upon him, he, shall put him to death.
text.KJV: 35:19 The reuenger of blood himselfe shall slay the murtherer: when he meeteth him, he shall slay him.
text.NABRE: The avenger of blood is the one who will kill the murderer, putting the individual to death on sight.

text.NRSV: Likewise, if someone pushes another from hatred, or hurls something at another, lying in wait, and death ensues,"
text.Rotherham: Or if with hate, he shall thrust at him, or hurl at him designedly, and he have died,
text.KJV: 35:20 But if he thrust him of hatred, or hurle at him by laying of waite that he die,
text.NABRE: If someone pushes another out of hatred, or throws something from an ambush, and death results,

text.NRSV: or in enmity strikes another with the hand, and death ensues, then the one who struck the blow shall be put to death; that person is a murderer; the avenger of blood shall put the murderer to death, when they meet."
text.Rotherham: or if with enmity, he hath smitten him with his hand and he is dead, then shall he that dealt the blow, be surely put to death. a manslayer, he is—the blood-redeemer shall put to death the man-slayer, when he lighteth upon him.
text.KJV: 35:21 Or in enmitie smite him with his hand, that he die: hee that smote him shall surely be put to death, for hee is a murderer: the reuenger of blood shall slay the murderer, when hee meeteth him.
text.NABRE: or strikes another with the hand out of enmity and death results, the assailant must be put to death as a murderer. The avenger of blood will kill the murderer on sight.

text.NRSV: But if someone pushes another suddenly without enmity, or hurls any object without lying in wait,"
text.Rotherham: But if suddenly, without enmity, he shall thrust him, or hurl upon him any missile, without design;
text.KJV: 35:22 But if hee thrust him suddenly without enmitie, or haue cast vpon him any thing without laying of wait,
text.NABRE: However, if someone pushes another without malice aforethought, or without lying in ambush throws some object at another,

text.NRSV: or, while handling any stone that could cause death, unintentionally drops it on another and death ensues, though they were not enemies, and no harm was intended,"
text.Rotherham: Or with any stone whereby one might die without seeing him, and it fall upon him, and he dieth; he, not being at enmity with him, nor seeking to harm him,
text.KJV: 35:23 Or with any stone wherewith a man may die, seeing him not, and cast it vpon him, that he die, and was not his enemie, neither sought his harme:
text.NABRE: or without seeing drops upon another some death-dealing stone and death results, although there was neither enmity nor malice—

text.NRSV: then the congregation shall judge between the slayer and the avenger of blood, in accordance with these ordinances;"
text.Rotherham: then shall the assembly judge, between him that smote and the blood-redeemer,—according to these regulations;
text.KJV: 35:24 Then the Congregation shall iudge betweene the slayer, and the reuenger of blood, according to these iudgements.
text.NABRE: then the community will judge between the assailant and the avenger of blood in accordance with these norms.

text.NRSV: and the congregation shall rescue the slayer from the avenger of blood. Then the congregation shall send the slayer back to the original city of refuge. The slayer shall live in it until the death of the high priest who was anointed with the holy oil."
text.Rotherham: and the assembly shall rescue the manslayer out of the hand of the blood-redeemer, and the assembly shall restore him unto his city of refuge whither he had fled,—and he shall dwell therein until the death of the high priest, who hath been anointed with the hallowing oil.
text.KJV: 35:25 And the Congregation shall deliuer the slayer out of the hand of the reuenger of blood, and the Congregation shal restore him to the city of his refuge, whither he was fled: and he shall abide in it vnto the death of the high Priest, which was annoynted with the holy oyle.
text.NABRE: The community will deliver the homicide from the avenger of blood and the community will return the homicide to the city of asylum where the latter had fled; and the individual will stay there until the death of the high priest who has been anointed with sacred oil.

text.NRSV: But if the slayer shall at any time go outside the bounds of the original city of refuge,"
text.Rotherham: But if the man-slayer, go out, of the bounds of his city of refuge, whereinto he hath fled;
text.KJV: 35:26 But if the slayer shall at any time come without the border of the citie of his refuge, whither he was fled:
text.NABRE: If the homicide leaves at all the bounds of the city of asylum to which flight had been made,

text.NRSV: and is found by the avenger of blood outside the bounds of the city of refuge, and is killed by the avenger, no bloodguilt shall be incurred."
text.Rotherham: and the blood-redeemer, find him, outside the bounds of his city of refuge, then may the blood-redeemer slay the slayer, without being guilty of blood;
text.KJV: 35:27 And the reuenger of blood finde him without the borders of the citie of his refuge, and the reuenger of blood kill the slayer, he shall not be guiltie of blood:
text.NABRE: and is found by the avenger of blood beyond the bounds of the city of asylum, and the avenger of blood kills the homicide, the avenger incurs no bloodguilt;

text.NRSV: For the slayer must remain in the city of refuge until the death of the high priest; but after the death of the high priest the slayer may return home."
text.Rotherham: for in his city of refuge, should he have remained, until the death of the high priest,—and, after the death of the high priest, might he have returned into the land which he doth possess.
text.KJV: 35:28 Because he should haue remained in the citie of his refuge, vntill the death of the high Priest: but after the death of the hie Priest, the slayer shal returne into the land of his possession.
text.NABRE: for the homicide was required to stay in the city of asylum until the death of the high priest. Only after the death of the high priest may the homicide return to the land of the homicide’s possession.

text.NRSV: These things shall be a statute and ordinance for you throughout your generations wherever you live."
text.Rotherham: So shall these serve you as a regulative statute unto your generations,—wheresoever ye may dwell.
text.KJV: 35:29 So these things shall be for a statute of iudgment vnto you, thorowout your generations in al your dwellings.
text.NABRE: This is the statute for you throughout all your generations, wherever you live, for rendering judgment.

text.NRSV: If anyone kills another, the murderer shall be put to death on the evidence of witnesses; but no one shall be put to death on the testimony of a single witness."
text.Rotherham: Whosoever taketh away life, at the mouth of witnesses, shall, the slayer be slain,—but, one witness, shall not testify against a person, to put him to death.
text.KJV: 35:30 Who so killeth any person, the murderer shall be put to death, by the mouth of witnesses: but one witnesse shall not testifie against any person, to cause him to die.
text.NABRE: Judgment. Whenever someone kills another, the evidence of witnesses is required to kill the murderer. A single witness does not suffice for putting a person to death.

text.NRSV: Moreover you shall accept no ransom for the life of a murderer who is subject to the death penalty; a murderer must be put to death."
text.Rotherham: And ye shall accept no ransom for the life of him that slayeth, if he have unlawfully caused death,—but he, must surely be put to death.
text.KJV: 35:31 Moreouer, yee shall take no satisfaction for the life of a murderer, which is guiltie of death, but he shalbe surely put to death.
text.NABRE: No Indemnity. You will not accept compensation in place of the life of a murderer who deserves to die, but that person must be put to death.

text.NRSV: Nor shall you accept ransom for one who has fled to a city of refuge, allowing the fugitive to return to live in the land before the death of the high priest."
text.Rotherham: And ye shall accept no ransom for him that hath fled to his city of refuge, if he should return to dwell in the land before the death of the priest.
text.KJV: 35:32 And yee shall take no satisfaction for him that is fled to the citie of his refuge, that hee should come againe to dwell in the land, vntil the death of the Priest.
text.NABRE: Nor will you accept compensation to allow one who has fled to a city of asylum to return to live in the land before the death of the high priest.

text.NRSV: You shall not pollute the land in which you live; for blood pollutes the land, and no expiation can be made for the land, for the blood that is shed in it, except by the blood of the one who shed it."
text.Rotherham: So shall ye not pollute the land wherein ye are, for blood, doth pollute the land, and for the land, no propitiatory-covering can be made, as touching blood, that is shed therein, save with the blood of him that shed it;
text.KJV: 35:33 So ye shall not pollute the lande wherein ye are: for blood, it defileth the land: and the land cannot bee cleansed of the blood that is shed therein, but by the blood of him that shed it.
text.NABRE: You will not pollute the land where you live. For bloodshed pollutes the land, and the land can have no expiation for the blood shed on it except through the blood of the one who shed it.

text.NRSV: You shall not defile the land in which you live, in which I also dwell; for I the Lord dwell among the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: thou must not then make unclean the land wherein, ye, are dwelling, in the midst of which, I, have my habitation; for, I,—Yahweh, am making my habitation in the midst of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 35:34 Defile not therefore the lande which yee shall inhabite, wherein I dwell: for I the Lord dwell among the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: Do not defile the land in which you live and in the midst of which I dwell; for I the Lord dwell in the midst of the Israelites.

text.NRSV: The heads of the ancestral houses of the clans of the descendants of Gilead son of Machir son of Manasseh, of the Josephite clans, came forward and spoke in the presence of Moses and the leaders, the heads of the ancestral houses of the Israelites;"
text.Rotherham: Then came near the heads of fathers, belonging to the family of the sons of Gilead son of Machir son of Manasseh, out of the families of the sons of Joseph,—and spake before Moses, and before the princes, heads of fathers belonging to the sons of Israel;
text.KJV: 36:1 And the chiefe fathers of the families of the children of Gilead, the sonne of Machir, the sonne of Manasseh, of the families of the sonnes of Ioseph, came neere, and spake before Moses, and before the Princes the chiefe fathers of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: Chapter 36 Inheritance of Daughters. The heads of the ancestral houses in a clan of the descendants of Gilead, son of Machir, son of Manasseh—one of the Josephite clans—came up and spoke before Moses and Eleazar the priest and before the leaders who were the heads of the ancestral houses of the Israelites.

text.NRSV: they said, "The Lord commanded my lord to give the land for inheritance by lot to the Israelites; and my lord was commanded by the Lord to give the inheritance of our brother Zelophehad to his daughters."
text.Rotherham: and they said—Unto my lord, did Yahweh give command, to bestow the land as an inheritance, by lot, unto the sons of Israel,—my lord, therefore, was commanded by Yahweh, to give the inheritance of Zelophehad our brother unto his daughters.
text.KJV: 36:2 And they said, The Lord commanded my lord to giue the lande for an inheritance by lot to the children of Israel: and my lord was commanded by the Lord, to giue the inheritance of Zelophehad our brother, vnto his daughters.
text.NABRE: They said: “The Lord commanded my lord to apportion the land by lot for a heritage among the Israelites; and my lord was commanded by the Lord to give the heritage of Zelophehad our kinsman to his daughters.

text.NRSV: But if they are married into another Israelite tribe, then their inheritance will be taken from the inheritance of our ancestors and added to the inheritance of the tribe into which they marry; so it will be taken away from the allotted portion of ours."
text.Rotherham: When, therefore, unto any one from among the sons of the other tribes of the sons of Israel, they become wives their inheritance shall disappear out of the inheritance of our fathers, and be added to the inheritance of the tribe to which they shall be received,—and so out of the lot of our inheritance, shall it disappear,
text.KJV: 36:3 And if they bee married to any of the sonnes of the other tribes of the children of Israel, then shall their inheritance be taken from the inheritance of our fathers, and shall bee put to the inheritance of the tribe, whereinto they are receiued: so shal it be taken from the lot of our inheritance.
text.NABRE: But if they marry into one of the other Israelite tribes, their heritage will be withdrawn from our ancestral heritage and will be added to that of the tribe into which they marry; thus the heritage that fell to us by lot will be diminished.

text.NRSV: When the jubilee of the Israelites comes, then their inheritance will be added to the inheritance of the tribe into which they have married; and their inheritance will be taken from the inheritance of our ancestral tribe."
text.Rotherham: And when the jubilee shall come to the sons of Israel, then shall their inheritance be added unto the inheritance of the tribe to which they shall be received—and, out of the, inheritance of the tribe of our fathers, shall their inheritance disappear.
text.KJV: 36:4 And when the Iubile of the children of Israel shall be, then shall their inheritance be put vnto the inheritance of the tribe, whereunto they are receiued: So shal their inheritance be taken away from the inheritance of the tribe of our fathers.
text.NABRE: When the Israelites celebrate the jubilee year, the heritage of these women will be added to that of the tribe into which they marry and their heritage will be withdrawn from that of our ancestral tribe.”
